title: Refresh the Products Provisioned to Your Docusign Account with the TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Refresh the Products Provisioned to Your Docusign Account with the TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn to activate and update Docusign for Salesforce apps and settings
using the Refresh Products feature.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

You can activate and update apps and settings in your Salesforce organization using the
Refresh Products feature.

- Switch any newly added products like Docusign Gen from
  Pending to Active
- Update the Account Products Last Checked custom setting
- Keep account feature settings up to date

Follow this procedure to use the Refresh Products feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.nFurgpRhlTMCEMfZj4pX1AERTqpLcehw_4GpqJJTiWg&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.nFurgpRhlTMCEMfZj4pX1AERTqpLcehw_4GpqJJTiWg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.
4. Refresh Products: Select Execute.

   The page refreshes. A message opens: Successfully refreshed
   products on Docusign account.

You have completed the process. You have updated settings and
activated pending apps.

title: Use Manage App Policies to Allow or Prevent Docusign User Access
heading_path: Use Manage App Policies to Allow or Prevent Docusign User Access
text:
Learn how to use Manage App Policies to allow or prevent users
from authorizing their Docusign access.

New Docusign app users see an Authorize button when
they access Docusign content on a record. New users must enter
their Salesforce and Docusign credentials to access Docusign apps. Administrators can access the Manage App
Policies page to allow or prevent users from self-authorizing.

Use this procedure to manage your Docusign app policies:

1. Select Manage App Policies.

   ![A screenshot displays the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page with the Docusign Manage App Policies link highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Salesforce-Manage-App-Policies-link.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NDQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.i_Kx0p8YqSxbOVt4y_T4XNCgJ8wdiL9D0BluQc9Qpr4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page with the Docusign Manage App Policies link highlighted.")
2. Select Edit Policies.
3. Permitted Users: Select an option from the dropdown
   menu:

   - All users may self-authorize: Users may select the
     Authorize button to use Docusign apps.
   - Admin approved users are pre-authorized: Administrators assign Docusign app access to specific groups.

     Note: Enabling the Admin approved users are
     pre-authorized option denies access to all current
     users. Administrators use Salesforce profiles to assign
     access.
4. Select Save.

   The page closes. The Docusign Connected App Detail
   page displays.

You have completed the process. You have allowed or prevented
users from authorizing their Docusign access in your Salesforce
organization.

title: Step 7: Create a Custom Button or Finish the Template
heading_path: Step 7: Create a Custom Button or Finish the Template
text:
This step is optional. You can choose to create a custom button instead of generating
invoices automatically.

Use this procedure if you want to create a custom
button instead of generating invoices automatically. If you add a custom button,
Docusign generates invoices when a user selects the button.

Tip:
[The beginning of this video
explains this step](https://bcove.video/3mHgtcg).

Important: If
you have chosen to automate invoice generation, skip these steps and select
Save and Finish.

1. Button Label: Enter a descriptive name to let users know
   the purpose.
2. Page Layouts: Select the check box for each layout you
   want to update.
3. Select Update Layouts.
4. (Optional) Post Generation Actions: Configure e-signature
   options for this template:

   - Enable the Send for Signature button, so generators can send
     generated documents out for signature. This option shows
     or hides the Send for Signature button after
     users generate documents.
   - Launch automatically after generation. This
     option opens the standard Send with Docusign
     experience after a user generates a document. Users do not need to
     select the Send for Signature button after generating.
   - Select an Envelope Template to streamline sending for
     signature: Enter the name of an envelope template. This
     option opens the envelope template sending experience after document
     generation.

     Note: You can link a send
     now envelope template to enable a [one-click document
     generation and sending experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sjm1686076461925.html&_LANG=enus).
5. Select Save and Finish.

You have completed the entire process. You have built a
Docusign Gen for Billing template. Invoices generate automatically or when users select
the custom button on an invoice record.

title: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to open the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool in your Salesforce
organization.

title: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
Before you begin
text:
You need Salesforce administrator and Docusign administrator
permissions.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

Use this procedure to open the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NzIsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.mrLETb8CrHNS3Wxo4R2GEThAJpp3aNrCLn6lwitffZE&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NzIsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.mrLETb8CrHNS3Wxo4R2GEThAJpp3aNrCLn6lwitffZE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

You have completed the process. You have opened the Docusign Apps
Launcher TroubleShooting tool. You can use this tool to execute account-wide changes.

title: Create a Gen Template With the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor
heading_path: Create a Gen Template With the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor
text:
Learn to create a Gen template with the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor.

You can create Gen templates using the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor instead of Microsoft
Word. Docusign uses Agreement Document Format (ADF) for generated documents. ADF documents
automatically re-size for phones, tablets, and computers.

The following are the benefits of using the Agreement Cloud Editor to build Gen templates:

- Build and edit templates in the browser instead of using Microsoft Word
- Stay within Salesforce without logging into Docusign
- Import and edit existing Word documents
- Drag Salesforce and Docusign fields to your template without pasting
  XML code
- Add conditional logic and tables to your template

Use this procedure to create a Gen template with the Agreement Cloud Editor:

1. Open the Create a Template Configuration page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Go to
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Io3GOORt2rPMiFZWYiKCoTVdzv5t8nT_OnRG_VNnhj0&_LANG=enus) > View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select Document Generation in the left column.
   3. Select Create a Template Configuration.

   The Create New Template dialog box opens.
2. Choose a name, data source, and editor for your template:
   1. Add Template Name: Enter a name for your template.
   2. Select Data Source: Select a Salesforce object to import
      data from.
   3. Select Template Editor: Select Docusign Agreement
      Cloud Editor.
   4. Select Create.

   The Create page opens.
3. Enter text or select Upload to create your template
   document.
4. Add Salesforce fields to your template document:
   1. Select the Salesforce Fields tab ![A screenshot displays an icon with two interlocking blue rectangles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacesalesforcefieldsicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Io3GOORt2rPMiFZWYiKCoTVdzv5t8nT_OnRG_VNnhj0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an icon with two interlocking blue rectangles.") in the left column.
   2. Drag a Salesforce field to the document or place the cursor in the text and select
      a field.![A screen capture displays a cursor dragging Salesforce fields to a document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/ACE-SFDC.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Io3GOORt2rPMiFZWYiKCoTVdzv5t8nT_OnRG_VNnhj0&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays a cursor dragging Salesforce fields to a document.")

   Note: The menu displays fields from your chosen data source and
   related objects. You can select up to four levels of child objects and two levels of
   Related Lists.
5. Add Docusign fields to your template document:
   1. Select the Docusign Fields tab ![A screenshot displays a blue rectangle icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210303-at-124311-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Io3GOORt2rPMiFZWYiKCoTVdzv5t8nT_OnRG_VNnhj0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a blue rectangle icon.") in the left column.
   2. Drag a Docusign field to the document or place the cursor in the text and select a
      field.
   3. Select Next.

title: Create a Gen Template With the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor
heading_path: Create a Gen Template With the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor
text:

   2. Drag a Docusign field to the document or place the cursor in the text and select a
      field.
   3. Select Next.

   The Deploy page opens.
6. Set File Naming Rules: Choose the naming convention used when
   users generate files from this template:
   1. File Name: Enter the beginning text for all generated file
      names. If you enter Fontara, the file name of each generated
      document begins with the word Fontara.
   2. File Suffix: [Choose the
      end text for all generated file names](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=nxb1586134245032.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the file name logic in the Gen template Upload Documents step."):

      - Main Data Source Name: Add your customer's name to the
        generated file name
      - Date: Add the document generation date to the generated

7. Add Custom Button: Add a new custom button to your Salesforce

   1. Button Label: Enter a descriptive name to demonstrate the

   2. Show button in: Select the check box for each layout that
      you want to update.
   3. Select Publish.

   A message displays: Template (name) buttons
   updated.

You have completed the process. You created an
Agreement Cloud Editor Gen template and added a custom button to a Salesforce layout. Approved
Gen users can generate contracts from Salesforce records.

title: Use the Docusign Status Tab to View the Status of an Envelope
heading_path: Use the Docusign Status Tab to View the Status of an Envelope
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign Status tab to view the status of your Docusign
envelopes.

Docusign envelopes are bundles of information sent to recipients for
electronic signature. Envelopes include documents, recipients, and Docusign and Salesforce fields.

The Docusign Status tab lets you view the status of your Docusign envelopes. Envelope status entries include these
details:

- Envelope #
- Docusign envelope ID
- Sender Name
- Sender Email
- Email Subject
- Status
- Owner
- Sent
- Expires
- Completed
- Declined or Voided Reason
- Time to Complete
- Created By
- Last Modified By
- Related Objects

Use this procedure to view the status details of a Docusign envelope in your
Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Status tab.
2. Select an envelope from the Envelope # column.

   The Docusign Envelope details page opens for that
   item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the status details of an
envelope sent from a Salesforce record.

title: Step 6: (Optional) Automatically Generate and Email Invoices
heading_path: Step 6: (Optional) Automatically Generate and Email Invoices
text:
This step is optional. You can choose to generate and email invoices automatically, instead of creating a custom button that your users select to generate invoices. This step is optional. Use this procedure to generate and email invoices automatically. If you choose to automate this task, invoices automatically generate when an invoice record stage changes to Posted. Invoices are saved in the Notes & Attachments section of the record. If you do not want to automate invoices, select the Next button on this page to create a custom button. Note: - You can only set up one automated invoice template at a time. If you try to set up another, a Skip button displays. Select it to move to step seven. - You can generate a maximum of 1,000 invoices per hour. Tip: [This step is explained in full at the beginning of this video](https://bcove.video/3uKM2F0). 1. Select the Set up automatic invoice PDF generation and save to parent invoice record check box. 2. (Optional) Send invoices by email: Select this check box to email the invoice to recipients. The Add Recipients section opens. Note: The Bill to Contact (Order) recipient is added by default. You can add up to four more recipients. 3. (Optional) Choose any steps from the Add Recipients section: - Add Recipient: Select this button and choose options from the dialog box that opens: - Record Fields: Dynamically add recipients from a record’s Bill To Contact or Created By field. - Name and Email: Add any recipient by entering a name and email address - Set as Bcc: Select the check box to hide this recipient from others - Add the recipient to the template: - Add: Save the recipient and close the dialog box - Add & New: Save the recipient and add more ![Add Recipient dialog box](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Add-Recipient-modal.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5MjksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.hHWifuMJptUGDN8s3Bir_ZTyww4mw40qtdlMqXFmOKQ&_LANG=enus "Add Recipient dialog box") - Message to All Recipients: - Email Subject: Edit text in the subject field. - Email Message: Edit text in the message field. Use the HTML tag
to include line breaks in your message. 4. Select Next. The Custom Button page opens. You have completed step six. Move to [Step 7: Create a Custom Button or Finish the Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=aup1614501330150.html&_LANG=enus "This step is optional. You can choose to create a custom button instead of generating invoices automatically.").

title: Add Conditional Logic to a Gen Template to Show or Hide Content
heading_path: Add Conditional Logic to a Gen Template to Show or Hide Content
text:
Learn to add conditional logic to your Word-based Gen template.

You can add conditional logic blocks to Gen template Anchor Text Tags. Conditional
logic creates "if-then" statements that add or exclude text from a generated
agreement.

You can use conditional logic to create one Word document that can generate different
content for different customers.

The following procedure demonstrates adding conditional logic to a customer
agreement:

1. Select Copy![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NTAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.z8fg0zJSgzmaXvyo7_qwbms_FBtbw2GbWXDjK7q-p-M&_LANG=enus)
    next to a field and choose
   Options. ![A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-description-field-with-the-copy-menu-open-to-options-and-remove.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NTAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.z8fg0zJSgzmaXvyo7_qwbms_FBtbw2GbWXDjK7q-p-M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.")

   The Table Options dialog box opens.
2. Create match logic:
   1. Select Include other content if the conditions set for this
      field are met.
   2. matches: Keep this value.
   3. Enter New York in the text field.
   4. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the display options field with the values set to matches and New York.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Add-conditional-logic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NTAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.z8fg0zJSgzmaXvyo7_qwbms_FBtbw2GbWXDjK7q-p-M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the display options field with the values set to matches and New York.")

   The Billing State/Province anchor text changes to `<#
   <Conditional Select="/Opportunity/Account.BillingState" Match="New York"
   /> #> YOUR CONTENT HERE <#
   <EndConditional/> #>`
3. Select Copy next to the Billing
   State/Province anchor text.
4. Add the conditional anchor text to your Word document:
   1. Open the Gen template's Word document.
   2. Where you want the New York legal clause to display, paste
      `<# <Conditional
      Select="/Opportunity/Account.BillingState" Match="New York" />
      #>`.
   3. Replace the YOUR CONTENT HERE text with your New
      York legal clause text.
   4. Enter `<# <EndConditional/> #>` at the end
      of the New York legal clause text. ![A screenshot displays conditional logic tags surrounding legal text for New York customers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Conditional-text.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NTAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.z8fg0zJSgzmaXvyo7_qwbms_FBtbw2GbWXDjK7q-p-M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays conditional logic tags surrounding legal text for New York customers.")
5. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").
6. Select Save and Close and select
   Save on the Do you want to save
   changes? dialog box.

You have completed the process. You have added conditional logic
for specific customers. Generated agreements from New York customers display the New
York state legal clause.

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
text: Learn to connect your IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")

To use Docusign apps in your Salesforce organization, you must first connect
Docusign and Salesforce. You create this connection by
entering Salesforce and Docusign administrator credentials on the
Docusign Setup page. The Docusign
administrator used to connect accounts is called the integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to a Docusign production account:

1. Update Session Settings as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2MjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.yeT2R0eE3BrlWzo5qqZO-dCBpvUKWDt9JC97wY2IWUU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Session Settings in the Quick
      Find field.
   3. Uncheck Lock sessions to the IP address from which they
      originated.
   4. Uncheck Lock sessions to the domain in which they were first
      used.![A screenshot displays two deselected options for Lock sessions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-deselected-options-for-Lock-sessions-to-the-IP-address-and-Lock-sessions-to-the-domain.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2MjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.yeT2R0eE3BrlWzo5qqZO-dCBpvUKWDt9JC97wY2IWUU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays two deselected options for Lock sessions.")
   5. Select Save.
2. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that opens.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that opens
      and select Log In.

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
Before you begin
text:
he Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that opens.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that opens
      and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page opens.
3. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login dialog box opens.
   2. Select Advanced Options.
   3. Other Environment: Enter
      USFED in the field and select Log
      in.![A screenshot displays the Connect to Docusign page with Advanced Options and Other Environment set to USFED.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Other-Environment-field-with-USFED-entered-on-the-Connect-Docusign-to-Salesforce-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2MjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.yeT2R0eE3BrlWzo5qqZO-dCBpvUKWDt9JC97wY2IWUU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect to Docusign page with Advanced Options and Other Environment set to USFED.")
   4. Enter Docusign administrator credentials in the
      dialog box that opens and select Log in.
   5. Select Use Custom Domain in the Salesforce
      dialog box that opens.
   6. Enter your Salesforce domain in the Custom
      Domain field and select
      Continue.![A screenshot displays the Use Custom Domain field filled with an example domain.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Use-Custom-Domain-field-filled-with-an-example-domain.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2MjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.yeT2R0eE3BrlWzo5qqZO-dCBpvUKWDt9JC97wY2IWUU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Use Custom Domain field filled with an example domain.")
   7. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      opens and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box opens.
4. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page opens.
5. Edit the Salesforce Connect URL in your Docusign
   account:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator
      and select Settings.
   2. Select Connect in the left column.
   3. Docusign Apps Launcher: Select
      Actions and choose
      Edit.
   4. ULR to Publish: Change
      act.mil in the URL to
      fed-act-event.

      - [Correct example](https://act-event.docusign.mil/listeners/connect-salesforce)
      - [Incorrect example](https://fed-act-event.docusign.com/listeners/connect-salesforce)
   5. Select SAVE CONFIGURATION.

You have completed the process. You have connected your
IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to your Docusign account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Delete a Gen Button
heading_path: Delete a Gen Button
text:
Learn to remove a Gen custom button from your Salesforce page layouts.

Use this procedure to remove a Gen custom button from
your Salesforce layouts:

1. Access the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.jP7ixuInar9F7kf2Lz2NZYJmEMMQGa-2E9ssc3yKzv8&_LANG=enus) icon and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select an object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) in the left navigation
      bar.
   4. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.jP7ixuInar9F7kf2Lz2NZYJmEMMQGa-2E9ssc3yKzv8&_LANG=enus) next to a custom button and
   choose Delete from the dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.jP7ixuInar9F7kf2Lz2NZYJmEMMQGa-2E9ssc3yKzv8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.")

3. Select Delete. The page refreshes with the button no longer

   Note: Salesforce may display an error:
   Your request to delete this custom button or link can not be completed
   because this button or link is being used by another feature. Remove the

You have completed the process. You have removed the
button from Salesforce layouts.

title: Use the Docusign Setup Page to Administer Your Docusign Gen Account
heading_path: Use the Docusign Setup Page to Administer Your Docusign Gen Account
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign Setup page to manage Gen and eSignature
users, components, templates, and more.

Docusign Setup is the main landing page for managing Gen and
eSignature for Salesforce users, components, templates, and more.

You access this page by [opening the Docusign Apps Launcher package in your Salesforce
organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.").

![A screenshot displays Docusign Setup page options like eSignature, document generation, User Management, and Components.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-Docusign-Setup-page-options-like-eSignature-Document-Generation-User-Management-and-Components.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2NTQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.P5xXopOOTqLZjW6opAUIBFvaJNoBNGaXzy5CVBJHtdU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Docusign Setup page options like eSignature, document generation, User Management, and Components.")

The Docusign Setup page displays the following resources:

- Home: Manage and configure the Gen and eSignature for
  Salesforce apps.
- [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xsw1586134361444.html&_LANG=enus): Configure your eSignature for Salesforce
  templates, buttons, and settings.
- [Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zgw1621457735347.html&_LANG=enus): Create, edit, and delete Docusign Gen templates from this page.
- [User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zbp1629859390541.html&_LANG=enus): Add and manage Docusign Gen and eSignature users.
- [Components](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=xkm1586134206424.html&_LANG=enus): Customize layouts and experiences by adding
  custom components.
- Help: Learn how to get the most from Docusign
  Apps Launcher:
  - [Support Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US): Customers learn to use and
    troubleshoot Docusign products.
  - [Community](https://support.docusign.com/forum): Docusign customers
    ask and answer questions.
  - [Docusign University](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/page/all-courses): Docusign customers build expertise.

title: Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts
heading_path: Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts
text:
Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your
Salesforce layouts.

You can uninstall Apps Launcher without removing your Docusign custom
buttons. But consider removing these buttons to maintain a clean layout and avoid
confusing your users.

Use this procedure to remove all Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce
layouts:

1. Open the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as an

Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.r02Nn6fmE9UmHiPaE9qvAri7lrCJPdK4XlX8de2PRrY&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick
      Find field.

   4. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions.

      The Buttons, Links, and Actions page displays.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.r02Nn6fmE9UmHiPaE9qvAri7lrCJPdK4XlX8de2PRrY&_LANG=enus) next to a Docusign button and choose
   Delete from the dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays the arrow menu with the delete button highlighted for a custom button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.r02Nn6fmE9UmHiPaE9qvAri7lrCJPdK4XlX8de2PRrY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the arrow menu with the delete button highlighted for a custom button.")

   A dialog box displays: Are you sure?

title: Remove and Close Docusign Users
heading_path: Remove and Close Docusign Users
text:
Learn to remove access and close Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature
users.

You can use the Remove and Close command to revoke a Salesforce user's
access to Docusign.

- Remove and Close removes a Salesforce user's access to
  Docusign apps and the connected Docusign account. This command does not delete
  Salesforce users.
- You can not remove and close your user while logged on.
- You can not remove and close the only eSignature administrator user.

Use this procedure to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access and close Docusign users in the connected account:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to remove and close.
2. Select Remove and Close from the Add
   Users
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MTIsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.17iKxAvDLAHEa1o7ULxZZuBEWwywZ_0uXPGyBAV2xsY&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu.
3. Select Remove.

You have completed the process. You removed a Salesforce user's
access to the Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce apps. You also
closed (removed) the user from the connected Docusign account. The user remains in your
Salesforce organization.

title: View the Related Objects of an Envelope
heading_path: View the Related Objects of an Envelope
text:
Learn to view objects related to the record from which the envelope was
sent.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that holds documents,
recipients, and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by completing the
fields assigned to them.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. The
Docusign Status tab lets you view the status of your
Docusign envelopes.

Docusign Status details include: Docusign envelope ID,
Sender Name, Sender Email,
Email Subject, and Related
Objects.

Use this procedure to view objects related to the record from which the envelope was
sent:

1. Select the Docusign Status tab.
2. Select an envelope from the Envelope # column.
3. Select Details.
4. Related Objects: Select a related object link.

   The related object page opens.

You have completed the task. You have opened an object
related to the record from which the envelope was sent.

title: Download Agreement Cloud Editor Documents as Word Files
heading_path: Download Agreement Cloud Editor Documents as Word Files
text:
Learn how to download Agreement Document Format Gen contracts as Microsoft Word
documents.

[Agreement Cloud
Editor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=bcp1586134298132.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a Gen template with the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor.") templates create documents in ADF (Agreement Document Format.) ADF is
an HTML-based document type that you can create and edit on a Salesforce page.

You
cannot send ADF Gen documents for internal approval or external review from a
browser. To send ADF documents for review or approval, you can first download them
as Microsoft Word documents.

Use this procedure to download ADF Gen contracts
as Word documents:

1. Select an Agreement Cloud Editor Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   The generated document displays in the browser.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NjcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.P04oZArzMjGRoVh6bAGSOeMQCnPQLACjnTAuft5_Q0o&_LANG=enus) next to the Send for
   Signature button.
3. Select Download as Word File.![A screenshot displays the Download as Word File option under the Send for Signature menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/gendownloadasword.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NjcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.P04oZArzMjGRoVh6bAGSOeMQCnPQLACjnTAuft5_Q0o&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Download as Word File option under the Send for Signature menu.")

   Docusign saves a copy of the document to your device
   in Microsoft Word format.

You have completed the process. You have downloaded a Microsoft
Word copy of the ADF contract. You can send this for internal approval, external review,
or use it to compare changes with a previous version.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
text:
Learn how to use table column XML tags in Word-based Gen templates to import Salesforce
data into a generated agreement.

You can use table column XML tags in Word-based Gen templates to import Salesforce data into a
generated agreement.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Table Column tag
text:
The Table Column tag is represented by the `<TableColumn>` XML
tag. You use a Table Column tag in a table that you add to your Word-based Gen
template. When users generate agreements, Table Column tags dynamically add a column
for each unique piece of record data.

Table Column tags are useful when creating tables for products whose prices change.
The `<TableColumn>` tag adds a new column each time prices
change.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Table Column tag example
text:
You add a Table Column tag to your Gen template. A user generates an agreement from a
Salesforce record that has five product categories. Docusign adds
five table columns; one for each product category.

- Example 2: You create a Table Column tag
  and add a Group Select tag. The Group Select tag groups Salesforce objects by
  your chosen field. You use fields from SBQQ Quotes and SBQQ Quote line items.
  You use content select and ./GroupName for the grouped
  table heading.

  You use the SBQQ Quote ‘Contracting Method’ field
  for suppression. Your picklist has two values: ‘By Subscription End Date’
  and ’Single Contract’.

  `<# <Group Select
  ="
  /SBQQ__Quote__c//SBQQ__LineItems__r
   "
  By="./SBQQ__ProductFamily__c " /> #>`

  `<# <Content Select="./GroupName" /> #>`

  |  |  |  |  |  |
  | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
  | Status | Select and Match | Test Field/String | Test Field/String 2 | Test Text Contains |
  | <#  <TableRow Select=”./G  roupItems/  SBQQ\_\_Li  neItems\_\_r  ” /> #>  <# <Content Select="./S  BQQ\_\_Pro  ductFamily  \_\_c"/> #> | <#  <SuppressTabl  eColumn Select="/SBQQ  \_\_Quote\_\_c/SB  QQ\_\_Contracti  ngMethod\_\_c" NotMatch=”By Subscription  End Date“/>#>  <# <Content Select="./SBQQ  \_\_ProductNam  e\_\_c"/> #> | <#  <SuppressTable  Column Test=”/SBQQ\_\_  Quote\_\_c/SBQQ  \_\_ContractingM  ethod\_\_c[text() =’Single Contract’]“/>#><# <Content Select="/SBQQ\_\_  Quote\_\_c/Creat  edDate"/> #> | <#  <SuppressTable  Column Test=”/SBQQ\_\_  Quote\_\_c/SBQQ  \_\_ContractingM  ethod\_\_c[text() !=’Single Contract’]“/>#> <# <Content Select="/SBQQ\_\_  Quote\_\_c/SBQQ  \_\_Contracting  Method\_\_c"/> #> | <#  <SuppressTableC  olumn Test=”contains(/  SBQQ\_\_Quote\_\_c/  SBQQ\_\_Contracti  ngMethod\_\_c, ‘Single’)“/>#>  <# <Content Select="./SBQQ  \_\_CustomerTotal\_\_  c"/> #> |

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Using Table Column tags in Gen templates
text:
You create custom tables in Word by copying tags from a Gen template and pasting them
after a table column header. Use the following guidelines when creating Table Column
tags:

- Add Table Column tags to the first row of a table.
- The Select attribute adds a new column in the table for each match that it
  finds.
- The Select attribute adds columns in order of the values that it finds on a
  record.
- You can place any merge tag in a table column.
- You can add multiple Table Column tags in a table.
- You can nest Table Column tags. Copy a table that contains a Table Column tag
  and paste it inside the cell of another table.
- A single table cannot contain both Table Row and Table Column tags.
- You can use a maximum of 63 columns in a Microsoft Word table.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Table Column attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in <TableColumn> tags:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath used to select data from an XML document |
| Tag | Can be any String value |
| TagRef | Used with a Tag attribute placed on a Repeat, TableRow, or TableColumn tag |
| TrackName | Specific to Term Sync |

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Column tag
text:
The Suppress Table Column tag is represented by the
`<SuppressTableColumn>` XML tag. You use a Suppress Table
Column tag in a table that you add to your Word-based Gen template. When users
generate agreements, Suppress Table Column tags dynamically hide columns based on
record data. Suppress Table Column tags are useful when a record has no data in a
field. You can also use Suppress Table Column tags to hide data from certain
recipients.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Column example
text:
You add a Suppress Table Column tag to your Gen template. A user generates an
agreement from a Salesforce record containing a Discount
column. The Salesforce record does not contain discounted products. Docusign does not add the Discount column to the generated
agreement.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Using Suppress Table Column tags in Gen templates
text:
You create custom tables in Word by copying tags from a Gen template and pasting them
after a table column header. Use the following guidelines when creating Suppress
Table Column tags:

- <SuppressTableColumn> removes an entire column from a table.
- <SuppressTableColumn> attributes are identical to Conditional tag
  attributes.
- Docusign removes the entire table if the column to be
  suppressed is the only one on the record.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Column attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in <SuppressTableColumn> tags:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath used to select data from an XML document |
| Match | Table column is suppressed (removed) if the data returned matches this value |
| NotMatch | Table column is suppressed (removed) if the data returned does not match this value |
| Test | Conditional option based on Boolean logic |
| TagRef | Used with a Tag attribute placed on a Repeat, Table Row, or Table Column tag |

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Use Suppress Table Column with Table Row Select
text:
You can use Suppress Table Column tags with Table Row Select tags to build a conditional
table. Using these tags together is useful if you want to suppress a column when a
field is null or empty. You can also combine these tags to suppress a column when a
check box field is selected in a table row.

| Name | Type | Quantity | List Price | Discount | Discount % | Unit Price | Total Price |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **<# <TableRow Select=" /Opportunity// Opportunity LineItems" /> #><# <Content Select=". /Name"/> #>** | **<# <Content Select=" ./Product2. Family" /> #>** | **<# <Content Select=" ./Quantity" /> #>** | **<# <Content Select= "./ListPrice "/> #>** | **<# < SuppressTableColumn Test=”count (//OpportunityLineItems/ Discount[text() !=’0,00%’])=0 “/>#><# <Content Select=". /Discount[text()!=’ 0,00%’]" Optional ="true"/> #>** | **Number of oppties <# <Content Select ="count (//OpportunityLineItems) "/> #> --<# <Content Select="count (//OpportunityLineItems/ Discount[text()= ’0,00%’])"/> #>--<# <Content Select="count (//OpportunityLineItems /Discount [text()!=’0,00%’])"/> #>** | **<# <Content Select=". /UnitPrice \_\_c"/> #>** | **<# <Content Select=". /TotalPrice" /> #>** |

title: Step 3: Remove Legacy eSignature Custom Buttons From Your Salesforce Layouts
heading_path: Step 3: Remove Legacy eSignature Custom Buttons From Your Salesforce Layouts
text:
Learn how to remove legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom buttons from your
Salesforce page layouts.

You need to remove legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom buttons before
you can uninstall the legacy app.

Use this procedure to remove all legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom
buttons from your Salesforce layouts:

1. Open the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as an
   administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.on6biMKbTNAOC21ZM2z3SayplrBVWsUw0rN2ddOPCP4&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) link in the left
      column.
   5. Select the Buttons, Links, and Actions link.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.on6biMKbTNAOC21ZM2z3SayplrBVWsUw0rN2ddOPCP4&_LANG=enus) next to the custom button you want to remove.
3. Select Delete from the drop-down menu.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MjAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.on6biMKbTNAOC21ZM2z3SayplrBVWsUw0rN2ddOPCP4&_LANG=enus)
4. Select the Delete button on the Are you
   sure? dialog box.

   The page refreshes with the button no longer displayed.

   Note: You may see a page that
   says: Your request to delete this custom button or link can not
   be completed because this button or link is being used by another
   feature. In this case, navigate to the Salesforce page
   layout for that object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) and remove the
   button from the page layout.
5. Repeat this entire procedure for each Salesforce layout (Account, Opportunity,
   and so on) that contains a Docusign legacy eSignature custom button.

You have completed step three of the process. You
have removed all Docusign legacy eSignature custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.
Move to [Step 4: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=the1642634056297.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your Salesforce organization.").

title: Create a Document Rule in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Create a Document Rule in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to create a document rule to enable conditional document generation in a Gen
template:

Use this procedure to set up a document rule to enable conditional document generation in a
Gen template:

1. Select the Upload Documents arrow. The arrow has no text
   until you select it.
2. Select + Add Rule.

   The Document Rule dialog box opens.
3. Create a document rule:
   1. Enter rule name: Enter a descriptive name for
      the rule.
   2. APPLY: Select an option from the dropdown
      menu:

      - All: Docusign triggers
        the rule if a record matches all conditions.
      - Any: Docusign triggers
        the rule if a record matches at least one condition.
   3. IF: Add a condition to your rule:

      1. Select an Option: Choose a Salesforce
         field to start the condition:
      2. Equals: Choose
         Equals or Does Not
         Equal from the dropdown menu.
      3. Enter conditional value: Enter a value
         for the condition. Example: You start a condition with Billing Country >  Equals and enter Brazil to finish
         the condition.
   4. (Optional)
      Duplicate Row:
      Select the copy ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Copy-Row-Button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5ODksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.5yqHtASr_tgLUbj-d9SksekOTI35Wy-tD9X1EJAg3zE&_LANG=enus) button to copy a
      condition.
   5. (Optional)
      + or if: Select the
      link to add conditions.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Document-Rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5ODksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.5yqHtASr_tgLUbj-d9SksekOTI35Wy-tD9X1EJAg3zE&_LANG=enus)
4. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You built a document rule that
selects the correct template based on record conditions.

title: Step 2: Remove Legacy eSignature Components from Your Salesforce Layouts
heading_path: Step 2: Remove Legacy eSignature Components from Your Salesforce Layouts
text:
Learn how to remove all legacy eSignature for Salesforce components from Salesforce
layouts.

Uninstalling the legacy eSignature for Salesforce app removes all Docusign
components. However, you cannot delete components that are present on any layouts.

Use this procedure to remove Docusign components from your layouts:

1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.NTvJcne7VPlGgHTQOoft5F1_stsiKgARr4keCUKGFds&_LANG=enus)and choose Edit Page from the
   dropdown menu.

   The Lightning App Builder page opens.
2. Remove legacy Docusign component from the canvas. Legacy
   components include: Docusign Sending, Docusign
   EnvelopeConfiguration, and Docusign
   EnvelopeConfigurationManageLayouts.
   1. Hover over a legacy component and select the trash icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-123257-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.NTvJcne7VPlGgHTQOoft5F1_stsiKgARr4keCUKGFds&_LANG=enus).![A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted for the Docusign Files component.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Remove-DocuSign-component.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.NTvJcne7VPlGgHTQOoft5F1_stsiKgARr4keCUKGFds&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted for the Docusign Files component.")
   2. Select Save.
   3. Repeat this procedure for all legacy Docusign
      components.

   Salesforce removes the components from the canvas.
3. Select the back arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-13251-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.NTvJcne7VPlGgHTQOoft5F1_stsiKgARr4keCUKGFds&_LANG=enus) when you have removed all legacy
   components.
4. Repeat this procedure for each Salesforce layout (Account, Opportunity, and so
   on) that contains legacy Docusign components.

You have completed step two of the process. You have
removed all legacy Docusign components from your Salesforce layouts.
Move to [Step 3: Remove Legacy eSignature Custom Buttons From Your Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ddz1642632234520.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom buttons from your Salesforce page layouts.").

title: Edit the Docusign App Permissions of Salesforce Users
heading_path: Edit the Docusign App Permissions of Salesforce Users
text:
Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce
users.

You can use the Edit Permissions
command to change the Docusign app permissions of Salesforce users.

Use this
procedure to edit the Docusign app permissions of Gen, CLM Negotiate, or eSignature for
Salesforce users:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to edit.
2. Select Edit Permissions from the Add
   Users
   ![A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon in a square.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MDcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.AQzDFmgELwMqjq0hWSNjUvkKzw44LUxjjz51JTGMxfM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon in a square.") drop-down menu.
3. Change access from the Edit Permissions dialog box that
   opens:

   1. eSignature
      - Administrator: Administrators can create
        templates and manage all Docusign users in Salesforce.
      - User: Users can send documents for
        signature.
   2. CLM
      - Administrator: Administrators can access
        everything in CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. They
        can't manage Docusign users in Salesforce.
      - User: Users can access workflows and
        view, edit, and upload documents.
      - Viewer: Viewers can only preview and
        upload documents.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        CLM app
   3. Gen
      - Generator: Generators can create new
        documents and templates.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        Gen app.
   4. Negotiate
      - Negotiator: Negotiators can collaborate
        with signers and viewers.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        Negotiate app.
   5. (Optional) Update permissions for any existing
      users: Select to update a user's permissions in the
      connected Docusign account.
4. Select Apply.

You have completed the process. You edited the permissions of Gen,
CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.

title: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
text:
Learn to add Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users and manage permissions
on the user management page.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of Docusign applications you can use in Salesforce. The
Apps Launcher package contains the Docusign
eSignature,
CLM, Gen, and
Negotiate applications. Each Salesforce user needing to use
these apps must have a Docusign user. Administrators create Docusign users and grant
permissions on the Docusign user management page.

The following is a list of apps and permission types administrators can assign:

Docusign Gen
:   Generator: Generators can generate contracts and send
    them for signature.

Docusign CLM
:   Administrator: Administrators can access everything in
    CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. They can't manage Docusign users in
    Salesforce.
:   User: Users can access workflows and view, edit, and
    upload documents.
:   Viewer: Viewers can only preview and upload
    documents.

Docusign eSignature
:   Administrator: Administrators create templates and manage
    all Docusign users in Salesforce.
:   User: Users can send documents for signature.

Docusign Negotiate
:   Negotiator: Negotiators can send content for review and
    signature.

You can take the following actions on the Docusign User Management page:

- [Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users
  in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=zbp1629859390541.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to grant Docusign app access to your Salesforce users.")
- [Create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or
  eSignature Users in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users on the user management page.")
- [Check the Activation Status of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=hak1586134277505.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to check the activation status of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.")
- [Search for Docusign Users in the Docusign CLM
  User Management Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=keb1628113418793.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the search feature to find Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users.")
- [Edit the Permissions of Gen, Negotiate, or
  eSignature for Salesforce Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=doi1628107496987.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.")
- [Remove Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature Access
  From a Salesforce User in the User Management Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=tha1628110439784.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from Salesforce users.")
- [Remove Access and Close Docusign Gen,
  Negotiate, and eSignature Users in the Docusign User Management
  Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=cmn1628112474213.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove access and close Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users.")

You can take the following actions to help your Docusign for Salesforce users:

title: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
text:
cmn1628112474213.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove access and close Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users.")

You can take the following actions to help your Docusign for Salesforce users:

- [Resend the Activation Email for a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=liv1586134278039.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to resend the activation email to a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.")
- [Reset the Password of a Docusign CLM, Gen,

- ["You Are Not the Owner of the
