title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
text:
Learn to use group tags in Word-based Gen templates to merge Salesforce data into
tables.

You can use the Group tag to create separate tables for each product grouped under a
main category.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Group tag
text:
The Group tag is represented by the `<Group>` XML tag, paired
with the `<EndGroup>` tag.

You can use Group tags to create templates that adjust when your organization adds
and removes products and services.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Group tag example
text:
You create a Salesforce category of Vehicles with line items
for cars, trucks, and
vans.

You add the `<Group>` tag to your Gen template to create separate tables
for cars, trucks, and vans in generated agreements.

Your organization stops selling vans, but starts selling motorcycles and minivans.
When users generate agreements, the `<Group>` tag removes the
vans table, and only displays tables for motorcycles and minivans.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Using Table Row tags in Gen templates
text:
Use the following guidelines when creating Group tags:

- Group tag data use an XPath selector.
- Docusign adds output using the context of the selected
  data.
- Place content to be repeated between the `<Group>` and
  `<EndGroup>` tags.
- You can place any merge tag in a Group tag.
- The Select attribute uses an XPath selector to determine
  what data to select.
- The By attribute specifies how to categorize results of
  the Select attribute.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Supported Group tag XPath
text:
You can use the following XPath in Group tags:

- `<GroupName>` : This tag adds the name of the group
  specified by the By attribute.
- `<GroupItems>`: This tag adds all group items specified by
  the By attribute.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Group tag attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in Group tags:

| Attributes | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath that selects data from an XML document |
| By | XPath that specifies how to categorize the results from the Select attribute |

title: Reset the Password for a Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Reset the Password for a Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn to reset the password of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for
Salesforce user.

Use this procedure to reset the password of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce user:

1. Access the Docusign
   Users page as an administrator:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account.
   2. Select Settings at the top of the page.
   3. Select Users in the left column.
2. Select Actions next to a user and choose Reset
   Password from the dropdown menu.

   A message opens: A reset password email has been sent to (user
   name).

You have completed the process. You sent a password
reset link to the user's email address.

title: Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for
Salesforce is a package that includes the following Docusign apps:

- [Docusign eSignature for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000001taX4EAI&tab=e)
- [Docusign CLM for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000004e3VUEAY&tab=e)
- [Docusign Gen for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N3A00000FeGX6UAN&tab=e)

When you install any of these apps, Docusign installs the complete Docusign Apps Launcher package.

Use this procedure to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, choose a Docusign app from the
   AppExchange:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2MTAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Ymr6banIKagVryy2ChuavyiM7WGfPc-KM27JkhACSjE&_LANG=enus) and choose View
      All.
   2. Select Visit AppExchange >  Go to AppExchange.
   3. Enter Docusign eSignature, Gen, or
      CLM in the search field.
   4. Select an app and choose Get it Now when the app page opens.
2. Log in to the AppExchange with Salesforce administrator credentials:

   Note: Enter production credentials even if installing in a sandbox
   organization. These credentials are used to access the AppExchange.

   1. Select Log In on the LET'S GET STARTED
      page.
   2. Select Salesforce on the Hello
      Trailblazer! dialog box that opens.
   3. Enter Salesforce production administrator credentials on the page that opens and
      select Log In.

      The Where do you want to install this package? dialog
      box opens.
3. Choose where to install the package:
   1. Select Install in Production or Install in
      Sandbox.
   2. Select I have read and agree to the terms and
      conditions.
   3. Select Confirm and Install.

      The page refreshes. The Salesforce login page opens.
4. Install the Apps Launcher package:
   1. Enter production or sandbox administrator credentials and select Log
      In.
   2. Select Install for All
      Users.

      Note: If you want to choose ​Install for Admins Only, or ​Install for Specific Profiles​, see this topic about [how Salesforce applies app permissions](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.distribution_installing_packages.htm&type=5).
   3. Select Install or Upgrade.
   4. Select Done when the page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have installed the
Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps. This package includes Docusign eSignature, CLM, and Gen. You receive an email notification when
Salesforce completes the installation.

title: Disconnect Your Docusign Account and Preserve User Settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Disconnect Your Docusign Account and Preserve User Settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user
settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

Occasionally you must disconnect and reconnect your Docusign connection. The following are reasons to disconnect and
reconnect:

- You want to connect your Salesforce organization to a different Docusign account
- An administrator deleted your Docusign integration
  user
- Salesforce applied [enhanced domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000393816&type=1) to your organization

You can use the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool to reset your
integration safely.

Follow this procedure to use the Disconnect Account and Preserve User
Settings feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NzcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.WJpVheNi3fqfRupVgp0m0gEK3m3R2YB0Ij0ZvBofxrU&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NzcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.WJpVheNi3fqfRupVgp0m0gEK3m3R2YB0Ij0ZvBofxrU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Disconnect Account and Preserve User Settings: Select
   Execute.

   A message opens: Successfully logged out from Docusign account. The
   Troubleshooting page displays the message: No Docusign Account
   connected.

You have completed the process. You have disconnected your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization. You can [re-connect Salesforce and Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus) or
connect to a different Docusign account.

title: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App From Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App From Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Find out how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your
Salesforce organization.

There are two Docusign eSignature apps that you can use in your Salesforce
organization:

- **Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce**: The first
  Docusign for Salesforce app. It was de-listed from the Salesforce
  AppExchange in 2020.
- **Docusign eSignature for Salesforce**: The current version of
  the Docusign for Salesforce app. It is part of the Docusign Apps Launcher
  for Salesforce package of apps, along with Docusign Gen, Negotiate, and CLM.

The legacy app is still supported, but is not being developed, and will
eventually be deprecated.

You can upgrade now to enjoy the latest advances of the current eSignature
for Salesforce app.

When you have upgraded and migrated your legacy eSignature users, records,
and buttons, you can uninstall the legacy app.

In order to uninstall the package, you must first remove all legacy eSignature for
Salesforce dependencies. Dependencies are references from the legacy eSignature for
Salesforce that are active in your Salesforce organization. These include legacy
eSignature permission sets, components, and custom buttons.

Use this procedure to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature app from your
Salesforce organization:

- [Step 1: Remove Users from Legacy eSignature for Salesforce Permission Sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=qwo1642613574891.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove all users from legacy eSignature for Salesforce permission sets.")
- [Step 2: Remove Legacy eSignature Components from Your Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=dlc1642622890465.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove all legacy eSignature for Salesforce components from Salesforce layouts.")
- [Step 3: Remove Legacy eSignature Custom Buttons From Your Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ddz1642632234520.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom buttons from your Salesforce page layouts.")
- [Step 4: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=the1642634056297.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your Salesforce organization.")

title: Remove Users From the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: Remove Users From the Docusign Login Permission Set
text:
Learn to remove users from the Docusign Login permission
set.

The Docusign
 Login permission set helps you manage Docusign access for your Salesforce users. Users with Docusign Login membership can authorize Docusign
app access themselves.

Use this procedure to remove users from the Docusign Login permission set:

1. Select Docusign Login.
2. Select Manage Assignments.
3. Select the check box next to each user that you want to remove.
4. Select Remove Assignments.

   The Are you sure you want to remove these users from this
   permission set? dialog box opens.
5. Select OK.
6. Select Done.

You have completed the process. You have removed one or
more users from the Docusign Login permission set.

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Learn the Docusign Apps Launcher requirements for browsers, operating
systems, and devices.

You can use the Docusign for Salesforce apps with the following browsers,
operating systems, and devices:

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported browsers
text:
Important: Salesforce and Docusign do not
support any version of Microsoft Internet Explorer.

| Browser | Supported version | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Google Chrome | Latest | n/a |
| Safari (Apple) | Latest | Desktop-only. Salesforce does not support the mobile version of Safari. Enabling third-party cookies is required. |
| Microsoft Edge | Latest | n/a |
| Mozilla Firefox | Latest | n/a |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported operating systems
text:
| Operating system | Version |
| --- | --- |
| Microsoft Windows | Windows 10 or newer |
| MacOS (Apple desktop) | 10.14 (Mojave) or newer |
| iOS (Apple mobile) | iOS 14.x or newer |
| Android OS | Android 8.0 or newer |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Salesforce mobile app requirements
text:
Required settings: Configure the following settings to use Docusign in the Salesforce mobile app:

- Enable third-party cookies in the [Salesforce mobile app settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.salesforce_app.htm&type=5).
- Turn off Include the instance name in Visualforce URLs when
  third-party cookies are blocked in Salesforce My
  Domain settings.

Approved Docusign apps: You can use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
- Docusign Gen

Non-approved Docusign apps: You cannot use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign Negotiate
- Docusign CLM
- Docusign CLM Essentials

Approved envelope template types: You can only use the following
envelope template types in the Salesforce mobile app:

- [Quick send envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Send now envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)

Mobile app tagging requirements: Meet the following requirements
to add fields to Docusign envelopes:

- Install the Docusign mobile app on your Android or iOS
  device.
- Turn off mobile app tagging when using iPads.

For more information about Salesforce requirements, see the [Supported Browsers and Devices](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.getstart_browser_overview.htm&type=5) page on the
Salesforce support site.

title: View the Documents of an Envelope on the Docusign Status Page
heading_path: View the Documents of an Envelope on the Docusign Status Page
text:
Learn how to view the documents of an envelope by accessing the Docusign Status page.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that holds documents,
recipients, and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by completing the
fields assigned to them.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. The
Docusign Status tab lets you view the status of your
Docusign envelopes.

Docusign Status details include: Docusign envelope ID, Sender
Name, Sender Email, Email
Subject, and Related Objects.

You can also view the documents of an envelope sent from a Salesforce record on the Docusign
Status page.

Use this procedure to view the documents of a sent envelope:

1. Select the Docusign Status tab.
2. Select an envelope from the Envelope # column.
3. Select View Envelope in the upper right.

   The envelope documents open in a preview window.

You have completed the task. You have viewed the documents
of an envelope sent from a Salesforce record.

title: Federated Security for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Federated Security for Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about how you can apply Salesforce security settings to your CLM content using
Federated Security.

There are two Docusign CLM apps that you can use in Salesforce:

- Docusign CLM: The current app that is part of the Docusign
  Apps Launcher package of apps
- Docusign Document Management: The legacy CLM app, formerly
  known as SpringCM File It

This article applies to the Docusign CLM app.

See this page for legacy app instructions: [Federated Security for Docusign Document Management (SpringCM File
It)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=fmk1599229525077.html&_LANG=enus).

You can use Federated Security to apply Salesforce security to content in your CLM
account. Federated Security lets you manage CLM security from Salesforce without needing
to set separate permissions.

Federated Security applies Salesforce Profile or
Role security to all CLM content. When you update security
settings in Salesforce, CLM content security also updates.

You can find Federated Security settings on the CLM Admin page.

Warning:

- Do not refresh a Salesforce organization with Federated Security applied
- Do not connect a Salesforce organization to a different CLM account when
  Federated Security is applied

[You must follow these steps before you refresh or
reconnect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ytt1602710410891.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about best practices for refreshing a Salesforce organization with Federated Security enabled."). Failure to do so destroys all CLM file security, and you must
rebuild the entire CLM directory.

Note: Federated Security cannot be applied to CLM reports because you can
only run reports in your CLM account.

**Example**: A user has no access to the
Finance folder in Salesforce. That user has
View access to the Finance folder in
CLM. The user runs a report in the CLM account. The user sees documents from the
Finance folder in the report results. Federated security does not apply because you
cannot run the CLM report in Salesforce.

title: Step 1: Remove Users from Apps Launcher Permission Sets
heading_path: Step 1: Remove Users from Apps Launcher Permission Sets
text:
Learn to remove all users from Docusign permission sets.

You are required to remove at least one user from the following permission sets:

- Docusign Administrator
- Docusign CLM Admin

You may be required to remove users from [other Docusign Apps Launcher
permission sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=xxm1623778416524.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the types and functions of Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets.").

1. Open the Permission Sets page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.XbsU5p9LqgpqUjUTsVZV7AdpsNZdFNQsLp1XtXI9pAk&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Permission Sets in the Quick
      Find field.

   The Permission Sets page opens.
2. Remove all users from the Docusign Administrator
   permission set:
   1. Select Manage Assignments.
   2. Select the Action check box to select all
      users.
   3. Select Remove Assignments.![A screenshot displays the Action check box and the remove assignments button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/daluninstallremoveassignments.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.XbsU5p9LqgpqUjUTsVZV7AdpsNZdFNQsLp1XtXI9pAk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Action check box and the remove assignments button highlighted.")

      A dialog box displays: Are you sure you want to remove
      these users from this permission set?.
   4. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes. The Docusign
      Administrator permission set page opens.
3. Select Done.
4. Repeat this procedure for the Docusign CLM Admin
   permission set.
5. Repeat this procedure for any other Docusign permission sets
   containing users.

You have completed step one of the process. You have
removed all users from Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets. Move to
[Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=uxu1623708462949.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce layouts.").

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
text: Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature.

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce
v5.2](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04t3u000002wLcp)

You can change your Salesforce domain name to update your brand, adopt [Enhanced Domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.domain_name_enhanced.htm&type=5), or enable partitioned domains. When
your My Domain name changes, your Salesforce-hosted URLs also change. You must
rebuild the connection to your Docusign account.

The Sync Domains feature of the Docusign
TroubleShooting tool rebuilds your connection to Docusign after a
domain change.

Use this procedure to rebuild your Gen, Negotiate,
eSignature, or eSignature Apex Toolkit
integration with the Sync Domains feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Yv5UAZNj5Q1gC-e0oNwVZWcbaEgGHKAX1M5sSrOUSRw&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MDAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Yv5UAZNj5Q1gC-e0oNwVZWcbaEgGHKAX1M5sSrOUSRw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Sync Domains: Select Execute.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Successfully synced domains on
   Docusign account.

You have completed the process. You have rebuilt the
Gen, Negotiate,
eSignature, or eSignature Apex Toolkit
integration with your Salesforce organization.

title: Rebuild Your Docusign Connection, Users, and Templates After Cloning or Refreshing a Salesforce Sandbox
heading_path: Rebuild Your Docusign Connection, Users, and Templates After Cloning or Refreshing a Salesforce Sandbox
text:
Learn how to rebuild your Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates after
refreshing or cloning a Salesforce sandbox.

When you refresh or clone a Salesforce sandbox organization, Salesforce imports all settings
from your production organization. After the cloning or refreshing process, your
Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates remain linked to your Salesforce
production organization. To get your Docusign account working in your sandbox, you
must rebuild your Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates.

For more information about refreshing or cloning sandbox organizations, see these sites:

- [Refresh a Salesforce sandbox](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.data_sandbox_refresh.htm&type=5)
- [Clone a Salesforce sandbox](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?language=en_US&id=sf.data_sandbox_clone.htm&type=5)

Use this procedure to rebuild envelope templates after you clone or refresh your
Salesforce sandbox organization:

1. [Disconnect the sandbox from Docusign and remove your
   Docusign users.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vli1610514116162.html&_LANG=enus)
2. [Connect a Docusign Demo account to your
   Salesforce organization.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uxq1614636863027.html&_LANG=enus)
3. [Rebuild your Docusign users.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus)
4. [Clone your Docusign envelope templates from Salesforce
   production to your sandbox.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uhn1664569454728.html&_LANG=enus)

You have completed the process. You have rebuilt your Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates after refreshing or cloning your Salesforce
sandbox organization.

title: Use Filter and Conditional Tags Together in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Filter and Conditional Tags Together in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to build tables to import conditional content from Salesforce
records.

You can build tables to import content from Salesforce records.

Filter tags only import certain fields from a Salesforce record.

Conditional tags display or hide blocks of text depending on
Salesforce field data.

You can use these two types of tags together to create custom Gen templates.

Example
: You want to create tables that add
products by category on your generated agreements. You create specific tables for each
category. Each table has tags that filter all line items in the product table, returning
only the ones you need. You add the tables to your Gen template.

Use this procedure to add a table with Filter and Conditional tags:

1. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
2. Add a table with custom XML to your Word template:
   1. Select Download.
   2. Open the Word document and go to where you want the table.
   3. Insert a table with a header, at least one row, and four columns.
   4. In the top row, enter a name for each column.
   5. Enter `<# <TableRow Select="/` in the first cell of the first
      row.

      |  |  |  |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Product Name | Product Code | Price |
      | **`<# <TableRow Select="/`** |  |  |
   6. Create a tag that imports Product Name content and
      suppresses content that does not match Services. Place this
      text directly after the <# <TableRow Select="/ tag.

      |  |  |  |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Product Name | Product Code | Price |
      | **`<# <TableRow Select="./Quote_Line_Items"/>#><#<SuppressTableRow Select="./Product_Category" NotMatch='Services'/>#><#<Content Select="./Product_Name" Optional="true />#>`** |  |  |
   7. Create a tag in the Product Code column that imports
      Product Code data from the Product
      Name field.

      |  |  |  |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Product Name | Product Code | Price |
      | **`<# <TableRow Select="./Quote_Line_Items"/>#><#<SuppressTableRow Select="./Product_Category" NotMatch='Services'/>#><#<Content Select="./Product_Name" Optional="true />#>`** | **`<#<Content Select="./Product_Code" Optional="true />#>`** |  |
   8. Create a tag for the Price column that imports items from
      the Line Net Total  column. Add the /mo
      tag at the end for services that match the Monthly Billing Unit
      code.

      |  |  |  |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Product Name | Product Code | Price |
      | **`<# <TableRow Select="./Quote_Line_Items"/>#><#<SuppressTableRow Select="./Product_Category" NotMatch='Services'/>#><#<Content Select="./Product_Name" Optional="true />#>`** | **`<#<Content Select="./Product_Code" Optional="true />#>`** | **`<#<Content Select="./Line_Net_Total" Optional="true />#><#<Conditional Select="./Billing_Unit" Match='Monthly" />#>/mo<#<EndConditional/>#>`** |
   9. Save the changes in Word and return to the Gen Upload
      Documents page.
3. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").
4. Select Save and Close and select Save on
   the Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

   You have added the table to your Gen template. When users generate an agreement,
   Gen only imports the Services content. Gen also adds Product Name,
   Product Code, and Line Net Total content
   with mo at the end. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/tableresult.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NjAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.fkc7S4sYjPTEUOyKUrCKFIOTGaik2HpY6rQPWABIfjs&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Filter and Conditional Tags Together in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Filter and Conditional Tags Together in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
en=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NjAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.fkc7S4sYjPTEUOyKUrCKFIOTGaik2HpY6rQPWABIfjs&_LANG=enus)
