table to your Gen template using filter and conditional tags. Generated agreements display or
hide content based on the tag values.

title: Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
Learn to add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.

You can add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor
template. Dynamic tables automatically add rows and columns based on the amount of data on a
Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor
Gen template:

1. Open the Gen template Create tab as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.QtJXMLxY-iClTPv556-y4SsMvzNn71sinfem70ba_E0&_LANG=enus) and go to View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.QtJXMLxY-iClTPv556-y4SsMvzNn71sinfem70ba_E0&_LANG=enus) next to a template and choose
      Edit.

   The Gen template page opens to the Create tab.
2. Place the cursor where you want to add a table.
3. Select the Salesforce Fields tab ![A screenshot displays two interlocking blue rectangle icons.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacesalesforcefieldsicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.QtJXMLxY-iClTPv556-y4SsMvzNn71sinfem70ba_E0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays two interlocking blue rectangle icons.") to the left of the Docusign Fields menu.

   The Salesforce Fields menu opens.
4. Search for and select the Opportunity Line Items field.

   ![A screenshot displays the Opportunity Line Items field displayed in the Salesforce Fields section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacelineitemsfield.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.QtJXMLxY-iClTPv556-y4SsMvzNn71sinfem70ba_E0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Opportunity Line Items field displayed in the Salesforce Fields section.")
5. Create your dynamic table:
   1. Drag items from the Fields menu to the Selected
      Columns section.![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field moving to the Selected Columns section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacelineitemsadd.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.QtJXMLxY-iClTPv556-y4SsMvzNn71sinfem70ba_E0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field moving to the Selected Columns section.")
   2. (Optional) Select X to remove a column.
   3. (Optional) Drag columns to change their order.

      ![A screenshot displays a column moving lower in the Selected Columns section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacelineitemsdrag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.QtJXMLxY-iClTPv556-y4SsMvzNn71sinfem70ba_E0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a column moving lower in the Selected Columns section.")
   4. Select Apply.

title: Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
xNTg5NzksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.QtJXMLxY-iClTPv556-y4SsMvzNn71sinfem70ba_E0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a column moving lower in the Selected Columns section.")
   4. Select Apply.

   A dynamic table with Salesforce merge fields displays in your template.
   Example:![A screenshot displays a dynamic table with Salesforce line items.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-a-dynamic-table-with-Salesforce-line-items.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzksInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.QtJXMLxY-iClTPv556-y4SsMvzNn71sinfem70ba_E0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a dynamic table with Salesforce line items.")
6. Select Next and Publish.

   A message displays: Template (your template name)
   updated.

You have completed the process. You have added a
dynamic table to your Gen template. The dynamic table automatically adds rows and columns
based on the amount of data on a Salesforce record. All text in the table matches the font
size and style of the template.

title: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
heading_path: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
text:
Learn to make a standard Salesforce user your Docusign integration
user.

Connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account to use Docusign Apps Launcher in Salesforce.

To create the connection, you must enter Docusign and Salesforce
credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The connecting
Salesforce user is called the integration user.

If you don't want to use a Salesforce administrator as your integration user, you can use
standard credentials with some modifications.

Use this procedure to make a standard Salesforce user your integration user:

1. Clone a standard Salesforce user profile and name it Docusign
   Integration User.
2. Add permissions to the Integration User profile:
   1. Open the new profile and select Edit.
   2. Administrative Permissions: Select the following
      options:

      - Manage Users
      - API Enabled
      - View Setup and Configuration
      - Modify Metadata Through Metadata API Functions
      - Customize Application
      - Manage Custom Permissions
   3. Standard Object Permissions and Custom
      Object Permissions:

      - View All: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must read.
      - Modify All: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must update.

      Note: These settings grant access to your Salesforce organization's
      ownership hierarchy. These permissions do not grant unrestricted
      access to all data on these objects. You can also restrict access to
      specific data points using [Field Level
      Security](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ind.fsc_admin_configure_field_level_security.htm&type=5) or [Sharing
      Settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ind.cg_task_update_sharing_settings.htm&type=5).
   4. Open Object Manager and set Field Level
      Security for all fields Apps Launcher must read or
      update. These settings grant access to features like document generation
      or workflow automation:

      - Visible: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must read.
      - Read-Only: Deselect for each object Apps
        Launcher must update, including all Docusign
        objects.![A screenshot displays field-level security settings for the Docusign Integration User profile.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-fieldlevel-security-settings-for-the-Docusign-Integration-User-profile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2MjUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.T9_ITnphNJXDM6Bw_0n7bFcrAm32eic4h-rOFko6pvI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays field-level security settings for the Docusign Integration User profile.")
   5. Select Save.
3. Create a standard Salesforce user to act as your integration user.

   Note: Create generic credentials not tied to a person, like
   docusign-integration-user@yourorg.com. View this
   topic for more information about [integration user best practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
4. Set Docusign access for your Salesforce integration
   user:

   1. Log on to your Docusign account as an
      administrator.
   2. Create a Docusign administrator using your Salesforce integration user
      credentials.
5. Add your integration user to the Docusign Integration User
   profile.
6. Add your integration user to the Docusign Administrator
   permission set.
7. [Connect your Docusign
   account to your Salesforce organization using the Docusign
   and Salesforce integration user credentials](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

title: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
heading_path: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
text:
credentials](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

You have completed the process. You made a standard Salesforce
user your integration user and connected Docusign and Salesforce. This
integration user maintains the connection between accounts without requiring
unrestricted administrator permissions.

title: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign
production account.

title: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")

To use Docusign applications, you must connect a Docusign
account to your Salesforce organization. You create this connection by entering
Salesforce and Docusign administrator credentials on the
Docusign Setup page. The Docusign
administrator used to connect accounts is called the integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your Salesforce organization to a Docusign
production account:

1. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that opens.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that opens
      and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page opens.
2. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login
      dialog box opens.
   2. Enter Docusign administrator credentials in the
      dialog box that opens and select Log in.
   3. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      opens and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box opens.
3. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page opens.

You have completed the process. You have connected your
Salesforce organization to your Docusign production account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Add Child Objects to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add Child Objects to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
Learn to add up to four levels of child objects and two levels of related
lists.

Agreement Cloud Editor templates can contain up to four levels
of child objects and two levels of related lists. You can use the following combinations of
relation:

| Relation type | Object path |
| --- | --- |
| Root | Opportunity |
| 1: 1 | Opportunity >  Account |
| 1: 1: 1 | Opportunity >  Account >  Parent Account |
| 1: 1: 1: 1 | Opportunity >  Account >  Parent Account >  Owner |
| 1: Many | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles |
| 1: Many: 1 | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles >  Contact |
| 1: Many: 1: 1 | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles >  Contact >  Reports To |
| 1: Many: Many | Account >  Contacts >  Cases |
| 1: Many: Many: 1 | Account >  Contacts >  Cases >  Owner |

Use this procedure to add child relations to parent objects in a Gen
template:

1. Open the Gen template Create tab as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9._EwxiuIiqt9nVg5RQy9n6AiqW__FhR-JgWWkv1YANdQ&_LANG=enus) and go to View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9._EwxiuIiqt9nVg5RQy9n6AiqW__FhR-JgWWkv1YANdQ&_LANG=enus) next to a template and choose
      Edit.

   The Gen template page opens to the Create tab.
2. Select the  Salesforce Fields
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacesalesforcefieldsicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9._EwxiuIiqt9nVg5RQy9n6AiqW__FhR-JgWWkv1YANdQ&_LANG=enus) tab.
3. Expand arrows to display child objects.

   ![A screenshot displays a tree of Salesforce objects starting with Account and expanding to Account Name.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genace4levels.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9._EwxiuIiqt9nVg5RQy9n6AiqW__FhR-JgWWkv1YANdQ&_LANG=enus)
4. Drag a child object field to the document text.

You have completed the process. You have added child object
fields to a Gen template.

title: Step 1: Remove Users from Legacy eSignature for Salesforce Permission Sets
heading_path: Step 1: Remove Users from Legacy eSignature for Salesforce Permission Sets
text:
Learn how to remove all users from legacy eSignature for Salesforce permission sets.

To continue uninstalling the legacy app, remove all
users from the following permission sets:

- Docusign Administrator
- Docusign Sender
- Docusign User

Important: You will see permission sets with
duplicate names. Do not edit permission sets with Docusign Apps
Launcher in the Description field.

![A screenshot displays three highlighted Docusign permission sets.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Remove-all-assignments-from-these-three-permission-sets.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Jr-pKDGX1Xhvdthql8U9AuenCYcUExZBYreLZxOWXyY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three highlighted Docusign permission sets.")

1. Open the Permission Sets page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Jr-pKDGX1Xhvdthql8U9AuenCYcUExZBYreLZxOWXyY&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Permission Sets in the Quick
      Find field.

   The Permission Sets page opens.
2. Select the link for the legacy Docusign Administrator
   permission set. Do not select the entry with Docusign Apps
   Launcher in the Description field.
3. Select Manage Assignments and select all users.
4. Select Remove Assignments.![A screenshot displays check boxes selected for all users of a permission set.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/daluninstallremoveassignments.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MTMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Jr-pKDGX1Xhvdthql8U9AuenCYcUExZBYreLZxOWXyY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays check boxes selected for all users of a permission set.")
5. Select OK on the Are you sure you want to
   remove these users from this permission set? dialog box.

   The Docusign Administrator page opens.
6. Select Done.
7. Repeat this procedure for the legacy Docusign Sender and
   Docusign User permission sets.

You have completed step one of the process. You
have removed all users from legacy eSignature for Salesforce permission sets. Move to
[Step 2: Remove Legacy eSignature Components from Your Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=dlc1642622890465.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove all legacy eSignature for Salesforce components from Salesforce layouts.").

title: Fix Account-Wide Issues Using the Docusign Apps Launcher Troubleshooting Page
heading_path: Fix Account-Wide Issues Using the Docusign Apps Launcher Troubleshooting Page
text:
Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting is a Salesforce page that
allows administrators to troubleshoot and resolve issues with these Docusign apps.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of apps that you install in your Salesforce organization.
These apps include Docusign Gen, Negotiate, CLM, CLM Essentials, and eSignature for
Salesforce.

Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting is a tool that lets
administrators troubleshoot and resolve issues with Docusign apps. The page is available
in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce administrators with
Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

The TroubleShooting page helps you resolve common administration issues. You can fix complex
problems by selecting the Execute button next to a task.

Here are the functions available on the Troubleshooting page:

[Disconnect Account and Preserve User Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Disconnect your Docusign account from Salesforce without deleting your Docusign
    users.

[Disconnect Account and Remove Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=vli1610514116162.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect a Docusign account and remove users with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Disconnect your Docusign account and remove all Docusign users and settings from
    Salesforce.

[Clear User Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ncl1610479844444.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix the CUSTOM_SETTINGS_LIMIT_EXCEEDED issue with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Delete cached user access tokens to free up Salesforce storage space.

[Folder Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=sqj1610517836441.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to reset folder permissions for Docusign content with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Trigger a root folder permissions update. This update resets user permissions for all
    content.

[Refresh Products](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=agy1623993092688.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to activate and update Docusign for Salesforce apps and settings using the Refresh Products feature.")
:   Activate and update apps and settings connected to your Salesforce organization.

[Sync Domains](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ynp1680122164220.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature. Sync Domains fixes issues with Enhanced Domains and any changes that you make to your Salesforce domain.")
:   Resolve errors related to Enhanced Domains or domain changes that you make.

[Manage CLM Integration User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=yyh1698871133582.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Reset the token used by your Docusign CLM integration user. This feature is only available
    for Docusign CLM for Salesforce accounts.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
text:
Learn about the actions that you must take to use Docusign apps after
Salesforce security changes.

Important: You are not required to make any changes if
you have taken the following steps:

- Installed the Docusign connected app.
- Set the OAuth policy to Admin-approved users are pre-authorized.

  Note: Changing policy revokes existing
  user tokens. Existing users must re-authorize.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What is changing with Salesforce connected app security?
text:
Starting in September 2025, Salesforce began enforcing [restrictions for uninstalled connected apps](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=005132365&type=1). This usage
restriction blocks access to uninstalled connected apps. If you have not installed
the Docusign connected app and assigned permissions, your users
cannot access Docusign apps.

As part of this change, Salesforce created the Approve uninstalled connected apps user permission (defaulted to System
Administrator standard profile.)

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How do these security changes impact Docusign integrations?
text:
Connected apps installed before the security change continue to function
without interruption. Take action only if you did not install the connected app
before the changes.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What is an uninstalled connected app?
text:
An [uninstalled connected app](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.connected_app_how_to_install.htm&type=5) is an application in your
organization that has not been installed by a System Administrator. Organizations
integrated with Docusign contain an uninstalled connected app
named DocuSign on the Connected Apps OAuth
Usage page. By default, this application is not installed, and must
be manually installed by a System Administrator. ![A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_install_button_highlighted_on_the_Docusign_connected_app_OAuth_Usage_page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.npMS4i1vI3x2C7mn-oiOegAryxrKg59D2RSbEWYBqXo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.")

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
Which Docusign managed packages contain the connected app?
text:
The following Docusign for Salesforce packages contain the Docusign connected app:

- Docusign Apps Launcher (Namespace: dfsle)
- Docusign for Salesforce (Legacy Namespace: dsfs)
- FileIt (Legacy Namespace: SpringCMEos)

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How are new users impacted?
text:
If you have not installed the app before the security change, your new
users cannot access the Docusign connected app. Users see the
authorization button, but a Salesforce OAuth error displays after they enter their
Docusign credentials: We can't authorize you because
of an OAuth error. For more information, contact your Salesforce
administrator.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How are existing users impacted?
text:
If you never installed the app, existing users can access it if they meet the
following criteria:

- The user has previously authorized the app.
- The app isn’t using the [OAuth 2.0 device flow](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.remoteaccess_oauth_device_flow.htm&language=en_US&type=5).

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
Does Docusign Apps Launcher use OAuth 2.0 device flow?
text: No. Docusign Apps Launcher does not use OAuth 2.0 device flow.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What actions should I take?
text:
[Install the connected app to grant Docusign
for Salesforce access to new users](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Install-the-DocuSign-Apps-Launcher-to-Manage-Connected-Apps-in-Salesforce-Setup).

Note: Salesforce
and Docusign recommend selecting Admin-approved
users are pre-authorized. Each user must enter credentials to
gain Docusign for Salesforce app access if you select
All users may self-authorize.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What happens when I change the connected app policy?
text:
Make the following changes when you update the Permitted Users
setting to Admin approved users are pre-authorized:

Administrators
:   - All Apps Launcher apps: Add all necessary
      profiles and permission sets to the Docusign
      connected app. An error message displays for users not included
      in these profiles and permission sets: User is not
      admin approved to access this app.
    - CLM for Salesforce: [Disconnect your account and
      preserve user settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.") if you use CLM Document
      Generation. Otherwise, a Salesforce session is
      invalid error displays when users try to generate
      agreements.

Existing users
:   Authorized users must re-authorize. Steps to re-authorize vary for each
    installed package:

    - Docusign Apps Launcher: [Activate your Docusign
      for Salesforce user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qob1643667997351&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html).
    - Docusign Connect for Salesforce (Legacy):
      [Reconfigure your login with Docusign Connect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=tsk6312c996-938f-4f24-b11f-9da2f7887493.html). There is no
      option to authorize within the legacy Docusign
      for Salesforce integration.
    - FileIt (Legacy SpringCM): Take no action.
      Existing users are already authorized to use SpringCM FileIt. If
      users must re-authorize, use these procedures:
      - [Configure FileIt app security
        settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=qgu1583188662524.html).
      - [Connect Salesforce to
        SpringCM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=btt1576610138405.html).

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How do I resolve connected app errors?
text:
User is not admin approved to access this app
:   Resolution: Install and configure the Docusign
    connected app. Add the missing profile or permission set if you have
    already installed the app.

You have not granted or have revoked your consent to be impersonated by Docusign Apps Launcher
:   Resolution: Install the Docusign connected app. [Refresh the user's consent flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/DAL-Error-You-have-not-granted-or-have-revoked-your-consent-to-be-impersonated-by-DocuSign-Apps-Launcher) if you have
    already installed the app.

We can't authorize you because of an OAuth error. For more information, contact your Salesforce administrator
:   Resolution 1: Install the Docusign connected app. Add access to missing profiles
    or permission sets on the Connected App Detail page
    if you have already installed the app.
:   Resolution 2: Install the Docusign connected app. Go to Setup >  Connected Apps OAuth Usage and choose All users may
    self-authorize for the Docusign
    connected app.

Salesforce session is invalid
:   Resolution 1: Install the Docusign connected app. [Disconnect and reconnect your account and
    preserve user settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.") after changing OAuth policy to
    All users may self-authorize.
:   Resolution 2: Enable Allow
    Visualforce pages to access APIs if either
    Restrict Access to APIs with Connected
    Apps or Restrict Customers and Partners
    from Access APIs is enabled.

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
text:
Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.

The integration user is a Docusign administrator that connects your Docusign account to your Salesforce organization. When you connect accounts, you must enter Docusign and Salesforce administrator credentials. When your accounts are connected, users can access Docusign functions like sending agreements for signature.

Docusign uses OAuth 2.0 for communicating between Salesforce and Docusign. These tokens continue to operate unless an administrator chooses to break them or force an expiration.

Follow these guidelines to avoid issues with your integration:

Create dedicated integration users
:   Create generic integration administrators for your Docusign account and your Salesforce organization. Ensure that these generic credentials aren't tied to a person. For example, you can create a user named integration-user@yourcompany.com. Creating generic integration users protects your integration when administrators leave or change roles. Deactivating your integration user deletes custom Docusign fields and templates created by that user. So it is best to create an integration user that you will never deactivate.

Enable password login for at least one Docusign administrator
:   If you enforce SSO login for your integration user, [enable password login for at least one Docusign administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fji1583359148678.html&_LANG=enus). Your Docusign integration breaks if your SSO setup experiences an outage and no administrator can log in with a password.

Utilize a compatible Salesforce user license
:   Your Docusign for Salesforce integration user must be a member of the Docusign Administrator permission set. Since you cannot add [Salesforce Integration Users](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=release-notes.rn_api_integration_license.htm&release=242&type=5) to permission sets, you cannot use this license type as your integration user. Docusign recommends that you use a Salesforce [Standard User](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.users_license_types_available.htm&type=5) license when creating your Docusign integration user.

Note: Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce organizations to a single Docusign account.

The following is a list of integration user scenarios:

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
Actions that break your integration
text:
You close (deactivate) the Docusign integration user
:   - Docusign deletes all envelope templates
    - Docusign send buttons do not work
    - Docusign deletes custom merge fields created by the Docusign integration user
:   Before closing your Docusign integration user, create a template in your Docusign account and re-save each custom merge field.

(Gen and CLM only) When you deactivate your Salesforce integration user
:   Your Gen and CLM for Salesforce functions break. The Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app continues to function without issue.

You enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for your Salesforce integration user
:   A Salesforce integration user needs the following access:

    - Salesforce System Administrator access
    - [Enable MFA for API logins](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus) for the System Administrator profile set to False

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
Actions that do not break your integration
text:
You change the Docusign integration user password
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Only enter the Docusign integration user's password for the initial authorization.

You change the Salesforce integration user password
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Only enter the Salesforce integration user's password for the initial authorization.

You deactivate the Salesforce integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Important:

    - Deactivating an integration user that's used for both Docusign Apps Launcher **and** legacy eSignature for Salesforce breaks the legacy app.
    - Deactivating the Salesforce integration user breaks your Docusign Gen and CLM for Salesforce functionality.
    - If the deactivated administrator is also a system sender, Apex toolkit flows relying on the system sender break. Add a new active user as the system sender to prevent this.

You replace the integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.

    Note: Closing (deactivating) the Docusign integration user before adding a new one breaks the integration.

You enable single sign-on (SSO) for the Docusign integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Enabling SSO in the Docusign account does not interfere with communications between Salesforce and Docusign.

title: Clone a Gen Template
heading_path: Clone a Gen Template
text:
Learn to clone a Gen template to create a new template that is similar to an existing
one.

You can copy or "clone" a Docusign
Gen template. This is helpful if you want to create a new template that is similar to an
existing one.

Use this procedure to clone a Gen template:

1. Access the Docusign Gen Templates page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4ODMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.VmzQJqeKFhVvXCWVe87Jtth1durPR33Fl2VZnVRagN4&_LANG=enus) and choose View
      All.
   2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
   3. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
2. Select the name of a Gen template.
3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4ODMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.VmzQJqeKFhVvXCWVe87Jtth1durPR33Fl2VZnVRagN4&_LANG=enus) next to the
   Gen template and choose Clone from the dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the Clone option selected from the Edit a Gen template dropdown menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Clone-a-Gen-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4ODMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.VmzQJqeKFhVvXCWVe87Jtth1durPR33Fl2VZnVRagN4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Clone option selected from the Edit a Gen template dropdown menu.")

   The page refreshes. The cloned copy is now the active template on the page. The
   template displays (copy 1) at the end of the name.

You have completed the process. You have cloned a Gen
template. Follow the instructions [to edit a Gen
template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ywu1586134208345.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to edit, delete, or change ownership for a Gen template or custom button.") to make any changes.

title: Access the Docusign Envelope Templates Tab to Build and Edit Envelope Templates
heading_path: Access the Docusign Envelope Templates Tab to Build and Edit Envelope Templates
text:
Learn to access the Docusign Envelope Templates tab in
Salesforce.

Docusign Envelope Templates allow users to select a button on a record to
launch an administrator-built sending experience.

Use this procedure to access the Docusign Envelope Templates tab in
your Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
2. Make any selection:

   - [New](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus): Create a new envelope template.
   - [Change Owner](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ain1596147068228.html&_LANG=enus): Change the owner of an envelope template.
   - [Import](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=juw1586134252760.html&_LANG=enus): Import your data into Salesforce. You can import up to 50,000 records at a time.
   - [Rename](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bzu1596146928480.html&_LANG=enus): Change the name of an envelope template.
   - [Edit](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xow1596147003565.html&_LANG=enus): Add or edit documents, recipients, Salesforce fields, Docusign fields, and envelope template options.
   - [Delete](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=yzr1596147036291.html&_LANG=enus): Delete an envelope template.

   You have completed the task. You accessed the Docusign Envelope
   Templates tab. From this page, you can create and edit Envelope
   Templates.

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn about using XPath/XML to customize your Word-based Gen template.

You can use XML to create advanced logic in Gen Word templates that is not available
in the Gen user interface.

The following are commonly-used XML expressions that you can add to your
template:

- Concatenate strings
- Add a comma-separated related list
- Add a comma-separated, concatenated list of fields
- Merge the current date into a document
- Merge owner information
- Insert an image

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Comma-separated related list
text:
```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Repeat Select="//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems" /> #> |
|  | <# <Content Select="./Name" /> #><# <Conditional Test="count(./preceding-sibling::*) |
|  | +1 < (count(//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems) - 1)" /> #>, |
|  | <# <EndConditional /> #><# <Conditional Test="count(./preceding-sibling::*)+1 = |
|  | (count(//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems) - 1)" /> #>, and <# <EndConditional /> #> |
|  | <# <EndRepeat /> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Concatenate strings
text:
```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Content Select="concat(//Opportunity/Account.Name, ' - ', //Opportunity/Name)" /> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Comma-separated, concatenated list of fields
text:
```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Repeat Select="//Field_1__c[text() != ''] | //Field_2__c[text() != ''] | |
|  | //Field_3__c[text() != '']" /> #><# <Content Select="." /> #><# <Conditional Test=" |
|  | (count(./preceding-sibling::Field_1__c[text() != ''] | ./preceding-sibling::Field_2__c[text() |
|  | != ''] | ./preceding-sibling::Field_3__c[text() != '']) + 1) < (count(//Field_1__c[text() != ''] | |
|  | //Field_2__c[text() != ''] | //Field_3__c[text() != '']) - 1)" /> #>, <# <EndConditional /> #> |
|  | <# <Conditional Test="(count(./preceding-sibling::Field_1__c[text() != ''] | ./preceding-sibling:: |
|  | Field_2__c[text() != ''] | ./preceding-sibling::Field_3__c[text() != '']) + 1) = |
|  | (count(//Field_1__c[text() != ''] | //Field_2__c[text() != ''] | //Field_3__c[text() != '']) - 1)" /> #>, |
|  | and <# <EndConditional /> #><# <EndRepeat /> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Merge the current date into a document
text:
Add the TODAY() formula field to the Salesforce parent object
(Account, Opportunity, and so on.)

Note: You can hide this from page
layouts.

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Merge owner information
text:
Add a formula field like Owner.FirstName&"
"&Owner.LastName to the Salesforce parent object (Account,
Opportunity, and so on.)

Note: You can hide this from page
layouts.

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Insert an image
text:
Use this example to insert an image into a document:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Image Select="string('https://<<IMAGE ADDRESS>>.png')" Height="2" Width="6" Optional="true"/> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Store an image in a Salesforce field with a URL
text:
```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Image Select="/Opportunity/MainCompetitors__c " Height="2" Width="4" |
|  | Optional="true"/> #> |
```

Note: Use inches for the Height and Width
numbers. The URL, Height, and Width specifications here are examples. Replace these
with dimensions from your own images.

title: Create a One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
heading_path: Create a One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
text:
Learn to build a one-click generation and sending experience for generating a contract
and sending it for signature.

title: Create a One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
heading_path: Create a One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
Before you begin
text:
- Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 5.3 or higher
- [Create a Docusign
  envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Create a Gen template with Microsoft
  Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.")

You can build a combined document generation and sending experience. A
generation and sending experience lets your users generate a document and send it
for signature with one click.

Example: As an administrator, you create a Docusign envelope template. You create a Gen template and link to
your envelope template on the Gen template Create Button page. You add a Gen custom
button to a layout.

A user visits a record and selects the Gen button. Docusign
generates an agreement with that record’s data and sends it to all envelope template
recipients.

When all recipients complete the signing process, Docusign saves the agreement to your Docusign account and Salesforce
record.

Note: You can only link templates built on the same Salesforce
object. Linking a Gen template and envelope template built on
Opportunity works. Linking an
Opportunity-based Gen template with an
Account-based envelope template does not work.

Use this procedure to build a generation and sending experience:

1. Change the sending experience of your envelope template to send
   now:
   1. [Access the envelope template Options page as a
      Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ete1620167625547.html&_LANG=enus).
   2. Sending Experience: Select Send
      Now.
   3. Select Next.
   4. Select Save and Close.
2. Link your envelope template to your Gen template:
   1. [Open the Create Custom
      Button page as a Salesforce
      administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=way1621663319299.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to access the Create Button step of a Microsoft Word-based Gen template.").
   2. Select Launch automatically after
      generation.
   3. Select an envelope template to streamline sending for
      signature: Enter and select the name of a
      send now envelope template.![A screenshot shows Launch automatically after generation selected and the Fontara Mobile NDA template linked.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-shows-Launch-automatically-after-generation-selected-and-the-Fontara-Mobile-Sales-envelope-template-linked.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4ODgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.hv5lWugp1K8KyefxtG5OnLbrGswKYno_v8FZmvIBBCg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot shows Launch automatically after generation selected and the Fontara Mobile NDA template linked.")
   4. Select Save and Finish.

   The Create Button page closes.

You have completed the process. You have linked your Gen template
to a send now envelope template. When users select the custom
button, Docusign generates a document and sends it to the envelope
template recipients.

title: Reset Folder Permissions Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Reset Folder Permissions Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to reset folder permissions for Docusign content with the
Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

You can reset folder permissions for Docusign content in your Salesforce
organization using the Folder Permissions feature of the Docusign TroubleShooting tool. Administrators can select one
button and quickly reset all Docusign folders to their default
permissions, removing all custom security changes.

Use this procedure to use the Folder Permissions feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3OTEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.AUBDQ5fTnIfzGP6nMrJgYaQc7VIw_Dc_Mu4uveS5szI&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3OTEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.AUBDQ5fTnIfzGP6nMrJgYaQc7VIw_Dc_Mu4uveS5szI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.
4. Disconnect Account and Reset Users: Select
   Execute.

   The page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have cleared all custom folder
permissions for your Docusign content.

title: Upload a New Version of a Word Document in Your Gen Template
heading_path: Upload a New Version of a Word Document in Your Gen Template
text:
Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.

When you add, edit, or delete Gen template fields, you must update the associated Word
document.

Example: You add the Opportunity ID field to
your Gen template. You download the Gen template Word document and paste the
Opportunity ID anchor text. You remove the old Word document from
your Gen template and upload the new version.

Use this procedure to update your Word document to reflect changes to your Gen
template:

1. [Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a
   Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nyj1621662912687.html&_LANG=enus):
2. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
3. Select Download and [update
   your Salesforce data fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ffk1586134297621.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to paste your Salesforce anchor text fields into a Microsoft Word document.").
4. Return to the Gen Upload Documents page and select
   X next to the old document version.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/deletewordtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5ODYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.h-kt8BVSDhsH4ioZkcknCm9Npn5WveaFof67iNT2nEY&_LANG=enus)

   A message displays: File was successfully
   deleted.
5. Select Upload Files and follow the prompts to upload the new
   version from your device.

   A message displays: File was successfully uploaded.
6. Select Save and Close.
7. Select Finish and choose Save in the
   Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

You have completed the process. You have uploaded a new
version of your Gen Word document.

title: Change the Owner of a Gen Template
heading_path: Change the Owner of a Gen Template
text:
Learn to change the owner of a Gen template to a different Salesforce user.

You can change the owner of a Gen template to a different user in your Salesforce
organization.

Use this procedure to change Gen template ownership:

1. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4NTIsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.Zb0uYAbkY9qKrSd51Im2HWKuWQqpB2OP7iiyoSxB5So&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose Change
   Owner from the dropdown menu.
3. Add a new owner from the Change Owner dialog box:
   1. Search Users: Enter the name of a Salesforce
      user.
   2. (Optional) Send notification email: Select this
      check box to notify the user that they are the new owner of the Gen
      template.
   3. Select Submit.

   A message displays: (New Salesforce user) now owns the record
   for (Gen template name).

You have completed the process. You have given ownership of
the Gen template to a new Salesforce user.

title: Use Hyperforce with Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Use Hyperforce with Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Learn to add domains to your allowlist so that Docusign apps work with
your Hyperforce organization.

[Hyperforce](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000388902&type=1) is a Salesforce infrastructure architecture
that is built for the public cloud. Hyperforce infrastructure is composed of code
rather than hardware. Hyperforce uses enhanced standards for compliance, security,
agility, scalability, and privacy.

You must update your Salesforce security settings to use Docusign apps in
your Hyperforce organization. Otherwise, Salesforce denies connections to your Docusign account and causes the integration to fail.

You can update your security settings by adding either IP ranges or
domains to your allowlist.

If you want to allow IP addresses, use the following ranges:

- [Salesforce](https://ip-ranges.salesforce.com/ip-ranges.json)
- [Docusign eSignature](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/esignature)
- [Docusign CLM](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/clm)

However, due to the dynamic nature of cloud environments, Salesforce recommends allowing
domains, rather than IP ranges.

Important:

To access Docusign Apps Launcher, Salesforce users must have valid
authorization to the Docusign connected app.

Perform the following actions for your IP-restricted user profiles:

- Copy all restricted Hyperforce IPs from your login history.
- Add the restricted Hyperforce IPs to your organization's allowlist.

For the complete list of Hyperforce IPs, visit the [Salesforce Help site](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=release-notes.rn_hyperforce_inboud_ips.htm&release=260&type=5).

Use this procedure to add domains to your allowlist:

1. Access the Trusted URLs page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.A6DHpSqV84ukXcwhnOHYIuMdOKnzi5Zaa5s17hSimvk&_LANG=enus)salesforcegear and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Trusted URLs in the Quick Find
      field.

   The Trusted URLs page opens.
2. Add a trusted URL to the list:
   1. Select New Trusted URL.
   2. API Name: Enter a simple display name, like
      salesforce for salesforce.com.
   3. URL: Enter
      https://\*.salesforce.com.
   4. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the API Name and URL fields of the Trusted URLs page filled with Salesforce domain information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-API-Name-and-URL-fields-of-the-Trusted-URLs-page-filled-with-salesforce-domain-information.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.A6DHpSqV84ukXcwhnOHYIuMdOKnzi5Zaa5s17hSimvk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the API Name and URL fields of the Trusted URLs page filled with Salesforce domain information.")
3. Repeat the process to add the following domains:

   - https://\*.force.com
   - https://\*.lightning.com
   - https://\*.visualforce.com
   - https://\*.docusign.com
   - https://\*.docusign.net
4. (Optional) [Add domains from this list](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.setup_domains.htm&type=5) if you
   control user or server access to the internet through allowlists.

You have completed the process. You have added domains to
your allowlist. You can use Docusign Apps Launcher apps in your
Hyperforce organization.

title: Edit a Gen Button
heading_path: Edit a Gen Button
text:
Learn to edit the label or details of a Gen custom button.

Use this procedure to edit the label or details of a
Gen custom button:

1. Access the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5OTUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.dcHvq7XpXA5o27yjcKOUp4gMOdQW4iDhc7zI7LEAPbc&_LANG=enus) icon and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select an object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) in the left navigation
      bar.
   4. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5OTUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.dcHvq7XpXA5o27yjcKOUp4gMOdQW4iDhc7zI7LEAPbc&_LANG=enus) next to a custom button and choose Edit from the
   dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5OTUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.dcHvq7XpXA5o27yjcKOUp4gMOdQW4iDhc7zI7LEAPbc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.")
3. Edit Label, Name, or other fields in the
   custom button detail page and select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the custom button detail page with all available fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/geneditcustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5OTUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.dcHvq7XpXA5o27yjcKOUp4gMOdQW4iDhc7zI7LEAPbc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the custom button detail page with all available fields.")

You have completed the process. You have edited the
custom button.

title: Remove Docusign App Access From a Salesforce User
heading_path: Remove Docusign App Access From a Salesforce User
text:
Learn to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from Salesforce
users.

You can use the Remove command to remove Docusign app access from
Salesforce users.

- Remove removes a Salesforce user's access to Docusign apps. This command does not remove users from the connected Docusign account or
  delete Salesforce users.
- You can not remove and close your user while logged on.
- You can not remove and close the only eSignature administrator user.

Use this procedure to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from
Salesforce users:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to remove.
2. Select Remove from the Add
   Users
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MTAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.gQYy5hZNCQUqa0Sk-Cap-TU0_k8bFLHDQdo-IDReZHU&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu.
3. Select Remove.

You have completed the process. You removed a user's access to the
Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce apps. The user remains in the Salesforce
organization and the connected Docusign account.

title: Step 4: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App from your Salesforce organization
heading_path: Step 4: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App from your Salesforce organization
text:
Learn how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your
Salesforce organization.

You have removed all legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce dependencies. You can now
uninstall the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your Salesforce organization
using this procedure:

1. Open the Installed Packages page as an
   administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.bMMyFODbwUGLNHY8wcqialAzTg0jWJOam9ZEghXSPXg&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Installed Packages in the Quick Find
      field.

   The Installed Packages page opens.
2. Select the Uninstall link next to the
   Docusign eSignature for Salesforce package
   name.

   The Uninstalling a Package page opens.
3. Scroll to the bottom of the page and select the Yes, I want to
   uninstall this package and permanently delete all associated
   components check box.
4. Select the Uninstall button.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/daluninstall.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.bMMyFODbwUGLNHY8wcqialAzTg0jWJOam9ZEghXSPXg&_LANG=enus)

   Note: If you have
   remaining dependencies from the legacy eSignature for Salesforce app, an
   Unable to uninstall package message opens.
   Remove the remaining dependencies listed, and then repeat the
   instructions to uninstall the app.

You have completed the entire process. You have
removed all legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce dependencies, and have uninstalled
the app from your Salesforce organization. Your migration from the legacy app to the
current eSignature for Salesforce app is complete.

title: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
heading_path: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
text:
Learn to install and configure the Docusign connected app for your Salesforce organization.

Important: You are not required to make any changes if you have taken the
following steps:

- Installed the Docusign connected app
- Set the OAuth Policy to Admin-approved users are
  pre-authorized

Starting in September 2025, Salesforce began enforcing [restrictions for uninstalled connected apps](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=005132365&type=1). These
restrictions impact some organizations using Docusign Apps
Launcher for Salesforce. If you have not installed the Docusign
connected app and assigned permissions, your users cannot access Docusign apps.

Use this procedure to install the Docusign connected app and grant
access to your users:

1. Access the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MzUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.za0w1TaEGaiIsvNn7JjuKiGvjBe2hnLZzf2nsznOn-U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Connected Apps OAuth Usage in the
      Quick Find field.

      The Connected Apps OAuth Usage page opens.
2. Install the Docusign connected app:
   1. Docusign: Select
      Install.

      The Install connected app? dialog box
      opens.
   2. Select Install.![A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_install_button_highlighted_on_the_Docusign_connected_app_OAuth_Usage_page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MzUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.za0w1TaEGaiIsvNn7JjuKiGvjBe2hnLZzf2nsznOn-U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.")

      The Docusign Connected App Detail page
      displays.
3. Edit the connected app policies:

   Note: Salesforce and Docusign recommend
   selecting Admin-approved users are pre-authorized.
   Each user must enter credentials to gain Docusign for
   Salesforce app access if you select All users may
   self-authorize.

   1. Select Edit Policies.

      The Connected App Edit page displays.
   2. Permitted Users: Select
      Admin-approved users are
      pre-authorized.![A screenshot displays the permitted users field set to Admin approved users are pre-authorized.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_permitted_users_field_set_to_Admin_approved_users_are_pre-authorized.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MzUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.za0w1TaEGaiIsvNn7JjuKiGvjBe2hnLZzf2nsznOn-U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the permitted users field set to Admin approved users are pre-authorized.")

      A dialog box opens: Enabling this option will result
      in all users currently using this app being denied access. Please
      reference the Connected Apps OAuth Usage Report if you are unsure
      who is using the app.
   3. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes.
   4. Select Save.

heading_path: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
text:
