      reference the Connected Apps OAuth Usage Report if you are unsure
      who is using the app.
   3. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes.
   4. Select Save.

      The Docusign Connected App Detail page
      displays.
4. Grant Apps Launcher access to Salesforce users:
   1. Select Manage Profiles or Manage
      Permission Sets at the bottom of the Connected App
      Detail page.![A screenshot displays the OAuth Usage page with the Manage Profiles and Manage Permission Sets buttons highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_OAuth_Usage_page_with_the_Manage_Profiles_and_Manage_Permission_Sets_buttons_highlighted_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MzUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.za0w1TaEGaiIsvNn7JjuKiGvjBe2hnLZzf2nsznOn-U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the OAuth Usage page with the Manage Profiles and Manage Permission Sets buttons highlighted.")
   2. Select the check box for each profile or permission set you want to
      include.
   3. Select Save.

   The Docusign Connected App Detail page
   displays.
5. If your organizations has API Access Control configured, take the following actions:
   1. Enable Allow Visualforce pages to access APIs.
   2. Go to Setup > Permission Sets > your Docusign permission set > Assigned Connected Apps and assign at least one permission set to the Docusign connected app.![A screenshot displays the API Access Control Docusign permission set page with the Assigned Connected Apps link highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/API_Access_Control_Assigned_Connected_Apps.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MzUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.za0w1TaEGaiIsvNn7JjuKiGvjBe2hnLZzf2nsznOn-U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the API Access Control Docusign permission set page with the Assigned Connected Apps link highlighted.")

You have completed the process. You have installed the Docusign connected
app and assigned permissions to your users. Users can access Docusign
apps in your Salesforce organization. It you selected All users may
self-authorize, your users must [re-authenticate to use Docusign apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html).

title: Disable Salesforce Lightning Web Security to Prevent Docusign App Errors
heading_path: Disable Salesforce Lightning Web Security to Prevent Docusign App Errors
text:
Learn how to prevent errors when using Docusign apps in your Salesforce
organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher uses Salesforce Lightning Aura components and Lightning Web components.
Customers who have Lightning Web Security enabled can see an
error message when opening Docusign apps.

The error message reads: This page has an error. You might just need to refresh it.
Error in $A.getCallback() [TypeError: d is not a function] Failing descriptor:
{ltng:outApp}

Important: This error applies to Docusign Apps
Launcher version 5.4 and lower. For Docusign Apps Launcher version 5.5 and higher,
you must enable Lightning Web Security. Visit Setup >  Installed Packages to check your version of Docusign Apps Launcher.

Use this procedure to prevent Lightning web components errors when opening Docusign apps:

1. Access the Session Settings page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.wFvOPtPGc9xG7ehsVEW6GJ2l9BV5WQlP_5RqrMWX0NU&_LANG=enus) icon and
      choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Session Settings in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Uncheck the Use Lightning Web Security for Lightning web
   components box.

   ![A screenshot of the Session Settings page displays Use Lightning Web Security for Lightning web components unchecked.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Disable-the-Use-Lightning-Web-Security-for-Lightning-Web-Components-check-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.wFvOPtPGc9xG7ehsVEW6GJ2l9BV5WQlP_5RqrMWX0NU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Session Settings page displays Use Lightning Web Security for Lightning web components unchecked.")
3. Select Save.
4. Empty your browser cache.

You have completed the process. You have disabled the
Use Lightning Web Security for Lightning web components
setting. Your users do not see an error when they use Docusign apps in your Salesforce
organization.

title: Step 4: Uninstall Apps Launcher from Your organization
heading_path: Step 4: Uninstall Apps Launcher from Your organization
text:
Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your
Salesforce organization.

You have removed all Docusign Apps Launcher dependencies.

Use this procedure to uninstall the Apps Launcher package from your organization:

1. Open the Installed Packages page as an
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.0mLPwoBvqrds1euCOY4a0mAY8DYFhzv4YXpter0H7S4&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in the Quick
      Find field.

      The Installed Packages page opens.
2. Select Uninstall next to **Docusign
   Apps Launcher**.

   The Uninstalling a Package page opens.
3. Scroll to the bottom and select Yes, I want to uninstall this
   package and permanently delete all associated components.
4. Select Uninstall. ![A screenshot displays the uninstall button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/daluninstall.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.0mLPwoBvqrds1euCOY4a0mAY8DYFhzv4YXpter0H7S4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the uninstall button highlighted.")

   Note: If an
   Unable to uninstall package message displays, you
   have remaining Docusign dependencies. Remove the listed
   dependencies and repeat the uninstall process.

You have completed the entire process. You have removed
all Docusign Apps Launcher dependencies, and have uninstalled the
package from your Salesforce organization. You can [install the
latest version from the Salesforce AppExchange](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.").

title: Create Docusign for Salesforce Users
heading_path: Create Docusign for Salesforce Users
text:
Learn to create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users on the user
management page.

Use this procedure to create Docusign
eSignature, CLM, Gen, or Negotiate users on the user management page. You can only add
permissions for Docusign products you have purchased.

Important: A new user displays a Pending
status [until activated by the user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus). After activation, user status displays as Active.

1. Select Add Users.
2. Complete the following tasks in the Select Users dialog box:
   1. Value: Enter a Salesforce user name or email address.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce user that you want to add.
   3. (Optional) Filtered by Filter your search by
      User, Profile, or
      Permission Set.
   4. Select Continue.![A screenshot displays the Select Users dialog box with filters added for User and Profile and a user check box selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Use-filters-to-add-a-user.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2OTQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.JIiuaipInfy2biQdHdurn7TP7SZU9qZ-Cq-7I6oypJA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Select Users dialog box with filters added for User and Profile and a user check box selected.")
3. Apply Permissions: Select permissions for your users:

   - eSignature:
     - Administrator: Administrators can create templates and
       manage all Docusign users in Salesforce.
     - User: Users can send documents for signature.
   - CLM:
     - Administrator: Administrators can access everything in
       CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. Administrators cannot manage Docusign users in Salesforce.
     - User: Users can access workflows and view, edit, and
       upload documents.
     - Viewer: Viewers can only preview and upload
       documents.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the CLM app.
   - Gen:
     - Generator: Generators can create new documents and
       templates.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the Gen app.
   - Negotiate:
     - Negotiator: Negotiators can collaborate with signers and
       viewers.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the Negotiate
       app.
   - (Optional) Update permissions for any existing users: Select
     to update a user's permissions in the connected Docusign
     account.
4. Select Apply.

   The Summary dialog box opens, displaying the message:
   (User name or number of users) was added.

You have completed the process. You have granted or updated Docusign permissions to Salesforce users.

title: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce org to your Docusign Demo
account.

title: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
- [Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")
- [Create a Docusign Developer account](https://developers.docusign.com/)

To use Docusign applications, you must connect a Docusign account to your Salesforce organization. You create this
connection by entering Salesforce and Docusign administrator
credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The Docusign administrator used to connect accounts is called the
integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your Salesforce organization to a Docusign
demo account:

1. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that opens.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that opens
      and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page opens.
2. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login
      dialog box opens.
   2. Select Advanced Options and choose
      Log in to Demo Account.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Docusign-login-page-with-Advanced-Options-and-Log-in-to-Demo-Account-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2MjAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.NejYAl9ngn8bt9Xmc7ijkDYAJ_6r14qunv2ONWmtwQw&_LANG=enus)
   3. Enter Docusign
      *demo* administrator credentials in the dialog box that opens and
      select Log in.
   4. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      opens and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box opens.
3. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page opens.

You have completed the process. You have connected
your Salesforce organization to your Docusign demo account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts
heading_path: Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts
text:
Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce
layouts.

Before you can uninstall Apps Launcher, you must remove all Docusign
components that are present on your layouts.

Use this procedure to remove Docusign components from your layouts:

1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.UUZAKHfYLLVUlWyUCKMW6bcPOmcVnbW40OfG6fFyj-o&_LANG=enus)and choose Edit Page from the dropdown
   menu.

   The Lightning App Builder page opens.
2. Remove all Docusign components from the layout:
   1. Select a Docusign component and select the trash icon
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-123257-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.UUZAKHfYLLVUlWyUCKMW6bcPOmcVnbW40OfG6fFyj-o&_LANG=enus) that displays. ![A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted on the envelope status component.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-trash-icon-highlighted-for-the-Docusign-Files-component.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.UUZAKHfYLLVUlWyUCKMW6bcPOmcVnbW40OfG6fFyj-o&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted on the envelope status component.")
   2. Select Save.
   3. Repeat this procedure for all Docusign components on
      this layout.

   Salesforce removes the components from the layout.
3. Select the back arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-13251-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MzQsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.UUZAKHfYLLVUlWyUCKMW6bcPOmcVnbW40OfG6fFyj-o&_LANG=enus) when you have removed all components.
4. Repeat this procedure for each Salesforce layout that contains Docusign Apps Launcher components.

You have completed step two of the process. You have
removed all Docusign Apps Launcher components from your Salesforce
layouts. Move to [Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=cif1623740064281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.").

title: Disable Docusign CLM Federated Security
heading_path: Disable Docusign CLM Federated Security
text:
Learn how to disable the Federated Security feature in your Salesforce
organization.

Federated Security can be disabled at any time. Here's how to disable Federated Security in
your Docusign CLM account:

1. Log in to your Docusign CLM account as an administrator.
2. Select the Admin tab.
3. Navigate to Integrations  >  Connected Apps.
4. Uncheck the Enable federated security box under the
   Security header.

   ![Disable Federated Security.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Disable-Federated-Security.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NTcsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.FvZW8-A5jQkjpy8Fkkp4QHCGRqLrWlo1wzKrdWV96ck&_LANG=enus "Disable Federated Security.")

   A modal opens, asking: Are you sure you want to disable federated
   security?
5. Select Disable federated security.
6. Select the Save button on the bottom of the page.

The process is complete. Salesforce Federated Security is disabled
on your CLM account. Salesforce and CLM security settings are separate, and
administrators must maintain security models for each.

title: Step 1: Add a Name, Data Source, and Template Editor
heading_path: Step 1: Add a Name, Data Source, and Template Editor
text:
Learn to set the main template details: template name, description, and Salesforce data
source (Invoice.)

Use this procedure to enter a template name and choose
Invoice as the Salesforce data source.

Important: To create a Gen for Billing
template, you must select Invoice as the Salesforce data
source.

1. Select Create a Template Configuration at the far right.
2. Complete the following tasks:
   1. Add Template Name: Enter a name for your
      template.
   2. (Optional) Add Template Description: Add details
      about the template.
   3. Select Data Source: Select
      Invoice from the drop-down menu.
   4. Select Template Editor: Select
      Microsoft Word.
   5. Select Create.

You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: Create Docusign and Salesforce Anchor Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=irc1586134297835.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign and Salesforce anchor text fields for your Gen template.").

title: Disconnect Docusign and Salesforce and Remove Users with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Disconnect Docusign and Salesforce and Remove Users with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to disconnect a Docusign account and remove users with the
Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

If you want to connect your Docusign account to a different
Salesforce organization, use Disconnect Account and Remove
Users. Docusign removes your connection to
Salesforce and deletes all Docusign for Salesforce users.

Important:
Disconnect Account and Remove
Users does not remove users from your connected Docusign account. This feature performs the following actions:

- Disconnects your Docusign account from your Salesforce
  organization
- Removes Docusign access from all Salesforce users
- Resets custom user settings
- Removes all permission set assignments
- Resets the integration user token stored in the connected CLM account

Follow this procedure to use the Disconnect Account and Remove
Users feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3ODMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.wp6k3VbtCkpZ99dK1PqMQcqwO0oV22xu4_dCGa6O0Vk&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3ODMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.wp6k3VbtCkpZ99dK1PqMQcqwO0oV22xu4_dCGa6O0Vk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Disconnect Account and Remove Users: Select
   Execute.

   A message opens: Successfully logged out from Docusign account. User custom
   settings and Docusign Apps Launcher Permission set assignments should be
   cleared shortly. The Troubleshooting page displays the message:
   No Docusign Account Connected.

You have completed the process. You have disconnected your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization. You can [connect Salesforce to a different Docusign
account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
text:
Learn about using conditional logic with check boxes, picklists, fields, and more
in the Test attribute.

You can add conditional logic blocks to Gen template Anchor Text Tags.
Conditional logic creates "if-then" statements that add or exclude
text from a generated agreement. You can also use conditional logic
with checkboxes, picklists, fields, and more.

Important: Content
is case-sensitive.

title: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
Check box example:
text:
Only display content if a check box field is marked
True:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/HasOverdueTask[text() = 'True']" /> |
|  | #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
Picklist examples
text:
- Option 1: Only display content if a multi-picklist
  contains certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/Preferred_Color__c[contains(., ‘Green;Blue’)]" /> #> |
  ```
- Option 2: Only display content if a multi-picklist
  contains certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/Preferred_Color__c[text() = ‘Green;Blue’]" /> #> |
  ```

title: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
Field examples
text:
- Only display content if a field matches certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/Type[text() = 'New Customer' or text() = 'Existing Customer']" |
  |  | /> #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```
- Only display content if two fields match certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/Type[text() = 'New Customer'] and //Opportunity/Industry[text() = |
  |  | 'Technology']" /> #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```
- Only display content if at least one item matches
  some
  value:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="count(//Quote//QuoteLineItems/Base_Product__c[text() = 'Digital Display']) > 0" /> |
  |  | #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```
- Only display content if at least one item contains
  certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test=“count(//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/Name[contains(., ‘Product XYZ’) |
  |  | or contains(., ‘Product ABC’)]) > 0” /> #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```
- Only display content if one field (Field 1) is empty
  and another (Field 2) is not
  empty:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="not(//Opportunity/Field_1__c/text()) and //Opportunity/Field_2__c/text()"/> |
  |  | #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```

Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NTIsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.jz8gn7l-793_ieVyuLcdi249lHd6WO-cwbJS3ak-MZk&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose

title: Use Repeat Tags in Gen to Import Data from Multiple Salesforce Objects
heading_path: Use Repeat Tags in Gen to Import Data from Multiple Salesforce Objects
text:
Learn to use XML Repeat tags in a Word-based Gen template to import data from multiple
Salesforce objects.

You can use XML Repeat tags in a Word-based Gen template to import data from multiple
Salesforce objects. Repeat tags look for Salesforce field data that exists on more
than one object.

When users generate agreements, Docusign imports field data from each
Salesforce object.

Example: You want to generate agreements that insert a Salesforce
contact that exists on multiple objects. You add Repeat tags to your Gen template to
import data from all instances of certain fields.

A user generates an agreement from your Gen template. The record contains a contact
who is the founder and CEO of a number of companies. She is listed in one parent
account and two child opportunities. Docusign generates an
agreement containing her contact information from all three businesses.

Use this procedure to add Repeat tags to your Gen template:

1. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
2. Add a table with custom XML to your Word template:
   1. Select Download.
   2. Open the Word document and enter the following XML code: `<#
      <Repeat Select="/Opportunity/Account/Name" /> #> Name:
      <# <Content Select="/Name" /> #><# <EndRepeat/>
      #>`
   3. Add a Repeat tag to import product information along with the name:

      `<# <Repeat Select="/Opportunity/Account/Name" />
      #>`

      Name: `<#
      <Content Select="/Name" />
      #>`

      Age: `<#
      <Content Select= “/Age” />
      #>`

      Companies:

      - `<# <Repeat Select=
        “/Opportunity/Account/Companies” /> #>`
      - Name: `<# <Content Select=
        “/Opportunity/Account/Companies/Name” />
        #>`
      - Products:
        - `<# <Repeat
          Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts”
          /> #>`
        - Name: `<# <Content
          Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts/Name”
          /> #>`
        - Founder: `<# <Content
          Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts/CompanyProductFounder”
          /> #>`
        - `<# <EndRepeat /> #>`
        - `<# <EndRepeat /> #>`
      - `<# <EndRepeat /> #>``<# <EndRepeat/> #>`
   4. Save the changes in Word and return to the Gen Upload
      Documents page.
3. Add a Repeat tag to import product information along with the name:

   **`<# <Repeat Select="/Opportunity/Account/Name" />
   #>`**

   **Name: `<# <Content Select="/Name" />
   #>`**

   **Age: `<# <Content Select= “/Age” />
   #>`**

   **Companies:**
   - **`<# <Repeat Select= “/Opportunity/Account/Companies”
     /> #>`**
   - **Name: `<# <Content Select=
     “/Opportunity/Account/Companies/Name” />
     #>`**
   - **Products:**
     - **`<# <Repeat
       Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts”
       /> #>`**
     - **Name: `<# <Content
       Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts/Name”
       /> #>`**
     - **Founder: `<# <Content
       Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts/CompanyProductFounder”
       /> #>`**
     - **`<# <EndRepeat /> #>`**
     - **`<# <EndRepeat /> #>`**
   - **`<# <EndRepeat /> #>`****`<# <EndRepeat/> #>`**
4. Save the changes in Word and return to the Gen Upload
   Documents page.
5. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").
6. Select Save and Close and select
   Save on the Do you want to save
   changes? dialog box.

   You have added the Repeat tags to your Gen template. The generated content from
   these Repeat tags displays as follows:

title: Use Repeat Tags in Gen to Import Data from Multiple Salesforce Objects
heading_path: Use Repeat Tags in Gen to Import Data from Multiple Salesforce Objects
text:
lose and select
   Save on the Do you want to save
   changes? dialog box.

   You have added the Repeat tags to your Gen template. The generated content from
   these Repeat tags displays as follows:

   ![A screenshot displays the Word document output of the repeat tags used in this Gen template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Repeat-tag-generated-output.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NjMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.qhyr9qhsUNVDW-ogCX3UW3FLF5WEP21j8s0oBWTwhWU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Word document output of the repeat tags used in this Gen template.")

You have completed the process. You have added group XML tags to
the Word document that you are using for your Gen template. Generated agreements add all
instances of the Salesforce accounts and opportunities related to a contact.

title: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
heading_path: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
text:
Learn to fix the issue of a spinning wheel icon displaying when you try to open Docusign applications.

A spinning wheel icon displays when I try to open Docusign applications. Docusign apps and features never load.

title: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
heading_path: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
Resolution: Upgrade your version of Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: You have Docusign Apps Launcher
version 5.4 or lower installed in your organization.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.9m5KXO2VkhZGtO3_5uCTDffUpO_Fgxnzw5irNDM8fNA&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. If your version number is 5.4 or lower, [upgrade Apps Launcher in your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.").
3. Select Save.

   You have completed the process. You have upgraded the Apps Launcher package in your Salesforce organization. Docusign
   apps and services load normally.

title: Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
Learn to format Agreement Cloud Editor templates to display, hide, or change content
depending on conditions.

You can format Agreement Cloud Editor templates to display, hide, or change content depending on
conditions. The Add logic rule button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genaceconditionalicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus)
displays when you highlight any amount of text in the template document. You can create
logic rules to display or hide this text.

For example, you can set a logic rule to display a paragraph of New York legal text if the
Billing State of a record equals New York.

You can also chain multiple rules together to display text if the record data matches a set
of conditions.

Example:

You create a rule to display text if the record has a Billing State
of New York. You add a second rule to display text if the
Employees field value is greater than 100. You
add a third rule to display text if the Annual Revenue field value is
greater than $1,000,000.

Result: Docusign only displays the text if a record
meets each of these conditions:

- The record has a billing state of New York
- The record has more than 100 employees
- The record has annual revenue exceeding $1,000,000

Docusign does not limit the number of rules you can apply to a block of
text.

Use this procedure to create a logic rule for your Agreement Cloud Editor template:

1. Use the cursor to highlight text and select the Add logic rule
   button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genaceconditionalicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus) that displays to the right.

   ![A screen capture demonstrates applying an Add logic rule to a selection of text.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screen-capture-demonstrates-applying-an-Add-logic-rule-to-a-selection-of-text.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus "A screen capture demonstrates applying an Add logic rule to a selection of text.")

   Rule Builder opens.
2. Create a name for your rule:
   1. Select the Edit
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genaceeditlogicname.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus) icon next to
      Name Rule.
   2. Enter a name for your rule.
   3. Select the check mark to save the new name.

   ![A screenshot displays a logic rule name with a green check mark highlighted in green.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacerenamerule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a logic rule name with a green check mark highlighted in green.")
3. Choose the conditions for your rule:

title: Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
NzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a logic rule name with a green check mark highlighted in green.")
3. Choose the conditions for your rule:

   - Apply any of the following conditions: Trigger your rule if
     at least one condition is met.
   - Apply all of the following conditions: Trigger your rule if
     all conditions are met.
4. Create If logic. Here you create the conditions that a record must
   meet to trigger your rule:
   1. Select the Salesforce Fields icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacesalesforcefieldsicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus)
      in the left column.
   2. Drag a Salesforce field to the If box.
   3. Equals: Keep this selection or select Does not
      equal from the dropdown menu.
   4. Enter text in the field next to Equals. For example, enter
      "New York" if you want a rule of Billing State/Province >  Equals >  New York.

      ![A screen capture demonstrates applying a Salesforce field to an if logic block.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screen-capture-demonstrates-applying-a-Salesforce-field-to-an-IF-logic-block.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus "A screen capture demonstrates applying a Salesforce field to an if logic block.")
   5. (Optional) OR IF: Add more conditions to your rule.
   6. (Optional): Move the cursor to a condition and select ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.pTXvGh_1p7X2IjhjuszN2rOMGXBe61BmU6sfWzknNN4&_LANG=enus)
      to delete it.
   7. (Optional) + ADD RULE: Add another rule to this block of
      text.
5. Decide whether to display or hide text when your rule is matched:

   - Then: Choose an option from the dropdown menu:
     - Show the following content
     - Hide the following content
   - Otherwise...: Choose an option from the dropdown menu:
     - Hide the following content
     - Show the following content
6. Save and publish your rule:
   1. Select Save at the top right of the Rule Builder
      section.
   2. Select Next.
   3. Select Publish.

   A message displays: Template (your template name)
   updated.

You have completed the process. You added a logic rule to your
Agreement Cloud Editor template. When users generate documents, Docusign
displays, hides, or changes content based on record data.

title: Edit a Gen Template

text:
Learn to edit a Gen template.

You can edit a Gen template to add or edit documents,
Docusign fields, and Salesforce fields. If you edit the name of a
Gen template, also edit the custom button name.

Use this procedure to edit a Gen
template:

1. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4NjIsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.WeAS-1ULXpUg-Ed8Mfi4d1gwDG2Wfj8ycWFf2HkX5g4&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose

   opens.

template for editing. You can add or edit documents, Docusign fields,
and Salesforce fields.

title: Add Split and Nested Conditional Tags to a Gen Template
heading_path: Add Split and Nested Conditional Tags to a Gen Template
text:
Learn how to add split and nested conditional tags to a Word-based Gen
template.

You can add conditional logic blocks to Gen template Anchor Text Tags. Conditional logic
creates "if-then" statements that add or exclude text from a generated agreement. For
example, you can add conditional logic that hides new customer language when generating
existing customer agreements.

You always start conditional logic blocks with a <Conditional>
tag and end with an <EndConditional> tag. You can place
<Conditional> and
<EndConditional> tags on the same line. You create split
conditionals by splitting conditional text over multiple lines of text.

You can also use nested conditional logic, which involves placing child conditional tags
inside of parent tags. For example, you have conditional text for new customers and add
child conditional text for all new California customers.

Use this procedure to add split and nested conditional tags to a Word-based Gen
template:

1. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
2. Add the conditional anchor text to your Word document. In this example, you split
   conditional tags over multiple lines and add a nested legal clause for California
   customers:
   1. Select Download and open the document in Microsoft
      Word.
   2. Where you want the conditional text to display, paste `<# <Conditional
      Select="Opportunity/Type" Match="New Customer"/ > #>Welcome, new
      customer.<# <Conditional Select="Opportunity/State" Match="California" />
      #>Custom California Legal clause< #<EndConditional/>
      #>`.
   3. Replace the Custom California Legal clause text with your
      California legal clause text.![A screenshot displays split and nested conditional text around California legal clause text.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-split-and-nested-conditional-text-around-California-legal-clause-text.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.IwEvtSuNVRx6UxPDDOh6s3P3MfhYyjlSXk2fRopKbgs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays split and nested conditional text around California legal clause text.")
   4. Save changes and go to the Gen Upload Documents page.
3. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.")
4. Select Save and Close and select Save on
   the Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

You have completed the process. You have added split and nested
conditional logic for new customers. Generated agreements for California customers display the
California legal clause. Generated agreements for all other new customers do not display the
California clause.

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
text:
You can customize the fields you add in the template creation process.

You can customize the Docusign fields you add in the template creation
process. You can use the following options:

- Required or read-only
- Add additional signers
- Add tooltips
- Customize data labels
- Add validation types
- Add character limits

You can use the following fields and customizations in your Gen templates:

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Add Recipient Views
text:
Use this Docusign Fields menu to add more recipients and signature
fields. You can create a customized view for each recipient, giving each their own
signature fields.

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Signature
text:
Place this field where the recipient adds their signature to the document. You can
configure the following options:

- Switch to a different recipient
- Required or optional
- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Initial
text:
Place this field where the recipient will add their initials. You can configure the
following options:

- Switch to a different recipient
- Required or optional
- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Date Signed
text:
Place this field where the signature date will be added. You can configure the
following options:

- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Name
text:
Add this field where the recipient name will be placed. You can configure the
following options:

- First name only
- Last name only
- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Email
text:
Add this field where the recipient email address will be placed. You can configure
the following options:

- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Company
text:
Add this field where the organization name will be placed. You can configure the
following options:

- Switch to a different recipient
- Required or optional
- Mark as read only
- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Title
text:
Add this field where the recipient title will be placed. You can configure the
following options:

- Switch to a different recipient
- Required or optional
- Mark as read only
- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Text
text:
Add this field where text can be added by the recipient. You can configure the
following options:

- Switch to a different recipient
- Required or optional
- Mark as read only
- Add a character limit
- Character type validation
- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce Record
text:
Learn to change the type of currency Salesforce displays on a record.

Your Salesforce organization has a default currency format. However, your administrator can
add new currencies to your organization. Adding currencies lets you change the
default currency on a record.

Example: Your Salesforce organization uses U.S.
Dollar as the default currency, but your administrator has added
Euro. You change the Opportunity
Currency field of a French company to Euro.
The Amount field displays the value in Euros.

Note: If you want to [choose the currency format when generating documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=hhz1686691127617.html&_LANG=enus), you must change a record's currency first.

Use this procedure to change the currency type of a Salesforce record:

1. Select the Details tab of a record.
2. Select the pencil icon ![A screenshot of the pencil icon used to edit fields on a Salesforce record.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-pencil-icon-used-to-edit-fields-on-a-Salesforce-record.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NjEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.yCjiCH_1_I-WCuVjb1affEwV5ohaWfCCOCNJzHC__d4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the pencil icon used to edit fields on a Salesforce record.") on the (object) Currency field.
3. Select a new currency type from the dropdown menu.
4. Select Save.

   The Amount field displays the new
   currency.

You have completed the process. You have changed the default
currency of a record.

title: Gen Template Advanced Options: Agreement Cloud Editor
heading_path: Gen Template Advanced Options: Agreement Cloud Editor
text:
The Agreement Cloud Editor is an in-browser option for creating and editing Docusign Gen for Salesforce templates.

The Agreement Cloud Editor is an in-browser option for creating Gen templates without using
Microsoft Word. You can quickly add Docusign and Salesforce fields to a
template, without copying and pasting XML tags. You can also Download Agreement Cloud Editor
templates as Word documents.

Administrators can use these advanced features to customize their Agreement Cloud Editor Gen
templates:

- [Download Agreement Cloud Editor Documents as Word Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=crs1588512498923.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download Agreement Document Format Gen contracts as Microsoft Word documents.")
- [Customize Agreement Cloud Editor Template Text](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=iat1586134315670.html&_LANG=enus "You can customize Agreement Cloud Editor template documents to change font size and style, add sections of conditional content and add tables.")
- [Preview an Agreement Cloud Editor Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=hht1586134306427.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to see what a document generated from your template looks like.")
- [Add Child Objects to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=qsk1586134322865.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add up to four levels of child objects and two levels of related lists.")
- [Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=wqi1586134323385.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to format Agreement Cloud Editor templates to display, hide, or change content depending on conditions.")
- [Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=zeh1586134329665.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.")
- [Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=xna1586134322115.html&_LANG=enus "You can customize the fields you add in the template creation process.")

title: Understanding Docusign Permission Sets in Salesforce
heading_path: Understanding Docusign Permission Sets in Salesforce
text:
Learn about the types and functions of Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets.

Permission sets are a collection of Salesforce settings and permissions that give users access to various tools and functions. Permission sets extend Salesforce users’ functional access without changing their profiles.

Docusign adds a group of permission sets when you install the Docusign Apps Launcher package. Docusign permission sets display which Salesforce users have Docusign app access.

Important:

- Administrators grant most Docusign app access with  [Docusign User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus). Administrators can only use the Docusign Login permission set to help grant access.
- Administrators should not manually add or remove users from Docusign permissions sets except Docusign Login.

The following is a list of the Docusign permission sets and their functions:

| Permission Set Name | Permission Set Function |
| --- | --- |
| Docusign Login | Allows users to authorize Docusign Apps Launcher and auto-assign permission sets corresponding to existing Docusign user permissions |
| Docusign Administrator | Allows administrative access to Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Admin | Allows administrative access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Experience Cloud User | Allows access to CLM in Communities (Experience Cloud) |
| Docusign CLM User | Allows subscriber access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Viewer | Allows guest access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign Document Generator | Allows users to generate documents with Docusign Gen |
| Docusign Document Negotiator | Allows users to send files for internal approval and external review with Docusign Negotiate |
| Docusign Sender | Allows users to send envelopes as the system sender in Docusign Apps Launcher﻿ |
| Docusign User | Allows access to eSignature in Docusign Apps Launcher |

Users Send and Sign Envelopes With the Apex Toolkit

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
text:
Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature. Sync Domains
fixes issues with Enhanced Domains and any changes that you make to your Salesforce
domain.

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce
v5.2](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04t3u000002wLcp)

You can change your Salesforce domain name to update your brand, adopt [Enhanced Domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.domain_name_enhanced.htm&type=5), or enable partitioned domains. When
your My Domain name changes, your Salesforce-hosted URLs also change. You must
rebuild the connection to your Docusign account.

The Sync Domains feature of the Docusign
TroubleShooting tool rebuilds your connection to Docusign after a
domain change.

Use this procedure to rebuild your CLM or CLM
Essentials integration with the Sync Domains
feature:

1. Access the My Domain page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.IXeFUGBopw7sE65p_-wNCAHtPV20ETqyL8N4K4e1Agg&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select My Domain in the Quick Find
      field.
2. Enable redirects for your domain:

   Note: The Domain Name System (DNS) server takes up to 72 hours to update
   the connection between Docusign and Salesforce. Keep the
   redirect setting enabled during this time.

   1. Routing: Select Edit.
   2. Select the Redirect previous My Domain URLs to your current
      My Domain check box.
   3. Select Save. The My Domain page opens.
3. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.IXeFUGBopw7sE65p_-wNCAHtPV20ETqyL8N4K4e1Agg&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
4. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4MDMsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.IXeFUGBopw7sE65p_-wNCAHtPV20ETqyL8N4K4e1Agg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
5. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
6. Sync Domains: Select Execute.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Successfully synced
   domains on Docusign account.
7. (Optional): After 72 hours, uncheck the Redirect previous My Domain
   URLs to your current My Domain box.

You have completed the process. You have rebuilt the CLM or CLM
Essentials integration with your Salesforce organization.

title: Refresh or Reconnect a Salesforce Organization With Docusign CLM Federated Security
heading_path: Refresh or Reconnect a Salesforce Organization With Docusign CLM Federated Security
text:
Learn about best practices for refreshing a Salesforce organization with Federated
Security enabled.

Salesforce organizations using CLM Federated Security should avoid taking these actions:

- Refreshing their organization
- Connecting their CLM account to a different Salesforce organization

If a Salesforce organization must be refreshed or re-connected, follow these steps
first (in order):

1. [Turn off Federated Security in your CLM
   account.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=vbn1599207323944.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disable the Federated Security feature in your Salesforce organization.")
2. Open the CLM **Other Sources** folder and rename the Salesforce folder:
   **Salesforce\_Old**.
3. Create a new **Salesforce** folder under Other Sources parent folder.

   ![Rename Salesforce folder.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Rename-Salesforce-folder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc4OTAsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.OnOlsgUBFgJtLKvh6rhOdmcKJoyeYCpEB7yq2b4JmUg&_LANG=enus "Rename Salesforce folder.")
4. [Perform a refresh of your Salesforce
   Sandbox](https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=data_sandbox_refresh.htm&type=5).
5. Add records to your Sandbox.
6. [Turn on CLM Federated Security](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=gqm1599194045432.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable standard Federated Security in your Docusign CLM account.").
7. Test your updates to ensure that all is working properly.

The process is complete. The Sandbox is refreshed and
Federated Security is properly applied to the new content. All archived content is found
in the **Salesforece\_Old** folder.

title: Edit or Delete a Gen Template or Custom Button
heading_path: Edit or Delete a Gen Template or Custom Button
text:
Learn how to edit, delete, or change ownership for a Gen template or custom
button.

You can edit, delete, or change ownership for a Gen template or custom button. If you edit
the name of a Gen template, also edit the custom button name. If you delete a Gen template,
remove that template's buttons from your Salesforce layouts.

Use the following links to edit, delete, or change ownership of a Gen template:

- [Edit the Name of a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=gww1586134337518.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the name of a Gen template.")
- [Edit a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=xcs1646713533209.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit a Gen template.")
- [Delete a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=mbd1586134342525.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to delete a Gen template.")
- [Change the Owner of a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=sut1646711991124.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the owner of a Gen template to a different Salesforce user.")
- [Edit a Gen Custom Button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=tet1586134342760.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit the label or details of a Gen custom button.")
- [Delete a Gen Custom Button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=bzt1586134341821.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove a Gen custom button from your Salesforce page layouts.")

Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc5NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.vGIiDLf_a0eQxzt8SdDvFtz1xaZ9j6PHS4BwiKNTA6Q&_LANG=enus) icon and choose

title: Uninstall Docusign Apps Launcher from Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Uninstall Docusign Apps Launcher from Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps from
your Salesforce organization.

You may be required to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from
your Salesforce organization. The Apps Launcher package contains the CLM, Gen,
Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce applications.

You might uninstall the package for the following reasons:

- You want to revert to an earlier version of the Apps Launcher package.
- You installed the latest version for Admins Only, and must reinstall
  for All Users.
- You installed a Beta version of the package and must install the official release.

To uninstall the package, you must first remove all Apps Launcher dependencies from your
Salesforce organization. Dependencies are relationships between Docusign apps and Salesforce buttons, layouts, and objects. The
following examples demonstrate the dependencies between Salesforce and Docusign:

- You add the **Docusign CLM Files** component to the Account
  layout.
- You create a Gen template and add a Gen button to the Opportunities layout.
- You add Salesforce users to the Docusign Generator
  permission set.

Salesforce prompts you to remove these dependencies when you uninstall Apps
Launcher.

Use these procedures to remove all dependencies and uninstall Docusign Apps
Launcher from your organization:

1. [Remove users from Docusign
   Apps Launcher permission sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=pnw1623705527946.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all users from Docusign permission sets.")
2. [Remove Docusign Apps
   Launcher components from Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=uxu1623708462949.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce layouts.")
3. [Remove Docusign Apps
   Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=cif1623740064281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.")
4. [Uninstall the Docusign Apps
   Launcher package from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ump1623741000284.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your Salesforce organization.")

Select the Salesforce user icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/salesforceusericon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc3NTEsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.THdM4E3bZfljM0CBLsARqW7a9nATMfFFiYq3gw2VVmI&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Settings.

chose.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
text:
Learn how to grant Docusign app access to your Salesforce
users.

There are several ways that you can grant Docusign app access to your
Salesforce users:

[Docusign user management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create Docusign users with the Docusign Apps Launcher
    user management page.

[Docusign Login permission set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xxm1623778416524.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create Docusign users by adding Salesforce users to the Docusign
     Login permission set.

Single sign-on (SSO)
:   Create Docusign users with the Docusign Login
    permission set and your SSO identity provider.

When you grant Docusign access to a Salesforce user, Docusign searches for a matching Docusign name
and email address. If matching credentials exist, Docusign links
the Salesforce and Docusign users. If matching credentials do not
exist, Docusign creates a new user.

Important: Never change the name or email address of a
Salesforce or Docusign user after activation. If Docusign and Salesforce credentials don't match, the user may be
unable to send envelopes and access Docusign objects.

You can use the following methods to grant Docusign access to Salesforce
users:

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates from an email link
text:
A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user receives an email from Docusign, selects the activation
link, and creates a password.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their Docusign and Salesforce credentials.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates with SSO credentials
text:
A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their SSO
credentials.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
An existing Docusign user activates with standard or SSO credentials
text:
You can grant app access to an existing Docusign user on the user management
page. Existing Docusign users can activate their Docusign app access from an email link or with SSO credentials.

Important: Make sure the Salesforce user's name and email
address match those of the existing Docusign user. If the
Salesforce user's credentials differ from the existing Docusign
user's, Docusign creates a new user.

A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their credentials:

- Standard authentication: The user enters Salesforce and Docusign credentials
- SSO authentication: The user enters SSO credentials

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A user with Docusign Login permissions self-activates
text:
You can let existing Docusign users activate their Docusign app access by adding them to the Docusign Login permission
set. Existing users can self-activate using Docusign and
Salesforce credentials or with SSO credentials. This permission set lets you manage
user activation in Salesforce, not on the Docusign user management
page.

A Salesforce administrator adds a Salesforce user to the Docusign
Login permission set.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their credentials:

- Standard authentication: The user enters Salesforce and Docusign credentials
- SSO authentication: The user enters SSO credentials

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates with SSO just-in-time provisioning
text:
You can create new Docusign users by enabling Docusign SSO
and adding Salesforce users to the Docusign Login permission set.
Users can self-activate Docusign app access using SSO credentials.

Important:

- You must enable just-in-time user provisioning for Docusign in your SSO
  identity provider.
- You can only use just-in-time provisioning to add new eSignature for Salesforce users. You
  can add Gen, Negotiate, or CLM permissions after you create eSignature
  users.

A Salesforce administrator adds a Salesforce user to the Docusign
Login permission set.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their SSO
credentials.

title: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
heading_path: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
text:
Learn to add a table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.

You can add the following table types to an Agreement Cloud Editor template:

title: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
heading_path: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
Standard Table
text:
You create a standard table by manually adding the information in your table. You decide
the number of rows and columns in your table, and you manually add data into the cells. You
do not add Salesforce merge fields to a standard table.

Example: You create a standard table in your Agreement Cloud Editor
template. You add one column and enter New England as the header. You
add six rows and enter the name of a New England state in each row. A user generates a
document from a record. The standard table that you created displays in the generated
document.

[Add a Standard Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=zqz1586134333505.html&_LANG=enus "You use the table button to add a standard table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.")

title: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
heading_path: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
Dynamic Table
text:
You create a dynamic table by adding Salesforce line item fields to columns. Docusign uses the line item fields to import Salesforce data to your table.
When you generate a document, Docusign creates a table that adds rows and
columns to match the record's data.

Example: You create a dynamic table in your Agreement Cloud Editor
template containing the Salesforce Region field. A user generates a
document from a record with the Region value of New England. Docusign creates a table with one column and six rows. The column is titled
New England and each row displays the name of a New England
state.

[Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=pzt1586134333317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.")

title: Learn About Connecting Salesforce and Docusign
heading_path: Learn About Connecting Salesforce and Docusign
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign
account.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a unified package that includes these
applications:

- CLM for Salesforce
- CLM Essentials for Salesforce
- Gen for Salesforce
- eSignature for Salesforce
- eSignature for Salesforce Apex Toolkit

You must connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account to
use these apps. To create the connection, you must enter Docusign and
Salesforce administrator credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The
Docusign administrator credentials that you use to connect accounts is
called the integration user.

The integration user must have Salesforce System Administrator access.
The integration user's Salesforce profile must have the Enable MFA for API
logins value set to False.

You can also follow these instructions to [make a
standard Salesforce user your integration user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qig1698704091391.html&_LANG=enus).

Note:

- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.
- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration user. For
  more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").

Example: An administrator at Fontara creates a generic Docusign administrator named
docusign-admin@fontara.com. No person at Fontara uses these
credentials to access their Docusign account. The Fontara administrator
connects Docusign and Salesforce by entering the
docusign-admin@fontara.com credentials on the Docusign
Setup page. Since no person uses these credentials, the integration is
protected from administrators leaving or changing roles.

Use a procedure to connect your Docusign account to your Salesforce
organization:

- [Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=qnq1614667628308.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign production account.")
- [Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=uxq1614636863027.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce org to your Docusign Demo account.")
- [Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=bye1699036089712.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.")

title: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
heading_path: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
text:
Learn how to find the Salesforce object from which users sent a Docusign envelope.

The Docusign Status page displays the status of Docusign envelopes sent from Salesforce records. You can find the page by opening
Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce and selecting the
Docusign Status tab.

However, Docusign Status does not display the name of the Salesforce object
from which you sent the envelope. You can use Salesforce object key
prefixes to identify the Salesforce object type.

Object key prefixes are the first three characters in a Docusign envelope's
Salesforce Source ID. Each three-character prefix
represents a different Salesforce object.

title: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
heading_path: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
Example
text:
You want to know from which objects (Accounts, Opportunities, and so on) you sent an envelope.
You follow these steps:

1. Open Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce.
2. Select the Docusign Status tab.
3. Check the first three digits of the envelope IDs in the Salesforce Source
   ID column.![A screenshot of the Docusign Status page highlights the first three digits of envelopes' Salesforce Source IDs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Docusign-Status-page-highlights-the-first-three-digits-of-envelopes-Salesforce-Source-IDs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc2NzUsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.UBsPtROfx9gcJzfb-71iyWK4l4gclYM2Lv-vBihSczQ&_LANG=enus)

The following table displays the list of Salesforce object key prefixes:
