title: Create Gen Templates to Automate Your Contract Process
heading_path: Create Gen Templates to Automate Your Contract Process
text:
Learn to build Docusign Gen templates to automate your contract
generation process.

You can build Docusign Gen templates to automate your contract
generation process. Gen templates create agreements by merging Docusign and Salesforce fields to a Word document that you upload.
When users select the Gen button, Docusign generates an agreement
based on the record data.

Important: To create a template, [grant Generator status to your
Docusign user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=doi1628107496987.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.").

You can create the following types of Gen templates:

[Microsoft Word Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.")
:   Build a Gen template using Microsoft Word. Copy Salesforce and Docusign fields from Gen into a Word document. Then upload
    the document to your Gen template.

    Benefit: More
    flexibility. This method gives you the most flexibility
    when building a template. You can use custom XML code to add custom
    tables, filters, and conditional logic to your template.

    Drawback: More manual work. You must
    copy and paste fields between Salesforce and Word and upload your Word
    document to the Gen template.

[Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=bcp1586134298132.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a Gen template with the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor.")
:   Build a Gen template using the Docusign Agreement Cloud
    Editor. Drag Salesforce and Docusign fields directly to an
    editable in-browser document.

    Benefit: Ease of use.
    You create templates in your browser without leaving Salesforce. You do
    not need to copy and paste anchor text into a Word
    document.

    Drawback: Less flexibility. You
    can not use some of the more advanced XML options in Word. You can still
    create tables, filters, and conditional logic with some limitations.

[Gen for Billing Word Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ldc1610646513281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Gen for Billing templates to generate invoices from a Salesforce record automatically or from a custom button.")
:   Build a Word Gen template based on the Salesforce
    Invoice object. You can choose to have invoices
    generate automatically when a record changes status to
    Posted. You can also create a custom button to let
    users generate an invoice manually.

    Note: Only Gen for
    Salesforce Billing customers can use this option.

[Person Account Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=ird1636867371186.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build a Gen template using the Salesforce Person Account object.")
:   Build a Word Gen template based on the Salesforce Person
    Account object.

    Docusign cannot
    display the Person Account object when you select a Salesforce data
    source at the start of the template creation process. You must select
    Account as the data source and manually add a custom button to the
    Person Account layout.

[Clone a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=rwn1586134208474.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to clone a Gen template to create a new template that is similar to an existing one.")
:   Copy or "clone" a Docusign Gen template. Cloning is
    helpful if you want to create a new template similar to an existing
    template.

title: Create Gen Templates to Automate Your Contract Process
heading_path: Create Gen Templates to Automate Your Contract Process
text:
reate a new template that is similar to an existing one.")
:   Copy or "clone" a Docusign Gen template. Cloning is
    helpful if you want to create a new template similar to an existing
    template.

[One-Click Generation and Sending Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=sjm1686076461925.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a one-click generation and sending experience for generating a contract and sending it for signature.")
:   Link a send now envelope template to a Gen template.
    When users select the button on a record, Docusign creates
    a document and sends it for signature. Docusign does not
    allow input from the sender in this experience.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
text:
Learn about using table row XML tags to import Salesforce data into a generated
agreement.

You can use table row XML tags in Word-based Gen templates to import Salesforce data into a
generated agreement.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Table Row tag
text:
The Table Row tag is represented by the <TableRow> XML tag. You
use a Table Row tag in a table that you add to your Word-based Gen template. When
users generate agreements, Table Row tags dynamically add a row for each unique
piece of record data.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Table Row tag example
text:
You add a Table Row tag to your Gen template. A user generates an agreement from a Salesforce
record that has five product fields. Docusign adds five table rows
to the generated agreement; one for each product.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Using Table Row tags in Gen templates
text:
You create custom tables in Word by copying tags from a Gen template and pasting them
after a table row header. Use the following guidelines when creating Table Row
tags:

- Place Table Row tags at the beginning of the first cell of the table.
- The Select attribute adds a new row in the table for each match that it
  finds.
- The Select attribute adds rows in order of the values that it finds on a
  record.
- If you place all merge tags inside of a table row, Docusign
  adds them based on their rules.
- You can place any merge tag in a table row.
- You can place multiple Table Row tags in a single table.
- You can nest Table Row tags. Copy a table that contains a Table Row tag and
  paste it inside the cell of another table.
- You can use an unlimited number of rows in a Microsoft Word table.
- <# <TableRow … /> #> must always be the first element on
  a table row, with no leading spaces.
- A single table cannot contain both Table Row and Table Column tags.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Table Row attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in <TableRow> tags:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath to select data from an XML document |
| Tag | Can be any String value |
| TagRef | Used with a Tag attribute placed on a Repeat, Table Row, or Table Column tag |
| TrackName | Specific to Term Sync |

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Row tag
text:
The Suppress Table Row tag is represented by the
<SuppressTableRow> XML tag. You use a Suppress
Table Row tag in a table you add to your Word-based Gen template. When users
generate agreements, Suppress Table Row tags dynamically hide rows based on record
data. Suppress Table Row tags are useful when a record has no data in a field. You
can also use Suppress Table Row tags to hide data from certain recipients.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Row example
text:
You add a Suppress Table Row tag to your Gen template. A user generates an agreement from a
Salesforce record with a row for a Service Add-On. The record's Service Add-On field
contains no data. Docusign does not add the Service Add-On row to
the generated agreement.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Using Suppress Table Row tags in Gen templates
text:
You create custom tables in Word by copying tags from a Gen template and pasting them
after a table column header. Use the following guidelines when creating Suppress
Table Row tags:

- <SuppressTableRow> removes an entire row from a table.
- <SuppressTableRow> attributes are identical to Conditional tag attributes.
- Docusign removes the entire table if the row to be suppressed is the only
  one on the record.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Row attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in <SuppressTableRow> tags:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath used to select data from an XML document |
| Match | Table row is suppressed (removed) if the data returned matches this value |
| NotMatch | Table row is suppressed (removed) if the data returned does not match this value |
| Test | Conditional option based on Boolean logic |
| TagRef | Used with a Tag attribute placed on a Repeat, Table Row, or Table Column tag |

title: Add a Standard Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add a Standard Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
You use the table button to add a standard table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.

You can add a standard or dynamic table to your Agreement
Cloud Editor template. Use this procedure to add a standard table to an Agreement Cloud Editor
Gen template using the table button.

Note: You cannot add Salesforce data tags to a standard table. Follow this link to [add Salesforce data tags to dynamic
tables](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=pzt1586134333317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.").

1. Open the Gen template Create tab as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.MHGf_1vZFzmz0APZqRp-YAL13hUCZgwMaja64IfkC3E&_LANG=enus) and go to View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.MHGf_1vZFzmz0APZqRp-YAL13hUCZgwMaja64IfkC3E&_LANG=enus) next to a template and choose
      Edit.

   The Gen template page opens to the Create tab.
2. Place the cursor where you want to add a table and select the
   Table button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacetable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.MHGf_1vZFzmz0APZqRp-YAL13hUCZgwMaja64IfkC3E&_LANG=enus).

   A dropdown menu displays a table grid.
3. Move the cursor to the right to add columns, and down to add rows. The cells change
   from white to blue.

   Note: This example creates a table with three columns and four
   rows.![A screenshot displays a table grid with three columns and four rows of blue squares.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/gab1637077302276/page/images/genacetableadd.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5NzYsInNoZWFmIjoiZ2FiMTYzNzA3NzMwMjI3NiJ9.MHGf_1vZFzmz0APZqRp-YAL13hUCZgwMaja64IfkC3E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a table grid with three columns and four rows of blue squares.")
4. Enter text to the rows and columns of the inserted table.
5. Select Next and Publish.

   A message displays: Template (your template name)
   updated.

You have completed the process. You have added a
standard table to a Gen template.

title: Envelope Template Recipient Types
heading_path: Envelope Template Recipient Types
text:
You can add many different recipient types to an envelope template. You can add
recipients from Salesforce, from Docusign, or manually enter them.

You can add many different recipient types to an envelope or envelope template. You can add
recipients from Salesforce, from Docusign, or manually enter them.

You can add the following recipient types:

Table 1. Recipient Types

| Recipient Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lookup Fields | Choose Lookup Fields when you want to automate the adding of recipients. Example: If you choose **Owner**, the name and email address of a record owner is automatically added.  Only fields related to the template Salesforce data source (Account, Opportunity, and so on) will appear. |
| Related Lists | You can choose fields to relate to a Salesforce object. Example: Contacts related list for an Account, OpportunityContactRole for an Opportunity, and so on.  This recipient type dynamically adds recipients from a Related List. Only fields previously related to the template data source (Account, Opportunity, and so on) appear.  You can only add standard Salesforce related lists, like CaseContactRoles, OpportunityContactRoles, and so on.  Custom Related Lists do not appear in results. |
| Users/Contacts | You can add a specific user from your Salesforce Users or Contacts. This user is not added dynamically. |
| Sender | Add the sender of the envelope to the recipient list. This sender is the person who selects the envelope template custom button on a record.  To add a Sender recipient, you must select +Add Role and enter a role, like Sender 1. |
| By Role | Add a generic recipient (ex: Approver) as a placeholder, if you do not yet know the names or types of recipients to be added. The sender can then add a recipient name or type during the sending process.  You can also add a specific recipient's name and email here.  You can use custom syntax to dynamically add a By Role recipient from a Salesforce object. |
| **Signing Group** | Add a signing group from your organization's Docusign account. Any signer in the group can complete the signature process for that group. |

title: Use a Send Now Envelope Template Button to Send an Envelope From a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Use a Send Now Envelope Template Button to Send an Envelope From a Salesforce Record
text:
Learn how to use a send now envelope template button to create and send an envelope from
a Salesforce record.

You can use a send now
envelope template button to instantly create and deliver an envelope from a Salesforce
record.

Send now templates are pre-built with
documents, recipients, Docusign fields, and Salesforce fields already added. This
speeds up the envelope creation and sending process.

All you have to do is
select the send now button on a Salesforce record and the envelope is instantly
built and sent to recipients.

Use this procedure to send an envelope using
a send now envelope template:

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
2. Select the send now envelope template button.

   ![A screenshot displays a record with the Send Now envelope template button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Send-Now-envelope-template-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.BjtpGHFfVM9meIW-924MItWVpFa-z30Smbl0Y_KGcY0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a record with the Send Now envelope template button highlighted.")

   Note: Your administrator
   chooses the button name. It may be hidden in the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.BjtpGHFfVM9meIW-924MItWVpFa-z30Smbl0Y_KGcY0&_LANG=enus) menu.

   A message opens: Your envelope was
   sent!

You have completed the process. You have created and sent an
envelope using a send now envelope template.

title: Place Fields Actions: Thumbnails, Document Settings, and Upload a Document
heading_path: Place Fields Actions: Thumbnails, Document Settings, and Upload a Document
text:
You can use the Thumbnails button on the Place Fields page to see
thumbnail previews of each page of the template document. You can also access a menu of
actions for the Place Fields step.

title: Place Fields Actions: Thumbnails, Document Settings, and Upload a Document
heading_path: Place Fields Actions: Thumbnails, Document Settings, and Upload a Document
Thumbnails menu
text:
You can use the Thumbnails button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Show-or-hide-thumbnails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.Jae09GkvziHf3griynY1e9Drksadta75SXEnmLYNnh4&_LANG=enus) on the Place
Fields page to see a preview of each template document. You can also access a menu
of actions for the Place Fields step.![A screenshot displays the thumbnails menu open to a document preview.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Show-or-Hide-document-thumbnails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.Jae09GkvziHf3griynY1e9Drksadta75SXEnmLYNnh4&_LANG=enus)

| Button | Button Name | Action |

|  | Thumbnails | Show or hide thumbnails menu pane |
|  | Document Settings | Menu for the Edit Documents and Add IDR Zones options |
|  | Rotate | Rotate a page |
|  | Delete | Remove a page |

title: Place Fields Actions: Thumbnails, Document Settings, and Upload a Document
heading_path: Place Fields Actions: Thumbnails, Document Settings, and Upload a Document
Document Settings menu
text:
The Document Settings gear icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Gear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.Jae09GkvziHf3griynY1e9Drksadta75SXEnmLYNnh4&_LANG=enus) opens a separate
menu:![A screenshot displays the Document Settings menu, including Edit Documents and Add IDR Zones.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Document-Settings-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.Jae09GkvziHf3griynY1e9Drksadta75SXEnmLYNnh4&_LANG=enus)

Table 2. Upload a Document Page Options

title: Place Fields Actions: Thumbnails, Document Settings, and Upload a Document
heading_path: Place Fields Actions: Thumbnails, Document Settings, and Upload a Document
Upload a Document menu
text:
The Edit Documents option opens the Upload a Document page,
where you can edit or upload content.![A screenshot displays document options, including Set as Supplement, Replace, Download, Delete, Rename, and View.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Upload_a_doc.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.Jae09GkvziHf3griynY1e9Drksadta75SXEnmLYNnh4&_LANG=enus)

Table 3. Upload a Document Page Options

| Menu Item | Action |
| --- | --- |
| **Get From Cloud** | Add documents from cloud storage (Google Drive, Box, Dropbox, OneDrive, and so on) |
| **Upload** | Add documents from your device |
| **More Options** | Menu for: Set as Supplement, Replace, Download Document, Rename Document, Delete Document, View Document |
| **Set as Supplement** | Switch documents from primary (active documents in the signing process) to supplementary (passive material) |
| **Replace** | Remove the document and upload a replacement from your device |
| **Download Document** | Download a copy of the document to your device |
| **Rename Document** | Change the document name |
| **Delete Document** | Remove the document from the template |
| **View Document** | Open a preview of the document |

title: Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”
text:
Set account defaults to Allow sender to override account defaults
to fix this. This allows the envelope template to save on the Documents page and be edited
later.

In the Envelope Template Creation process, selecting the Save and Close
button on the Documents page results in an error: "You are not authorized to access
the envelope." This error occurs only on the Documents page. Selecting Save and
Close on the pages for Recipients, Place Fields, Options, and Custom Button do not
result in errors.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
2. Click the New button in the upper-right of the page.
3. Enter a template name and a standard object (Accounts, Opportunities, etc.)
4. Immediately click the Save and Close button on the Documents
   page.

   **Result**: An error message reads: You are not
   authorized to access the envelope. The user is unable to
   proceed with the template creation process.

title: Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”
Resolution: Enable "Allow sender to override account defaults" in the connected Docusign account
text:
1. Log in to the connected Docusign account as an Administrator.
2. Select the Admin tab.
3. Select Reminders and Expiration in the left
   column.
4. Select Allow senders to override account
   defaults.
5. Days before request expires: Enter any number
   higher than zero.

   Note: If the Days before request
   expires: field is blank or has a value of zero, you
   cannot save settings changes.

   ![A screenshot displays the Admin page with the Allow senders to override account defaults option selected and the Expiration field set to 31 days.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/DAL_esign_troubleshooting_admin_reminders_and_expiration.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0ODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.HDKK7h1D4xgwf13_i3ecHeLpv0K0_cXstABAnrRhRac&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Admin page with the Allow senders to override account defaults option selected and the Expiration field set to 31 days.")
6. Select Save Changes.

   A message displays: Changes saved. You have completed the process. You have set the account defaults to Allow
   senders to override account defaults. You can save
   envelope template settings on the Documents page and move to the
   Recipients page.

title: Step 1: Select Documents for Your Envelope
heading_path: Step 1: Select Documents for Your Envelope
text:
Step one of the signature process involves selecting the content that will be loaded into the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce envelope.

Step one of the Negotiate signature experience involves selecting the content that will be loaded into the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce envelope.

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, etc.)
2. Select the Share button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/negotiateactionsarrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0i4cw53iAA6RP_YUhf9SwDwY4-xjiY-oZxaEH3B84fs&_LANG=enus) to the right of any file in the Docusign Files section.
3. Select Send for Signature from the drop-down menu. The Send with Docusign page opens.

   ![Send for eSignature](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Send-for-eSignature.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0i4cw53iAA6RP_YUhf9SwDwY4-xjiY-oZxaEH3B84fs&_LANG=enus "Send for eSignature")
4. Check the box to the left of each document to be included in the envelope. You can add up to ten documents.
5. **(Optional Steps)**: To add or rearrange content:

   - Upload: Select this button and follow the prompts to add more documents from your device
   - Add from Salesforce: Select this button to add documents from Salesforce Files
   - Drag documents to change their order in the envelope![Reorder uploaded files](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/esigdecreorderfiles.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0i4cw53iAA6RP_YUhf9SwDwY4-xjiY-oZxaEH3B84fs&_LANG=enus "Reorder uploaded files")
6. Select the Next button in the upper-right of the page.

   The first step of the process is complete. Documents are added to the envelope. Move to [Step 2](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dfp1596477842847.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Place Fields Actions: Copy, Paste, Undo, Redo, and Zoom
heading_path: Place Fields Actions: Copy, Paste, Undo, Redo, and Zoom
text:
You can use the action menu on the Place Fields page to take
actions on signature fields that are placed on the document.

You can use the action menu on the Place Fields page to take actions on
signature fields that are placed on the document.![A screenshot displays the place fields action menu, with undo, redo, copy, paste, and zoom buttons.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-place-fields-action-menu-with-undo-redo-copy-paste-and-zoom-buttons-1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.HQW8PB7vqbpFkYFw8rooLI1Q38tonPkwmxNMJ7jQqfc&_LANG=enus)

Table 1. Place Field Actions

| Button | Button Name | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Undo | Undo the last action taken on the field |
|  | Redo | Redo the last action taken on the field |
|  | Copy | Copy the selected field |
|  | Paste | Paste the selected field |
|  | Zoom | Zoom in and out on the signature page |

title: I See an “Object Reference Is Not Set to an Instance of an Object” Error When Invoking Salesforce APIs
heading_path: I See an “Object Reference Is Not Set to an Instance of an Object” Error When Invoking Salesforce APIs
text:
I see a web service error: "Object Reference is not set to an instance of an object"
when listing layouts, publishing buttons, or fetching documents from Salesforce when sending
for signature.

I see a web service error: "Object Reference is not set to an instance of an object"
when I invoke a Salesforce API command.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Invoke one of the following Salesforce API commands:
   - List layouts
   - Publish buttons
   - Fetch documents from Salesforce when sending for signature

   **Result**: I see an error message: "Object Reference is
   not set to an instance of an object". I am unable to execute
   my API command.

title: I See an “Object Reference Is Not Set to an Instance of an Object” Error When Invoking Salesforce APIs
heading_path: I See an “Object Reference Is Not Set to an Instance of an Object” Error When Invoking Salesforce APIs
Resolution: Disable the "Lock sessions to the IP address from which they originated" function in Salesforce
text:
1. Access the Session Settings page as a Salesforce
   administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.nMetS5AfI6Z2cDhXvI9vjJ0Wkw7aDEWDN-0ebKbE9Ts&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Session Settings in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Session Settings link in the
      left navigation bar.
2. Uncheck the Lock sessions to the IP address from which they
   originated box under the Session
   Settings header.
3. Select the Save button.
4. Log out of Salesforce and log back in.
5. Retry your API transaction.

   You have completed the process. You have disabled
   theLock sessions to the IP address from which they
   originated function in your Salesforce
   organization.

title: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Glossary of Terms
heading_path: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Glossary of Terms
text:
Learn about key terms used in the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
app.

title: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Glossary of Terms
heading_path: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Glossary of Terms
eSignature for Salesforce terminology
text:
Anchor text A literal character string indicated by the user to
perform an action on or replace with another specified data or data input.

Bulk document creation A configuration with multiple template
documents associated with it that generates all documents at once when used.

Data fields Specific information, such as quantities, characters, or
symbols, stored in electronic or digital format, relating to a record of a specific
type.

Data source The location from which data being used or merged
comes.

Docusign Gen users Users who will have access to a Docusign Gen account as users or administrators.

Docusign fields Elements designed by Docusign that
your recipients can use to sign or enter in specific data when you send a document for
signature. Optionally, Docusign fields can also be used to update data in
Salesforce when the information is changed by a signer. Examples: Signature, Signature date,
Title.

Importing merge fields Selecting merge fields from your existing
templates, such as Docusign, to include in template configurations.

Main data source The Salesforce object from which the document
generation action initiates. For example, if you choose Opportunity to be your main data
source, you’ll be able to pull any data from that object as well as from related objects,
such as Account or Opportunity Line Items.

title: Change Envelope Template Recipient Actions
heading_path: Change Envelope Template Recipient Actions
text:
There are many different recipient actions that can be assigned to recipients in an
envelope template.

Use this procedure to change the action of an envelope template recipient:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMzksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.giVa204DaaFjGGeOJ9biYBvsmhKBRmkse4YCQpN96oY&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu to the far-right of a recipient
   name.
2. Select Edit.

   ![A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplateeditrecipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMzksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.giVa204DaaFjGGeOJ9biYBvsmhKBRmkse4YCQpN96oY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.")
3. Select the Action drop-down menu.
4. Select an action from the list.
5. Select the Add button to save the action. The Add
   Recipient dialog box closes.
6. Select the Save and Close button at the top-right of the
   page.

   You have completed the process. You have added or changed the recipient
   action.

title: Void Active Docusign Envelopes
heading_path: Void Active Docusign Envelopes
text:
Learn to use the void envelope feature to cancel the signature process of active
envelopes.

The Docusign Envelope Status
component of a Salesforce page displays the status of all envelopes sent for signature.
You can use the void envelope feature to cancel active envelopes.
Void envelope is helpful when you discover a problem with a
contract and want to stop the signing process. Recipients who have finished signing
receive an email notification with your reason for voiding. Recipients who still need to
sign cannot view or sign the enclosed documents.

Note: The
original sender can void active envelopes from the Docusign Envelope
Status component. Users with [shared access to the sender](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=ssa1578456277708.html) can void the sender's
envelope using the legacy [Shared Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=xbn1578456279661.html) feature.

Use this procedure to void an envelope from the
Docusign Envelope Status component:

1. Open a Salesforce record and locate the Docusign Envelope
   Status component.
2. Select Void Envelope from the arrow ![A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyOTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.pOTWaqsiL1q_ptHpjTo6OzzjbWTD4A4yZWgpeiXd3FE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon.") drop-down menu of an in-flight envelope.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign Envelope Status component displays a drop-down menu with Send, View, Correct, and Void options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/DocuSign-Envelope-Status-dropdown-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyOTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.pOTWaqsiL1q_ptHpjTo6OzzjbWTD4A4yZWgpeiXd3FE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Envelope Status component displays a drop-down menu with Send, View, Correct, and Void options.")
3. Enter a message to recipients in the Reason for voiding
   envelope field.

   Note: The message has a 200-character limit
   and is a required step.
4. Select Void.
5. Select Back to Record to return to the Salesforce
   record.

You have completed the process. Recipients who have finished
signing receive an email notification with your reason for voiding. Recipients who still
need to sign cannot view or sign the enclosed documents.

title: Step 2: Add Recipients to Your Envelope (Envelope Template)
heading_path: Step 2: Add Recipients to Your Envelope (Envelope Template)
text:
Use this procedure to add recipients to the envelope. You can add recipients from your
Salesforce organization, from Docusign or by entering a name and email address.

You can add recipients from your Salesforce organization, from Docusign or
by entering a name and email address. [Follow these rules if you want to edit envelope template recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=uig1740079031042.html&_LANG=enus).

Use this procedure to add recipients to the envelope:

1. Select the Add Recipient button. The Add Recipient
   dialog box opens.
2. Add a recipient to your envelope:

   1. Select a recipient type from the left column:
      - From Salesforce: Enter the name of a
        Salesforce user or contact
      - By Name & Email: Enter any name and
        email address
      - Signing Group: Add a group of recipients
        from the connected Docusign account
   2. Action: Select a recipient action from the drop-down menu.
   3. (Optional) Select any option:
      - Delivery: [Send an envelope by email or
        SMS](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=psq1595994131623.html&_LANG=enus) (text message)

        Note:
        Allow SMS Delivery must be
        enabled in your account.
      - Add Role: Add a placeholder title (like
        Approver) that allows the sender to specify a name and email
        address later
      - Add Access Authentication
        - SMS/Phone: Send an access code to
          a recipient's mobile device to add an extra layer of
          security to the signing process
        - Access Code: Create a code that a
          recipient must enter to start the signing process
      - Add Private Message: Send a message to
        just this recipient or group
   4. Select the Select button.

   The Add Recipient dialog box closes. The recipient appears in the
   Recipients list.
3. (Optional):

   - Email Subject: Enter up to 100 characters of
     text.
   - Email Message: Enter up to 2,000 characters of
     text.
   - Reminders: Set the number of days between
     recipient reminders from the drop-down menu
   - Expiration: Enter the number of days before the
     envelope expires
4. Select the Next button in the upper-right of the page.

   The Docusign eSignature place fields page opens.

You have completed step two. Move to [Step 3](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xmx1596487118055.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Add Sending Options to an Envelope Template Recipient
heading_path: Add Sending Options to an Envelope Template Recipient
text:
Learn how to add the role, access code, filter, private message, in-person signing, SMS,
or sign now options when creating an envelope template recipient.

You can add custom options to any recipient in the Add Recipient dialog box.

Use this procedure to add recipient options to an envelope template:

Note: You can also use
this procedure on the Recipients step when sending an envelope.

1. Select the name of the recipient you want to update

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
2. Add any option to your recipient:

   - Add Role: Add a placeholder title (like Approver)
     that allows the sender to specify a name and email address later
   - Delivery
     - Email: Send an envelope to a recipient by
       email.
     - Email + SMS: Send an envelope to up to
       four recipients by email and text message. This Delivery menu
       only appears for customers with Allow SMS
       Delivery and Signer can sign on a
       mobile device enabled.

       Important: You must enter a
       space between the country code and the phone
       number.
   - Add Filter: Add recipients based on [a set of conditions being met or not
     met](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=gkj1612219694379.html&_LANG=enus "You can add filters to Related Lists recipients in the Add Recipient dialog box of a Docusign envelope template. This allows you to only add Related List recipients to an envelope if certain conditions are met.")
   - Make the Sender Host: The person who sends the
     envelope becomes the host of the in-person signing session
   - Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now): The sender is asked to
     sign their own envelope immediately after sending it
   - Add Name and Email: Specify the name and email
     address of a recipient
   - Add Access Authentication
     - SMS/Phone: Send an access code to a
       recipient's mobile device to add an extra layer of security to
       the signing process
     - Access Code: Create a code that a
       recipient must enter to start the signing process
   - Add Private Message: Send a message to just this
     recipient or group
3. Select the Add button.

   The Add Recipient dialog box closes.
4. Select the Save and Close button.

You have completed the process. You have added or edited a
recipient action in your envelope template.

title: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
heading_path: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
text:
Learn to upgrade Docusign Apps Launcher to avoid seeing an error when
accessing components.

I can't access any Docusign components on my layouts.

A red OK error displays when I select any component on a record. This
includes any of the following components:

- Docusign Files
- Docusign Envelope Status
- Docusign CLM Files
- Docusign CLM Obligations
- Docusign CLM Tasks

title: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
heading_path: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
Resolution: Upgrade to the Latest Version of Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: Your organization has an older version of Docusign Apps Launcher installed. Upgrade to the latest
version.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1NTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.-Jo0mUVAGd7ApOQg_chfNFSzOmMaxy8UDlUZ4HZba5I&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. Choose an upgrade path:

   - If your version is higher than 6.0, [upgrade to
     the latest Apps Launcher version](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus).
   - If your version is 6.0 or lower, [use this process to upgrade
     to the latest version](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/DocuSign-Apps-Launcher-Is-there-a-recommended-upgrade-path-for-an-older-version-of-the-package).

   You have completed the process. You have upgraded your older Docusign Apps Launcher package. You can open Docusign components on Salesforce records.

title: Open Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Open Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in
your organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce is a package you install from the
Salesforce AppExchange. The package contains the following applications:

- Docusign CLM for Salesforce
- Docusign CLM Essentials for Salesforce
- Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
- Docusign Gen for Salesforce
- Docusign Negotiate for Salesforce
- Docusign eSignature Apex Toolkit for Salesforce

When you install one of these apps in your organization, Docusign
installs all apps. Depending on the apps you purchase, you see a custom Docusign administrator dashboard when you open Apps Launcher.

Docusign Gen customers see administrator controls for
Gen and eSignature.

Docusign CLM customers see administrator controls for
CLM, Gen, and eSignature.

CLM Essentials customers see controls for CLM
Essentials, Gen, and eSignature.

Use this procedure to access Docusign Apps Launcher in your Salesforce
organization:

1. Select the App Launcher grid ![A screenshot displays a square icon made of nine colored dots](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNzksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.sutLOtBy9hNhsmBDh1vC35Hn2-lkmOPWYBEHkBCa06E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a square icon made of nine colored dots") and select View All.
2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.

You have completed the process. You have opened all
default Docusign Apps Launcher tabs and the Docusign
Setup page.

title: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
heading_path: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
text:
Learn to fix issues with sending and signing from an Apple iOS device.

Note: This document refers to sending and signing from an Apple
iOS device, like an iPhone. Apple iPadOS users
can send and sign from Safari by changing these settings:

- Set Prevent Cross-Site Tracking to
  OFF.
- Navigate to Request Desktop Website and set
  All Websites to Enabled.

iPadOS users can also utilize the [Lightning for Mobile Browser](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000381165&type=1) for a
desktop experience.

When I try to send or sign a Docusign envelope from my iOS Apple mobile device, I see one of
these issues:

- I get a blank screen when trying to add Docusign fields to an envelope
- I get a blank screen when trying to sign an envelope
- I get an error page stating "URL has moved here..."

This happens when I am using the Safari mobile browser and the Salesforce mobile app.

I am unable to send or sign an envelope when these errors occur.

title: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
heading_path: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
Resolution 1 (Safari Mobile Browser): Use a different iOS browser
text:
Neither Docusign nor Salesforce support using the Safari mobile
browser. If you are signing in an iOS mobile browser, use Google Chrome
or Mozilla Firefox instead of Safari.

title: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
heading_path: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
Resolution 2 (Salesforce Mobile Application for iOS): Change iOS settings
text:
Docusign and Salesforce administrator

Important: Apple iOS/iPadOS
devices use Safari pages to display Docusign content in
the Salesforce mobile app. So Apple uses Safari in the Salesforce mobile app
regardless of your default browser. You must follow these instructions to
use Docusign in the Salesforce mobile app.

1. Update your device's iOS version.
2. Update the Salesforce mobile app on your iOS device.
3. Navigate to Settings >  Safari and change the following settings:

   1. Disable Prevent Cross-Site Tracking.
   2. Disable Block All Cookies.
4. Navigate to Settings >  Salesforce and enable Allow Cross-Website
   Tracking.

   You have completed the process. You have updated your iOS
   settings. You can use the Salesforce mobile app to send and sign with
   Docusign on your Apple iOS device.

title: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
heading_path: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
text:
Learn to fix the "User is not the Owner of the Lock" error when editing a Gen or
envelope template.

I tried to edit a Docusign Gen or envelope template. An error displayed:
The user is not the owner of the lock. The template is locked by
another user or in another application.

I cannot edit the template or save changes.

title: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
heading_path: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
Resolution: Re-Login to Salesforce or Empty Your Browser Cache
text:
Cause: You closed the browser tab while editing a template
without selecting Save and Close. You opened another
browser tab and tried to edit the template you closed.

1. Select the Salesforce user icon and select Log
   Out from the dropdown menu.
2. Log back in to Salesforce.
3. Try to edit your Gen template again.
4. If the error displays, empty your browser cache and re-login.

   You have completed the process. You have removed the template
   lock. You can edit or save changes to the Gen template or envelope
   template.

title: Envelope Creation Options: Place Fields Step
heading_path: Envelope Creation Options: Place Fields Step
text:
Learn to use options on the Place Fields page to customize your envelope.

As an envelope creator, you have many advanced options available to you. In the Place Fields step, you add Docusign and Salesforce fields to your envelope. Recipients use these fields to add data or signatures to your agreement.

This guide demonstrates the advanced options that you can use in the Place Fields step:

title: Envelope Creation Options: Place Fields Step
heading_path: Envelope Creation Options: Place Fields Step
text:
nd Salesforce fields to your envelope. Recipients use these fields to add data or signatures to your agreement.

You can add Salesforce fields, Notary fields, Seal fields, Smart Sections or build custom fields.")

You can also access a menu of actions for the Place Fields step.")

- [Use Document Custom AutoPlace Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ryo1673648971228.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use document custom auto-place fields for recipient information that does not write back to Salesforce.")

title: Add a Filter for Related List Recipients in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Add a Filter for Related List Recipients in an Envelope Template
text:
You can add filters to Related Lists recipients in the Add Recipient dialog box of a Docusign envelope template. This allows you to only add Related List recipients to an envelope if
certain conditions are met.

You can add filters to related lists recipients in the Add Recipient dialog box of a Docusign envelope template. This allows you to only add related list recipients to an
envelope if certain Salesforce record conditions are met.

There are two types of filter groupings:

- Any: "OR" statements that apply the filter if any one of your
  conditions is met:
  ![A screenshot displays the Related List Any dialog box options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Related-List-Any-modal-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNjEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.dmBlt-eTes6L2FfOq_ESIp4YsmDcM5rPmzPMXcTaLB8&_LANG=enus)
- All: "AND" statements that apply the filter only if all of the
  conditions are met:
  ![A screenshot displays the Related List All dialog box options](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Related-List-All-modal-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNjEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.dmBlt-eTes6L2FfOq_ESIp4YsmDcM5rPmzPMXcTaLB8&_LANG=enus)

Table 1. ADD FILTER ICONS

| Icon | Name | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Copy Row | Copy a row of conditions and paste it below |
|  | Delete Row | Delete a row of conditions |
|  | or | Add a new blank row of "OR" conditions |
|  | and | Add a new blank row of "AND" conditions |

Table 2. ADD FILTER OPTIONS

| Name | Action |
| --- | --- |
| All | A group of conditions that add Related List recipients if all listed conditions are met |
| Any | A group of conditions that add Related List recipients if any listed conditions are met |
| equals | A Salesforce field exactly matches your condition |
| contains | Any part of a Salesforce field contains your condition |
| starts with | A Salesforce field begins with your condition |
| ends with | A Salesforce field ends with your condition |
| does not equal | A Salesforce field is not present in your condition |
| greater than | A Salesforce field (number, date, currency) is greater than your condition |
| less than | A Salesforce field (number, date, currency) is less than your condition |
| less than or equals | A Salesforce field (number, date, currency) is less than or equals your condition |
| greater than or equals | A Salesforce field (number, date, currency) is greater than or equals your condition |
| APPLY | The Any/All drop-down menu header |
| IF | The first condition you add |
| OR IF | Any additional "OR" conditions you add |
| AND IF | Any additional "IF" conditions you add |

title: Create and Send a Standard Send With Docusign Envelope
heading_path: Create and Send a Standard Send With Docusign Envelope
text:
Learn to send an envelope from a Salesforce record using a standard Send with Docusign template.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that holds documents, recipients, and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by completing the fields assigned to them. Docusign saves the document to the Salesforce record when all recipients finish signing.

You can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. You can add recipients and documents manually, from a template, or dynamically from Salesforce records, users, or contacts. Docusign saves the signed document to the Salesforce record when all recipients complete their assigned fields, .

Use this procedure to add documents or templates to a standard Send with Docusign envelope:

1. Select the Send with Docusign button in the upper right of a Salesforce record. Salesforce may hide the button in the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.5gnIsFvmZBaWenpKwcpA2goV9XwjCyBa-6WuCVimWno&_LANG=enus) menu.

   The Documents page opens, displaying the latest ten documents from the record's Notes & Attachments.
2. Complete the actions on the Documents page:

   1. Choose any method to add documents to your envelope:
      - Documents: Select the check box for up to ten documents
      - Upload: Add a document from your device
      - [Add from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qva1617752033779.html&_LANG=enus): Add documents from Salesforce Files
      - [Select Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=fne1682396685506.html&_LANG=enus): Add documents, recipients, and signature fields from Docusign envelope templates
   2. Select Next. ![A screenshot displays the Documents page with a document selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Add-documents.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.5gnIsFvmZBaWenpKwcpA2goV9XwjCyBa-6WuCVimWno&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Documents page with a document selected.")

   The Recipients page opens.
3. Complete the actions on the Recipients page:

   1. Select Add Recipient.
   2. Select a recipient type from the left column:
      - From Salesforce: Enter the name of a Salesforce user or contact
      - By Name & Email: Enter any name and email address
      - Signing Group: Enter the name of a signing group from your Docusign account
   3. Action: Select a recipient action:
      - Needs to Sign: Recipient must sign the document
      - Receives a Copy: Recipient receives a copy of the document
      - Needs to View: Recipient must view the document
      - Specify Recipients: Recipient manages the signing process for other recipients
      - Allow to Edit: Recipient can make changes to the document
      - In Person Signer: Recipient is the host of a multi-recipient signing session where all signers are in the same room
   4. (Optional) Add recipient options: [Recipient Sending Options: Access Code, In Person Signing, Sign Now, SMS, and More](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xch1620151662151.html&_LANG=enus).
   5. Select Select.
   6. Envelope Custom Fields: Add values for all required fields. Required fields are marked with asterisks \*.

      Note: You only see Envelope Custom Fields if your administrator has created fields in the connected Docusign account.
   7. Select Next.

   The Prepare & Send page opens.
4. Complete the actions on the Prepare & Send page:

title: Create and Send a Standard Send With Docusign Envelope
heading_path: Create and Send a Standard Send With Docusign Envelope
text:
lope Custom Fields if your administrator has created fields in the connected Docusign account.
   7. Select Next.

   The Prepare & Send page opens.
4. Complete the actions on the Prepare & Send page:

   1. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatestandardsfields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.5gnIsFvmZBaWenpKwcpA2goV9XwjCyBa-6WuCVimWno&_LANG=enus) Standard Fields: Drag Docusign fields to the document.
   2. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatemergefields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.5gnIsFvmZBaWenpKwcpA2goV9XwjCyBa-6WuCVimWno&_LANG=enus) Merge Fields: Drag Salesforce fields to the document.
   3. (Optional) Select another recipient from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.5gnIsFvmZBaWenpKwcpA2goV9XwjCyBa-6WuCVimWno&_LANG=enus) menu in the upper left and drag Docusign fields to the document.

      Note: Each recipient's fields are a different color (yellow, blue, and so on.)

      ![A screen capture displays switching to a new recipient and dragging fields to the document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Switch-recipients.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.5gnIsFvmZBaWenpKwcpA2goV9XwjCyBa-6WuCVimWno&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays switching to a new recipient and dragging fields to the document.")
5. Select SEND.

You have completed the process. You built and sent an envelope with a standard Send with Docusign template.

title: Configure Standard eSignature Fields
heading_path: Configure Standard eSignature Fields
text:
You can customize Docusign eSignature fields that you add in the template creation
process.

You can customize Docusign eSignature fields that you add in the template creation process.
Options include:

- Required
- Read Only
- Add additional signers
- Add tooltips
- Customize data labels
- Add validation types
- Add character limits

Here's how to access the eSignature fields page in an existing Envelope Template:

1. Access the Envelope Template Place Fields tab:
   1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
   2. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu to the far-right of a
      template name.
   3. Select Edit from the menu. The Docusign Envelope
      Template creation page opens.
   4. Select the Place Fields tab.![Place Fields tab](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Place-Fields-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus "Place Fields tab")
2. Use the actions in the menu.
3. Select the Save button when changes are complete.

   **Result**:
   The process is complete. You have added or edited fields in your Envelope
   Template.

Here are the standard Docusign signature fields and how to configure them for your
Envelope Template:

ADD RECIPIENT VIEWS

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/genacesignermenu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus)

Use this drop-down menu under the Docusign Fields header to add additional recipient
views to the template. Each recipient can have a customized version of the same
document, with different Docusign fields and placements specifically crafted for
them.

1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus) and select **Edit Recipients** from the drop-down
   menu.
2. Select the Add Another button to add additional recipient
   views.

title: Configure Standard eSignature Fields
heading_path: Configure Standard eSignature Fields
text:
E1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus) and select **Edit Recipients** from the drop-down
   menu.
2. Select the Add Another button to add additional recipient
   views.

   Note: You may add more than one additional recipient.
3. Enter a name for the signer in the **Name of Placeholder (Required\*)** text
   field.
4. **(Optional)**: Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/genacex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus) button to the right of a signer to remove that
   recipient.
5. Select the Done button when the list is complete.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/genaceaddrecipients.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus)
6. Add Docusign Fields to the Signer 1 view.
7. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus) and select the next signer from the
   drop-down menu.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/genaceswitchsigner.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.fIKi_bY8zIjL9kOH87onAjfIPUlIOyIGA2ub2E180RY&_LANG=enus)
8. Add Docusign Fields to the Signer 2 view.
9. Repeat this process for each recipient view.
10. Select the Next button in the upper-right of the
    page.
11. Select the Publish button in the upper-right of the page.

    A banner notifies: Template (your template name)
    updated.

**Result:** The process is complete. Each recipient gets an envelope specifically
customized for them.

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
text:
Learn to fix the issue that prevents linking an envelope template and Gen
template.

I built a Gen template and tried to link it to an envelope template. On the Create
Button page, I searched in the Envelope
Templates field. The envelope template that I wanted to link did not
display in the results.

I built the Gen template and envelope templates on the Opportunity object, so I should be able
to link them.

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
Resolution 1: Switch to a New Source Object and Switch Back
text:
Cause: Docusign is not reading the Gen
template data source correctly.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open the Gen template Select
   Source page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ktj1621660808301.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a different object from the Salesforce
   Object dropdown menu and select
   Next.
3. Select Back.
4. Select the original object from the Salesforce
   Object dropdown menu and select Save and
   Close.
5. [Open the Gen template Create
   Button page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=way1621663319299.html&_LANG=enus).
6. Envelope Templates: Search for and select your
   envelope template.
7. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have reset the source object
   in your Gen template. You can link the envelope template in the
   Create Button step.

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
Resolution 2: Use the Developer Console to Fix the Error
text:
Cause: Docusign is not reading the Gen
template data source correctly.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open the Salesforce Developer
   Console](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.code_dev_console.htm&type=5).
2. Update the dfsle\_SourceObjectc on an affected
   dfsleGenTemplate\_c to the correct Application
   Programming Interface (API) value.

   You have completed the process. You used the Developer Console
   to reset the source object in your Gen template. You can link the
   envelope template in the Create Button
   step.

title: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
heading_path: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
text:
If administrator credentials from a Docusign Demo account are used to connect Docusign and Salesforce, you get the error: There were some problems with your document
generation when reaching the Preview Documents step of the template creation
process.

Identified an intermittent issue with creating an envelope template when a Docusign Demo
account is connected to Salesforce. If administrator credentials from a Docusign Demo account are used to connect Docusign and Salesforce, you get the error:
There were some problems with your document generation
when reaching the Preview Documents step of the template creation process. This
results in the following issues:

- Recipients' Docusign Roles are replaced with Salesforce Roles
- Docusign signature fields for those recipients are deleted from the Envelope
  Template

**Salesforce Roles Delete Docusign Role and Signature Fields from Envelope
Template**

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Add a Docusign recipient to an Envelope Template.
2. Add a Salesforce recipient from a Related List (like
   OpportunityContactRole).
3. Save the Envelope Template.
4. Start the envelope creation process with eSignature for Salesforce.
5. Advance to the Recipients step. Recipients have their Docusign Roles replaced with
   Salesforce Roles.

   Note: This only applies to recipients with Salesforce Roles
   (Decision Maker, Evaluator, etc.).
6. Advance to the Prepare and Send step. Docusign recipients' signature
   fields are missing.

   **Result**: Both the recipient's Docusign Role and
   eSignature tags are missing from the template.

title: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
heading_path: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
**Resolution 1: Remove the Role field from the Salesforce Contact**
text:
1. Visit the Salesforce record page containing the Contact Role.
2. Find the Contact Roles section.
3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0ODgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.bczH8uENBaAZi5pzhZY_lKtYiv5I3Wg_d0bsb-Thx-w&_LANG=enus) to the left of the Contact to be
   edited.
4. Select Edit from the drop-down menu. The Edit
   Contact Role dialog box opens.

   ![Edit Salesforce Role](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Edit-Salesforce-Role.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0ODgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.bczH8uENBaAZi5pzhZY_lKtYiv5I3Wg_d0bsb-Thx-w&_LANG=enus "Edit Salesforce Role")
5. Select the Role drop-down menu.
6. Select --None-- from the list.
7. Select the Save button.

   ![Clear Salesforce Role](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Clear-Salesforce-Role.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0ODgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.bczH8uENBaAZi5pzhZY_lKtYiv5I3Wg_d0bsb-Thx-w&_LANG=enus "Clear Salesforce Role")You are returned to the Contact Roles section. The
   Role field is now empty.

title: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
heading_path: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
**Resolution 2: Disconnect Docusign from Salesforce, clear cache and reconnect**
text:
1. Select the Docusign Setup tab.
2. Select the Disconnect link on the Docusign Setup tab in the upper-right
   of the page.
3. Log out of Salesforce.
4. Clear your browser cache.
5. Log back in to Salesforce.
6. Open Docusign Apps Launcher as an administrator. The Connect Docusign to Salesforce page opens.
7. Follow the instructions to [Connect Salesforce and
   Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=zwk1586134361708.html&bundleId=ygy1586134394050&_LANG=enus).

   Note: Connect using the same Docusign Demo administrator
   credentials.

   The process is complete. The recipient's Salesforce Role no
   longer conflicts. Docusign Roles and signature fields remain as expected
   when using an Envelope Template to send for signature.

title: Automatic AutoPlace Field Syntax
heading_path: Automatic AutoPlace Field Syntax
text:
Here are the automatic AutoPlace fields you can assign to Salesforce roles.

You can add automatic AutoPlace fields to envelope template documents.

These fields merge Docusign fields into your envelope.

You do not need to create, configure, or modify any Docusign fields to use
automatic AutoPlace fields.

Here are the automatic AutoPlace fields you can use:

| Field Type | Signer 1 | Signer 2 | Signer 3 | Signer 4 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Signature | \s1\ | \s2\ | \s3\ | \s4\ |
| Initial | \i1\ | \i2\ | \i3\ | \i4\ |
| Optional Initial | \oi1\ | \oi2\ | \oi3\ | \oi4\ |
| Name | \n1\ | \n2\ | \n3\ | \n4\ |
| Company | \co1\ | \co2\ | \co3\ | \co4\ |
| Title | \t1\ | \t2\ | \t3\ | \t4\ |
| Date Signed | \d1\ | \d2\ | \d3\ | \d4\ |

Here are some sample signer and AutoPlace field combinations:

- **Signer 2 signature field**: Enter **\s2\** in the document.
- **Signer 3 company field**: Enter **\co3\** in the document.
- **Signer 1 date signed field**: Enter **\d1\** in the document.

title: Step 3: Add Fields and Send the Envelope (Envelope Template Button)
heading_path: Step 3: Add Fields and Send the Envelope (Envelope Template Button)
text:
One or more fields are already present on the documents. These have been added to the
envelope template by your administrator. Use this procedure to view the fields or add
more.

Note: One or more fields may be present on
the documents. These have been added to the envelope template by your administrator.
Use this procedure to view the fields or add more.

1. (Optional) If your Salesforce organization has any Docusign envelope templates,
   the Select Matching Templates dialog box opens. Choose an
   option:

   - Add a template:
     1. Select the check box of each template you want to add to your
        envelope.
     2. Select the Apply button.
   - Cancel: Select the Cancel
     button if you do not want to add envelope template documents![Optional: Select Matching Templates](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Optional--Use-Envelope-Template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xUsqd-Lt_kUzm8px2xoVKluZ8et2ZaTS-HZuNQKo9EY&_LANG=enus "Optional: Select Matching Templates")

   The Select Matching Templates dialog box
   closes.
2. Add Docusign fields to the document:

   1. Select a Docusign field from the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatestandardsfields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xUsqd-Lt_kUzm8px2xoVKluZ8et2ZaTS-HZuNQKo9EY&_LANG=enus)
      Standard Fields menu in the left column.
   2. Drag the field to the document.![Add Docusign fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Add-DocuSign-fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xUsqd-Lt_kUzm8px2xoVKluZ8et2ZaTS-HZuNQKo9EY&_LANG=enus "Add Docusign fields")
3. Add Salesforce fields to the document:

   1. Select the Merge Fields button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatemergefields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xUsqd-Lt_kUzm8px2xoVKluZ8et2ZaTS-HZuNQKo9EY&_LANG=enus) from the left
      column. The Salesforce Merge Fields menu opens.
   2. Select a field from the menu. You can expand arrows to find more fields,
      or enter text in the Search Fields bar.
   3. Drag the field to the document text.![Add Salesforce fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Add-Salesforce-fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xUsqd-Lt_kUzm8px2xoVKluZ8et2ZaTS-HZuNQKo9EY&_LANG=enus "Add Salesforce fields")
4. (Optional) Choose any option:

   - Preview: Select this button to see a preview of
     the document.
   - Add fields for more recipients:
     1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xUsqd-Lt_kUzm8px2xoVKluZ8et2ZaTS-HZuNQKo9EY&_LANG=enus)
     2. Select another recipient from the drop-down menu.
     3. Drag Docusign fields for this recipient to the document
        text.

title: Step 3: Add Fields and Send the Envelope (Envelope Template Button)
heading_path: Step 3: Add Fields and Send the Envelope (Envelope Template Button)
text:
NzkyMSJ9.xUsqd-Lt_kUzm8px2xoVKluZ8et2ZaTS-HZuNQKo9EY&_LANG=enus)
     2. Select another recipient from the drop-down menu.
     3. Drag Docusign fields for this recipient to the document
        text.

        Note: Each recipient's fields are represented by a separate color
        (yellow, blue, and so on.)![Switch recipients](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Switch-recipients.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xUsqd-Lt_kUzm8px2xoVKluZ8et2ZaTS-HZuNQKo9EY&_LANG=enus "Switch recipients")
5. Select SEND.

You have completed the process. You have created an envelope and
sent it to the specified recipients.

title: Step 2: View or Add Recipients (Envelope Template Button)
heading_path: Step 2: View or Add Recipients (Envelope Template Button)
text:
One or more recipients are already present in the envelope. These have been added to the envelope template by your administrator. Use this procedure to view the recipients or add more.

You see one or more recipients if your administrator added them to the envelope template. [Follow these rules if you want to edit envelope template recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=uig1740079031042.html&_LANG=enus).

Use this procedure to add recipients or move to the next step:

1. Select the Add Recipient button.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens, displaying the recipients that have been added by your administrator.
2. Add a recipient to your envelope:

   1. Select a recipient type from the left column:
      - From Salesforce: Enter the name or email of a Salesforce user or contact
      - By Name & Email: Enter the name and email of a recipient
      - Signing Group: Add a group of recipients from the connected Docusign account
   2. Action: Select a recipient action from the drop-down menu:
      - Needs to Sign: Must sign the document
      - Receives a Copy: Does not need to view or sign
      - Needs to View: Must view the document
      - Specify Recipients: Can add names and emails for placeholder recipients (like Approver.)
      - Allow to Edit: Can make changes to the document
   3. (Optional) Add recipient options: [Recipient Sending Options: Access Code, In-Person Signing, Sign Now, SMS and More](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xch1620151662151.html&_LANG=enus).
   4. Select the Select button.
3. Envelope Custom Fields: Add values for all required fields. Required fields are marked with asterisks \*.

   Note: You only see Envelope Custom Fields if your administrator has created fields in the connected Docusign account. ![A screenshot displays four fields on the Envelope Custom Fields section of the Recipients page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Envelope-custom-fields-on-the-Recipients-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.GmKmwXO5PoXLqcHrq_kGS2asIiPFovXj-oftI8-d5jk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays four fields on the Envelope Custom Fields section of the Recipients page.")
4. (Optional):
