   - View: Select this button to see a visual diagram of the signing order
   - Email Subject: Enter up to 100 characters of text.
   - Email Message: Enter up to 2,000 characters of text.
   - More Options: Choose any options:
     - Reminders: Set the number of days between recipient reminders. Select Custom to add a reminder of up to 999 days.
     - Expiration: Set a date or a number of days (up to 999) after which the envelope expires.
5. Select Next in the upper right.

   The Docusign eSignature fields page opens.

You have completed the second step of the process. Move to [Step 3: Add Fields and Send the Envelope (Envelope Template Button)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=hke1596562836794.html&_LANG=enus "One or more fields are already present on the documents. These have been added to the envelope template by your administrator. Use this procedure to view the fields or add more.").

title: Docusign -Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Docusign-Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
As a Gen for Billing administrator, you see an Unable to uninstall
package error when uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher.
You must first remove extra relationships, permission sets, and Apex class components from
your organization.

As a Gen for Billing administrator, you see an Unable to uninstall
package error when uninstalling Docusign Apps
Launcher. You must first remove extra relationships, permission sets, and Apex class
components from your organization.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. You remove the standard Docusign-Salesforce dependencies (Docusign components, permission set assignments, and so
   on.)
2. You navigate to Setup > Installed
   Packages in your Salesforce organization.
3. You select the Uninstall link next to the Docusign
   Apps Launcher package of apps.
4. You follow the Salesforce prompts to finish the uninstallation process.

**Result**: You see an Unable to uninstall package error
message and an extra list of Gen for Billing dependencies to be removed.

title: Docusign -Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Docusign-Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher
Resolution: Remove all Gen for Billing dependencies before uninstalling the package
text:
1. Access the Installed Packages page as a
   Salesforce administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1MDksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.soPXzAEN3nbEXl92QKSXk6Fw3rdpJARGs9exlZnfWqI&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Installed Packages in the Quick
      Find field.
   4. Select the Installed Packages link in the
      left column.
2. Select the Uninstall link next to the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps.
3. Follow the Salesforce prompts to finish the uninstallation process. The
   Unable to uninstall package error message
   appears, with a list of Docusign-Salesforce dependencies to be removed.
4. Remove all standard Docusign-Salesforce dependencies
   (Docusign components, permission set assignments,
   and so on.)
5. Remove the additional Gen for Billing
   dependencies:

   - InvoiceTriggerGenBilling: Delete this
     trigger from the Apex Trigger Detail page.
   - Docusign Log: Delete this custom object
     from the Fields & Relationships page
   - Docusign Billing Admin: Remove all users
     from this permission set
6. Follow the Salesforce prompts to finish the Docusign
   Apps Launcher uninstallation process.

   You have completed the process. You have removed all Docusign-Salesforce dependencies and uninstalled the
   Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
text: Learn how to use envelope custom fields in Salesforce envelopes.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
What is a Docusign envelope?
text:
A Docusign envelope is a digital container that you build that holds
documents, recipients, and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by
completing the fields assigned to them. Docusign marks the
envelope as Signed when all recipients complete their
fields.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. Users can add
recipients and documents manually, from a template, or dynamically from Salesforce
records, users, or contacts. Docusign saves the document to the
Salesforce record when all recipients finish signing.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
What is an envelope custom field?
text:
- Envelope custom fields (ECFs) let you tag and identify the envelopes you send for signature.
  Administrators create these fields, and senders enter values when preparing an
  envelope.
- Values are not visible to envelope recipients when signing. Only the sender, users with
  shared access, and account administrators see envelope custom fields and values.
- You can build envelope custom fields such as text fields or lists of values. You can mark
  ECFs as required or optional.
- Only the sender, users with shared
  access, and account administrators see
  envelope custom fields and values in the envelope details. Recipients do not see
  envelope custom fields and values.
- When senders prepare an envelope for signature, they must enter or select field values for
  all required ECFs. Senders can only advance to the next step if they complete
  all the required fields.
- You can enter up to 100 characters in an ECF text field. The values are visible to the
  sender and any account member with shared access.
- Users can enter ECF values from the Manage page to search for envelopes. Users can also
  generate an envelope report that includes ECF values.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do I create envelope custom fields?
text:
Log in to your Docusign account and go to Settings > Envelope Custom Fields to open the ECF page.

![A screenshot of the Docusign eSignature Settings page displays four envelope custom fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Envelope-custom-fields-in-DocuSign-Settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.t2GH9W7CMAZNsu8T63tJeTOzv_uuUSC11AGSi8yc5hk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign eSignature Settings page displays four envelope custom fields.")

Use this procedure to create envelope custom fields:

1. Select ADD FIELD.
2. Add a Field Name and configure the field.
3. Select ADD.

Important: Select Show field to envelope
creators, or the field does not display to senders.

![A screenshot of the Add a New Custom Fields dialog box displays the Field Name, Field Type, and List of values fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Show-field-to-envelope-creators-check-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.t2GH9W7CMAZNsu8T63tJeTOzv_uuUSC11AGSi8yc5hk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a New Custom Fields dialog box displays the Field Name, Field Type, and List of values fields.")

See the [Custom Fields for
Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mvu1583277383029.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information about adding and managing ECFs.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do I set default values for ECFs?
text:
You can customize envelope custom fields in your Docusign account. One
customization you can make is assigning default values to envelope custom fields.
Default values are data that you pre-populate for senders.

Set default field values and log into your Docusign account as an
administrator. Go to Settings > Templates >  (template name) >  Edit and add values for envelope custom fields.

Adding default values makes the envelope creation process more straightforward and
less error-prone for your users.

**Example**: Most of Fontara's customers operate in the United States. Fontara
administrators set the default value of the Country field to
United States and Currency to
Dollar. When a sender creates an envelope,
United States displays in the Country field, and
Dollar displays in the Currency field. Senders can change
the Country and Currency values if needed.

In this image, the Country and Currency
fields have default values. The Account Name field has no
default value.

![A screenshot of the Add envelope custom fields page displays the Account Name, Country, and Currency fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Default-values-for-envelope-custom-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.t2GH9W7CMAZNsu8T63tJeTOzv_uuUSC11AGSi8yc5hk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add envelope custom fields page displays the Account Name, Country, and Currency fields.")

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do envelope custom fields work in Salesforce?
text:
You cannot create new envelope custom fields in Salesforce. You can only create ECFs in your
Docusign account. When you have created envelope custom
fields, Salesforce senders see ECFs on the Recipients page.

![A screenshot of the envelope Recipients page displays the Envelope Custom Fields section with four envelope custom fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Envelope-custom-fields-on-the-Recipients-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.t2GH9W7CMAZNsu8T63tJeTOzv_uuUSC11AGSi8yc5hk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the envelope Recipients page displays the Envelope Custom Fields section with four envelope custom fields.")

The following rules apply to envelope custom fields in Salesforce:

- Only envelopes sent from standard Send with Docusign
  buttons display envelope custom fields. Envelopes created from custom envelope
  template buttons do not display ECFs.
- Only envelope custom fields with Show field to envelope creators
  enabled display on the Recipients page.
- The Recipients page displays all envelope custom fields
  with Show field to envelope creators enabled.
- All "shown" ECFs from your Docusign account display on the envelope
  Recipients page.
- Senders must enter values for all required fields to advance to the Prepare &
  Send page.
- Senders can add envelope templates to their envelopes on the Documents
  page. Adding templates imports template documents, recipients, and signature
  fields to the envelope.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do default values work in Salesforce envelopes?
text:
When senders select multiple envelope templates, default values only display for the
last template in the list. So if a sender selects three templates, only the third
template's fields display default values. Fields from the first two templates are
blank.

In this image, default values from the Tally NDA template will display
for the sender. Envelope custom fields from the first two templates will be blank:
![A screenshot of the Select Docusign Template dialog box displays three envelope templates, each with a selected check box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Select-Docusign-Template-dialog-box-displays-three-envelope-templates-each-with-a-selected-check-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.t2GH9W7CMAZNsu8T63tJeTOzv_uuUSC11AGSi8yc5hk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select Docusign Template dialog box displays three envelope templates, each with a selected check box.")

Senders not adding templates see all ECFs from the Docusign account
displayed with blank values.

**Example**: Your Docusign account has 11 envelope custom fields with
Show field to envelope creators enabled. You select a
standard Send with Docusign button on a Salesforce record.
You do not add envelope templates on the Documents page. You
advance to the Recipients page and see all 11 envelope custom
fields from your Docusign account. All 11 fields are blank. You
can enter values for any of the 11 fields.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do I create envelopes with envelope custom fields?
text:
There are two ways to create Salesforce envelopes with envelope custom fields:

- Add envelope templates:
  1. Select a Send with Docusign button on a
     Salesforce record.
  2. Select the Select Template button on the
     Documents page.
  3. Select each template that you want to add from the Select Docusign
     Template dialog box.
  4. Advance to the Recipients page.
     - All ECFs marked with Show field to envelope
       creators display
     - Fields from the *last* of the selected templates display
       default values
     - Fields from all other templates are blank.
  5. Enter values for all the required fields.
- Do not add envelope templates:
  1. Select a Send with Docusign button on a
     Salesforce record.
  2. Add documents on the Documents page.
  3. Advance to the Recipients page.

     All ECFs marked
     with Show field to envelope creators display.
     All values are blank.
  4. Enter values for all the required fields.

title: Upload a Document to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Upload a Document to an Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to upload documents from your device to an envelope template.

You can add up to ten documents to an envelope template.

Use this procedure to upload documents to an envelope template from your device:

1. Select the Upload button.
2. Follow the prompts to add a document from your device. You can add up to ten
   documents.

   Note: You can only upload one document at a time.
3. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have deleted the uploaded
   document.

title: Send an Envelope From a Mobile Browser
heading_path: Send an Envelope From a Mobile Browser
text: Learn how to send a Docusign envelope from a browser on your mobile device.

title: Send an Envelope From a Mobile Browser
heading_path: Send an Envelope From a Mobile Browser
Before you begin
text:
You can send a Docusign envelope from a Salesforce record using the browser on your mobile
device.

Here are the mobile browsers you can use to send a Docusign envelope:

- Google Chrome
- Safari

Note: If you see an error message, your envelope will not
send. Tell your Salesforce administrator if you see either of these error
messages:

- At least one document is required
- Failed to send documents. No recipient found. Contact your
  administrator for help.

Log in to Salesforce on your mobile browser and follow these steps to send a Docusign envelope from a Salesforce record:

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity and so on.)
2. Select the Docusign eSignature custom button.
3. One of two actions will follow, depending on the type of template used:

   - Send now: The envelope sends without input from
     you.

     A message opens: Sent for Signature. The
     screen refreshes to the Salesforce record.
   - Quick send: [You are presented with the quick send
     sending experience.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ygy1586134394050&topicId=wji1611295987221.html&_LANG=enus)

You have completed the process. You have sent a Docusign envelope
from a Salesforce record using a browser on your mobile device. The signed document is
added to the Salesforce record when all recipients complete the signature
process.

title: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
heading_path: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
text:
Learn how to enable the SMS + Email feature and use it to send envelopes by text
message.

You can send envelopes to recipients by text message using the Email +
SMS option.

SMS delivery is a paid feature that is activated when your Docusign account manager
enables these settings:

- Allow SMS Delivery
- Signer can sign on a mobile device

Only your Docusign account manager can activate these settings for you.

Once activated, a Delivery drop-down menu appears in the Add Recipients
step of an envelope or envelope template:![Email + SMS Delivery option](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Email--SMS-Delivery-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczODAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.X1y8FdOsMyTTcLkzdMt7b7t0v0e8IhGaw4R83p4yugo&_LANG=enus "Email + SMS Delivery option")

title: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
heading_path: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
SMS guidelines:
text:
- You can use Email + SMS for up to four (4) recipients per envelope. If you attempt to add
  more than four SMS recipients, an error message opens: You're limited to 4 SMS
  recipients.
- You must enter a space between the country code and the phone number.
- You do not need to enter a phone number for dynamic recipients, like contacts, that already
  have phone numbers saved.
- You can not send an SMS message without also sending the recipient an email.
- Email + SMS can only be used for these recipient types:
  - Needs to Sign
  - Needs to View
  - Receives a Copy
- You can only choose the Access Code option in the Add
  Access Authentication field when Email + SMS is selected.
- You cannot use Email + SMS with Docusign signing groups.

title: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
heading_path: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
SMS number formatting:
text:
You can use the following phone number formats:

- 9251111111 (country code defaults to 1)
- +1 9251111111
- +1 925-111-1111
- +1 (925)-111-1111

Note: If you enter a number format that is not
allowed, an error message displays. You must update the number format to save
the recipient to the envelope or template.

title: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
heading_path: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
SMS country code formatting:
text:
- You must enter a space between the country code and phone number.
- Customer phone numbers are stored in Salesforce in two format types:
  - **United States and Canada**: (+1 PhoneNumber) format
  - **All other regions**: (+CountryCode PhoneNumber) format
- If you enter a phone number with the wrong country code format, 1 is
  automatically added as the country code.
- The (+CountryCode PhoneNumber) format is required when adding SMS + Email to a By
  Role recipient (not a Users/Contacts recipient).

title: Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device
heading_path: Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device
text:
Learn how to activate a new device for use with your Docusign account.

You must enter a verification code to access your Docusign account on a new device. You must verify your device again if
you empty your browser cache.

Use this procedure to activate your Docusign account on a new device:

1. Enter Docusign credentials when prompted.

   The Login from a new device dialog box
   displays.
2. Select Email. Docusign sends a verification code to your
   email address.
3. Open the Verify a New Device email from Docusign. Check
   your junk mail folder if you do not see the message in your Inbox.
4. Copy the verification code from the email.
5. Enter the verification code in the Login from a new device
   dialog box.
6. Select Confirm.

You have completed the process. You can access your Docusign account on this device.

title: Envelope Creation Options: Recipients Step
heading_path: Envelope Creation Options: Recipients Step
text:
This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the Recipients step of the
envelope creation process.

As an envelope creator, you have many advanced options available to you. Each step of
the envelope creation process contains its own set of advanced options. In the
Recipients step, you add signers to your envelope. You can
add signers dynamically, like adding the owner of any record, or manually.

This guide demonstrates the advanced options that you can use in the
Recipients step:

title: Envelope Creation Options: Recipients Step
heading_path: Envelope Creation Options: Recipients Step
text:
add signers to your envelope. You can
add signers dynamically, like adding the owner of any record, or manually.

This guide demonstrates the advanced options that you can use in the
Recipients step:

- [Recipient Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=aow1596147146062.html&_LANG=enus "You can add many different recipient types to an envelope template. You can add recipients from Salesforce, from Docusign, or manually enter them.")
- [Recipient Actions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=byi1596432658895.html&_LANG=enus "There are many different recipient actions that can be assigned to recipients in an envelope template.")
- [Recipient Sending Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=dli1612207739511.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add the role, access code, filter, private message, in-person signing, SMS, or sign now options when creating an envelope template recipient.")
- [Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=jlx1682455699540.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use envelope custom fields in Salesforce envelopes.")
- [Edit the Details of a Recipient](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=rto1596147300694.html&_LANG=enus "You can edit the details of recipients in an existing envelope template at any time.")
- [Add a Filter for Related List
  Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=gkj1612219694379.html&_LANG=enus "You can add filters to Related Lists recipients in the Add Recipient dialog box of a Docusign envelope template. This allows you to only add Related List recipients to an envelope if certain conditions are met.")
- [Change the Recipient Signing
  Order](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=uvf1596147174549.html&_LANG=enus "You can change the signing order of recipients in an envelope template. This lets you change the order in which the envelope must be signed for the signature process to complete.")
- [View a Diagram of the Recipient Signing
  Order](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=klk1596147268691.html&_LANG=enus "You can select the View button to see a diagram of the recipient signing order, from start to completion. This option gives administrators a quick snapshot of the signing process.")
- [Customize the Email Subject and Message Seen
  by Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ubf1611183413019.html&_LANG=enus "You can customize the email subject and message to recipients by editing the envelope template Recipients step.")
- [Delete a Recipient From an
  Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=lex1596147333759.html&_LANG=enus "Administrators needing to delete recipients from an existing envelope template can do so at any time.")
- [Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=jrj1624483779897.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable the SMS + Email feature and use it to send envelopes by text message.")
- [Use Merge Fields to Add Recipients to an
  Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use merge field syntax to add By Role recipients to an envelope template.")
- [Use the Sender Option to Add the Sender of the Envelope as a Recipient](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=lac1629265045695.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the Sender option to add the sender of the envelope as a recipient.")
- [Add In Person Signing to Envelope Template
  Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=tmo1629344318451.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the In Person Signing feature to create a multi-recipient signing session, with all signers in the same room.")
- [Use Sign Now to Let a Sender Sign Their Own
  Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=sjz1629349704101.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Sign Now option to let the sender to sign their own envelope immediately after sending it.")

title: View a Diagram of the Recipient Signing Order
heading_path: View a Diagram of the Recipient Signing Order
text:
You can select the View button to see a diagram of the recipient signing order, from
start to completion. This option gives administrators a quick snapshot of the signing
process.

You can select the View button to see a
diagram of the recipient signing order, from start to completion. This option gives
administrators a quick snapshot of the signing process.

Use this procedure to
view the Signing Order Diagram of an existing envelope template:

1. Select the View button.

   The Signing Order Diagram dialog box opens.
2. Select the X button in the upper-right of the dialog box to
   close the diagram.

   ![Signing Order Diagram](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplaterecipientsigningorder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.Lc9u4XsaAo_MDlX3B9cPrfOUp4n_9Y1LcwMrM-M1Q38&_LANG=enus "Signing Order Diagram")
3. Select the Save and Close button to exit the
   template.

   You have completed the process. You have viewed the recipient signing
   order.

title: Use the Sender Option to Add the Sender of the Envelope as a Recipient
heading_path: Use the Sender Option to Add the Sender of the Envelope as a Recipient
text:
Learn to use the Sender option to add the sender of the envelope as a
recipient.

You can add the Sender recipient type to an envelope or envelope
template. Using the Sender option adds the sender of the envelope to the recipient
list. The sender is the person who selects the envelope template custom button on a
Salesforce record.

The following specifications apply to the Sender recipient type:

- You can place the sender anywhere in the recipient order (Signer 1, Signer 2, and so
  on.)
- Docusign turns off the In Person Signer option for Sender
  recipients.
- Docusign turns off the Delivery option (SMS or
  Email + SMS) for Sender recipients.
- You can only use the Sign Now option when a Sender recipient is first
  in the recipient order.

Use this procedure to add the sender of the envelope as a recipient in an envelope
template:

1. Select the name of the recipient you want to update.
2. Complete the following tasks in the Add Recipient dialog
   box:

   1. Select Sender in the left column.
   2. Action: Select a recipient action.
   3. (Optional) Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now): Select this
      check box if you want the sender to sign the envelope immediately after
      sending.
   4. Select + Add Role and enter a recipient role
      (Signer 1, Signer 2, and so on.)
   5. Select the Add button.

   1. Select Sender in the left column.
   2. Action: Select a recipient action.
   3. (Optional) Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now): Select this check box if you want the sender to sign the envelope immediately after sending.

      Note: This
      option only displays when you select Needs to
      Sign as the action.
   4. Select + Add Role and enter a recipient role (Signer 1, Signer 2, and so on.)
   5. Select the Add button.
3. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You added the sender as a
recipient. When a sender creates an envelope using this template, Docusign adds them as
a recipient.

title: Preview a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Preview a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
text:
You can preview uploaded documents in the envelope template by following these
steps.

You can preview uploaded documents in the envelope template by following these steps:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0RSqF1t4ayrTAjpNOJHLdPpgRHPogWgJzEyXOV4-u5g&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu to the right of a document.
2. Select Preview from the options.

   ![Preview uploaded document](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatedocumentoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0RSqF1t4ayrTAjpNOJHLdPpgRHPogWgJzEyXOV4-u5g&_LANG=enus "Preview uploaded document")

   A new tab opens with the document preview page.
3. Select the document pane to open the preview in full-screen view.
4. Select the X in the upper-right of the page to close the
   preview.

   You have completed the process. You have previewed the uploaded
   document.

title: Place Fields Options: Actions Button
heading_path: Place Fields Options: Actions Button
text:
You can use the Actions button on the Place Fields page to access a menu of Docusign and
Salesforce options.

You can use the Actions button on the
Place Fields page to access a menu of Docusign and Salesforce options.

![A screenshot displays the Place Fields Actions button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Place-Fields-Actions-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.5SwW1PWforVDugufIB4ZbiyPDEfjg1kG1ru4MgB6RNk&_LANG=enus)

Options include:

Table 1. Actions Button Options

| Menu Item | Action |
| --- | --- |
| **Discard Changes** | Undo all actions taken in the field placement process |
| Delete | Delete the envelope template and all attached documents |
| Edit Message | Change the email subject and email message seen by recipients |
| Edit Recipients | Edit details and actions of existing recipients or add new recipients |
| Edit Documents | View, replace, download, rename, delete, upload, or switch documents from primary (active documents in the signing process) to supplementary (passive material) |

title: Use Envelope Templates to Create and Send a Standard Envelope
heading_path: Use Envelope Templates to Create and Send a Standard Envelope
text:
Learn how to add envelope template content to a standard Send with Docusign
envelope.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that holds
documents, recipients, and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by
completing the fields assigned to them. Docusign saves the document to the
Salesforce record when all recipients finish signing.

Salesforce users have two options for sending envelopes:

[Docusign envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=zgh1596562751041.html&_LANG=enus "This guide demonstrates the process for sending a document for eSignature using an envelope template custom button.")
:   An administrator builds this three-stage sending experience with documents,
    recipients, and signature fields. Senders can add to or edit the contents of
    the envelope.

[Standard Send with Docusign template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=gsh1675832324336.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to send an envelope from a Salesforce record using a standard Send with Docusign template.")
:   This is a three-stage sending experience with no pre-built content. Senders
    select a Send with Docusign button and add documents,
    recipients, and signature fields.

You can also add content from envelope templates to a standard Send with
Docusign envelope. Adding templates lets you combine the content of
one or more envelope templates in a single envelope. Senders save time by adding
pre-built content, while keeping the flexibility that a Send with Docusign template
provides.

Use this procedure to add Docusign envelope template content to a standard Send with
Docusign envelope:

1. Select the Send with Docusign button in the upper right
   of a Salesforce record. The button may be hidden in the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.m1mCVO6ndVpQMZXCU-_pM3caY-WBBK440PAJJda3XtU&_LANG=enus) menu.

   The Documents page opens, displaying all documents
   from the Notes & Attachments section of the record.
2. Add Docusign templates to your envelope:

   1. Select Select Template. The Select
      Docusign Template dialog box opens.
   2. Select the check box next to each template you want to add.
   3. Select the Select button.![Select Docusign envelope templates to add to your envelope](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Select-DocuSign-envelope-templates-to-add-to-your-envelope.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.m1mCVO6ndVpQMZXCU-_pM3caY-WBBK440PAJJda3XtU&_LANG=enus "Select Docusign envelope templates to add to your envelope")

   The selected templates add documents, recipients, envelope custom fields, and
   Salesforce and Docusign fields to your envelope. You can choose to add more
   documents, recipients, or fields. Or you can advance to the Prepare
   & Send page and send the envelope.
3. (Optional) [Add documents to your
   envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dad1595995063747.html&_LANG=enus).
4. (Optional) [Add recipients to your
   envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=psq1595994131623.html&_LANG=enus).
5. (Optional) [Add signature fields to
   your envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uws1596054466754.html&_LANG=enus).
6. Select SEND.

You have completed the process. You have built and sent an
envelope by adding envelope templates to a standard envelope.

title: Delete a Recipient From an Envelope Template
heading_path: Delete a Recipient From an Envelope Template
text:
Administrators needing to delete recipients from an existing envelope template can do so
at any time.

Administrators needing to delete recipients from an existing envelope template can do so at
any time.

Warning: If you remove a recipient that
has signature fields assigned to them, those fields will need to be removed or
re-assigned to another recipient.

**Example**: You delete **Signer 1** from
an envelope template. **Document 1** in the template contains signature
fields assigned to Signer 1. The signature process will fail unless **Signer
1**'s fields are removed from **Document 1** or reassigned to another
recipient.

Use this procedure to delete recipients from an existing envelope template:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.dAabmSpgt3Qp40pip7Tjw4fuXZnrnBPALr26OPmYHp8&_LANG=enus)arrowhead drop-down menu to the far-right of a recipient
   name.
2. Select Delete.

   ![A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplateeditrecipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.dAabmSpgt3Qp40pip7Tjw4fuXZnrnBPALr26OPmYHp8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.")

   The recipient no longer displays on the page.
3. Select the Save and Close button at the top-right of the
   page.

   You have completed the process. You have removed the recipient from the
   template.

title: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
heading_path: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
text:
Learn how to work around the file size limit error in an envelope template.

When I try to upload a file to the envelope template Documents step, I
sometimes see an error. This does not happen with every file.

Note: This error displays when administrators try to upload files larger
than 3 MB to an envelope template. However, you can access the same envelope
templates directly in your connected Docusign account. Templates
in your Docusign account have a file size limit of **50
MB**.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. I upload a file to an envelope template at the Documents step. The document fails to
   load.
2. An error displays: This file exceeds the maximum size
   limit.

   Result: The file is not added
   to the envelope template.

title: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
heading_path: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
Resolution 1: Upload the file to the envelope template in your connected Docusign account
text:
1. Select the Save and Close button for the
   envelope template in your Salesforce organization.
2. Log in to your Docusign account on another browser tab.
3. Select Templates.
4. Select the envelope template's name and choose
   Edit.

   A page with the template settings opens.
5. Select Upload and upload the file from your
   device.
6. Select SAVE AND CLOSE.

   ![A screenshot displays the Save and Close button on a Docusign template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Save-and-close-changes-to-your-DocuSign-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1NDQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.nK5S7B-aHDeU7pauOy7NJDs9-vZ7xaK1wI9xdwYrM9E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Save and Close button on a Docusign template.")

   You have completed the process. You have avoided the Salesforce file size
   limitation by uploading the file to your Docusign
   template. This document is included in all envelopes that are sent from
   a Salesforce record using this template.

   Important: If you later edit this envelope template in
   Salesforce, you will get an error and will be unable to make
   additional changes. You must edit the template in your Docusign account.

title: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
heading_path: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
Resolution 2: Upload the file to Salesforce Files Home or Notes and Attachments
text:
1. Upload the file to your Salesforce Files Home, or
   the record's Notes and Attachments section.
2. Complete the envelope template creation process.
3. Select the envelope template custom button from a Salesforce
   record.
4. Select the Add from Salesforce button. The Add
   from Salesforce dialog box opens.
5. Check the box for the file saved in Notes and Attachments.
6. Select the Add button.
7. Complete the envelope sending process.

   The process is complete. Docusign attaches the large file from
   Notes and Attachments during the sending process.

title: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
heading_path: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
text:
This occurs when no phone number is specified for a Salesforce Users/Contacts or By Role
recipient with SMS authentication applied.

Recipients sometimes see a Security Requests From Sender dialog box when
they open envelopes sent from my envelope template. This dialog box presents the
recipient with a long list of security questions. The recipient can not open and
complete the envelope until they have answered all of the questions.

Note: This error only occurs for envelope templates matching these
criteria:

- The template contains at least one By Role or
  Users/Contacts recipient.
- At least one By Role or Contact recipient has
  SMS/Phone authentication applied, but with no
  phone number entered.
- There is no space entered between the country code and the phone
  number.
- The envelope template has the Send Now radio button
  selected on the Options step.
  ![Send Now radio button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Send-Now-radio-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1NTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.PokHil9fMfRoByp90SMnVeWcgVKwGZSbXjDM-PyfbZ8&_LANG=enus "Send Now radio button")

title: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
heading_path: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
Resolution 1: Upgrade to Docusign Apps Launcher version 4.3 or higher
text:
Docusign and Salesforce administrator

1. Log in to your Salesforce organization as an administrator.
2. [Follow these instructions to upgrade
   the Docusign Apps Launcher package to version 4.3 or
   higher.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus)

   You have completed the process. The new package will not allow
   senders to add recipients with SMS/Phone security applied and no phone
   number present.

title: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
heading_path: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
Resolution 2: Add a phone number to all By Role or Users/Contacts recipients
text:
Docusign and Salesforce administrator

Note: Only use this
resolution if you are unable to upgrade to the latest version of the
Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package.

1. Select the name of a By Role or
   Users/Contacts recipient. The Add Recipient
   dialog box opens.
2. Enter the recipient phone number if the
   SMS/Phone field is blank.

   Important: You must enter a space
   between the country code and the phone number.
3. Select the Add button. The Add Recipient dialog
   box closes.
4. Repeat the process for any other By Role or
   Users/Contacts recipient in the
   template.
5. Select the Save and Close button in the
   upper-right. The page refreshes. The envelope template page opens.

   You have completed the process. You have added the missing phone number to any
   By Role or
   Users/Contacts recipient. By Role and
   Users/Contacts recipients can open and complete envelopes without
   answering security questions.

title: Correct In-Flight Envelopes From the Envelope Status Component
heading_path: Correct In-Flight Envelopes From the Envelope Status Component
text:
Learn to use the correct envelope feature to add recipients or documents to an in-flight
envelope.

You can add recipients or documents to in-flight
envelopes using the correct envelope feature. You cannot use the
correct envelope feature for envelopes with a voided,
declined, or completed status.

Note: The original sender can
use Docusign Envelope Status to correct an active envelope.
Users with [shared access to the sender](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=ssa1578456277708.html) can correct the
sender's envelope using the legacy [Shared Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=xbn1578456279661.html) feature.

Use
this procedure to correct an in-flight envelope:

1. Open a Salesforce record and locate the Docusign Envelope
   Status component.
2. Select Correct Envelope from the arrow ![A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyODksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.HgPrHv00EQYIsVCbd3z9hciaRyLbjJlaxDvh96X7MkM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon.") drop-down menu of an in-flight envelope.

   ![A screenshot displays the Docusign Envelope Status drop-down menu with Send, View, Correct, and Void options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/DocuSign-Envelope-Status-dropdown-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyODksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.HgPrHv00EQYIsVCbd3z9hciaRyLbjJlaxDvh96X7MkM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Envelope Status drop-down menu with Send, View, Correct, and Void options.")

   The envelope opens in your Docusign account.
3. (Optional) Take any action:

   - Upload: Add documents to the envelope
   - Add Recipient: Add a new recipient to the envelope
   - Edit the details of an existing recipient
   - Edit the Email Subject or Email Message
4. Select Next.
5. (Optional): Drag recipient fields to the document.
6. Select Correct.
7. Select Back to Record to return to the Salesforce
   record.

You have completed the process. You have corrected an in-flight
envelope by adding documents, recipients, or signature fields.

title: Send an Envelope Using WhatsApp
heading_path: Send an Envelope Using WhatsApp
text:
Learn to use WhatsApp to deliver an envelope to a recipient.

You can use WhatsApp to deliver an envelope to a recipient. When you send an envelope by
WhatsApp, you cannot also send via SMS or email.

Note: This option does not display if your Docusign account does not have WhatsApp delivery turned on.

Use this procedure to send an envelope to a recipient by WhatsApp:

1. Open a Salesforce record and select a Docusign button.
2. [Add Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=mdb1596562820671.html&_LANG=enus "One or more documents are already present in the envelope. These have been added to the envelope template by your administrator. Use this procedure to view the documents or add more.").
3. [Add recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=htx1596574100545.html&_LANG=enus "One or more recipients are already present in the envelope. These have been added to the envelope template by your administrator. Use this procedure to view the recipients or add more.").
4. [Add Docusign and
   Salesforce fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=hke1596562836794.html&_LANG=enus "One or more fields are already present on the documents. These have been added to the envelope template by your administrator. Use this procedure to view the fields or add more.").
5. When you have added fields, select ACTIONS >  Edit Recipients.

   ![A screenshot displays the Actions menu with Edit Recipients selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Actions-menu-with-Edit-Recipients-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9._f-z1DFXgniLC_kZMjAPvfShRjKJxu7AN_pAGnbAPKk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Actions menu with Edit Recipients selected.")
6. Select WhatsApp for any recipient and select
   DONE.

   ![A screenshot displays the WhatsApp option checked in the Edit Recipients dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-WhatsApp-option-checked-in-the-Edit-Recipients-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9._f-z1DFXgniLC_kZMjAPvfShRjKJxu7AN_pAGnbAPKk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the WhatsApp option checked in the Edit Recipients dialog box.")
7. Select SEND.

You have completed the process. You have created an envelope
and sent it to a recipient by WhatsApp.

title: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
heading_path: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
text:
Learn to prevent sending duplicate envelopes when using Docusign in Salesforce
console view.

When I send an envelope from a record in console view, Docusign sends a duplicate
envelope.

Example:

1. I visit the Burlington Textiles record on a console view
   tab.
2. I select a Docusign eSignature button.
3. A Docusign console tab opens. I send an envelope from this
   tab.
4. I take any of these actions after sending the envelope:
   - I select the Burlington Textiles tab, then return to the
     Docusign tab.
   - I refresh the browser tab.
   - I select the Return to Record button on the
     sending tab.

   Result: Docusign sends a duplicate envelope.

title: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
heading_path: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
Resolution: Close the eSignature console tab
text:
Docusign envelope sender

Close the Docusign console tab that says
Your envelope was sent!

The signature process closes without sending a duplicate
envelope.

title: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
heading_path: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
text:
Learn to prevent sending duplicate envelopes when using Docusign in Salesforce
console view.

When I send an envelope from a record in console view, Docusign sends a duplicate
envelope.

Example:

1. I visit the Burlington Textiles record on a console view
   tab.
2. I select a Docusign eSignature button.
3. A Docusign console tab opens. I send an envelope from this
   tab.
4. I take any of these actions after sending the envelope:
   - I select the Burlington Textiles tab, then return to the
     Docusign tab.
   - I refresh the browser tab.
   - I select the Return to Record button on the
     sending tab.

   Result: Docusign sends a duplicate envelope.

title: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
heading_path: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
Resolution: Close the eSignature console tab
text:
Docusign envelope sender

Close the Docusign console tab that says
Your envelope was sent!

The signature process closes without sending a duplicate
envelope.

title: Step 1: View or Add Documents (Envelope Template Button)
heading_path: Step 1: View or Add Documents (Envelope Template Button)
text:
One or more documents are already present in the envelope. These have been added to the
envelope template by your administrator. Use this procedure to view the documents or add
more.

Note: One or more documents may be present in
the envelope. These have been added to the envelope template by your administrator.
Use this procedure to include these documents or add more.

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
2. Select the envelope template custom button in the upper-right of the
   page.

   The button is named by your administrator. It may be hidden in the arrow
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMDksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.dGWQgTj4gDj6pf0488kX36hq6YKeoYI7GvZRt7olvQQ&_LANG=enus) menu.

   ![Send with envelope template button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Send-with-Envelope-Template-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMDksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.dGWQgTj4gDj6pf0488kX36hq6YKeoYI7GvZRt7olvQQ&_LANG=enus "Send with envelope template button")

   The Documents page opens, displaying the documents that have been added by
   your administrator.
3. (Optional) Choose any option to upload or arrange documents for your envelope.
   You can add up to ten documents.

   - Upload: Select this button and follow the prompts
     to add more documents from your device
   - Add from Salesforce: Select this button to add
     documents from Salesforce Files
   - Drag documents to change their order in the envelope![Reorder uploaded files](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/esigdecreorderfiles.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMDksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.dGWQgTj4gDj6pf0488kX36hq6YKeoYI7GvZRt7olvQQ&_LANG=enus "Reorder uploaded files")
4. Select the Next button in the upper-right of the
   page.

   You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: View or Add Recipients (Envelope Template Button)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=htx1596574100545.html&_LANG=enus "One or more recipients are already present in the envelope. These have been added to the envelope template by your administrator. Use this procedure to view the recipients or add more.").

title: Envelope Template Error When Creating Merge Fields: “Failed To Connect to the Salesforce Service.”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error When Creating Merge Fields: “Failed To Connect to the Salesforce Service.”
text:
Disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account and Salesforce organization to reset
the connection. This will fix the issue.

When I try to add merge fields to a Docusign envelope template, I see one or more of
these error messages:

- Save Error: Some fields might be out of sync.
- Unable to connect to the service.
- Failed to connect to the Salesforce service.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
2. Click the Edit button in the upper-right of the
   page.
3. Select the Merge Fields tab.
4. Select the Add Field button.
5. Immediately click the Save and Close button on the
   Documents page.

   **Result**: I see an error message: Save Error:
   Some fields might be out of sync. Unable to connect to the service.
   Failed to connect to the Salesforce service.. I am unable to
   add new merge fields.

title: Envelope Template Error When Creating Merge Fields: “Failed To Connect to the Salesforce Service.”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error When Creating Merge Fields: “Failed To Connect to the Salesforce Service.”
Resolution: Disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account and your Salesforce organization
text:
1. Select the [Disconnect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus) link on the
   Docusign Setup tab in Salesforce.
2. Select the [Log in to Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus)
   button.

   You have completed the process. You have reset the connection
   between your Docusign account and Salesforce organization. You can now
   add merge fields to a Docusign envelope template.

title: Add SMS (Text Message) Delivery to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Add SMS (Text Message) Delivery to an Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to add the Email + SMS option to a recipient in your envelope
template.

Use this procedure to add the Email + SMS option to a recipient in your
envelope template.

Note: You can also use this procedure on the Recipients step when sending an
envelope.

1. Configure Email + SMS options, depending on the
   recipient type:

   Important: You must enter a space
   between the country code and the phone number.

   - Lookup Fields: Select Email + SMS from the Delivery drop-down menu
   - Related Lists: Select Email + SMS from the Delivery drop-down menu
   - Users/Contacts:
     1. Delivery: Select Email + SMS
        from the drop-down menu
     2. (Required) Phone Number: Enter
        the recipient's phone number
   - By Role:
     1. Delivery: Select Email + SMS
        from the drop-down menu.
     2. (Optional) Name: If known,
        enter the recipient's name.
     3. (Optional) Email: If known,
        enter the recipient's email address.
     4. (Optional) Phone Number: If
        known, enter the recipient's phone number. You can
        use Email + SMS for up to four (4) recipients per
        envelope.
2. Select a save option:
   - Add&New: Save the recipient and add another
   - Add: Save the recipient and close the dialog box

You have completed the process. You have added
theEmail + SMS option to a recipient in your envelope
template. When users send an envelope from this template, the recipient receives a text
message and an email. The recipient can choose to open the envelope using either method.

title: Add Salesforce Documents to an Envelope
heading_path: Add Salesforce Documents to an Envelope
text:
Learn how to add Salesforce documents to your envelope.

You can add Salesforce documents to your envelope when using the standard Send with Docusign experience, or the eSignature experience from the Docusign Negotiate action
menu.

Here are the types of Salesforce Files you can add:

Table 1. Salesforce File Types

| Name | File Type |
| --- | --- |
| Upload Documents | Add files from your device. |
| Owned by Me | Add Salesforce files that have you listed as Owner. |
| Shared with Me | Add Salesforce files that others have shared with you. |
| Recent | Add recently-viewed files from a record. |
| Following | Add all files that you are following on a record. |
| Libraries | Add all files that are grouped in a Salesforce Library. |

Use these procedures to add Salesforce files to an envelope using:

- [A standard Send with Docusign button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=tco1611350907305.html&_LANG=enus)
- [The Docusign Negotiate action
  menu](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=zwk1586134361708.html&_LANG=enus)

title: When I Select a Docusign Button in Salesforce, I See an Error Message: "The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again."
heading_path: When I Select a Docusign Button in Salesforce, I See an Error Message: "The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again."
text:
Work around an error message when you select a Docusign object in Salesforce as a new
Docusign user.

I am a Salesforce user and just got assigned a Docusign user account.

When I select a Docusign button or component in Salesforce, I am asked to authorize.

I enter my Docusign and Salesforce credentials, but see an error message.

The message reads: The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session.
Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again.

I am unable to access Docusign apps and features in Salesforce.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Receive a user activation email from Docusign.
2. Create a Docusign password and activate my user.
3. Select a Docusign button or component in Salesforce.
4. Enter my Docusign and Salesforce credentials when prompted to
   authorize.

   **Result**: I see an error message: The link
   you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page,
   refresh it, and try again. I am unable to access Docusign apps and features in Salesforce.

title: When I Select a Docusign Button in Salesforce, I See an Error Message: "The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again."
heading_path: When I Select a Docusign Button in Salesforce, I See an Error Message: "The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again."
Resolution: Select the "back" button on your browser and then refresh the page
text:
1. Select the "back" button on your browser.
2. Select the "refresh" button on your browser.
3. Select the Docusign object again.

   You have completed the process. You have finished the
   authorization process for Docusign objects in Salesforce. You can now
   use all Docusign apps and services to which your administrator granted
   you access.

title: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce User Overview
heading_path: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce User Overview
text:
Learn about how Docusign Apps Launcher apps help you manage agreements
in your Salesforce organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce is a package of apps that work together
to generate and send agreements for signature.

- CLM for Salesforce: Build workflows and document generation
  configurations, store documents, and send agreements for approval or signature
- Gen for Salesforce: Select a button to generate agreements
- eSignature for Salesforce: Send completed agreements for electronic
  signature

This guide covers use of the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.

It includes the following topics:

title: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce User Overview
heading_path: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce User Overview
text:
te agreements
- eSignature for Salesforce: Send completed agreements for electronic
  signature

This guide covers use of the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.

It includes the following topics:

- [Accessing Docusign Apps Launcher
  Features](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.")
- [Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=pay1650779719005.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the Docusign Apps Launcher requirements for browsers, operating systems, and devices.")
- [Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=jtj1614664835837.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate a new device for use with your Docusign account.")
- [Activate Your Docusign
  for Salesforce User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.")
- [Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=yzs1586134269459.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher apps.")
- [Create and Send an Envelope with Docusign eSignature for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=wxo1617594601811.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create and send an envelope with Docusign eSignature for Salesforce.")
- [Sign with Docusign as a Salesforce Experience Cloud User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=pib1618381448481.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to sign a document from a record as a Salesforce Experience Cloud user.")

- [Envelope Creation Options: Recipients Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=jwv1729543099566.html&_LANG=enus "This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the Recipients step of the envelope creation process.")
- [Envelope Creation Options: Place Fields Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=git1729544437800.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use options on the Place Fields page to customize your envelope.")

title: CCRM Scripting in My URLFOR Button Does Not Reassign Roles for CRCL Entries
heading_path: CCRM Scripting in My URLFOR Button Does Not Reassign Roles for CRCL Entries
text:
Learn about a known issue that prevents CCRM scripting from reassigning roles for CRCL
entries.

I have a problem with my URLFOR custom button that uses CCRM scripting for CRCL
entries.

When I try to use the button to assign Docusign roles to custom Salesforce objects,
it fails.

I am unable to use the URLFOR custom button in my Salesforce organization.

Note:

- CCRM: Custom Contact Role Map
- CRCL: Custom Related Contact List

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Create a custom Salesforce object for use with CRCL scripting.
2. Create three CRCL entries with three different Salesforce roles:
   - Influencer
   - Business User
   - Evaluator
3. Create a custom button in Salesforce with this URLFOR script:
   {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId =
   Case.Id, CRCL =
   'CRCLs\_\_r,Email~Email\_\_c;FirstName~FirstName\_\_c;LastName~LastName\_\_c;Role~Role\_\_c',
   CCRM = 'Evaluator~Signer 1;Influencer~Signer 2;Decision Maker~Signer 3'
   ])}
4. Add the custom button to a Salesforce layout.
5. Select the custom button on a record to run the URLFOR script.

   **Expected
   result**: The three custom Salesforce roles are reassigned to their
   respective Docusign roles.

   **Result**: The custom roles are not
   reassigned.

Important: There is currently no remedy for this. This
issue will be fixed in a future release.

title: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
heading_path: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
text:
Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or
Login IP Ranges to fix issues with Restricted
IP errors in login history for your Docusign connected app.

If you see Restricted IP errors in login history for your Docusign connected app, there are two possible causes:

- The Salesforce organization has Multi-Factor Authentication for
  API Logins enabled on the user's' profile. This is
  incompatible with Docusign’s server-to-server API updates.
- Login IP Ranges are specified on the user's profile.
  This prevents API logins for the user from Docusign servers.

title: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
heading_path: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
Resolution 1: Disable Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1MDIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.mPCgZVKFq2FQYcpBP0RSGkS5GGuepzP3Mu2C0dC0XOg&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Edit button.
4. Uncheck the Multi-Factor Authentication for API
   Logins box.
5. Select the Save button.

   The process is complete. You no longer see Restricted
   IP errors in login history for your Docusign Connected
   App.

title: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
heading_path: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
Resolution 2: Disable Login IP Ranges on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1MDIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.mPCgZVKFq2FQYcpBP0RSGkS5GGuepzP3Mu2C0dC0XOg&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User) The
   Profile page opens.
3. Select the Login IP Ranges link at the top of the Profile
   page.
4. Select the Del link to the left of an IP
   range.
5. Select the OK button on the Are you sure you
   want to delete the IP Login Range dialog box.
6. Repeat the Del action for all remaining
   ranges.

   The process is complete. You no longer see Restricted
   IP errors in login history for your Docusign Connected
   App.

title: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
heading_path: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
Resolution 3: Add the relevant Docusign endpoint IP addresses to the allow-list
text:
1. [Add Trusted IP Ranges for Docusign Gen for
   Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=lip1592574174158.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus).
2. Add any other restricted IP addresses shown in the user’s login
   history.

   The process is complete. You no longer see Restricted
   IP errors in login history for your Docusign Connected
   App.

title: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
text:
To fix this issue, first select Disconnect on the Docusign Setup tab.
Then delete the Salesforce link in the Connect settings of your Docusign
account.

When I try to add merge fields to a Docusign envelope template, I see one or
more of these error messages:

- Save Error: Some fields might be out of sync.
- Unable to connect to the service.
- Failed to connect to the Salesforce service.

When I disconnect and reconnect my Docusign account in Salesforce, I still
have problems with some functions, like adding merge fields to a Docusign envelope template. Using the [Docusign TroubleShooting page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=rag1597638195677.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus)
to disconnect and reconnect does not fix the issue, either.

Note: This is not a typical scenario. Administrators can
normally select the Disconnect link on the Docusign Setup tab to
cleanly disconnect and reconnect.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Select the Disconnect link on the Docusign Setup tab.
2. Select the Log in to Docusign button.
3. Reconnect Salesforce and Docusign.
4. Open a Docusign envelope template.
5. Try to add new merge fields.

   **Result**: I see an error message:
   Save Error. Some fields might be out of sync. I
   am unable to add any merge fields.

title: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
Resolution 1: Disconnect Docusign and then delete the Salesforce link in the Connect settings of your Docusign account
text:
1. Select the [Disconnect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus) link on the
   Docusign Setup tab in Salesforce.
2. Log in to the connected Docusign account as an
   Administrator.
3. Select the Settings tab.
4. Select Connect on the left-hand navigation
   pane.
5. Select the Actions drop-down menu next to the
   Docusign Apps Launcher listing.
6. Select Delete from the list.
7. Select the Delete button on the Delete
   Connect Configuration dialog box.
8. Return to Salesforce and select the [Log in to Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus)
   button.

   You have completed the process. You have disconnected the account links in both
   your Docusign account and your Salesforce
   organization. You have then reconnected the accounts in Salesforce. You
   can now add merge fields to an envelope template.

title: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
Resolution 2: Reconfigure the Legacy Connect Configuration
text:
1. Log in to the connected Docusign account as the
   Connect for Salesforce integration user.
2. Go to Settings >  Integrations >  Connect
3. Select Actions and choose
   Edit from the drop-down menu.
4. Select Reconfigure Login.
5. Select OAuth and follow this link to [refresh the
   connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=psy1581360698967&topicId=voz1581360675739.html&_LANG=enus).

   You have completed the process. You have refreshed the Connect
   configuration between Docusign and Salesforce. You can
   now add merge fields to an envelope template.

title: Create New Custom Envelope Template Fields
heading_path: Create New Custom Envelope Template Fields
text:
Learn how to use the Custom Field feature on the Place Fields tab.

You can use the custom field feature on the Place Fields page to create
your own fields that are not included in the standard Docusign fields menu.

These fields can be custom radio buttons, drop-down menus, formula fields, fields requiring
users to attach a document, and many more.

Once created, these fields are added to uploaded documents in your envelope template.

Use this procedure to create a custom field:

1. Select the Custom Fields button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatecustomfields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NTAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.a_Lai05HItFPbQbMP24jszO7YuS7dtUsXdz0mE1OxH0&_LANG=enus) in the left bar.
2. Select the + button to the right of the
   Custom Fields header.

   ![Select + button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatecustomfieldsadd.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NTAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.a_Lai05HItFPbQbMP24jszO7YuS7dtUsXdz0mE1OxH0&_LANG=enus "Select + button")

   The Custom Field Details dialog box opens.
3. Complete the following fields:

   1. Name: Enter a descriptive name for the
      field.
   2. Type: Select an option (Checkbox, Formula,
      Attachment, and so on) from the drop-down menu.
   3. Checkbox: Select the check box for Required, Read
      Only, or Shared.
   4. Options: Depending on the Type selected, a list
      of options appear (Initial Value, Default Selection, and so on.)
   5. Formatting: Custom Fields with text show options
      for customizing Font, Font Color, Font Size, and so on.
   6. Default Advanced: Add a Tooltip or enter
      AutoPlace Text from an existing AutoPlace tag on the document.

You have completed the process. You have configured the new
custom field and can use it in the Place Fields step of an envelope template.

title: Best Practices for Creating AutoPlace Fields
heading_path: Best Practices for Creating AutoPlace Fields
text:
Learn about using AutoPlace fields to add Docusign fields to specified
places in a document.

AutoPlace fields are a feature that you can use when building a Docusign
envelope template. AutoPlace automatically adds Docusign fields to
specified places in a document.

This saves time and reduces error because senders do not manually place fields for their
recipients.

Consider the following guidelines when creating AutoPlace fields:

- [Use generic Docusign credentials to
  create custom fields.](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Best-Practices-for-Setting-up-DocuSign-for-Salesforce-Connected-User-accounts-in-both-DocuSign-and-Salesforce) These fields are linked to the creating Docusign user, and fail if someone deletes that user. If you
  have a [Docusign system
  sender](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsg1646663268427&topicId=jxz1611642321527.html&_LANG=enus), create merge fields with those credentials.
- [Create a Connect configuration for
  Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=ctv1583277395112.html&_LANG=enus). A Connect configuration gives you the following advantages:
  - Writes data back to Salesforce when envelopes are completed.
  - Enables the creation of merge fields in the Connect page of your Docusign account.
  - Solves some known issues with merge field functionality.
- Use automatic AutoPlace fields when adding the following fields to a
  template:
  - Signature
  - Date Signed
  - Initial
  - Optional Initial
- Use Document custom fields and Salesforce merge fields when adding the
  following types of text value fields to a template:
  - Name
  - Company
  - Title
- Use the following guidelines when you create Document custom fields and
  Salesforce merge fields:
  - Use a font size of seven points or larger when you create anchor text
    strings.
  - Change the text color of the anchor text to match the background color
    of your document. So if your document is white, change the font color to
    white to hide it from recipients.
  - Use text strings with no spaces in them, like
    \cb\_{r}\_yes\ for a check box field.
