  - Create unique anchor text strings for all custom fields. Docusign adds an AutoPlace field to your document
    wherever it finds matching text.

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Learn the Docusign Apps Launcher requirements for browsers, operating
systems, and devices.

You can use the Docusign for Salesforce apps with the following browsers,
operating systems, and devices:

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported browsers
text:
Important: Salesforce and Docusign do not
support any version of Microsoft Internet Explorer.

| Browser | Supported version | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Google Chrome | Latest | n/a |
| Safari (Apple) | Latest | Desktop-only. Salesforce does not support the mobile version of Safari. Enabling third-party cookies is required. |
| Microsoft Edge | Latest | n/a |
| Mozilla Firefox | Latest | n/a |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported operating systems
text:
| Operating system | Version |
| --- | --- |
| Microsoft Windows | Windows 10 or newer |
| MacOS (Apple desktop) | 10.14 (Mojave) or newer |
| iOS (Apple mobile) | iOS 14.x or newer |
| Android OS | Android 8.0 or newer |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Salesforce mobile app requirements
text:
Required settings: Configure the following settings to use Docusign in the Salesforce mobile app:

- Enable third-party cookies in the [Salesforce mobile app settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.salesforce_app.htm&type=5).
- Turn off Include the instance name in Visualforce URLs when
  third-party cookies are blocked in Salesforce My
  Domain settings.

Approved Docusign apps: You can use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
- Docusign Gen

Non-approved Docusign apps: You cannot use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign Negotiate
- Docusign CLM
- Docusign CLM Essentials

Approved envelope template types: You can only use the following
envelope template types in the Salesforce mobile app:

- [Quick send envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Send now envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)

Mobile app tagging requirements: Meet the following requirements
to add fields to Docusign envelopes:

- Install the Docusign mobile app on your Android or iOS
  device.
- Turn off mobile app tagging when using an iPad.

For more information about Salesforce requirements, see the [Supported Browsers and Devices](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.getstart_browser_overview.htm&type=5) page on the
Salesforce support site.

title: Rename a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Rename a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
text:
You can change the file names of uploaded documents in the envelope template by
following these steps.

You can change the file names of uploaded documents in the envelope template by following
these steps:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.3mUwB6nxHTVtFjHpDQwRmjMB2cq9PzbdyTaezc6mCIk&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu to the right of a document.
2. Select Rename from the options.

   ![Rename uploaded document](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatedocumentoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.3mUwB6nxHTVtFjHpDQwRmjMB2cq9PzbdyTaezc6mCIk&_LANG=enus "Rename uploaded document")

   The Rename Document dialog box opens.
3. Enter a new name for the document.
4. Select the Rename button.
5. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have changed the name of the
   uploaded document.

title: Sign with Docusign as a Salesforce Experience Cloud User
heading_path: Sign with Docusign as a Salesforce Experience Cloud User
text:
Learn to sign a document from a record as a Salesforce Experience Cloud user.

Salesforce Experience Cloud users cannot send Docusign envelopes to recipients. However,
Experience Cloud users can use Docusign to sign documents themselves.

Use this procedure to sign a document as an Experience Cloud user:

1. Open a Salesforce Experience Cloud site and select a record.
2. Select a Docusign button. Your Salesforce administrator chooses the button
   name.
3. Select I agree to use electronic records and signatures
   and Continue.
4. Select Sign and complete the following fields:

   1. Full Name: Enter or edit your full name.
   2. Initials: Enter or edit your initials.
   3. Choose a tab to set your signature:
      - Select Style: Pick a signature style from
        a list
      - Draw: Use the cursor to draw your
        signature
      - Upload: Upload an image of your signature
        from your device
   4. Select Adopt and Sign.
5. Select Finish.

   A copy of the signed document appears in Notes &
   Attachments.

You have completed the process. You signed a document with
Docusign as a Salesforce Experience Cloud user.

title: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
heading_path: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
text:
Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or
Login IP Ranges to fix issues with users being prompted to
re-enter credentials every hour.

Some users are prompted to enter their credentials every hour. This could occur for
two reasons:

- The Salesforce organization has Multi-Factor Authentication for API
  Logins enabled on the user's' profile. This is incompatible with
  Docusign’s server-to-server API updates.
- Login IP Ranges are specified on the user's profile. This
  prevents API logins for the user from Docusign servers.

title: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
heading_path: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
Resolution 1: Disable Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0JzDyX4hU7x35WvVs-wqFA_Z5vOSgoINLJkXe0k9wsc&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Edit button.
4. Uncheck the Multi-Factor Authentication for API
   Logins box.
5. Select the Save button.

   The process is complete. Users are no longer prompted to enter
   credentials every hour.

title: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
heading_path: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
Resolution 2: Disable Login IP Ranges on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0JzDyX4hU7x35WvVs-wqFA_Z5vOSgoINLJkXe0k9wsc&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Login IP Ranges
4. Select the Del link to the left of an IP
   range. link at the top of
   the Profile page.
5. Select the OK button on the link at the top of
   the Are you sure you want to delete the IP Login
   Range? dialog box.
6. Repeat the Del action for all remaining
   ranges.

   The process is complete. Users are no longer prompted to enter
   credentials every hour.

title: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
heading_path: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
Resolution 3: Add the relevant Docusign endpoint IP addresses to the allow-list
text:
1. [Add the IP ranges from the Docusign Trust
   Center](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/esignature).
2. (Optional): If needed, add any restricted IP addresses from the user’s
   Salesforce login history logs, including **[Salesforce IP addresses](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000321501&type=1)**.

   The process is complete. Users are no longer prompted to enter
   credentials every hour.

title: Envelope Template Field Options
heading_path: Envelope Template Field Options
text:
How to customize e-signature field options in a Docusign envelope template.

You can customize Docusign envelope template fields from the Place Fields
page of an existing envelope template.

Select any field to open an options panel on the far-right of the page.

Different options are shown, depending on the field type that is selected.

Follow this procedure to change a field that is placed on a page:

- Enlarge field: Drag a field anchor outward
- Shrink field: Drag a field anchor inward
- Delete field: Select the
  Delete button in the lower-right of the page to
  remove it

![Enlarge field](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Enlarge-field.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.S_odLd69uoGz7jHvI4EqiQs0O_A571cyYfi8c_kY7i4&_LANG=enus)

Here are the configurable options available:

Table 1.
Docusign Field Options

| Option | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Required Field | The recipient must take action. |
| Read Only | This field option cannot be changed. |
| Formatting | Change font type, size, color, etc. |
| Data Label | Link fields of the same type by using a shared label. When one field is completed, the rest are as well. |
| Tooltip | Instructive text that appears when the cursor hovers over a field. |
| Location | Use pixels from the left and top to set the exact field location on the page. |
| AutoPlace | Fields are auto-placed on document every time a keyword is matched. |
| Sender Permissions | Make a field mandatory, or prevent senders from making changes. |
| Add Text | Add a text box field to the document. |
| Validation | Force user input into a set format, like xxx-xx-xxxx for Social Security number fields, xx/xx/xxxx for Date, etc. |
| Collaboration | Allow recipients to collaborate on envelope content. |
| Conditional Logic | Fields only appear to the recipient when a specified condition is met. |
| Collect Payment | Allow recipient to pay for services. |
| Save Payment Method | Allow recipient to store payment info for future transactions. |
| Payment Gateway | Set the method for transferring funds. |
| Payment Amount | Set the amount to be paid. |
| Payment Description | Add a note about the purpose of a payment. |
| Edit Formula | Edit an existing formula built from number and date fields in an envelope. |
| Save as Custom Field | Add a customized field to the Custom Fields menu for re-use. |
| Delete | Remove a selected field from the page. |
| Resize Field | Drag field corners to enlarge or shrink field boundaries on the page. |

title: Add Salesforce Files to a Standard Send With Docusign Envelope
heading_path: Add Salesforce Files to a Standard Send With Docusign Envelope
text:
Learn how to access the Add from Salesforce button in the
standard Send with Docusign signature experience or the Negotiate action menu.

Use this procedure to access the Add from Salesforce button in the
standard Send with Docusign signature experience:

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
2. Select the Send with Docusign button in the upper-right
   of the page.

   Note: It may be hidden in
   the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.k7lR_HDGuAhImDruROsBIuPISLIgmZF8tHs1uwQs37M&_LANG=enus)
   menu.

   ![Send with Docusign button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Send-with-DocuSign-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.k7lR_HDGuAhImDruROsBIuPISLIgmZF8tHs1uwQs37M&_LANG=enus "Send with Docusign button")

   The Documents page opens.
3. Select the Add from Salesforce button at the upper-right
   of the page.
4. Add a document using any method. You can add up to ten documents.

   - Upload Documents: Follow the prompts to add files
     from your device
   - Owned by Me: Select check boxes to add files with
     you listed as Owner
   - Shared with Me: Select check boxes to add
     documents from Salesforce files that others have shared with you
   - Recent: Select check boxes to add documents from
     recently-viewed files
   - Following: Select check boxes to add files that
     you are following
   - Libraries: Open Salesforce Asset
     Libraries and select check boxes to add files
5. Select the Add button.

   You have completed the process. You have added a Salesforce document to
   an envelope in the standard Send with Docusign experience.

title: Add a By Role Recipient to an Envelope Using Merge Field Syntax
heading_path: Add a By Role Recipient to an Envelope Using Merge Field Syntax
text:
Find out how to add merge field syntax to a By Role recipient in an envelope
template.

Use this procedure to add merge field syntax to a By Role recipient in an
envelope template:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczOTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.jtikmxuDRfghgIzzXEuDlZ6w-EBA4ILEFfci51rl8Tc&_LANG=enus)
   dropdown menu next to a recipient name.
2. Select Edit from the options.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
3. Complete the following from the Add Recipient dialog box:
   1. Select By Role in the left column.
   2. Role: Enter a recipient role like
      Signer 1.
   3. Add Name and Email: Select the link to add merge
      field syntax.
   4. Name: Add merge field syntax for the object name,
      like `{!Contact.Name}` for a Salesforce contact.
   5. Email Address: Add merge field syntax for the
      object email address, like `{!Contact.Email}` for a
      Salesforce contact.
   6. Select the Add button to save the recipient and
      close the dialog box.

      ![Using merge field syntax for a contact](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210706-at-20211-PM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczOTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.jtikmxuDRfghgIzzXEuDlZ6w-EBA4ILEFfci51rl8Tc&_LANG=enus "Using merge field syntax for a contact")

You have completed the process. You have added a By
Role recipient to an envelope template for a Salesforce user, contact, or
lead. When a user selects the envelope template button on a record, the recipient name
and email is dynamically added.

title: Envelope Template Field Categories and Functions
heading_path: Envelope Template Field Categories and Functions
text:
The Place Fields page in the envelope template process contains more than just standard
eSignature fields. You can add Salesforce fields, Notary fields, Seal fields, Smart Sections
or build custom fields.

The Place Fields page in the envelope template process contains
more than just standard eSignature fields. You can add Salesforce fields, Notary
fields, Seal fields, Smart Sections or build custom fields.

Here are all categories of fields that can be placed on documents:

Table 1. Field Categories and Functions

| Button | Field Type | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Standard Fields | Standard Docusign fields such as Signature, Initial, Date Signed, etc. |
|  | Custom Fields | Build custom radio buttons, check boxes, formulas, etc. to place on document |
|  | Merge Fields | Fields that link to Salesforce data |
|  | Notary Fields | Add fields for a signer and notary (in the same physical location) to complete signing together |
|  | Seal Fields | Add stamps for legal entities to show evidence of document origin and integrity |
|  | Smart Sections | Select blocks of text that can collapse and expand for mobile viewers |

title: Edit the Details of an Envelope Template Recipient
heading_path: Edit the Details of an Envelope Template Recipient
text:
You can edit the details of recipients in an existing envelope template at any time.

Use this procedure to edit the details of recipients in an existing envelope template:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.2jgK8HgA0sG0O3QY9gDkGWMopOhVAAQaijza92kiE9M&_LANG=enus) next to a recipient
   name and choose Edit.

   ![A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplateeditrecipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.2jgK8HgA0sG0O3QY9gDkGWMopOhVAAQaijza92kiE9M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.")

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens

   Note: You can also select the name link (i.e. Owner) to edit the
   recipient.
2. Edit recipient details or actions.
3. Select the Add button. The Add Recipient dialog box
   closes.
4. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have edited recipient details.

title: Learn About Using AutoPlace Fields in Envelope Templates
heading_path: Learn About Using AutoPlace Fields in Envelope Templates
text:
Learn how to build AutoPlace fields in template documents to specify where signer fields
appear.

AutoPlace fields are a feature you can use when building a Docusign envelope
template. AutoPlace automatically adds Docusign fields to specified places in a
document.

AutoPlace fields save time and reduce errors because senders do not have to manually place
fields for recipients.

- You can add AutoPlace fields throughout your documents for each recipient role.
- You can add automatic AutoPlace fields for up to 99 recipients
  (Signer 1, Signer 2, and so
  on.)
- You can build custom fields to add Salesforce fields and additional recipients
  to your document template.

Table 1. AutoPlace terms

| Term | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Anchor text | Text that tells Docusign what field to add to a document that is sent for e-signature. For example, if you add  \s1\ to a document, Docusign automatically adds a signature field to that location. This text is typically white, so it blends into the background of the page, and is not visible to the signer. |
| AutoPlace | A type of field that Docusign places over anchor text. Docusign automatically places these fields so that senders do not have to manually add them. |
| Envelope template recipient roles | Placeholder text that tells Docusign the role and signing order of recipients. |
| Merge field | A Docusign custom field that is connected to a Salesforce field. These fields are connected by the Relate to Salesforce property. |
| Writeback | A Docusign field that updates a Salesforce field when the signature process is complete. |

Administrator view:

An
administrator builds a Docusign envelope template. The
administrator wants to add AutoPlace fields in the template document. The
administrator adds documents and recipients to the template. The administrators
moves to the Place Fields page and takes the following actions:

Add
AutoPlace fields: The administrator adds these AutoPlace fields to
the document for the Signer 1 role:
![A screenshot displays AutoPlace field examples for Name, Company, Signature, and Date Signed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/AutoPlace-field-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.O_S_Pl0F2pZKFr0Q2JM_WDlqGK0L30KNPmHep7NE6vY&_LANG=enus)

Change AutoPlace text color: The administrator
changes the font color of the fields to match the page color of the template
document. This ensures that the AutoPlace text is not visible to recipients. In this
example, the administrator changes the font color to white:
![A screenshot displays the Name, Company, Signature, and Date Signed fields appearing blank because the text is white.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/AutoPlace-fields-with-white-font.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.O_S_Pl0F2pZKFr0Q2JM_WDlqGK0L30KNPmHep7NE6vY&_LANG=enus)

Sender view:

A sender uses the
administrator's template to create an envelope. The AutoPlace fields add the correct
signature fields for Signer 1. Note that the fields are
visible to the sender, but not the AutoPlace text, which has white font:
![A screenshot displays what senders see when AutoPlace adds Name, Company, Signature, and Date Signed fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/AutoPlace-fields-example-output.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.O_S_Pl0F2pZKFr0Q2JM_WDlqGK0L30KNPmHep7NE6vY&_LANG=enus)

Recipient view:

title: Learn About Using AutoPlace Fields in Envelope Templates
heading_path: Learn About Using AutoPlace Fields in Envelope Templates
text:
I1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.O_S_Pl0F2pZKFr0Q2JM_WDlqGK0L30KNPmHep7NE6vY&_LANG=enus)

Recipient view:

The
Signer 1 recipient opens your envelope, and sees the
fields you assigned to them. In this example, Signer 1's Name,
Company, and Date Signed fields are
already displayed. Signer 1 only needs to complete the
Signature field:
![A screenshot displays what recipients see when AutoPlace adds Name, Company, Signature, and Date Signed fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/What-your-recipient-sees.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.O_S_Pl0F2pZKFr0Q2JM_WDlqGK0L30KNPmHep7NE6vY&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Document Custom AutoPlace Fields
heading_path: Use Document Custom AutoPlace Fields
text:
Learn to use document custom auto-place fields for recipient information that does not
write back to Salesforce.

You can add document custom field AutoPlace fields to envelope template
documents.

Use document custom AutoPlace fields in templates that don’t need Docusign fields
mapped to Salesforce fields. When recipients complete their assigned fields,
Docusign does not update any fields in Salesforce.

Important:
[You must create document custom fields in your
Docusign account before you can use them in your envelope template.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zus1583277381502.html&_LANG=enus)

Follow this procedure to add document custom AutoPlace fields to an envelope template
document:

1. Select the name of the document you will edit.

   The document preview page opens.
2. Select the Download button in the upper-right.

   The document is saved to the downloads folder of your
   device.
3. Add document custom AutoPlace fields to your template document:

   1. Open the document and move the cursor to where you will add document custom AutoPlace
      fields.
   2. Enter the text for AutoPlace fields (\s1\ for
      Signer 1 signature, \n1\ for Signer 1 name, and
      so on.)
      ![AutoPlace field examples](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/AutoPlace-field-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.E2OhqjGKkqAEyZChhqiojHFjZ2lvA3P84UMQC0TNbH8&_LANG=enus "AutoPlace field examples")
   3. Highlight the AutoPlace text and change the font color to match the page
      background. So, if the page is white, change the AutoPlace font to
      white.
      ![AutoPlace fields with white font](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/AutoPlace-fields-with-white-font.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.E2OhqjGKkqAEyZChhqiojHFjZ2lvA3P84UMQC0TNbH8&_LANG=enus "AutoPlace fields with white font")

      Note: This prevents the AutoPlace text from
      being visible in your envelope. Your recipients only see the
      signature fields.
   4. Save the changes to your template document.
4. Replace your old template document with the new version:

   1. Return to the envelope template Documents
      page.
   2. Select the arrow drop-down menu next to the far-right of the document
      you updated.
   3. Select Delete. The document disappears.
      ![Delete template document](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Delete-template-document.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.E2OhqjGKkqAEyZChhqiojHFjZ2lvA3P84UMQC0TNbH8&_LANG=enus "Delete template document")
   4. Select the Upload button.
   5. Select the name of the updated file from your device.
   6. Select Open. The Upload File dialog box
      opens.
   7. Select the Done button.

   The dialog box disappears. The name of your updated document
   appears.
5. Select the Save and Close button in the
   upper-right.

   The envelope template page opens.

You have completed the process. You have added standard
AutoPlace fields to your envelope template document. When you send an envelope from this
template, Docusign or Salesforce fields are automatically placed where you added
AutoPlace fields. The AutoPlace text is not visible to your recipients, because you
colored it to match the page. Your recipients only see the fields they need to complete.

title: Take Action on In-Flight Envelopes From the Docusign Envelope Status Component
heading_path: Take Action on In-Flight Envelopes From the Docusign Envelope Status Component
text:
Learn to view or act on active envelopes using the Docusign Envelope
Status component.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that contains
documents, recipients, and signature fields. When all recipients complete their assigned
fields, Docusign saves the completed contract.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. The Docusign
 Envelope Status component displays the
status of all envelopes sent for signature for a Salesforce record.

You can also use the Docusign Envelope Status component to take
action on “in-flight” envelopes. In-flight envelopes have been sent for signature and
have not been completed or voided (canceled.)

Note: If you do not see the Docusign
 Envelope
Status component, your administrator must [add the component to the Salesforce layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xkm1586134206424.html&_LANG=enus).

You can select the drop-down menu next to any envelope in the Docusign Envelope
Status component to take action:

- [Send Reminders for Active Docusign Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=tjm1597250379140.html&_LANG=enus)
- [View In-Flight Envelopes From the Docusign Envelope Status Component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ver1669069746803.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Correct In-Flight Envelopes From the
  Envelope Status Component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=loj1656359679073.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Void Active Docusign
  Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=daq1597261217945.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Sign Now to Let a Sender Sign Their Own Envelope
heading_path: Use Sign Now to Let a Sender Sign Their Own Envelope
text:
Learn how to use the Sign Now option to let the sender to sign their own envelope
immediately after sending it.

The Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now) option lets the
sender sign their own envelope immediately after sending it. This simplifies the
signing process for the sender if they are also a recipient.

Here are the specifications of the Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign
Now) option:

- This option only appears when you select In Person Signer
  as the recipient action
- When Signing Order is *enabled*, this option only appears if the previous
  recipient has Sign Now enabled
- If the first recipient in the signing order has Sign Now *unchecked*, no
  recipients can use the Sign Now option
- When Signing Order is *disabled*, you can add the Sign Now option for any
  In Person Signer recipient

Use this procedure to add the Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign
Now) option to an envelope template recipient.

Note: You can also use this procedure on the Recipients step when sending an
envelope.

1. Select the name of the recipient you want to update.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
2. Select In Person Signer from the Action drop-down
   menu.
3. Select the Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now)
   check box.
4. Select the Add button.
5. Select the Save and Close button.

You have completed the process. You have added
theInitiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now) option to your
envelope template recipient. The sender is prompted to sign their own envelope
immediately after they send it.

title: Envelope Template Field Types and Functions
heading_path: Envelope Template Field Types and Functions
text:
Here are the field types that can be used in an envelope template.

The Docusign envelope template Place Fields tab contains many
different fields that can be placed on uploaded documents.

Here are the fields that can be used in an envelope template:

Table 1.
Docusign field icons and actions

| Field Icon | Action |
| --- | --- |
|  | Recipient signature |
|  | Add recipient initials |
|  | Add date of recipient's signature |
|  | Add the full name of a recipient |
|  | Add a recipient's email address |
|  | Add recipient's company |
|  | Add recipient's title |
|  | Add a free-form field for recipient to enter text |
|  | Add a check box for recipient options |
|  | Add a drop-down menu for recipient options |
|  | Add a radio button for recipient options |
|  | Add a field to accept recipient payments A payment account must first be set up |
|  | Add a formula to calculate a value based on number or date fields in your document |
|  | Add a field to request additional documentation from a recipient |
|  | Add a free form text field visible only to the assigned recipient and only during the signing session |
|  | Add an alternative to a required Signature or Initial field Selecting Approve is the equivalent of signing |
|  | Add a field that terminates the recipient signing session |

title: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
heading_path: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
text:
Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or
Login IP Ranges to fix issues with envelope and recipient
statuses not updating.

If your Docusign Envelope Status and Docusign Recipient Status do not update, there are two
possible causes:

- The Salesforce organization has Multi-Factor Authentication for API
  Logins enabled on the user's' profile. This is incompatible with
  Docusign’s server-to-server API updates.
- Login IP Ranges are specified on the user's profile. This
  prevents API logins for the user from Docusign servers.

title: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
heading_path: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
Resolution 1: Disable Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0OTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.cF23NRC24CIeIf2BskIh19Nte_gi_awCbB1GkIqgj8o&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Edit button.
4. Uncheck the Multi-Factor Authentication for API
   Logins box.
5. Select the Save button.

   The process is complete. Your Docusign Envelope Status and
   Docusign Recipient Status now update.

title: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
heading_path: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
Resolution 2: Disable Login IP Ranges on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0OTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.cF23NRC24CIeIf2BskIh19Nte_gi_awCbB1GkIqgj8o&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Login IP Ranges link at the top of
   the Profile page.
4. Select the Del link to the left of an IP
   range.
5. Select the OK button on the Are you
   sure you want to delete the IP Login Range dialog box.
6. Repeat the Del action for all remaining
   ranges.

   The process is complete. Your Docusign Envelope Status and
   Docusign Recipient Status now update.

title: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
heading_path: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
Resolution 3: Add the relevant Docusign endpoint IP addresses to the allow-list
text:
1. [Add Trusted IP Ranges for Docusign Gen for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=lip1592574174158.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus)
2. Add any other restricted IP addresses shown in the user’s login
   history.

   The process is complete. Your Docusign Envelope Status and
   Docusign Recipient Status now update.

title: Send Reminders for Active Docusign Envelopes
heading_path: Send Reminders for Active Docusign Envelopes
text:
Learn to use the Send Reminder action for active envelopes from the Docusign Envelope Status component.

The Docusign
 Envelope Status section of a
Salesforce page displays the status of all envelopes sent for signature. The
Send Reminder feature helps you remind envelope
recipients to complete their assignments.

You cannot use the Send Reminder feature for
envelopes with a voided, declined, or completed status.

Recipients with pending tasks receive an email notifying: (sender name) sent you a
document to review and sign.

Use this procedure to send a reminder for an envelope from the Docusign
Envelope Status component:

1. Open a Salesforce record and locate the Docusign
   Envelope Status component.
2. Select Send Reminder from the arrow ![A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyODIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.Ya24Md0nckyFO0_URz-ONrRlobsOcO7gBscToB9pzjg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon.") drop-down menu of an in-flight envelope.

   ![A screenshot displays the Docusign Envelope Status drop-down menu with Send, View, Correct, and Void options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/DocuSign-Envelope-Status-dropdown-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyODIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.Ya24Md0nckyFO0_URz-ONrRlobsOcO7gBscToB9pzjg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Envelope Status drop-down menu with Send, View, Correct, and Void options.")

   A message displays: Success Your reminder was
   sent!

You have completed the process. Recipients with pending
assignments receive an email notifying them: (sender name) sent you a document
to review and sign.

title: Add In Person Signing to Envelope Template Recipients
heading_path: Add In Person Signing to Envelope Template Recipients
text:
Learn how to use the In Person Signing feature to create a multi-recipient signing
session, with all signers in the same room.

In Person signing creates a multi-recipient signing
session, where all signers must be in the same room. The host opens the envelope,
and then each signing recipient signs on the host's device.

The following
rules apply to In Person signing sessions:

- The host of the in-person signing session must be a Docusign eSignature for
  Salesforce user
- You can add an In Person Signer recipient anywhere in the signing order
- Other In Person Signer recipients do not need to be Docusign users
- You can add other recipients to the envelope that are not in person signers
  (Needs to Sign, Receives a Copy, and so on)
- You can set the signer name using Lookup Fields,
  Related Lists, Users/Contacts,
  and By Role
- You cannot use SMS delivery for In Person Signer
  sessions
- You can only use the Sign Now option if the In Person
  Signer is first in the signing order (serial or parallel)
- You can use [merge field syntax](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus) to complete the Host Name and Host Email
  Address fields

Example:

Your customer's president and head of finance
meet in your office to review a contract. You create an envelope for an In Person Signer
session. You host the session and guide your customers through the signing process. You
answer questions they have as they sign. You pass your tablet to each signer until all
have completed the session.

Use this procedure to add an In Person
Signer to an envelope template:

Note: You can also use this procedure on the
Recipients step when sending an envelope.

1. Select the name of the recipient you want to update

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
2. Complete the following fields in the Add Recipient dialog box:
   1. Action: Select In Person Signer from the dropdown menu.
   2. (Optional): Specify a host for the session:

      1. De-select the Make the Sender Host
         box.
      2. Host Name: Enter the name of the Docusign user who will host the In Person Signer session.
      3. Host Email: Enter the email address of the Docusign user who will host the In Person
         Signer session.

         Note: You can also [use merge field
         syntax](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus) to add a host name and email from Salesforce objects.
   3. (Optional) Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now): Select this check box if you want the in person signing session to begin as soon as the envelope is sent.
   4. Select Add.

   The Add Recipient dialog box closes.
3. (Optional): Add Recipient: Add more In Person Signer
   recipients to your template.
4. (Optional): + Signing Order: Set the order in which
   recipients sign the envelope.
5. Select the Save and Close button.

You have completed the process. You have added theIn
Person Signer option to your envelope template recipient.

title: Customize the Email Subject and Message Seen by Recipients
heading_path: Customize the Email Subject and Message Seen by Recipients
text:
You can customize the email subject and message to recipients by editing the envelope
template Recipients step.

Envelope templates have a pre-built email subject and
message for all recipients. You can customize the email subject and message to
recipients by editing the envelope template Recipients step.

Use this procedure to
change a message to recipients in an existing envelope template:

1. Customize any field:
   - Email Subject: Enter up to 100 characters of text
   - Email Message: Enter up to 2,000 characters of text
2. Select the Save and Close button at the top-right of the
   page.

   You have completed the process. You have customized the recipient
   message.

title: Rules for Editing Envelope Template Recipients
heading_path: Rules for Editing Envelope Template Recipients
text:
Learn about recipient settings that you can change when sending from an envelope
template.

A Docusign envelope template is a sending experience that your
administrator builds. When you select an envelope template button on a record, a
pre-built envelope opens, containing documents, recipients, and signature fields.
You can add to or edit the contents of the envelope.

If you want to change details of an existing envelope template recipient, Docusign applies the following rules:

title: Rules for Editing Envelope Template Recipients
heading_path: Rules for Editing Envelope Template Recipients
"From Salesforce" Recipients
text:
| Recipient field | Sender options |
| --- | --- |
| Action | Senders cannot change the selection. |
| Delivery | Senders cannot change the selection. |
| Delivery = Email + SMS | Senders cannot change the Delivery selection, but can edit the Phone Number field. The phone number merges with the phone number field from the new User or Contact. |
| User or Contact | Senders can delete or add users and contacts. |

title: Rules for Editing Envelope Template Recipients
heading_path: Rules for Editing Envelope Template Recipients
"By Name & Email" Recipients
text:
| Recipient field | Sender options |
| --- | --- |
| Action | Senders cannot change the selection. |
| Delivery | Senders cannot change the selection. |
| Delivery = Email + SMS | Senders cannot change the Delivery selection, but can edit the Phone Number field. |
| Name | Senders can edit the field. |
| Email Address | Senders can edit the field. |

title: Rules for Editing Envelope Template Recipients
heading_path: Rules for Editing Envelope Template Recipients
"Signing Group" Recipients
text:
| Recipient field | Sender options |
| --- | --- |
| Action | Senders cannot change the selection. |
| Select Group | Senders can delete or add a Signing Group. |

title: Delete a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Delete a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
text:
You can delete documents from an envelope template at any time.

You can delete documents from an envelope template at any time.

Warning: If you delete a document that
contains signature fields for one of the template recipients, those fields will need
to be re-added to another document in the template.

**Example**: You delete a
document containing **Sender 1**'s signature fields. The signature process
will fail unless **Sender 1** is removed from the envelope template
recipients list OR **Sender 1**'s fields are added to another document in the
template

Use this procedure to delete a document from your envelope template:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMjQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.k7rLlEseMgGroZVZzmx2XwnxIipiYQ7gjeR6e4aNHtQ&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu to the right of a document.
2. Select Delete from the options.

   ![Delete uploaded document](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatedocumentoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMjQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.k7rLlEseMgGroZVZzmx2XwnxIipiYQ7gjeR6e4aNHtQ&_LANG=enus "Delete uploaded document")

   The document disappears from the page.
3. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have deleted the uploaded
   document.

title: When I Save Changes to an Envelope Template, Multiple Headers Display on My Browser with the Message: “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”
heading_path: When I Save Changes to an Envelope Template, Multiple Headers Display on My Browser with the Message: “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”
text:
Learn to fix the issue of multiple browser headers displaying the error: “You Have
Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”.

When I save changes to an envelope template, my browser opens multiple headers, each
displaying the message: You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode
Enabled.

![A screenshot displays three browser bars with the message “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-three-browser-bars-with-the-message_-You-Have-Lightning-Components-Debug-Mode-Enabled.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.QjjXEUVboIkrnSQ09Z0M8ukx4SMjm_SlNnTBbw_UjqE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three browser bars with the message “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”.")

title: When I Save Changes to an Envelope Template, Multiple Headers Display on My Browser with the Message: “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”
heading_path: When I Save Changes to an Envelope Template, Multiple Headers Display on My Browser with the Message: “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”
Resolution: Disable Lightning Debug Mode in your Salesforce user settings
text:
Salesforce administrator

1. Log in to your Salesforce organization as an administrator.
2. Select Setup.
3. Enter and select Debug Mode in the Quick Find
   field.
4. Select the check box next to your user and choose
   Disable.

   You have completed the process. You have disabled Lightning Debug Mode for your
   user. The error messages no longer display when you save changes to Docusign envelope templates.

title: Change the Recipient Signing Order in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Change the Recipient Signing Order in an Envelope Template
text:
You can change the signing order of recipients in an envelope template. This lets you
change the order in which the envelope must be signed for the signature process to complete.

You can change the signing order of recipients in an envelope template. This lets you change the order in which the envelope must be signed for the signature
process to complete. You can drag recipients to a new order, or change their recipient numbers (1, 2, 3,
etc.) Envelope templates can also be configured to have recipients sign at the same time, or in no
particular order.

Use this procedure to change the recipient signing order in an existing envelope template:

Note: You can also use this procedure on the Recipients step when sending an
envelope.

1. Select the Signing Order button.
2. Pick an option:
   - **Drag recipients**: Select a recipient and drag it up or down in the list.
     ![Change signing order](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatechangesigningorder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.KM0Z3W8Wf8BnQOKpHg7XmacoDCsVppjiKglxrqK8aD0&_LANG=enus "Change signing order")
   - **Change recipient number**: Enter a new number in the field to the left of the recipient.
     ![Signing Order options](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Signing-Order-options.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczNjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.KM0Z3W8Wf8BnQOKpHg7XmacoDCsVppjiKglxrqK8aD0&_LANG=enus "Signing Order options")
3. Select the Save and Close button at the top-right of the
   page.

   You have completed the process. You have changed the recipient order.

title: Envelope Template Recipient Actions
heading_path: Envelope Template Recipient Actions
text:
Find out about the actions you can assign to the recipients of an envelope built with a
Docusign envelope template.

There are many different recipient actions that can be assigned to recipients in an envelope
or envelope template.

You can use the following Action types:

Table 1. Recipient Actions

| Action Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Needs to Sign | The recipient must sign the envelope. |
| Receives a Copy | The recipient receives a copy of the envelope. They do not have to take action. |
| Needs to View | The recipient must open and view the document. They do not have to sign. |
| In Person Signer | Conduct a multi-recipient signing session. The host signs, and then each additional recipient signs on the host's device.  All signers must be in the same room for the signing session. |
| Specify Recipients | The recipient can fill in the name and email address of other recipients in their stage of the signing order or later. |
| Allow to Edit | Grant another Docusign user full edit rights for an envelope. This recipient can edit the entire envelope, using the correct mode to make changes. The recipient is not assigned any fields. |
| Update Recipients | The recipient can fill in the name and email address of other recipients in their stage of the signing order or later. |
| Embedded Signer | The recipient can edit the details of any remaining recipients in the envelope. |

title: View In-Flight Envelopes From the Docusign Envelope Status Component
heading_path: View In-Flight Envelopes From the Docusign Envelope Status Component
text:
Learn to view the documents of an in-flight or completed envelope by using the correct
feature.

Using the view envelope feature, you can view the documents from an
in-flight or completed envelope.

View envelope allows you to view documents from a sent envelope.

Use this procedure to view the documents of an envelope:

1. Open a Salesforce record and locate the Docusign Envelope
   Status component.
2. Select View Envelope from the arrow ![A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyODYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xyIulvdc2lmpRqBUil3Cd8YtQoiTK19jXCB8UnFt0XU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon.") drop-down menu of an in-flight envelope.

   ![A screenshot of the Docusign Envelope Status component displays a drop-down menu with Send, View, Correct, and Void options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/DocuSign-Envelope-Status-dropdown-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyODYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xyIulvdc2lmpRqBUil3Cd8YtQoiTK19jXCB8UnFt0XU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Envelope Status component displays a drop-down menu with Send, View, Correct, and Void options.")
3. (Optional) OTHER ACTIONS: Take any action:

   - Void: Cancel the signature process for all
     recipients
   - Correct: Make changes to the documents, recipients,
     or signature fields of the envelope
   - Clone: Create a copy of the envelope
4. Select CLOSE.
5. Select Back in the upper left to return to the
   record.

You have completed the process. You viewed an in-flight envelope
from the Docusign Envelope Status component.

title: Envelope Creation Options: Documents Step
heading_path: Envelope Creation Options: Documents Step
text:
Learn about the options that you can use in the Documents step of the envelope
creation process.

As an envelope creator, you have many advanced options available to you. Each step of the
envelope creation process contains its own set of advanced options. In the
Documents step, you add documents to your envelope. These
documents form the basis of the envelopes your recipients sign. You can add
documents from Salesforce or from your device.

This guide demonstrates the advanced options that you can use in the
Documents step:

- [Upload documents from your device](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=jme1682397601214.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upload documents from your device to an envelope template.")
- [Rename an uploaded document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=phb1596147423215.html&_LANG=enus "You can change the file names of uploaded documents in the envelope template by following these steps.")
- [Preview an uploaded document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=lav1596147478944.html&_LANG=enus "You can preview uploaded documents in the envelope template by following these steps.")
- [Delete an uploaded document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=uqf1596147449716.html&_LANG=enus "You can delete documents from an envelope template at any time.")

title: Add Automatic AutoPlace Fields to Documents
heading_path: Add Automatic AutoPlace Fields to Documents
text:
Learn how to add AutoPlace fields to documents used in envelope templates.

You can add automatic AutoPlace fields to envelope template documents.

These fields merge Docusign fields into your envelope.

You do not need to create, configure, or modify any Docusign fields to use automatic
AutoPlace fields.

Use automatic AutoPlace fields for these signer fields:

- Signature
- Date Signed
- Initial
- Optional Initial

Note: Use [document custom AutoPlace
fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ryo1673648971228.html&_LANG=enus) or merge field AutoPlace fields for Name,
Company, Title, and other
text-based fields.

Follow this procedure to add AutoPlace fields to an envelope template document:

1. Select the name of the document you will edit.

   The document preview page opens.
2. Select the Download button in the upper-right.

   The document is saved to the downloads folder of your
   device.
3. Add AutoPlace fields to your template document:

   1. Open the document and move the cursor to where you will add AutoPlace
      fields.
   2. Enter the text for AutoPlace fields (\s1\ for
      Signer 1 signature, \n1\ for Signer 1 name, and
      so on.)![AutoPlace field examples](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/AutoPlace-field-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NjgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uiNVBEpTCJx4Nv4knhV93rVC1pIb1t1Rm46tEAWsFv8&_LANG=enus "AutoPlace field examples")
   3. Highlight the AutoPlace text and change the font color to match the page background. So,
      if the page is white, change the AutoPlace font to white.![AutoPlace fields with white font](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/AutoPlace-fields-with-white-font.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NjgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uiNVBEpTCJx4Nv4knhV93rVC1pIb1t1Rm46tEAWsFv8&_LANG=enus "AutoPlace fields with white font")

      Note: This prevents the AutoPlace text from
      being visible in your envelope. Your recipients only see the
      signature fields.
   4. Save the changes to your template document.
4. Replace your old template document with the new version:

   1. Return to the envelope template Documents
      page.
   2. Select the arrow drop-down menu next to the far-right of the document
      you updated.
   3. Select Delete. The document disappears.![Delete template document](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Delete-template-document.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0NjgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uiNVBEpTCJx4Nv4knhV93rVC1pIb1t1Rm46tEAWsFv8&_LANG=enus "Delete template document")
   4. Select the Upload button.
   5. Select the name of the updated file from your device.
   6. Select Open. The Upload File dialog box
      opens.
   7. Select the Done button.

   The dialog box disappears. The name of your updated document
   appears.
5. Select the Save and Close button in the
   upper-right.

   The envelope template page opens.

You have completed the process. You have added automatic AutoPlace
fields to your envelope template document. When you send an envelope from this template,
Docusign or Salesforce fields are automatically placed where you added AutoPlace fields.
The AutoPlace text is not visible to your recipients, because you colored it to match
the page. Your recipients only see the fields they need to complete.

title: Add Salesforce Files to a Docusign Negotiate Envelope
heading_path: Add Salesforce Files to a Docusign Negotiate Envelope
text:
Learn how to access the Add from Salesforce button in the Negotiate Send for Signature
experience.

Follow this procedure to access the Add from Salesforce button in the
Negotiate Send for Signature experience:

1. Open a Salesforce record and select the Negotiate Share
   button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/negotiateactionsarrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMDMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.1mO-S5mg-b714AWgrMO5WWWnzcCEIAh3XvA9bV6Jj4g&_LANG=enus)
   next to a file.
2. Select Send for Signature from the drop-down menu. The
   Send with Docusign page opens.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Send-for-eSignature.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczMDMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.1mO-S5mg-b714AWgrMO5WWWnzcCEIAh3XvA9bV6Jj4g&_LANG=enus)
3. Select Add from Salesforce.
4. Add a file using any method. You can add up to ten documents.

   - Upload Documents: Follow the prompts to add files from your
     device.
   - Owned by Me: Select check boxes to add files with you listed as
     Owner.
   - Shared with Me: Select check boxes to add documents from Salesforce
     files that others have shared with you.
   - Recent: Select check boxes to add documents from recently-viewed
     files.
   - Following: Select check boxes to add files that you are
     following.
   - Libraries: Open Salesforce Asset Libraries
     and select check boxes to add files.
5. Select the Add button.

   You have completed the process. You have added a Salesforce document to an envelope
   in the Docusign Negotiate experience.

title: Send an Envelope from the Salesforce Mobile App
heading_path: Send an Envelope from the Salesforce Mobile App
text:
Learn how to send a Docusign envelope from your mobile device using
the Salesforce mobile app.

You can send a Docusign envelope from your mobile device using the
Salesforce mobile app.

Note: If you see an error message, your envelope will not send. Tell your
Salesforce administrator if either error message displays:

- At least one document is required.
- Failed to send documents. No recipient found. Contact your
  administrator for help.

Use this procedure to send a Docusign envelope from the Salesforce mobile
app:

1. Open a Salesforce record.
2. Select the Docusign eSignature custom button at the top.

   Note: The button may be hidden in the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Salesforce-More-button.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMzcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.klZlX0iw2_MF_DB52486CuagAg1NY9QvgF9AFLBurPY&_LANG=enus)
   More menu.
3. A sending experience displays:

   - Send now: The envelope sends without input from
     you.
   - Quick send: [Complete the quick send experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=wji1611295987221.html&_LANG=enus).

You have completed the process. You have sent a Docusign envelope using the Salesforce mobile app. Docusign adds the signed document to the record when all recipients
complete the signature process.

title: Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”
text:
A workaround is needed when adding a Salesforce picklist merge field to an envelope
template.

A workaround is needed when adding a Salesforce picklist merge field to an envelope
template.

When you try to add that field to a document on the Place Fields page, the action fails. You
see an error message that reads: One or more of the dropdown fields
doesn't have any options.

This is because the Place Fields page is a Docusign account page, and can not access fields
from your Salesforce organization.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a Docusign envelope template as a Salesforce administrator.
2. Select the Place Fields arrow. The Place Fields page opens.
3. Select the Merge Fields menu in the left column.
4. Drag a Salesforce picklist field to the document.

**Result**: You see an error message that reads: One or more of the
dropdown fields doesn't have any options.

title: Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”
Resolution: Select the Back button and then the Save and Close button
text:
1. Select the Back button at the lower-right of the
   page. The Template Name and Description page
   opens.
2. Select the Save and Close button at the
   lower-right of the page.

   The picklist field is correctly added to the envelope template.
   This issue will be fixed in a future release.

title: Send an Envelope Using the Docusign Quick Send Envelope Template Experience
heading_path: Send an Envelope Using the Docusign Quick Send Envelope Template Experience
text:
The Quick Send signature experience gives you a faster way to send from an envelope
template. Use this procedure to send an envelope in the Quick Send experience.

You can use a Quick Send envelope template button to
create and deliver a Docusign envelope from Salesforce.

Documents, Recipients and
eSignature fields may already be present from the envelope template built by your
Salesforce Administrator.

Use this procedure to send an envelope using a
Quick Send envelope template:

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
2. Select the Quick Send button. The Send with Docusign page opens.

   ![Quick send button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Quick-Send-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.YE2bmnfCJvzX7T65eUliib44NtB1lGoE8zD_9j2qaUE&_LANG=enus "Quick send button")

   Note: Your administrator chooses the button name. It may be hidden in the arrow
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.YE2bmnfCJvzX7T65eUliib44NtB1lGoE8zD_9j2qaUE&_LANG=enus)
   menu.
3. (Optional):

   - Add Recipient:
     1. Select the Add Recipient button.
     2. Add a recipient using the From
        Salesforce, By Name and Email
        or Signing Group options.

        Note: You can only use the Receives a Copy
        option when adding a recipient in Quick Send.
   - Edit recipient details:
     1. Select a recipient name. The Add
        Recipient dialog box opens.
     2. Edit any active fields. [Follow
        these rules if you want to edit envelope template
        recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=uig1740079031042.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about recipient settings that you can change when sending from an envelope template.").
     3. Select the Select button. Any changes are
        saved. The dialog box closes.
   - View: See a diagram of the recipient signing
     order, from start to completion.
   - Message to Recipients:
     1. Email Subject: Enter a new subject for
        your recipient.
     2. Email Message: Enter a new message for
        your recipient.
   - More Options:
     - Reminders: Add a reminder to your recipients from the drop-down
       menu. Select Custom from the drop-down
       menu to add a custom reminder of up to 999 days.
     - Expiration: Set a date or a number of days (up to 999) after
       which the envelope expires.
4. Select the Send button at the upper-right of the
   page.

   A message opens: Sent for Signature. The record
   page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have created and sent an
envelope using the Quick Send experience.

title: Recipient Preview Actions: Viewing As and Form Factor View
heading_path: Recipient Preview Actions: Viewing As and Form Factor View
text:
You can use the Actions button on the Place Fields page to access a menu of Docusign and
Salesforce options.

You can use the Actions button on the Place Fields page to access a menu
of Docusign and Salesforce options.

Table 1. Recipient Preview Actions

| Button | Name | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Viewing As | Select from drop-down menu to preview as Owner or Recipient |
|  | Desktop View | Preview document as it would appear on a computer |
|  | Tablet View | Preview document as it would appear on a tablet |
|  | Mobile View | Preview document as it would appear on a mobile device |
|  | View Comments | Scroll to the bottom of document to view existing comments |
|  | Close | Close preview screen and return to Place Fields page |

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
text: Learn to use merge field syntax to add By Role recipients to an envelope template.

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Using By Role recipients
text:
Using the By Role recipient type, you can dynamically add recipients to
a Docusign envelope template. By Role allows you to add a placeholder recipient like
Signer 1. The sender can complete the recipient's details
when creating an envelope.

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Using merge field syntax for By Role recipients
text:
You can use merge field syntax to add By Role recipients and recipient details.

Example: You want a template to add a
recipient using any record's Contact field. You add a By Role
recipient with {!Contact.Name} and
{!Contact.Email} as the recipient details. A sender selects
the envelope template button, and Docusign adds the record's contact as a recipient.
![A screenshot displays a By Role recipient with Salesforce contact merge field syntax in the Name and Email Address fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210706-at-20211-PM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczODgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.xcbxzM2imrf5xTiH45f21QrLWrCZsgFxZCgecqwj0jg&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Common objects used with merge field syntax
text:
Administrators commonly use the following objects when creating merge field syntax:

- Users
- Contacts
- Leads
- Person Account
- Account owner when sending from Opportunities

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Using Lookup Fields
text:
You can use the Lookup Fields option to add recipients from
Users, Contacts, and Leads without using merge field syntax. You must use merge
field syntax when adding recipients from the same object as the custom button.

Example: The envelope template button
is on a Contact layout and is importing the name and email from a
contact.

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Finding merge field syntax
text:
You can copy Salesforce merge field syntax from the following locations:

- Object syntax: Setup >  Object Manager > API NAME
- Field syntax: Setup >  Object Manager >  (select object) >  Fields & Relationships  > FIELD NAME

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Using custom merge field syntax

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
User, Contact, and Lead examples
text:
| Merge field syntax | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `{!$User.FirstName}` | Specify a Salesforce user's first name |
| `{!$User.LastName}` | Specify a Salesforce user's last name |
| `{!$User.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce user's email address |
| `{!Contact.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce contact name |
| `{!Contact.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce contact email address |
| `{!Lead.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce lead name |
| `{!Lead.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce lead email address |

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Lookup Relationship examples
text:
| Merge field syntax | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `{!Account.Owner.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce Account owner name |
| `{!Account.Owner.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce Account owner email address |
| `{!Opportunity.Owner.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce Opportunity owner name |
| `{!Opportunity.Owner.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce Opportunity owner email address |
| `{!Opportunity.LastModifiedBy.Name}` | Specify the name of the Salesforce user who last modified a record |
| `{!Opportunity.LastModifiedBy.Email}` | Specify the email address of the Salesforce user who last modified a record |
| `{!Opportunity.Account.Owner.Name}` | Specify the name of an Opportunity account owner |
| `{!Opportunity.Account.Owner.Email}` | Specify the email address of an Opportunity account owner |
| `{!Opportunity.OpportunityContactRoles.Role}` | Specify the role of an Opportunity contact |

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Salesforce Person Account examples
text:
You can add Salesforce Person Account merge fields if you have any of these
Salesforce editions: Professional, Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and
Developer.

| Merge field syntax | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `{!Account.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce person account name |
| `{!Account.PersonEmail}` | Specify a Salesforce person account email address |
| `{!Account.PersonBirthdate}` | Specify a Salesforce person account birthday |
| `{!Account.PersonTitle}` | Specify a Salesforce person account title |
| `{!Account.IsPersonAccount}` | Determine whether you are working with a Salesforce person account or a business account |

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Access Code syntax
text:
You can add merge text to the recipient Access Code field to
create an access code using Salesforce data.

Example: You want to secure an envelope but
avoid the added risk of messaging an access code to a recipient. You add
`{!Contact.Last4SSN__c}` to the recipient's Access
Code field. When sending, Docusign creates an access code using the last
four digits of the recipient's Social Security number. You inform the recipient that the
access code is the last four digits of their Social Security number.

title: Create and Send an Envelope with Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: Create and Send an Envelope with Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
text:
Learn how to create and send an envelope with Docusign eSignature for
Salesforce.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that holds documents, recipients,
and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by completing the fields assigned to
them. Docusign marks the envelope as Signed when all recipients
complete their fields.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. Users can add
recipients and documents manually, from a template, or dynamically from Salesforce
records, users, or contacts. Docusign saves the document to the Salesforce record when
all recipients finish signing.

You can create and send an envelope with Docusign eSignature for Salesforce using any of
these methods:

[Standard Send with Docusign template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=gsh1675832324336.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to send an envelope from a Salesforce record using a standard Send with Docusign template.")
:   The Send with Docusign template is a pre-built, three-stage sending experience. An
    administrator does not build this template. Senders select a Send
    with Docusign button on a record and add documents, recipients,
    and signature fields.

[Use envelope templates with a standard Send with Docusign template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=lee1682365097112.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add envelope template content to a standard Send with Docusign envelope.")
:   Add existing envelope templates to your Send with Docusign envelope. Senders
    select a Send with Docusign button on a record and add
    templates from the Select Template button. The selected
    templates add documents, recipients, Envelope Custom Fields, and Salesforce and
    Docusign fields to the standard envelope.

[Default envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=zgh1596562751041.html&_LANG=enus "This guide demonstrates the process for sending a document for eSignature using an envelope template custom button.")
:   An administrator builds this three-stage sending experience. Senders may see documents,
    recipients, and signature fields in the envelope pre-added by the administrator.
    Senders can add to or edit the contents of the pre-built envelope.

[Quick Send envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=wji1611295987221.html&_LANG=enus "The Quick Send signature experience gives you a faster way to send from an envelope template. Use this procedure to send an envelope in the Quick Send experience.")
:   An administrator builds this one-stage sending experience. Senders may see documents,
    recipients, and signature fields pre-added by the administrator. Senders are
    unable to edit the pre-added documents or signature fields. Senders can edit the
    Reminders, Expiration,
    Email Subject, and Email
    Message fields. Senders can also add non-signing recipients
    (like Receives a Copy recipients) to the envelope.

[Place Fields envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=zet1644034684923.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a place fields envelope template button to create and send an envelope from a Salesforce record.")
:   An administrator builds this one-stage sending experience. Senders only see
    the Place Fields step, where they can add Docusign and
    Salesforce fields to the envelope. Senders can still change envelope
    settings and add documents and recipients on the Place Fields page. The
    Place Fields envelope template is the most versatile quick-sending template
    type. Senders can place fields and send or choose to add many other
    options.

title: Create and Send an Envelope with Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: Create and Send an Envelope with Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
text:
ients on the Place Fields page. The
    Place Fields envelope template is the most versatile quick-sending template
    type. Senders can place fields and send or choose to add many other
    options.

[Send Now envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=aql1644036603539.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use a send now envelope template button to create and send an envelope from a Salesforce record.")
