:   An administrator builds this one-stage sending experience. When senders select the envelope
    template button on a record, Docusign instantly sends a pre-built envelope. The
    sender does not see the envelope's contents and can not add to or change the
    envelope.

[In Person Signer envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=yyp1699651453088.html&_LANG=enus)
:   You build an envelope for a multi-recipient signing session, with all signers in
    the same room.

[Send from the Salesforce Mobile app](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=wdk1660587409378.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to send a Docusign envelope from your mobile device using the Salesforce mobile app.")
:   Send an envelope from a Docusign template using the Salesforce
    mobile app.

[Send from a mobile browser](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=jny1660579105302.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to send a Docusign envelope from a browser on your mobile device.")
:   Send an envelope from a Docusign template using the browser on your mobile device.

[Docusign Files action menu](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ygy1586134394050&topicId=zwk1586134361708.html&_LANG=enus)
:   The Send with Docusign option on the Docusign Files share
    ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/negotiateactionsarrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.-05LZP-VZtGP8MTQu5jbaBeJoZTLh4LVmPVCAXHDifE&_LANG=enus)
    menu launches the pre-built, three-stage experience. An administrator does not
    build this template. Senders add documents, recipients, and signature
    fields.

[Send an Envelope Using WhatsApp](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=mab1727461358374.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use WhatsApp to deliver an envelope to a recipient.")
:   Build a Docusign envelope and deliver it by WhatsApp.

title: eSignature for Salesforce UserTroubleshooting Guide

text:
Users learn to troubleshoot issues they are having with the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.

This guide helps users troubleshoot issues they are having with the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.

This guide contains the following troubleshooting topics:

title: eSignature for Salesforce UserTroubleshooting Guide
heading_path: eSignature for Salesforce UserTroubleshooting Guide
text:
n eSignature for Salesforce app.

This allows the envelope template to save on the Documents page and be edited later.")

This does not happen with every PDF file.")

You must first remove extra relationships, permission sets, and Apex class components from your organization.")

Then delete the Salesforce link in the Connect settings of your Docusign account.")

- [When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=lir1644253741567.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to work around the file size limit error in an envelope template.")

title: Recipient Sending Options: Access Code, In-Person Signing, Sign Now, SMS and More
heading_path: Recipient Sending Options: Access Code, In-Person Signing, Sign Now, SMS and More
text:
Learn how to add the role, access code, filter, private message, in person signing, SMS,
or sign now options when creating an envelope recipient.

You can add many options when creating an envelope or envelope template recipient.

Here are the option types and actions you can add to a recipient:

Table 1. Recipient Options

| Option Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add Role | Enter a placeholder title (like Approver) that allows the sender to specify a name and email address later. |
| Add Filter | Add recipients based on a set of conditions being met or not met. This option only appears when you select the Related Lists recipient type. |
| Make the Sender Host | The person who sends the envelope becomes the host of the in-person signing session. The host signs, and then each additional recipient signs on the host's device.  This option only appears when you select the In Person Signer action for a recipient. |
| Host Name | Enter the name of the Docusign user who will host the In Person Signer session. This field only appears when you uncheck the Make the Sender Host box. |
| Host Email | Enter the email address of the Docusign user who will host the In Person Signer session. This field only appears when you uncheck the Make the Sender Host box. |
| Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now) | The sender is asked to sign their own envelope immediately after sending it. This option only appears in the following cases:   - You select Sender as the recipient   type and Needs to Sign as the action - You select any other recipient type (Users/Contacts, By   Role, and so on) and In Person Signer   as the action |
| Delivery | Select this drop-down menu to show the Email and SMS/Email options. The Delivery field only appears for customers with Allow SMS Delivery and Signer can sign on a mobile device enabled.  Contact your Docusign account manager to enable these features. |
| Email | Send an envelope to a recipient by email. |
| Email + SMS | Send an envelope to up to four recipients by email and text message. You must enter a space between the country code and the phone number. |
| WhatsApp | Send an envelope to a recipient by email and WhatsApp message. |
| Add Access Authentication | Select this link to show the SMS/Phone and Access Code options. |
| SMS/Phone | Send an access code to a recipient's mobile device to add an extra layer of security to the signing process. |
| Access Code | Create a code that a recipient must enter to start the signing process. You must send the code to the recipient separately by email or another messaging platform. |
| Add Private Message | Send a message to just this recipient or group. |
| Add Name and Email | Enter the name and email address of a recipient. This option only appears when you select the By Role recipient type. |

title: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
heading_path: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
text:
Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or
Login IP Ranges to fix issues with documents and data not being
written back to Salesforce.

If your Docusign templates have writeback enabled and the process is not working, there are
two possible causes:

- The Salesforce organization has Multi-Factor Authentication for API
  Logins enabled on the user's' profile. This is incompatible with
  Docusign’s server-to-server API updates.
- Login IP Ranges are specified on the user's profile. This
  prevents API logins for the user from Docusign servers.

title: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
heading_path: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
Resolution 1: Disable Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0Sl8ZEJ6qZq_YpQ4gl1T54vkCZIx9tzRIAPU9CVaMIU&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Edit button.
4. Uncheck the Multi-Factor Authentication for API
   Logins box.
5. Select the Save button.

   The process is complete. Salesforce records are now updated when
   changes are made to Salesforce merge fields on your
   envelope.

title: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
heading_path: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
Resolution 2: Disable Login IP Ranges on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0Sl8ZEJ6qZq_YpQ4gl1T54vkCZIx9tzRIAPU9CVaMIU&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Login IP Ranges link at the top of
   the Profile page.
4. Select the Del link to the left of an IP
   range.
5. Select the OK button on the Are you
   sure you want to delete the IP Login Range dialog box.
6. Repeat the Del action for all remaining
   ranges.

   The process is complete. Salesforce records are now updated when
   changes are made to Salesforce merge fields on your
   envelope.

title: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
heading_path: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
Resolution 3: Add the relevant Docusign endpoint IP addresses to the allow-list
text:
1. [Add Trusted IP Ranges for Docusign Gen for
   Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=lip1592574174158.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus).
2. Add any other restricted IP addresses shown in the user’s login
   history.

   You have completed the process. You have now fixed the document writeback feature
   of your template.

title: Step 3: Add Docusign and Salesforce Fields and Send Your Envelope (Send With Docusign Button)
heading_path: Step 3: Add Docusign and Salesforce Fields and Send Your Envelope (Send With Docusign Button)
text:
The third step of the process involves placing Docusign and Salesforce fields into the
document text.

Follow this procedure to place Docusign and Salesforce fields
into the document text.

1. Add Docusign fields to the document:

   1. Select a Docusign field from the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatestandardsfields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.kyl-iAZgOm6bW4GWc-PB4UlRlSh1D4Bi3FeLfGBKk0E&_LANG=enus)
      Standard Fields menu in the left column.
   2. Drag the field to the document.![Add Docusign fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Add-DocuSign-fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.kyl-iAZgOm6bW4GWc-PB4UlRlSh1D4Bi3FeLfGBKk0E&_LANG=enus "Add Docusign fields")
2. Add Salesforce fields to the document:

   1. Select the Merge Fields button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatemergefields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.kyl-iAZgOm6bW4GWc-PB4UlRlSh1D4Bi3FeLfGBKk0E&_LANG=enus) from the left
      column. The Salesforce Merge Fields menu opens.
   2. Select a field from the menu. You can expand arrows to find more fields, or enter text in
      the Search Fields field.
   3. Drag the field to the document text.![Add Salesforce fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Add-Salesforce-fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.kyl-iAZgOm6bW4GWc-PB4UlRlSh1D4Bi3FeLfGBKk0E&_LANG=enus "Add Salesforce fields")
3. (Optional) Choose any option:

   - Preview: Select this button to see a preview of
     the document.
   - Add fields for more recipients:
     1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.kyl-iAZgOm6bW4GWc-PB4UlRlSh1D4Bi3FeLfGBKk0E&_LANG=enus) next to the recipient name in the
        upper-left of the page.
     2. Select another recipient from the drop-down menu.
     3. Drag Docusign fields for this recipient to the document
        text.

        Note: Each recipient's fields are
        represented by a separate color (yellow, blue, and so
        on.)![Switch recipients](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Switch-recipients.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.kyl-iAZgOm6bW4GWc-PB4UlRlSh1D4Bi3FeLfGBKk0E&_LANG=enus "Switch recipients")
4. Select the Send button in the upper-right of the
   page.

You have completed the process. You have built and sent an
envelope.

title: Host an In Person Signing Session on the Salesforce Mobile App
heading_path: Host an In Person Signing Session on the Salesforce Mobile App
text:
You can host an In Person signing session from the
Salesforce mobile app.

You can host an In Person signing session from the
Salesforce mobile app.

With In Person signing, a host guides a Docusign signing session. The host must be a
Docusign user. Signing recipients are required to be in the room with the host
during the session. The host launches the signing session from their device and
guides other signing recipients through the process.

Use this procedure to host an In Person signing session from the Salesforce mobile
app:

1. Open a Salesforce record.
2. Select the In Person signer envelope template button.

   Note: The button may be hidden in the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Salesforce-More-button.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyNDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.bNMWV1_TDlbbM6-QsD7ZrLdKyNACv7WNnuRAoQSppTY&_LANG=enus)
   More menu.
3. Complete all required fields and select Send.

   A message opens: Sent for Signature. The screen
   refreshes. The Security Requests from Sender page
   opens.
4. Sign the document as the host:

   1. Select Start on the Security Requests
      from Sender page.
   2. Follow the prompts to sign the document as the host.
   3. Select CONTINUE on the Request a
      Copy page.
   4. Select CONTINUE on the Pass
      Control page.
5. Pass the device to the first recipient and guide them through the signing
   process:

   1. Instruct the recipient to add or complete signature fields.
   2. Instruct the recipient to select FINISH.
   3. Instruct the recipient to select CONTINUE on the
      Pass Control page.
6. Repeat the previous step for each additional signing recipient.

   The page refreshes. You see the Salesforce record.

You have completed the process. You have hosted an In Person
signing session on the Salesforce mobile app.

title: An "INVALID_SESSION_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
heading_path: An "INVALID\_SESSION\_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
text:
I see a web service error: INVALID\_SESSION\_ID when listing layouts, publishing buttons,
or fetching documents from Salesforce when sending for signature.

I see a web service error: INVALID\_SESSION\_ID when I invoke a Salesforce API
command. The error displays when I execute the following commands:

- List layouts
- Publish buttons
- Fetch documents from Salesforce when sending for signature

title: An "INVALID_SESSION_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
heading_path: An "INVALID\_SESSION\_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
Resolution 1: Disable "Lock sessions to the IP address from which they originated"
text:
1. Access the Session Settings page as a Salesforce
   administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc1MzcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.bQ5hLmEXxqjIy-4R7VEexjEW-uvhPuFwhJsT6VYuk1w&_LANG=enus) in the upper right
      of the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Session Settings in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Session Settings link in the
      left navigation bar.
2. Uncheck the Lock sessions to the IP address from which they
   originated box under the Session
   Settings header.
3. Select the Save button.
4. Log out of Salesforce and log back in.
5. Retry your API transaction.

   You have completed the process. You have disabled the Lock sessions to the IP
   address from which they originated function in your
   Salesforce organization.

title: An "INVALID_SESSION_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
heading_path: An "INVALID\_SESSION\_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
Resolution 2: Add Docusign IP ranges to your allowlist
text:
1. [Add Docusign IP
   ranges to your Salesforce allowlist](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=lip1592574174158.html).
2. Add any other restricted IP addresses shown in the user’s login
   history.

   You have completed the process.
   INVALID\_SESSION\_ID errors no longer
   display.

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
text:
Learn to work around the duplicate files issue when sending Gen agreements for
signature.

I built a Gen template that is connected to an envelope template. When I send generated
agreements for signature, I see duplicate documents in the
Documents step.

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
Resolution 1: Uncheck the Boxes of Duplicate Salesforce Files Before Sending
text:
Cause: You selected the Attach documents from
Salesforce record check box on the envelope template
Documents page. Selecting this option in an envelope
template that is linked to a Gen template causes file duplication.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select a Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   Gen creates a new agreement, and opens a preview page.
2. Select Send for Signature.

   Your linked envelope template displays the
   Documents page.
3. De-select the check box for any duplicate Salesforce documents that
   display.
4. Proceed with the rest of the envelope creation process.

   You have completed the process. You have removed any duplicate Salesforce
   documents and sent the envelope.

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
Resolution 2: Uncheck the "Attach Documents from Salesforce Record" Box Before Sending
text:
Cause: You selected the Attach documents from
Salesforce record check box on the envelope template
Documents page.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select a Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   Gen creates a new document, and opens a preview page.
2. Select Send for Signature.

   Your linked envelope template displays the
   Documents page.
3. De-select the Attach documents from Salesforce
   record check box.
4. Proceed with the rest of the envelope creation process.

   You have completed the process. You have removed any duplicate
   Salesforce documents and sent the envelope.

title: Activate Your Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Activate Your Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or
eSignature for Salesforce user.

Your administrator can create a Docusign for Salesforce user for you. You
can access approved Docusign apps in Salesforce with this user.
Administrators can create these types of Docusign for Salesforce
users:

- CLM: Access CLM files, folders, and features (workflow, document
  generation, review, and so on.)
- Gen: Generate contracts and send them for signature.
- Negotiate: Send Docusign files for external
  review, internal approval, or signature.
- eSignature: Create envelopes and send them for signature.

You receive an activation email from Docusign when your administrator
creates your user. Activate your user to access Docusign apps in
Salesforce.

Use this procedure to activate your Docusign user:

1. Open the Account Activation email from Docusign. Check your spam
   folder if you don't see the message in your inbox.
2. Select Activate.
3. Follow the activation prompts on the Docusign
   Log In page:

   - Existing
     Docusign
      users:
     Enter your Docusign credentials and select Accept
     Invitation.
   - New
     Docusign
      users:
     Create a Password and Security Question,
     and select Activate.
4. Complete your Docusign authentication in Salesforce:

   Important: Your Docusign user is not
   active until you complete these steps.

   1. Select a Docusign component or custom button on a record and
      select Authorize.

      The Docusign Log In dialog box opens.
   2. Enter your Docusign credentials or select Company Login to log in with SSO credentials.
   3. Select Log In.
   4. Select Accept.
   5. Select Allow Access.
5. Enter your Salesforce credentials.

   Note: Salesforce may require you to select
   Authorize and enter your credentials twice.

You have completed the process. Your Docusign user is active. You can use approved Docusign apps
in Salesforce.

title: Add SMS Delivery to an Envelope Recipient
heading_path: Add SMS Delivery to an Envelope Recipient
text:
Learn how to add the Email + SMS option to a recipient in your envelope.

Use this procedure to add the Email + SMS option to a
recipient in your envelope.

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, etc.)
2. Select the envelope template custom button in the upper-right of the
   page.

   The button is named by your administrator. It may be hidden in the arrow
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczODYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.u5UCTxBz1fieMnAhBOu3O9bpk0umqbf7q7ADK7081i0&_LANG=enus) menu.

   ![Send with envelope template button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Send-with-Envelope-Template-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTczODYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.u5UCTxBz1fieMnAhBOu3O9bpk0umqbf7q7ADK7081i0&_LANG=enus "Send with envelope template button")

   The Documents page opens, displaying the documents that have been added by
   your administrator.
3. Select the Next button in the upper-right of the
   page.

   The Recipients page opens.
4. Select the name of a recipient.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
5. Delivery: Select the Email + SMS
   option from the drop-down menu.

   Note: Delivery only appears
   for Docusign customers with Allow SMS Delivery
   enabled in their account.
6. (Optional) Phone Number: If known, enter the recipient's
   phone number.

   Important: You must enter a space between the
   country code and the phone number.
7. Select Add.

   The Add Recipients dialog box closes.

You have completed the process. You have added theEmail
+ SMS option to a recipient in your envelope. When users send an
envelope from this template, the recipient receives a text message and an email. The
recipient can choose to open the envelope using either method. [Complete the sending process by adding Docusign and
Salesforce fields and sending your envelope.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xmx1596487118055.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and eSignature Tags are Missing
heading_path: Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and eSignature Tags are Missing
text:
When adding recipients to an Envelope Template in Docusign eSignature for Salesforce,
conflicts between Docusign Role and Salesforce Roles can cause problems.

When adding recipients to an Envelope Template in Docusign eSignature for Salesforce ,
conflicts between Docusign Role and Salesforce Roles can cause problems.

When a user prepares a document for signature using an Envelope Template that
contains recipients with both Salesforce Roles **and**
Docusign Roles, the
following can occur:

- Recipients' Docusign Roles are replaced with Salesforce Roles
- Docusign signature fields for those recipients are deleted from the Envelope
  Template

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Add a Docusign recipient to an Envelope Template.
2. Add a Salesforce recipient from a Related List (like
   OpportunityContactRole).
3. Save the Envelope Template.
4. Start a signature process with eSignature for Salesforce.
5. Advance to the Recipients step. Recipients have their Docusign Roles replaced with
   Salesforce Roles.

   Note: This only applies to recipients with Salesforce Roles
   (Decision Maker, Evaluator, etc.).
6. Advance to the Prepare and Send step. Docusign recipients' signature
   fields are missing.

   **Result**: Both the recipient's Docusign Role and
   eSignature tags are missing from the template.

title: Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and eSignature Tags are Missing
heading_path: Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and eSignature Tags are Missing
Resolution: Remove the Role field from the Salesforce Contact
text:
1. Visit the Salesforce record page containing the Contact Role.
2. Find the Contact Roles section.
3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.c_dS2gQlm8WwSwLxFran7VVkuAosAsAEEJpCKEPiMA4&_LANG=enus) to the left of the Contact to be
   edited.
4. Select Edit from the drop-down menu. The Edit
   Contact Role dialog box opens.

   ![Edit Salesforce Role](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Edit-Salesforce-Role.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.c_dS2gQlm8WwSwLxFran7VVkuAosAsAEEJpCKEPiMA4&_LANG=enus "Edit Salesforce Role")
5. Select the Role drop-down menu.
6. Select --None-- from the list.
7. Select the Save button.

   ![Clear Salesforce Role](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Clear-Salesforce-Role.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0ODEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.c_dS2gQlm8WwSwLxFran7VVkuAosAsAEEJpCKEPiMA4&_LANG=enus "Clear Salesforce Role")You are returned to the Contact Roles section. The
   Role field is now empty.

   The process is complete. The recipient's Salesforce Role no longer conflicts.
   Docusign Roles and signature fields remain as expected when using an
   Envelope Template to send for signature.

title: Create and Send an Envelope for an In Person Signing Session
heading_path: Create and Send an Envelope for an In Person Signing Session
text:
Learn to use the In Person Signer feature to create a multi-recipient signing session with all signers in the same room.

In Person signing creates a multi-recipient signing session, where all signers must be in the same room. The host opens the envelope and each signing recipient signs on the host's device.

The following rules apply to In Person signing sessions:

- The host of the in-person signing session must be a Docusign eSignature for Salesforce user
- You can add an In Person Signer recipient anywhere in the signing order
- Other In Person Signer recipients do not need to be Docusign users
- You can add recipients to the envelope that are not in person signers (Needs to Sign, Receives a Copy, and so on)
- You can set the signer name using Lookup Fields, Related Lists, Users/Contacts, and By Role
- You cannot use SMS delivery for In Person Signer sessions
- You can only use the Sign Now option if the In Person Signer is first in the signing order (serial or parallel)
- You can use [merge field syntax](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=psq1595994131623.html&_LANG=enus) to complete the Host Name and Host Email Address fields

Example:

Your customer's president and head of finance meet in your office to review a contract. You create an envelope for an In Person Signer session. You host the session and guide your customers through the signing process. You answer questions they have as they sign. You pass your tablet to each signer until all have completed the session.

Use this procedure to create and send an envelope for an In Person signing session:

1. Select a Send with Docusign button on a record. Your administrator may choose a different name for the button.
2. Complete the actions on the Documents page.

   1. Use any option to add documents to your envelope:
      - Documents: Select the check box for up to ten documents
      - Upload: Add a document from your device
      - [Add from Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qva1617752033779.html&_LANG=enus): Add documents from Salesforce Files
      - [Select Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=fne1682396685506.html&_LANG=enus): Add documents, recipients, and signature fields from Docusign envelope templates
   2. Select Next.

   The Recipients page opens.
3. Add an In Person Signer on the Recipients page. [Follow these rules if you want to edit envelope template recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=uig1740079031042.html&_LANG=enus).

   1. Select Add Recipient.
   2. Select a recipient type from the left column:
      - From Salesforce: Enter the name of a Salesforce user or contact
      - By Name & Email: Enter any name and email address
      - Signing Group: Enter the name of a signing group from your Docusign account
   3. Action: Select In Person Signer.
4. Choose a host for the signing session:

   - Make yourself the host: Leave the Make me the Host box selected
   - Make another recipient the host: Uncheck the Make me the Host box and enter a Host Name and Host Email in the fields that open

     Note: You can also [use Salesforce merge fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus) to dynamically add a host name and email from standard and custom Salesforce objects.
   - (Optional) Add recipeint options: [Recipient Sending Options: Access Code, In Person Signing, Sign Now, SMS, and More](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xch1620151662151.html&_LANG=enus).
5. Select Select and Next.

   The Prepare & Send page opens.
6. Complete the actions on the Prepare & Send page:

heading_path: Create and Send an Envelope for an In Person Signing Session
text:
m/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xch1620151662151.html&_LANG=enus).
5. Select Select and Next.

   The Prepare & Send page opens.
6. Complete the actions on the Prepare & Send page:

   1. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatestandardsfields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMzIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0K57oMXAUs8j0rJKl8_TB83Ruidnt__9gKA4JeA6T8Y&_LANG=enus) Standard Fields: Drag Docusign fields to the document.
   2. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatemergefields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMzIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0K57oMXAUs8j0rJKl8_TB83Ruidnt__9gKA4JeA6T8Y&_LANG=enus) Merge Fields: Drag Salesforce fields to the document.
   3. (Optional) Select another recipient from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMzIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0K57oMXAUs8j0rJKl8_TB83Ruidnt__9gKA4JeA6T8Y&_LANG=enus) menu in the upper left and drag Docusign fields to the document.

      Note: Each recipient's fields are a different color (yellow, blue, and so on.)

      ![Switch recipients](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Switch-recipients.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMzIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.0K57oMXAUs8j0rJKl8_TB83Ruidnt__9gKA4JeA6T8Y&_LANG=enus "Switch recipients")
   4. Select SEND.

You have completed the process. You created and sent an envelope for an In Person signing session.

title: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User
heading_path: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher
apps.

You can change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher apps by changing the
language settings for your Salesforce user.

1. Access the Salesforce Language & Time Zone settings:
   1. Select the Salesforce user icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/salesforceusericon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxOTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.JPjoy0hcA74xCJ8dmy4yxA25IQ48l90CAGds6xvt05s&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Settings.
   2. Select Language & Time Zone.
2. Language: Select a language from the dropdown menu.
3. Select Save.

   A message displays: Your settings have been successfully
   saved.
4. Empty your browser cache and restart the browser.

You have completed the process. Docusign Apps Launcher content displays in the language that you
chose.

title: Use a Place Fields Envelope Template Button to Send an Envelope From a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Use a Place Fields Envelope Template Button to Send an Envelope From a Salesforce Record
text:
Learn how to use a place fields envelope template button to create and send an envelope
from a Salesforce record.

You can use a place fields
envelope template button to create and deliver a Docusign envelope from
Salesforce.

Your administrator builds place fields envelope templates and adds
custom buttons to your Salesforce layouts.

Place fields templates are
pre-built with documents and recipients already added. This speeds up the envelope
creation and sending process.

You only have to add Docusign and Salesforce
fields to the envelope before sending.

Use this procedure to send an
envelope using a place fields envelope template:

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
2. Select the place fields envelope template button. The place fields page
   opens.

   ![Place Fields envelope template button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Place-Fields-envelope-template-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus "Place Fields envelope template button")

   Note: Your administrator
   chooses the button name. It may be hidden in the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus) menu.
3. **(Optional)** Follow these steps if you need to add Docusign fields:

   1. Select the Standard Fields tab ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatestandardsfields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus) in the left
      column. The Docusign Fields menu opens.
   2. Select a Docusign field (Signature,
      Initial, and so on.)
   3. Drag the field to the document.![Add Docusign fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Add-DocuSign-fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus "Add Docusign fields")
4. **(Optional)** Follow these steps if you need to add Salesforce
   fields:

   1. Select the Merge Fields button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/envelopetemplatemergefields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus) from the left
      column. The Salesforce Merge Fields menu opens.
   2. Select a field from the menu. You can expand arrows to find more fields,
      or enter text in the Search Fields bar.
   3. Drag the field to the document.![Add Salesforce fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Add-Salesforce-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus "Add Salesforce fields")
5. (Optional):

title: Use a Place Fields Envelope Template Button to Send an Envelope From a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Use a Place Fields Envelope Template Button to Send an Envelope From a Salesforce Record
text:
.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus "Add Salesforce fields")

     fields for more recipients:
     1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus) next to the recipient name in the upper-left of

     2. Select another recipient from the drop-down menu.
     3. Drag Docusign fields for this recipient to the document.

        Note: Each recipient's fields are represented by a separate color
        (yellow, blue, and so on.)![Switch recipients](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Switch-recipients.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9.uvVSzZyyj2C9141RyPyF1BGWGZlz0ujlH5zZG5Qw8UQ&_LANG=enus "Switch recipients")
6. Select the Send button at the upper-right of the

envelope using a place fields envelope template.

title: Create an Envelope from an Envelope Template Button
heading_path: Create an Envelope from an Envelope Template Button
text:
This guide demonstrates the process for sending a document for eSignature using an
envelope template custom button.

This guide demonstrates the process for sending a document for eSignature using an Envelope
Template custom button. Your administrator builds envelope templates to simplify the sending
process for you. When you select an envelope template button on a record, your envelope may
contain documents, recipients and signature fields. These options were added by your
Salesforce administrator.

You can build an envelope from an envelope template by completing the following steps:

- [Step 1: View or Add Documents (Envelope Template
  Button)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=mdb1596562820671.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Step 2: View or Add Recipients (Envelope Template
  Button)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=htx1596574100545.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Step 3: View or Add [companyname] and Salesforce
  Fields and Send the Envelope (Envelope Template Button)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=hke1596562836794.html&_LANG=enus)

Note:
[Follow these rules when editing envelope
template recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=uig1740079031042.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Send a Document for eSignature Using the Docusign Negotiate Action Menu
heading_path: Send a Document for eSignature Using the Docusign Negotiate Action Menu
text:
This guide demonstrates the process for sending a Docusign Negotiate for Salesforce
document for eSignature using Docusign eSignature for Salesforce.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that contains documents, recipients, and
signature fields. Recipients complete the fields assigned to them to continue the signature
process. When all recipients have completed their assigned fields, the signing process is
complete. The signed document is saved to the Docusign account.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. You can add
recipients and documents manually, or dynamically from Salesforce records, users, or contacts.
When all recipients have completed their assigned fields, the signed document is saved to the
Salesforce record.

You can send a document for eSignature from the Docusign Negotiate action menu. Negotiate
files are found in the Docusign Files section of a Salesforce record.

The Negotiate sending experience has three steps:

- [Step 1: Select Documents for Your Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=bea1596472476377.html&_LANG=enus "Step one of the signature process involves selecting the content that will be loaded into the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce envelope.")
- [Step 2: Add Recipients to Your Envelope (Envelope Template)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=dfp1596477842847.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to add recipients to the envelope. You can add recipients from your Salesforce organization, from Docusign or by entering a name and email address.")
- [Step 3: Add Docusign and Salesforce Fields and Send Your Envelope (Send With Docusign Button)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=xmx1596487118055.html&_LANG=enus "The third step of the process involves placing Docusign and Salesforce fields into the document text.")

  Use this procedure to send a document for
  signature using the Docusign Negotiate action menu:

title: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
heading_path: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
text:
There is an intermittent issue with adding PDF files to the Documents step of the
envelope template creation process. This does not happen with every PDF file.

I sometimes see an error message when I try to upload a PDF file to my envelope template:
The Validation of the PDF File Failed.

This does not happen with every PDF file.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Upload a PDF file to an envelope template at the Documents step. The document fails to
   load.
2. An error displays: The validation of the PDF file
   failed

   **Result**: The PDF is not added to the envelope
   template.

   Note: Envelope templates currently
   support PDF file sizes of 3MB or smaller. This limit will be increased in a
   future release.

title: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
heading_path: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
**Resolution 1: Upload the PDF file to the envelope template in your connected Docusign account**
text:
1. Select the Save and Close button for the
   envelope template in your Salesforce organization.
2. Log in to your Docusign account on another browser tab.
3. Select the Templates tab.

   The My Templates page opens.
4. Select the envelope template's name.

   The template's page opens.
5. Select the Edit button.

   A page with the template settings opens.
6. Select the Upload button.
7. Upload the PDF file from your device.
8. Select the SAVE AND CLOSE button.

   ![Save and close changes to your Docusign template](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/iqc1594156517921/page/images/Save-and-close-changes-to-your-DocuSign-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTc0OTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXFjMTU5NDE1NjUxNzkyMSJ9._9aDZaBZa53d1R4jsoR3K7DTfwBEtqW6vwyJiTsxb6Q&_LANG=enus "Save and close changes to your Docusign template")

   You have completed the process. You have added the PDF file that
   was too large to add to the template in Salesforce. This document is
   included in all envelopes that are sent from a Salesforce record using
   this template.

   Important: If you later
   edit this envelope template in Salesforce, you will get an error and
   will be unable to make additional changes.

title: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
heading_path: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
**Resolution 2: Upload the PDF file to Salesforce first and then attach it when creating an Envelope**
text:
1. Upload the PDF file to Notes and Attachments in
   a Salesforce record.
2. Complete the Envelope Template creation process.
3. Select the Envelope Template custom button from a Salesforce
   record.
4. Select the Add from Salesforce button. The Add
   from Salesforce dialog box opens.
5. Check the box for the PDF saved in Notes and Attachments.
6. Select the Add button.
7. Complete the envelope sending process.

   The process is complete. The PDF file that could not be added to
   the Envelope Template is added from Notes and Attachments during the
   sending process.

title: Refresh the Products Provisioned to Your Docusign Account with the TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Refresh the Products Provisioned to Your Docusign Account with the TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn to activate and update Docusign for Salesforce apps and settings
using the Refresh Products feature.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

You can activate and update apps and settings in your Salesforce organization using the
Refresh Products feature.

- Switch any newly added products like Docusign Gen from
  Pending to Active
- Update the Account Products Last Checked custom setting
- Keep account feature settings up to date

Follow this procedure to use the Refresh Products feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MDEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.KDrmzOsW_d8LZHE4Yq-mbz-Ji-xqPio5LnPepRZxV6E&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MDEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.KDrmzOsW_d8LZHE4Yq-mbz-Ji-xqPio5LnPepRZxV6E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.
4. Refresh Products: Select Execute.

   The page refreshes. A message opens: Successfully refreshed
   products on Docusign account.

You have completed the process. You have updated settings and
activated pending apps.

title: Change the Owner of an Envelope Template
heading_path: Change the Owner of an Envelope Template
text:
Learn to change the owner of envelope templates.

Use this procedure to change the owner of an envelope template:

1. Access an envelope template as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NDEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MAfaTSwMLEM7lr22V7JfekhjF9jqkRUYtnZoWOxhTFw&_LANG=enus) and choose
      View All.
   2. Select the Docusign Apps Launcher tile.
   3. Select the Docusign envelope templates tab.
   4. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NDEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MAfaTSwMLEM7lr22V7JfekhjF9jqkRUYtnZoWOxhTFw&_LANG=enus) next to the Gen template to be edited.
   5. Select Change Owner from the options.

      The Change Owner dialog box displays.
2. Complete the following:
   - Search People: Enter the name of a different
     Salesforce user.
   - (Optional) Send notification email: Check this box to
     notify the new template owner.
3. Select Submit.

   ![A screenshot displays the Change Owner dialog box with Test User highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplatechangeowner.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NDEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MAfaTSwMLEM7lr22V7JfekhjF9jqkRUYtnZoWOxhTFw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Change Owner dialog box with Test User highlighted.")

   A message displays: (new user) now owns the record for (template
   name).

You have completed the process. You have changed the template
owner.

title: Use Manage App Policies to Allow or Prevent Docusign User Access
heading_path: Use Manage App Policies to Allow or Prevent Docusign User Access
text:
Learn how to use Manage App Policies to allow or prevent users
from authorizing their Docusign access.

New Docusign app users see an Authorize button when
they access Docusign content on a record. New users must enter
their Salesforce and Docusign credentials to access Docusign apps. Administrators can access the Manage App
Policies page to allow or prevent users from self-authorizing.

Use this procedure to manage your Docusign app policies:

1. Select Manage App Policies.

   ![A screenshot displays the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page with the Docusign Manage App Policies link highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Salesforce-Manage-App-Policies-link.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3ODUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.t2CK3Y-t9poTG5JvvlWFy36SIDicpZ62rQNbCq_0tak&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page with the Docusign Manage App Policies link highlighted.")
2. Select Edit Policies.
3. Permitted Users: Select an option from the dropdown
   menu:

   - All users may self-authorize: Users may select the
     Authorize button to use Docusign apps.
   - Admin approved users are pre-authorized: Administrators assign Docusign app access to specific groups.

     Note: Enabling the Admin approved users are
     pre-authorized option denies access to all current
     users. Administrators use Salesforce profiles to assign
     access.
4. Select Save.

   The page closes. The Docusign Connected App Detail
   page displays.

You have completed the process. You have allowed or prevented
users from authorizing their Docusign access in your Salesforce
organization.

title: Add the Envelope Status Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Envelope Status Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add the Docusign Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout.

Docusign  Envelope Status is a component that you can add to your Salesforce layouts. The component displays the progress of all Docusign envelopes sent from a record. The component displays envelopes sent using the following methods:

- A Send with Docusign action
- An Envelope Template action
- [A Start with Docusign workflow button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskcae26f13-f2e5-4afd-85a7-145a70b12977.html&_LANG=enus)
- [An Agreement Desk for Salesforce request](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk151c1542-bdf4-4c8e-ba20-37affb7adc7e.html&_LANG=enus)
- [eSignature actions in Salesforce Flows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzi1705696883404.html&_LANG=enus)

Salesforce users with Docusign access can view and act on envelopes without logging on to Docusign.

Tip: This procedure is explained in this [short how-to video](https://docusign.gallery.video/watch?videoId=6250567558001).

Use this procedure to add the Docusign Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays the a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.1nuGSqRBudLuQAbvJCVwLdEIKDQfBYcuok7Lz2QIWOg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose Edit Page from the dropdown menu.
2. Drag the Docusign Envelope Status component to the page layout on the right.

   ![A screen capture displays a cursor dragging the Docusign Envelope Status component to an Opportunity layout and saving the change.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Add-the-DocuSign-Envelope-Status-component.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.1nuGSqRBudLuQAbvJCVwLdEIKDQfBYcuok7Lz2QIWOg&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays a cursor dragging the Docusign Envelope Status component to an Opportunity layout and saving the change.")
3. Activate the change to your layout:
   1. Select Activation.
   2. Select a tab to set your default options: Org Default, App Default, or App, Record Type, and Profile.
   3. Select Assign and choose options from the dialog box.
   4. Select Close.
4. Select Save.
5. Select the back arrow ![A screenshot displays the Salesforce Edit Page Back arrow icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.1nuGSqRBudLuQAbvJCVwLdEIKDQfBYcuok7Lz2QIWOg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Salesforce Edit Page Back arrow icon.") to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the Docusign Envelope Status component to the layout. Users with Docusign access can view and act on Docusign envelopes.

title: Envelope Template Recipient Types
heading_path: Envelope Template Recipient Types
text:
You can add many different recipient types to an envelope template. You can add
recipients from Salesforce, from Docusign, or manually enter them.

You can add many different recipient types to an envelope or envelope template. You can add
recipients from Salesforce, from Docusign, or manually enter them.

You can add the following recipient types:

Table 1. Recipient Types

| Recipient Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Lookup Fields | Choose Lookup Fields when you want to automate the adding of recipients. Example: If you choose **Owner**, the name and email address of a record owner is automatically added.  Only fields related to the template Salesforce data source (Account, Opportunity, and so on) will appear. |
| Related Lists | You can choose fields to relate to a Salesforce object. Example: Contacts related list for an Account, OpportunityContactRole for an Opportunity, and so on.  This recipient type dynamically adds recipients from a Related List. Only fields previously related to the template data source (Account, Opportunity, and so on) appear.  You can only add standard Salesforce related lists, like CaseContactRoles, OpportunityContactRoles, and so on.  Custom Related Lists do not appear in results. |
| Users/Contacts | You can add a specific user from your Salesforce Users or Contacts. This user is not added dynamically. |
| Sender | Add the sender of the envelope to the recipient list. This sender is the person who selects the envelope template custom button on a record.  To add a Sender recipient, you must select +Add Role and enter a role, like Sender 1. |
| By Role | Add a generic recipient (ex: Approver) as a placeholder, if you do not yet know the names or types of recipients to be added. The sender can then add a recipient name or type during the sending process.  You can also add a specific recipient's name and email here.  You can use custom syntax to dynamically add a By Role recipient from a Salesforce object. |
| **Signing Group** | Add a signing group from your organization's Docusign account. Any signer in the group can complete the signature process for that group. |

title: Place Fields Actions: Document Settings and Upload a Document
heading_path: Place Fields Actions: Document Settings and Upload a Document
text:
Learn about using the menu of
actions for the Place Fields step.
