title: Place Fields Actions: Document Settings and Upload a Document
heading_path: Place Fields Actions: Document Settings and Upload a Document
Upload a Document menu
text:
The Edit Documents option opens the Upload a Document page,
where you can edit or upload content.![A screenshot displays document options, including Set as Supplement, Replace, Download, Delete, Rename, and View.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Upload_a_doc.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNDAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.dHBtxQPwvyeo3Pxw0wcxWKrmT3CD0hi7oNW0OuuuS-s&_LANG=enus)

Table 2. Upload a Document Page Options

| Menu Item | Action |
| --- | --- |
| **Get From Cloud** | Add documents from cloud storage (Google Drive, Box, Dropbox, OneDrive, and so on.) |
| **Upload** | Add documents from your device. |
| **More Options** | Menu for: Set as Supplement, Replace, Download Document, Rename Document, Delete Document, View Document. |
| **Set as Supplement** | Switch documents from primary (active documents in the signing process) to supplementary (passive material). |
| **Replace** | Remove the document and upload a replacement from your device. |
| **Download Document** | Download a copy of the document to your device. |
| **Rename Document** | Change the document name. |
| **Delete Document** | Remove the document from the template. |
| **View Document** | Open a preview of the document. |

title: Create, Edit, Delete, or Download an Envelope Template from Docusign Setup
heading_path: Create, Edit, Delete, or Download an Envelope Template from Docusign Setup
text:
Learn how to create, edit, or delete a Docusign envelope template from
the Docusign Setup page.

When you open the Docusign Setup page, a list of controls displays. One of
these controls is the Templates tab on the eSignature
Configuration page. You can use the Templates tab to create, edit, or delete
envelope templates.

Envelope templates are pre-built sending experiences. Administrators add documents,
recipients, Docusign fields, and Salesforce fields to templates.

Users select an envelope template button on a record and launch your pre-built sending
experience.

Use this procedure to create, edit, or delete an envelope template from the Docusign Setup page.

1. Select eSignature in the left column.

   The eSignature Configuration page displays the
   Templates tab.
2. Make any selection:
   - [Create Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus): Select this button to create a new envelope template.
   - [Link Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=rwo1660537941538.html&_LANG=enus): Select from the Create Template![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NzIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ar68b7D8PGviSiOHlRXCymlPvfTUwywFZEe9mZSRrI8&_LANG=enus) arrow menu to create a new envelope template by linking to existing templates.
   - Edit: Select the arrow next to a template and choose Edit from the dropdown menu.

     The envelope template displays for editing.
   - Delete: Select the arrow next to a template and choose Delete > Delete.
   - Download: Select the arrow next to a template and choose Download from the dropdown menu.

   Docusign downloads the envelope template to your device. You can [upload the template to Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uhn1664569454728.html&_LANG=enus) and use or edit the template there.

You have completed the process. You have created, edited, deleted,
or downloaded a template from the Templates tab from the Docusign Setup page.

title: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to open the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool in your Salesforce
organization.

title: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
Before you begin
text:
You need Salesforce administrator and Docusign administrator
permissions.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

Use this procedure to open the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3ODQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.0UnF3KgNuy9vtY7G17YLkBKSmnnsP0r7rU6rAH1_rVA&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3ODQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.0UnF3KgNuy9vtY7G17YLkBKSmnnsP0r7rU6rAH1_rVA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

You have completed the process. You have opened the Docusign Apps
Launcher TroubleShooting tool. You can use this tool to execute account-wide changes.

title: Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”
text:
Set account defaults to Allow sender to override account defaults
to fix this. This allows the envelope template to save on the Documents page and be edited
later.

In the Envelope Template Creation process, selecting the Save and Close
button on the Documents page results in an error: "You are not authorized to access
the envelope." This error occurs only on the Documents page. Selecting Save and
Close on the pages for Recipients, Place Fields, Options, and Custom Button do not
result in errors.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
2. Click the New button in the upper-right of the page.
3. Enter a template name and a standard object (Accounts, Opportunities, etc.)
4. Immediately click the Save and Close button on the Documents
   page.

   **Result**: An error message reads: You are not
   authorized to access the envelope. The user is unable to
   proceed with the template creation process.

title: Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”
Resolution: Enable "Allow sender to override account defaults" in the connected Docusign account
text:
1. Log in to the connected Docusign account as an Administrator.
2. Select the Admin tab.
3. Select Reminders and Expiration in the left
   column.
4. Select Allow senders to override account
   defaults.
5. Days before request expires: Enter any number
   higher than zero.

   Note: If the Days before request
   expires: field is blank or has a value of zero, you
   cannot save settings changes.

   ![A screenshot displays the Admin page with the Allow senders to override account defaults option selected and the Expiration field set to 31 days.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/DAL_esign_troubleshooting_admin_reminders_and_expiration.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NzgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.R0lYxxxeB1UBsY5Qs1ncmIlnf7f4eSgc5mmB2F4jGrE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Admin page with the Allow senders to override account defaults option selected and the Expiration field set to 31 days.")
6. Select Save Changes.

   A message displays: Changes saved. You have completed the process. You have set the account defaults to Allow
   senders to override account defaults. You can save
   envelope template settings on the Documents page and move to the
   Recipients page.

title: Place Fields Actions: Copy, Paste, Undo, Redo, and Zoom
heading_path: Place Fields Actions: Copy, Paste, Undo, Redo, and Zoom
text:
You can use the action menu on the Place Fields page to take actions on signature fields that are placed on the document.

You can use the action menu on the Place Fields page to take actions on signature fields that are placed on the document.![A screenshot displays the place fields action menu, with undo, redo, copy, paste, zoom, and enable field assist buttons.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-place-fields-action-menu-with-undo-redo-copy-paste-and-zoom-buttons-1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxMzAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.TLjDnpMYXtyG6PhxqJTqZvISXcmxqEGHn5Oa1cSVKNc&_LANG=enus)

Table 1. Place Field Actions

| Button | Button Name | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Undo | Undo the last action taken on the field. |
|  | Redo | Redo the last action taken on the field. |
|  | Copy | Copy the selected field. |
|  | Paste | Paste the selected field. |
|  | Zoom | Zoom in and out on the signature page. |
|  | Enable Field Assist | Turn on the Field Assist/Auto-Tagging feature for your eSignature account. |

title: Use the Docusign Status Tab to View the Status of an Envelope
heading_path: Use the Docusign Status Tab to View the Status of an Envelope
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign Status tab to view the status of your Docusign
envelopes.

Docusign envelopes are bundles of information sent to recipients for
electronic signature. Envelopes include documents, recipients, and Docusign and Salesforce fields.

The Docusign Status tab lets you view the status of your Docusign envelopes. Envelope status entries include these
details:

- Envelope #
- Docusign envelope ID
- Sender Name
- Sender Email
- Email Subject
- Status
- Owner
- Sent
- Expires
- Completed
- Declined or Voided Reason
- Time to Complete
- Created By
- Last Modified By
- Related Objects

Use this procedure to view the status details of a Docusign envelope in your
Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Status tab.
2. Select an envelope from the Envelope # column.

   The Docusign Envelope details page opens for that
   item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the status details of an
envelope sent from a Salesforce record.

title: I See an “Object Reference Is Not Set to an Instance of an Object” Error When Invoking Salesforce APIs
heading_path: I See an “Object Reference Is Not Set to an Instance of an Object” Error When Invoking Salesforce APIs
text:
I see a web service error: "Object Reference is not set to an instance of an object"
when listing layouts, publishing buttons, or fetching documents from Salesforce when sending
for signature.

I see a web service error: "Object Reference is not set to an instance of an object"
when I invoke a Salesforce API command.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Invoke one of the following Salesforce API commands:
   - List layouts
   - Publish buttons
   - Fetch documents from Salesforce when sending for signature

   **Result**: I see an error message: "Object Reference is
   not set to an instance of an object". I am unable to execute
   my API command.

title: I See an “Object Reference Is Not Set to an Instance of an Object” Error When Invoking Salesforce APIs
heading_path: I See an “Object Reference Is Not Set to an Instance of an Object” Error When Invoking Salesforce APIs
Resolution: Disable the "Lock sessions to the IP address from which they originated" function in Salesforce
text:
1. Access the Session Settings page as a Salesforce
   administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3NDEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.4LR905BM2Xnt3_0dRswZ4MR3jrq0esG93Bd5LSDo3_E&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Session Settings in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Session Settings link in the
      left navigation bar.
2. Uncheck the Lock sessions to the IP address from which they
   originated box under the Session
   Settings header.
3. Select the Save button.
4. Log out of Salesforce and log back in.
5. Retry your API transaction.

   You have completed the process. You have disabled
   theLock sessions to the IP address from which they
   originated function in your Salesforce
   organization.

title: Step 1: Add a Template Name and Select the Account Object
heading_path: Step 1: Add a Template Name and Select the Account Object
text:
The first step of the process involves naming the template and selecting the Salesforce
Data Source.

Use this procedure to name the template and select the Salesforce Data Source.

Tip:
[Step 1 is explained in full at the beginning of this
video](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6171917923001).

Important:
Person
Account does not display in the list of available objects in this
step. You must select the Account object here to use the
Person Account object later in the process.

1. Select the Create Template button at the far-right of
   the page. The Create Template dialog box opens.
2. Complete the following:
   1. Name: Enter a descriptive template name
   2. Select Data Source: Select Account from the dropdown menu.
   3. Select the Next button.

title: Step 1: Add a Template Name and Select the Account Object
heading_path: Step 1: Add a Template Name and Select the Account Object
Next steps
text: You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: Add Documents to Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hya1596146614785.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add documents to your envelope template.").

title: Customize eSignature for Salesforce on the Settings Page
heading_path: Customize eSignature for Salesforce on the Settings Page
text:
Additional options are available for customizing the eSignature for Salesforce app in
the Docusign Setup tab.

Additional options are available for customizing the
eSignature for Salesforce app in the Docusign Setup page:

[Enable Built-In eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=kor1611642110382.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to enable Docusign as your eSignature app or disable it to use another app.")
:   When you enable this feature, users can send Docusign envelopes from
    records. Disable this feature if you want to use another app for your electronic
    signatures.

[Enable System Sender](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jxz1611642321527.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to enable a System Sender so that non-Docusign users can send and sign envelopes.")
:   When you enable this feature, non-Docusign users can send and sign
    envelopes. You save a Docusign administrator's credentials as your
    System Sender. Docusign uses these credentials to send envelopes on
    behalf of non-Docusign users. The System Sender's username displays
    as the sender to all envelope recipients.

Important:

- The Enable System Sender option only works with the [Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Apex
  Toolkit](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/salesforce/salesforce101/apex-toolkit/).
- Add non-Docusign users to the **Docusign Sender** permission set
  to let them send envelopes through the System Sender.

title: Link a Gen Template to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Link a Gen Template to an Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to link a Docusign Gen template to a Docusign envelope template to simplify
your contract process.

title: Link a Gen Template to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Link a Gen Template to an Envelope Template
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 5.3 or higher.

[Create a Docusign
envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus).

[Create a Gen template with Microsoft
Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus).

Important:

- Use a Word-based Gen template. You cannot link an [Agreement Cloud
  Editor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=bcp1586134298132.html&_LANG=enus)-based Gen template to an envelope template.
- You can only link templates built on the same Salesforce object. Linking a
  Gen template and envelope template built on Opportunity
  works. Linking an Opportunity-based Gen template with
  an Account-based envelope template does not work.
- You can only link the following types of envelope templates: Default,
  Quick Send, Place Fields, and
  Send Now.

You can link a Docusign Gen template to a Docusign
envelope template. When users generate an agreement with Gen and select
Send for Signature, your linked envelope template opens.

Linking templates simplifies your business process by automating contract generation and
signing.

Use this procedure to link a Gen template to an envelope template:

1. [Open the Create Button step as a
   Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=way1621663319299.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Envelope Templates: Enter the name of an envelope
   template that uses the same data source as this Gen template.
3. Select Save and Finish.

You have completed the process. You have linked a Gen template to
an envelope template. When users generate a document from a record, the linked envelope
template sending experience opens.

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
text: Learn to connect your IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")

To use Docusign apps in your Salesforce organization, you must first connect
Docusign and Salesforce. You create this connection by
entering Salesforce and Docusign administrator credentials on the
Docusign Setup page. The Docusign
administrator used to connect accounts is called the integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to a Docusign production account:

1. Update Session Settings as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2MzMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.aR8XLE56hKPkeiLYvSbdQ3-QaRDUf4XM6RgS6F_GhlI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select `Session Settings` in the Quick
      Find field.
   3. Uncheck Lock sessions to the IP address from which they
      originated.
   4. Uncheck Lock sessions to the domain in which they were first
      used.![A screenshot displays two deselected options for Lock sessions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-deselected-options-for-Lock-sessions-to-the-IP-address-and-Lock-sessions-to-the-domain.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2MzMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.aR8XLE56hKPkeiLYvSbdQ3-QaRDUf4XM6RgS6F_GhlI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays two deselected options for Lock sessions.")
   5. Select Save.
2. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that displays.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that displays
      and select Log In.

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
Before you begin
text:
horize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that displays.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that displays
      and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page displays.
3. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login dialog box displays.
   2. Select Advanced Options.
   3. Other Environment: Enter
      `USFED` in the field and select Log
      in.![A screenshot displays the Connect to Docusign page with Advanced Options and Other Environment set to USFED.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Other-Environment-field-with-USFED-entered-on-the-Connect-Docusign-to-Salesforce-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2MzMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.aR8XLE56hKPkeiLYvSbdQ3-QaRDUf4XM6RgS6F_GhlI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect to Docusign page with Advanced Options and Other Environment set to USFED.")
   4. Enter Docusign administrator credentials in the
      dialog box that displays and select Log in.
   5. Select Use Custom Domain in the Salesforce
      dialog box that displays.
   6. Enter your Salesforce domain in the Custom
      Domain field and select
      Continue.![A screenshot displays the Use Custom Domain field filled with an example domain.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Use-Custom-Domain-field-filled-with-an-example-domain.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2MzMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.aR8XLE56hKPkeiLYvSbdQ3-QaRDUf4XM6RgS6F_GhlI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Use Custom Domain field filled with an example domain.")
   7. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box displays.
4. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page displays.
5. Edit the Salesforce Connect URL in your Docusign
   account:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator
      and select Settings.
   2. Select Connect in the left column.
   3. Docusign Apps Launcher: Select
      Actions and choose
      Edit.
   4. ULR to Publish: Change
      act.mil in the URL to
      fed-act-event.

      - [Correct example](https://act-event.docusign.mil/listeners/connect-salesforce)
      - [Incorrect example](https://fed-act-event.docusign.com/listeners/connect-salesforce)
   5. Select SAVE CONFIGURATION.

You have completed the process. You have connected your
IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to your Docusign account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Change Envelope Template Recipient Actions
heading_path: Change Envelope Template Recipient Actions
text:
There are many different recipient actions that can be assigned to recipients in an
envelope template.

Use this procedure to change the action of an envelope template recipient:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._UX6SOHKmNdr-UkVzVZosYxzjCncH8SeuL1xZScR9AE&_LANG=enus) dropdown menu next to a recipient
   name.
2. Select Edit.

   ![A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplateeditrecipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._UX6SOHKmNdr-UkVzVZosYxzjCncH8SeuL1xZScR9AE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.")
3. Select the Action dropdown menu.
4. Select an action from the list.
5. Select the Add button to save the action. The Add
   Recipient dialog box closes.
6. Select the Save and Close button at the top-right of the
   page.

You have completed the process. You have added or changed the recipient
action.

title: Rename an Envelope Template
heading_path: Rename an Envelope Template
text:
Learn to rename envelope templates.

Follow these steps to rename your envelope template:

1. Select the Docusign envelope templates tab.
2. Check the box to the left of a template name.
3. Select Edit.![A screenshot displays the template name in edit mode with the Name field active for renaming.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-template-name-in-edit-mode.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2MjcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.szvJDBz-MXvW_53ikIC0xBSAx1tEROCRtvhSyJYcMHQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the template name in edit mode with the Name field active for renaming.")

   The template Name opens in a text
   field.
4. Enter a new template name.
5. Select Save.

   A message displays: Your changes are saved.

You have completed the process. You have renamed the
template.

title: Use the Docusign Setup Page to Administer Your Docusign Gen Account
heading_path: Use the Docusign Setup Page to Administer Your Docusign Gen Account
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign Setup page to manage Gen and eSignature
users, components, templates, and more.

Docusign Setup is the main landing page for managing Gen and
eSignature for Salesforce users, components, templates, and more.

You access this page by [opening the Docusign Apps Launcher package in your Salesforce
organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.").

![A screenshot displays Docusign Setup page options like eSignature, document generation, User Management, and Components.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-Docusign-Setup-page-options-like-eSignature-Document-Generation-User-Management-and-Components.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2OTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.rd-HrB22krkAfHxh0Nhq_5aSiofjJXkrQyY6ikUiG60&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Docusign Setup page options like eSignature, document generation, User Management, and Components.")

The Docusign Setup page displays the following resources:

- Home: Manage and configure the Gen and eSignature for
  Salesforce apps.
- [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xsw1586134361444.html&_LANG=enus): Configure your eSignature for Salesforce
  templates, buttons, and settings.
- [Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zgw1621457735347.html&_LANG=enus): Create, edit, and delete Docusign Gen templates from this page.
- [User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zbp1629859390541.html&_LANG=enus): Add and manage Docusign Gen and eSignature users.
- [Components](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=xkm1586134206424.html&_LANG=enus): Customize layouts and experiences by adding
  custom components.
- Help: Learn how to get the most from Docusign
  Apps Launcher:
  - [Support Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US): Customers learn to use and
    troubleshoot Docusign products.
  - [Community](https://support.docusign.com/forum): Docusign customers
    ask and answer questions.
  - [Docusign University](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/page/all-courses): Docusign customers build expertise.

title: Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts
heading_path: Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts
text:
Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your
Salesforce layouts.

You can uninstall Apps Launcher without removing your Docusign custom
buttons. But consider removing these buttons to maintain a clean layout and avoid
confusing your users.

Use this procedure to remove all Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce
layouts:

1. Open the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as an
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.3p5bLAk2eqZw6T5H20hUfCD8qJBFXEgPLDIOtFub1MM&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick
      Find field.
   3. Select an object (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
   4. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions.

      The Buttons, Links, and Actions page displays.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.3p5bLAk2eqZw6T5H20hUfCD8qJBFXEgPLDIOtFub1MM&_LANG=enus) next to a Docusign button and choose
   Delete from the dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays the arrow menu with the delete button highlighted for a custom button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.3p5bLAk2eqZw6T5H20hUfCD8qJBFXEgPLDIOtFub1MM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the arrow menu with the delete button highlighted for a custom button.")

   A dialog box displays: Are you sure?
3. Select Delete.

   The page refreshes with the button no longer displayed.

   Note: You may see:
   Your request to delete this custom button or link can not be
   completed because this button or link is being used by another
   feature. Open the object's page layout and remove the button
   there.
4. Repeat this entire procedure for each layout that contains a Docusign Apps Launcher button.

You have completed step three of the process. You have
removed all Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts. Move to
[Step 4: Uninstall Apps Launcher from Your organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ump1623741000284.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your Salesforce organization.").

title: Remove and Close Docusign Users
heading_path: Remove and Close Docusign Users
text:
Learn to remove access and close Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature
users.

You can use the Remove and Close command to revoke a Salesforce user's
access to Docusign.

- Remove and Close removes a Salesforce user's access to
  Docusign apps and the connected Docusign account. This command does not delete
  Salesforce users.
- You can not remove and close your user while logged on.
- You can not remove and close the only eSignature administrator user.

Use this procedure to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access and close Docusign users in the connected account:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to remove and close.
2. Select Remove and Close from the Add
   Users
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NTcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.bTjLfJCYPtDQkh4aYHzZ98aRpWLi-zet7bfeYYJgZ1o&_LANG=enus) dropdown menu.
3. Select Remove.

You have completed the process. You removed a Salesforce user's
access to the Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce apps. You also
closed (removed) the user from the connected Docusign account. The user remains in your
Salesforce organization.

title: View the Related Objects of an Envelope
heading_path: View the Related Objects of an Envelope
text:
Learn to view objects related to the record from which the envelope was
sent.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that holds documents,
recipients, and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by completing the
fields assigned to them.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. The
Docusign Status tab lets you view the status of your
Docusign envelopes.

Docusign Status details include: Docusign envelope ID,
Sender Name, Sender Email,
Email Subject, and Related
Objects.

Use this procedure to view objects related to the record from which the envelope was
sent:

1. Select the Docusign Status tab.
2. Select an envelope from the Envelope # column.
3. Select Details.
4. Related Objects: Select a related object link.

   The related object page opens.

You have completed the task. You have opened an object
related to the record from which the envelope was sent.

title: Envelope Template Options: Documents Step
heading_path: Envelope Template Options: Documents Step
text:
This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the Documents step of the
envelope template creation process. This includes: previewing an uploaded document, renaming
an uploaded document, and deleting an uploaded document.

In the Documents step of the envelope template creation process, you add
documents to your envelope template. These documents form the basis of the envelopes
your recipients sign. You can add documents from Salesforce or from your device.

This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the
Documents step of the envelope template creation process.
The following options are available:

[Access the Documents Step of an Envelope Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qpa1620149203357.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to access the Documents step to add, edit, or delete envelope template files.")
:   Open the Documents step of an envelope template to change options

[Upload documents from your device](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jme1682397601214.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upload documents from your device to an envelope template.")
:   Add documents to an envelope template from your device

[Attach documents from Salesforce records](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jng1682398197678.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add Salesforce documents to an envelope template on the Documents page.")
:   Dynamically attach documents from Salesforce Files to your envelope
    template.

[Preview an uploaded document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=lav1596147478944.html&_LANG=enus "You can preview uploaded documents in the envelope template by following these steps.")
:   Preview what recipients see when they open your envelope.

[Rename an uploaded document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=phb1596147423215.html&_LANG=enus "You can change the file names of uploaded documents in the envelope template by following these steps.")
:   Change the file name of an uploaded document.

[Delete an uploaded document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uqf1596147449716.html&_LANG=enus "You can delete documents from an envelope template at any time.")
:   Remove an uploaded document from an envelope template.

title: Step 3: Remove Legacy eSignature Custom Buttons From Your Salesforce Layouts
heading_path: Step 3: Remove Legacy eSignature Custom Buttons From Your Salesforce Layouts
text:
Learn how to remove legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom buttons from your
Salesforce page layouts.

You need to remove legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom buttons before
you can uninstall the legacy app.

Use this procedure to remove all legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom
buttons from your Salesforce layouts:

1. Open the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as an
   administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.wZhwPPVYLZugoTXvvPNcRfIIRONF6E2hHEEmFoBfh6g&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) link in the left
      column.
   5. Select the Buttons, Links, and Actions link.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.wZhwPPVYLZugoTXvvPNcRfIIRONF6E2hHEEmFoBfh6g&_LANG=enus) next to the custom button you want to remove.
3. Select Delete from the dropdown menu.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.wZhwPPVYLZugoTXvvPNcRfIIRONF6E2hHEEmFoBfh6g&_LANG=enus)
4. Select the Delete button on the Are you
   sure? dialog box.

   The page refreshes with the button no longer displayed.

   Note: You may see a page that
   says: Your request to delete this custom button or link can not
   be completed because this button or link is being used by another
   feature. In this case, navigate to the Salesforce page
   layout for that object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) and remove the
   button from the page layout.
5. Repeat this entire procedure for each Salesforce layout (Account, Opportunity,
   and so on) that contains a Docusign legacy eSignature custom button.

You have completed step three of the process. You
have removed all Docusign legacy eSignature custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.
Move to [Step 4: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=the1642634056297.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your Salesforce organization.").

title: Step 2: Remove Legacy eSignature Components from Your Salesforce Layouts
heading_path: Step 2: Remove Legacy eSignature Components from Your Salesforce Layouts
text:
Learn how to remove all legacy eSignature for Salesforce components from Salesforce
layouts.

Uninstalling the legacy eSignature for Salesforce app removes all Docusign
components. However, you cannot delete components that are present on any layouts.

Use this procedure to remove Docusign components from your layouts:

1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.iRNNPL1pgFdestjLCzWXrA3WSSKspg0iwdq4d4x7RKY&_LANG=enus)and choose Edit Page from the
   dropdown menu.

   The Lightning App Builder page opens.
2. Remove legacy Docusign component from the canvas. Legacy
   components include: Docusign Sending, Docusign
   EnvelopeConfiguration, and Docusign
   EnvelopeConfigurationManageLayouts.
   1. Hover over a legacy component and select the trash icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-123257-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.iRNNPL1pgFdestjLCzWXrA3WSSKspg0iwdq4d4x7RKY&_LANG=enus).![A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted for the Docusign Files component.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Remove-DocuSign-component.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.iRNNPL1pgFdestjLCzWXrA3WSSKspg0iwdq4d4x7RKY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted for the Docusign Files component.")
   2. Select Save.
   3. Repeat this procedure for all legacy Docusign
      components.

   Salesforce removes the components from the canvas.
3. Select the back arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-13251-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.iRNNPL1pgFdestjLCzWXrA3WSSKspg0iwdq4d4x7RKY&_LANG=enus) when you have removed all legacy
   components.
4. Repeat this procedure for each Salesforce layout (Account, Opportunity, and so
   on) that contains legacy Docusign components.

You have completed step two of the process. You have
removed all legacy Docusign components from your Salesforce layouts.
Move to [Step 3: Remove Legacy eSignature Custom Buttons From Your Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ddz1642632234520.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom buttons from your Salesforce page layouts.").

title: Add Sending Options to an Envelope Template Recipient
heading_path: Add Sending Options to an Envelope Template Recipient
text:
Learn how to add the role, access code, filter, private message, in-person signing, SMS,
or sign now options when creating an envelope template recipient.

You can add custom options to any recipient in the Add Recipient dialog box.

Use this procedure to add recipient options to an envelope template:

Note: You can also use
this procedure on the Recipients step when sending an envelope.

1. Select the name of the recipient you want to update

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
2. Add any option to your recipient:

   - Add Role: Add a placeholder title (like Approver)
     that allows the sender to specify a name and email address later
   - Delivery
     - Email: Send an envelope to a recipient by
       email.
     - Email + SMS: Send an envelope to up to
       four recipients by email and text message. This Delivery menu
       only appears for customers with Allow SMS
       Delivery and Signer can sign on a
       mobile device enabled.

       Important: You must enter a
       space between the country code and the phone
       number.
   - Add Filter: Add recipients based on [a set of conditions being met or not
     met](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gkj1612219694379.html&_LANG=enus "You can add filters to Related Lists recipients in the Add Recipient dialog box of a Docusign envelope template. This allows you to only add Related List recipients to an envelope if certain conditions are met.")
   - Make the Sender Host: The person who sends the
     envelope becomes the host of the in-person signing session
   - Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now): The sender is asked to
     sign their own envelope immediately after sending it
   - Add Name and Email: Specify the name and email
     address of a recipient
   - Add Access Authentication
     - SMS/Phone: Send an access code to a
       recipient's mobile device to add an extra layer of security to
       the signing process
     - Access Code: Create a code that a
       recipient must enter to start the signing process
   - Add Private Message: Send a message to just this
     recipient or group
3. Select the Add button.

   The Add Recipient dialog box closes.
4. Select the Save and Close button.

You have completed the process. You have added or edited a
recipient action in your envelope template.

title: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
heading_path: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
text:
Learn to upgrade Docusign Apps Launcher to avoid seeing an error when
accessing components.

I can't access any Docusign components on my layouts.

A red OK error displays when I select any component on a record. This
includes any of the following components:

- Docusign Files
- Docusign Envelope Status
- Docusign CLM Files
- Docusign CLM Obligations
- Docusign CLM Tasks

title: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
heading_path: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
Resolution: Upgrade to the Latest Version of Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: Your organization has an older version of Docusign Apps Launcher installed. Upgrade to the latest
version.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3NTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.cp1tY_6pibwFXhVrlmTIe4xIyPfTPY1jytMBLeVeqTg&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. Choose an upgrade path:

   - If your version is higher than 6.0, [upgrade to
     the latest Apps Launcher version](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus).
   - If your version is 6.0 or lower, [use this process to upgrade
     to the latest version](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/DocuSign-Apps-Launcher-Is-there-a-recommended-upgrade-path-for-an-older-version-of-the-package).

   You have completed the process. You have upgraded your older Docusign Apps Launcher package. You can open Docusign components on Salesforce records.

title: Step 1: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
heading_path: Step 1: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
text: Learn how to prepare your Salesforce organization before creating a Docusign Experience Cloud envelope template.

title: Step 1: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
heading_path: Step 1: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
Before you begin
text:
Complete the following tasks before building an Experience
Cloud envelope template:

- Purchase a Docusign Business Professional or higher
  license.
- Turn on Enable Community Cloud in your Docusign account. Only your Docusign account
  representative can activate this feature.

Use this procedure to prepare your Salesforce
organization for creating Experience Cloud envelope templates:

1. [Enable a system sender in your Docusign for Salesforce app.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jxz1611642321527.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to enable a System Sender so that non-Docusign users can send and sign envelopes.")
2. Change the Docusign Envelope Template Sharing Settings in
   Salesforce to Public Read Only. This grants Experience
   Cloud users basic access to Docusign envelope template custom
   objects:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NDYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.r8VMlSRd1VU28aq7bzUijDAaPRDdqKMyg8tsjDZCttE&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Sharing Settings in the Quick
      Find field.
   3. Select Edit.
   4. Select Public Read Only from the Docusign Envelope Template dropdown.

      ![A screenshot displays the Public Read Only setting for the Docusign Envelope Template object.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Public-Read-Only-setting-for-DocuSign-Envelope-Template-object.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NDYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.r8VMlSRd1VU28aq7bzUijDAaPRDdqKMyg8tsjDZCttE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Public Read Only setting for the Docusign Envelope Template object.")
   5. Select Save.

   The Sharing Settings page refreshes, displaying your changes.
3. Add Docusign Administrator status to your delivered by user.
   Messages sent from this template display the delivered by
   user as the sender.

   Note: Add the delivered by user to the
   Salesforce System Administrator permission
   set.
4. Set the delivered by user as an organization-wide email
   address:
   1. Enter and select Organization-Wide Addresses in
      the Quick Find field.
   2. Display Name: Add a name for the
      delivered by user.
   3. Email Address: Add the email address of the
      delivered by user.
   4. Profiles: Select your Experience Cloud user
      profiles.
   5. Select Save.

   The Organization-Wide Email Addresses page
   refreshes, displaying your changes.
5. Enable Digital Experiences in Salesforce:
   1. Enter Digital Experiences in the Quick Find field
      and select the Settings link that displays under
      Digital Experiences.
   2. Select the Enable Digital Experiences check
      box.
   3. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the Enable Digital Experiences check box on the Experiences page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Enable-Digital-Experiences-check-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NDYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.r8VMlSRd1VU28aq7bzUijDAaPRDdqKMyg8tsjDZCttE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Enable Digital Experiences check box on the Experiences page.")
   4. Select OK in the Enable digital
      experiences dialog box that displays.

   4. Select OK in the Enable digital

   The Settings page closes. The All Sites page displays.
6. [Create and publish a new Experience Cloud

   3. Select OK and choose Save
      on the dialog box that displays.

   The page refreshes. The Setup Home page

8. Activate your Experience Cloud site:
   1. Enter Digital Experiences in the Quick Find field
      and select All Sites.
   2. Select Workspaces next to the Experience Cloud

   3. Select Administration.
   4. Select Activate.

   6. Select Publish.

9. Grant access to the Experience Cloud page:

   5. Select Publish.
   6. Select Publish on the Publish your
      site? dialog box.
10. Add a Docusign custom button to your Experience Cloud
    site:
    1. Enter Digital Experiences in the Quick Find field

    2. Select Builder next to the Experience Cloud

    3. Select the Settings

    4. Select the Default Navigation dropdown arrow and
       choose Edit.
    5. Select + Add Menu Item.
    6. Create a new menu item:

    4. Select the Default Navigation dropdown arrow and
       choose Edit.
    5. Select + Add Menu Item.
    6. Create a new menu item:

       1. Name: Enter the name of a Salesforce

title: Step 1: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
heading_path: Step 1: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
Next steps
text:
You have completed step 1 of the process. You have enabled all
prerequisites for your Salesforce organization. Move to [Step 2: Prepare Your Salesforce Users for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=tska4688422-0c61-4376-8b45-fb2262a80599.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce users for Experience Cloud envelope template use.").

title: Edit the Docusign App Permissions of Salesforce Users
heading_path: Edit the Docusign App Permissions of Salesforce Users
text:
Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce
users.

You can use the Edit Permissions
command to change the Docusign app permissions of Salesforce users.

Use this
procedure to edit the Docusign app permissions of Gen, CLM Negotiate, or eSignature for
Salesforce users:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to edit.
2. Select Edit Permissions from the Add
   Users
   ![A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon in a square.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.SPYfiV5j_Uf-86PSut0Ce9My5PECzKAc4x74L6Du3eo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon in a square.") dropdown menu.
3. Change access from the Edit Permissions dialog box that
   opens:

   1. eSignature
      - Administrator: Administrators can create
        templates and manage all Docusign users in Salesforce.
      - User: Users can send documents for
        signature.
   2. CLM
      - Administrator: Administrators can access
        everything in CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. They
        can't manage Docusign users in Salesforce.
      - User: Users can access workflows and
        view, edit, and upload documents.
      - Viewer: Viewers can only preview and
        upload documents.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        CLM app
   3. Gen
      - Generator: Generators can create new
        documents and templates.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        Gen app.
   4. Negotiate
      - Negotiator: Negotiators can collaborate
        with signers and viewers.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        Negotiate app.
   5. (Optional) Update permissions for any existing
      users: Select to update a user's permissions in the
      connected Docusign account.
4. Select Apply.

You have completed the process. You edited the permissions of Gen,
CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.

title: Step 3: (Optional) Add Recipients to Your Template
heading_path: Step 3: (Optional) Add Recipients to Your Template
text:
In the third step of creating a linked envelope template, you add recipients and a
message to recipients.

Use this procedure to add recipients to your linked envelope template. You cannot view, edit,
or remove recipients from the linked templates. You can access recipient details
when you send an envelope from this template.

Note: This step is optional. You can
add new recipients, or keep the recipients imported from your linked templates.

1. Select the Add Recipient button.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
2. Select a recipient type from the left column:

   - From Salesforce:
     - Lookup Fields: Salesforce fields like
       Owner or Created
       By that are used to dynamically add recipients
       from a Salesforce record.
     - Related Lists: A Salesforce field that an
       administrator chooses to relate to a parent object (Account,
       Opportunity, and so on.)
     - Users/Contacts: Salesforce Users or
       Contacts saved in your organization.
     - Sender: Add the sender of the envelope as
       a recipient. You must select +Add Role
       and enter a role, like Sender 1.
   - Other Sources:
     - By Role: A placeholder title (like Approver) that allows the
       sender to specify a name and email address later. You can use
       custom syntax to [dynamically add a By
       Role recipient](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus) from a Salesforce object.
     - Signing Group: A group of recipients in
       the connected Docusign account; any signer can complete the
       signature for the group
3. (Optional) Delivery: Select an option from the dropdown
   menu:

   - Email: Send an envelope by email.
   - Email + SMS: Send an envelope by email and text
     message.

     Important: You must enter
     a space between the country code and the phone number.

   Note: The Delivery field
   only displays for customers with Allow SMS Delivery
   and Signer can sign on a mobile device enabled.
   Contact your Docusign account team to enable these
   features. You can use the email + SMS option for up to four recipients per
   envelope.
4. Action: Select a recipient option from the dropdown
   menu:

   - Needs to Sign: Must sign the document
   - Receives a Copy: Does not need to view or
     sign
   - Needs to View: Must view the document
   - Specify Recipients: Can add names and emails for
     placeholder recipients (like Approver.)
   - Allow to Edit: Can make changes to the
     document
   - In Person Signer: A multi-recipient signing
     session where all signers must be in the same room
5. Select a save option:

   - Add & New: Save the recipient and add
     another
   - Add: Save the recipient and close the dialog
     box
6. (Optional):

title: Step 3: (Optional) Add Recipients to Your Template
heading_path: Step 3: (Optional) Add Recipients to Your Template
text:
n where all signers must be in the same room
5. Select a save option:

   - Add & New: Save the recipient and add
     another
   - Add: Save the recipient and close the dialog
     box
6. (Optional):

   - Signing Order:
     1. Select the Signing Order button. Numbers
        display next to the recipients.
     2. Drag recipients to create a new signing order.![A screenshot displays the Signing Order dialog with recipients listed and numbered, showing how to drag recipients to change the signing order.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/DAL_Envelope_Template_drag_recipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5MzIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._vHoV2wAzRAbkHmZ8A6uM_bcN2tcbRSqagZQfXc3qfc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Signing Order dialog with recipients listed and numbered, showing how to drag recipients to change the signing order.")
   - View: Select this button to see a visual diagram
     of the signing order
   - Email Subject: Enter up to 100 characters of
     text.
   - Email Message: Enter up to 2,000 characters of
     text.
   - More Options: Select the arrow
     > to open the menu and choose any
     options:
     - Reminders: Set the number of days between recipient reminders
       from the dropdown menu
     - Expiration: Enter the number of days
       before the envelope expires
7. Select Next.

title: Delete Merge Fields From an Envelope Template
heading_path: Delete Merge Fields From an Envelope Template
text:
You can delete a merge field in an existing envelope template at any time.

You can delete a merge field in an envelope template at any
time.

Use this procedure to delete merge fields in an envelope
template:

1. Select from the arrow ![A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxMDcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Fz7nu0aZmhbQCyQekx7X3XIa8PXpiRwI3ATVUfOJ2pI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.") dropdown menu next to a merge field
   name.
2. Select Delete.

   The Are you sure you want to delete this merge field?
   dialog box opens.
3. Select Delete.

   A message displays: Merge field deleted. The dialog box closes.

You have completed the process. You have deleted a merge field.

title: Step 8: Add Options to Your Experience Cloud Envelope Template
heading_path: Step 8: Add Options to Your Experience Cloud Envelope Template
text:
Learn to add options like Delivered by to your Experience Cloud envelope
template.

Use this procedure to enter a Delivered by user, as well as add
options to your Envelope Template:

1. Delivered by: Enter a Salesforce user who also has
   administrator status in the connected Docusign account.
   Messages sent from this template will display the Delivered by user as the
   sender.

   Important: This user must also be in the
   Salesforce System Administrator profile or permission set.

   ![A screenshot displays the Delivered By option selected in the Set Delivery Email field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210404-at-30925-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NzIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Gly4C01mCmqykY46q0q3f5ES3AbZHbqSVek3-5q0APo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Delivered By option selected in the Set Delivery Email field.")
2. (Optional): Configure any of the following
   options:
   - Reminders: Add a reminder to your recipients from
     the dropdown menu. Select Custom from the dropdown
     menu to add a custom reminder of up to 999 days.
   - Expiration: Set a date or a number of days (up to
     999) after which the envelope expires.
   - Document Writeback: Select the check box for any option:
     - Save completed documents back to the Opportunity
       Record.
     - Save certificate of completion (COC) document back to the
       Opportunity Record.

     Important: Do
     not turn on Multi-Factor Authentication for API
     Logins. Writeback for documents, merge fields, and data
     fails when you use this option.
   - Field Updates: Automatically update Salesforce
     fields when envelope events occur:
     1. Select the + Add Update button.

        A row of
        fields displays.
     2. Envelope Event: Select an event to trigger a
        Salesforce field update, like Envelope
        Completed.
     3. Field Name: Enter or select the Salesforce
        field name, like Amount, that updates when
        the envelope event occurs.
     4. Field Update: Enter or select a value that
        updates the Salesforce field name.![A screenshot displays the Field Updates page, with events created for Envelope Completed and Envelope Voided.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Field-Updates-events.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NzIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Gly4C01mCmqykY46q0q3f5ES3AbZHbqSVek3-5q0APo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Field Updates page, with events created for Envelope Completed and Envelope Voided.")

        Note:
        - You can add 5 envelope events for each type (like Envelope Completed)
          with a maximum of 25 events.
        - Envelope events of the same type cannot have the same
          field name. For example, two Envelope
          Completed events cannot use
          Stage as the field name.
3. Select Next.

title: Add Docusign Buttons, Links, or Actions to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add Docusign Buttons, Links, or Actions to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add Docusign buttons, links, or actions to your Salesforce
layouts.

You can add Docusign buttons, links, or actions to your Salesforce
layouts. Your users can access Docusign features with these controls. For
example, when you add a Gen button to a layout, your users can select it to generate an
agreement.

Use this procedure to add Docusign buttons, links, or actions to a
layout:

1. Access a page layout as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![Salesforce gear icon used to access Setup in the header.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.hfQhhGjPv52uPLDcrgJqCFuzkJP1vt_rjmwfa8MWkQQ&_LANG=enus "Salesforce gear icon used to access Setup in the header.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select `Object Manager` in the Quick Find
      field.
2. Select a Salesforce object from the Label column.
3. Select Page Layouts.
4. Select a layout from the Page Layout Name column.
5. Drag a button, link, or action from the menu to the layout.

   ![Salesforce page layout editor showing a custom button being dragged from the menu to the page layout canvas.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/dragtocanvas.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.hfQhhGjPv52uPLDcrgJqCFuzkJP1vt_rjmwfa8MWkQQ&_LANG=enus "Salesforce page layout editor showing a custom button being dragged from the menu to the page layout canvas.")
6. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have added a
button, link, or action to a layout. The items you added display on every record of that
object type.

title: eSignature for Salesforce Flow
heading_path: eSignature for Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to build Send with Docusign actions for Salesforce
flows.

Important:
You must meet the following requirements to trigger Docusign envelopes from Salesforce flows:

- Install Docusign Apps Launcher v6.1 or higher.
- Purchase a Salesforce plan that supports flows and Invocable Apex.
- Create Docusign user accounts for Salesforce users who will trigger envelopes
  from flows.
- Add triggering users to the Docusign Sender permission
  set.

Salesforce Flow is a tool that helps you automate your business processes.
Flow replaces Salesforce Process Builder. Visit the [Salesforce admin
site](https://admin.salesforce.com/) for more information.

Flows can use any of these events to trigger the creation of a Docusign
envelope:

- Record created
- Record updated
- Platform event message received
- Flow triggered by another Flow
- Date and time from a Schedule-Triggered Flow

Example: You build a Record-Triggered Flow and link it to a Docusign envelope template. You configure the Flow to trigger envelope
creation when a record's stage changes to Negotiation/Review. A
user changes the stage of the Burlington Textiles opportunity to
Negotiation/Review. Salesforce triggers the Docusign envelope template
linked to your Flow. Docusign creates an envelope using data from the
record and sends it to Burlington Textile representatives.

The following topics demonstrate how to build or edit Send With Docusign
actions in a Salesforce Flow:

- [Add a Send with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=mkk1697579504882.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Send With Docusign action to your Salesforce Flow to trigger envelope creation.")
- [Change the Docusign Template Used in a Salesforce Flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jik1697735894052.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the Docusign template or Salesforce object connected to your Flow.")

title: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
text:
Learn to add Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users and manage permissions
on the user management page.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of Docusign applications you can use in Salesforce. The
Apps Launcher package contains the Docusign
eSignature,
CLM, Gen, and
Negotiate applications. Each Salesforce user needing to use
these apps must have a Docusign user. Administrators create Docusign users and grant
permissions on the Docusign user management page.

The following is a list of apps and permission types administrators can assign:

Docusign Gen
:   Generator: Generators can generate contracts and send
    them for signature.

Docusign CLM
:   Administrator: Administrators can access everything in
    CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. They can't manage Docusign users in
    Salesforce.
:   User: Users can access workflows and view, edit, and
    upload documents.
:   Viewer: Viewers can only preview and upload
    documents.

Docusign eSignature
:   Administrator: Administrators create templates and manage
    all Docusign users in Salesforce.
:   User: Users can send documents for signature.

Docusign Negotiate
:   Negotiator: Negotiators can send content for review and
    signature.

You can take the following actions on the Docusign User Management page:

- [Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users
  in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zbp1629859390541.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to grant Docusign app access to your Salesforce users.")
- [Create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or
  eSignature Users in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users on the user management page.")
- [Check the Activation Status of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hak1586134277505.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to check the activation status of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.")
- [Search for Docusign Users in the Docusign CLM
  User Management Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=keb1628113418793.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the search feature to find Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users.")
- [Edit the Permissions of Gen, Negotiate, or
  eSignature for Salesforce Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=doi1628107496987.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.")
- [Remove Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature Access
  From a Salesforce User in the User Management Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=tha1628110439784.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from Salesforce users.")
- [Remove Access and Close Docusign Gen,
  Negotiate, and eSignature Users in the Docusign User Management
  Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=cmn1628112474213.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove access and close Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users.")
