title: Learn How to Use Merge Fields to Customize the Email Subject and Message of an Envelope
heading_path: Learn How to Use Merge Fields to Customize the Email Subject and Message of an Envelope
text:
Learn how to use Docusign and Salesforce merge fields to dynamically populate an
envelope's Email Subject and Email Message fields.

You can use merge field syntax to dynamically fill the Email
Subject and Email Message fields of a
Docusign envelope. Merge field syntax can add this information from standard and
custom Salesforce objects.

Using merge field syntax is helpful if you want Docusign to fill email fields with Salesforce
record names or descriptions.

**Example**: You create an envelope template for the Opportunity object. When users create envelopes, you want Docusign to add the record's description in the
Email Subject field.

You enter `{!Opportunity.Description}` in the Email
Subject field of your envelope template.

When a user creates an envelope, Docusign adds the Opportunity
Description of that record to the Email
Subject.

Note: You must use the source Salesforce object at the start of
your merge field syntax.

These are examples of Salesforce syntax you can use:

| Salesforce Merge Field Syntax | Salesforce Data Added to Email |
| --- | --- |
| `{!Opportunity.FieldName}` | Field data from a Salesforce Opportunity |
| `{!Opportunity.Account.FieldName}` | Field data from a Salesforce Account |
| `{!User.FirstName}` | Salesforce user's First Name |
| `{!User.LastName}` | Salesforce user's Last Name |
| `{!User.Email}` | Salesforce user's Email Address |

For information about using merge fields to import Salesforce recipient
names, visit these sites:

- [Salesforce help](https://help.salesforce.com/s/)
- [Salesforce Trailhead learning
  modules](https://trailhead.salesforce.com/content/learn/trails/learn_salesforce_with_trailhead)

You can also use Docusign merge fields to customize your
Email Subject and Email Message
fields.

For example, use [[Data:RecipientName]] to insert the recipient's name
into the email.

These are examples of Salesforce syntax you can use:

| Docusign Merge Field Syntax | Docusign Data Added to Email |
| --- | --- |
| `[[Data:EnvelopeID]]` | Docusign envelope ID |
| `[[Data:EnvelopeExpirationDate]]` | Docusign envelope expiration date |
| `[[Data:SenderName]]` | Docusign envelope sender name |
| `[[Data:SenderEmail]]` | Docusign envelope sender email address |
| `[[Data:RecipientName]]` | Docusign envelope recipient name |
| `[[Data:RecipientEmail]]` | Docusign envelope recipient email address |

Tip: You can find Docusign merge fields in the [Docusign Email Resource File](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=yum1583277332870.html&_LANG=enus). You can only
use merge field syntax when it is relevant to an envelope. For example, if no one
voided an envelope, using `[[Data:VoidReason]]` does not
produce any data.

Here is an example of how you can use Docusign and Salesforce merge fields for a Docusign
envelope. In this example, the sender creates an envelope for the Opportunity
object:

Email Subject: Contract for
`{!Opportunity.Account}`

Email Message:

Hi, `[[Data:RecipientName]]`,

Please use the link to review and complete your contract for
`{!Opportunity.Name}`.

You must complete this document by `[[Data:EnvelopeExpirationDate]]`, or
it will be automatically voided.

If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to contact me. Please reference this
transaction ID for a faster response:`[[Data:EnvelopeID]]`.

Thank you,

`[[Data:SenderName]]`

`[[Data:SenderEmail]]`

title: Set Up Document Writeback in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Set Up Document Writeback in an Envelope Template
text:
Use this procedure to set up document writeback in your envelope template.

Use this procedure to set up document writeback in your envelope template:

1. Document Writeback: Select any option:
   - Save completed documents back to the (object name)
     Record: Check this box to save signed
     documents back to Salesforce when the Docusign signing
     process is complete.
     - Filename: Set the naming convention for all
       completed documents saved back to Salesforce:
       - Document Name: Retain the current
         filename with no changes
       - Document Name + Envelope Status: Docusign status (Completed, Voided, and so on)
         added to the end of the current filename
       - Document Name + .pdf: Current
         filename, saved as a PDF
       - Document Name + Envelope Status +
         .pdf: Docusign status
         (Completed, Voided, and so on) and .pdf added to the end of the
         current filename
   - Combine all documents into one PDF file: Check
     this box to merge all documents from the envelope into one PDF file.

     Note: All signed documents are saved in PDF format.
     You can preview the file in Salesforce by adding +.pdf to the filename format. If you do not add +.pdf, you must download the document to
     view it.
   - Filename: Set the naming convention for all
     completed documents saved back to Salesforce as a single PDF:
     - Email Subject: Filename is created from the
       Subject line of the Docusign email
     - Email Subject + Envelope Status: Docusign status (Completed, Voided, and so on) added to
       the end of the Subject line of the Docusign email
     - Email Subject + .pdf: Subject line of the Docusign email, saved as a PDF
     - Email Subject + Envelope Status + .pdf: Docusign status (Completed, Voided, and so on) and .pdf
       added to the end of the Subject line of the Docusign email
     - "Envelope" + Envelope ID + .pdf: Filename
       begins with the word "Envelope", is followed by the Docusign Envelope ID and ends with .pdf
   - Save certificate of completion (COC) document back to the
     (object name) Record: Check this box to save a
     certificate containing the complete details of all envelope events from
     the signing process. This includes details about each signer, signer IP
     addresses, signature image, and other key event time stamps.
2. Select Save and Close when all options are set.

   You have completed the process. You have set up document writeback in
   your envelope template. Docusign updates files, file names,
   Salesforce fields, or stages in your Salesforce organization when the signature
   is complete.

title: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn how to use Translation Workbench to enable choosing a default Salesforce
language.

Use this procedure to enable Translation Workbench in your organization and let users
choose their default Salesforce language.

1. Access Translation Settings as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3OTQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.gfkufjlOQYOGX9umhUi-8tqCWhw--rKuWoHKOsxo0iE&_LANG=enus) icon and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Translation Settings in the Quick Find
      field.
2. Select Enable on the Translation Settings page.

   ![A screenshot of the Translation Settings page displays the Enable button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Enable-Salesforce-Translation-settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3OTQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.gfkufjlOQYOGX9umhUi-8tqCWhw--rKuWoHKOsxo0iE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Translation Settings page displays the Enable button highlighted.")

   The Translation Workbench page opens. A message displays: The
   Translation Workbench is currently enabled for your
   organization.
3. (Optional): Select Add to add additional
   languages to the organization.

You have completed the process. Users in your
Salesforce org can switch languages and view Docusign Apps Launcher content in any of the
enabled languages.

title: Docusign Envelope Template Merge Field Types
heading_path: Docusign Envelope Template Merge Field Types
text:
Here are the options available on the Add Merge Fields dialog box:

Here are the options available on the Add Merge Fields dialog box:

Table 1. FIELD PROPERTIES

| Field Properties Options | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Allow Writeback to Salesforce | When a signer changes Merge Field data, those changes are written back to the Salesforce record. This is triggered by an envelope event (Envelope Sent, Envelope Completed, and so on.) A maximum of 255 characters per field may be written back. |
| Allow Sender to Edit | Allow a sender to edit Salesforce merge field data. |
| Required Field | Check this box to prevent signers from leaving a field blank. Allow signed envelope content to update fields on a Salesforce record. |
| Read Only (for signer) | Prevent signers from editing a field. |

Table 2. MORE OPTIONS FIELDS

| Option Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Tooltip | Enter written guidance for the sender (ex: "Adds the customer's full billing address".) |
| Anchor Text | Automatically place merge fields on key words in a document. |
| Validation dropdown menu | Set rules for text that users can and can not enter in a field. |
| - No Validation | Any text can be entered in the field. |
| - Email | Text entered must be in the form of an email address. |
| - Number | Text entered can only be numbers. |
| - Date | Text entered must be in date format. |
| - SSN | Text entered must be numbers in a xxx-xx-xxxx format. |
| Error Message | (Only if Validation is selected) Enter a custom error message if illegal characters are entered. |

Table 3. MORE OPTIONS CONDITIONAL FIELDS

| Merge Field Type Selected | More Options Items Available |
| --- | --- |
| Picklist | Anchor Text |
| Boolean | Tooltip, Anchor Text |
| String | Tooltip, Anchor Text, Validation Allow Writeback: Maximum of 100 characters allowed |
| Textarea | Tooltip, Anchor Text, Validation Allow Writeback: Maximum of 255 characters allowed |

title: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
text:
Learn to fix this account connection issue.

I cannot connect Docusign to my Salesforce organization. The "Select an
Account" page returns every time. I do not see an error message.

title: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
Resolution: Use Credentials That Are Only Connected to One Docusign Account
text:
Cause: Your integration user's email address is connected to
more than one Docusign account.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select Log in to Docusign.
3. Enter Docusign administrator credentials.

   Important: Ensure that the email address of this
   administrator is only connected to one Docusign
   account.
4. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials.
5. Select an Account: Choose a Docusign account and
   select Next.
6. Authorize: Select Allow
   Access and enter Salesforce administrator
   credentials.

   A message displays: Successfully connected and authorized to
   Docusign.

   You have completed the process. You have connected your Docusign
   account to your Salesforce organization.

title: Use the Docusign Logs Tab to View Docusign Errors
heading_path: Use the Docusign Logs Tab to View Docusign Errors
text:
Learn to use the Docusign Logs tab for viewing Docusign error messages in your Salesforce organization.

The Docusign Logs tab opens a page that displays all Docusign error messages. These include all
DEBUG, INFO,
WARN, and ERROR logs.

Log entries include these details:

- Message
- Severity
- Version
- Created By
- Docusign entity ID
- Salesforce source ID
- Transaction ID
- Class
- Method

Use this procedure to view the Docusign Logs tab in your Salesforce
organization:

1. Select the Docusign Logs tab.

   The Docusign Logs page opens.
2. Select a log from the Docusign Log column.

   The Docusign log details page opens for that item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the details of an error
from your Docusign account.

title: eSignature for Salesforce Admin Troubleshooting Guide
heading_path: eSignature for Salesforce Admin Troubleshooting Guide
text:
Administrators learn to troubleshoot issues they are having with the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.

This guide helps administrators troubleshoot issues they are having with the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.

This guide contains the following troubleshooting topics:

title: eSignature for Salesforce Admin Troubleshooting Guide
heading_path: eSignature for Salesforce Admin Troubleshooting Guide
text:
ure for Salesforce app.

This guide helps administrators troubleshoot issues they are having with the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.

This guide contains the following troubleshooting topics:

- [Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and
  eSignature Tags are Missing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ysm1596832592574.html&_LANG=enus "When adding recipients to an Envelope Template in Docusign eSignature for Salesforce, conflicts between Docusign Role and Salesforce Roles can cause problems.")
- [Envelope Template Error: “You Are Not Authorized to Access the Envelope”](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=azw1597029242182.html&_LANG=enus "Set account defaults to Allow sender to override account defaults to fix this. This allows the envelope template to save on the Documents page and be edited later.")
- [Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hjr1597180864391.html&_LANG=enus "If administrator credentials from a Docusign Demo account are used to connect Docusign and Salesforce, you get the error: There were some problems with your document generation when reaching the Preview Documents step of the template creation process.")
- [When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zxy1614033730888.html&_LANG=enus "There is an intermittent issue with adding PDF files to the Documents step of the envelope template creation process. This does not happen with every PDF file.")
- [Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qdc1614148431275.html&_LANG=enus "Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or Login IP Ranges to fix issues with users being prompted to re-enter credentials every hour.")
- [Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=syc1614152829438.html&_LANG=enus "Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or Login IP Ranges to fix issues with envelope and recipient statuses not updating.")
- [Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xde1614153589691.html&_LANG=enus "Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or Login IP Ranges to fix issues with documents and data not being written back to Salesforce.")
- [“Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ntf1614154123960.html&_LANG=enus "Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or Login IP Ranges to fix issues with Restricted IP errors in login history for your Docusign connected app.")
- [Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=whi1616792913585.html&_LANG=enus "A workaround is needed when adding a Salesforce picklist merge field to an envelope template.")
- [After Upgrading From Gen to CLM, Administrators Need To Wait an Hour for CLM Permissions To Take Effect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zqy1617892151289.html&_LANG=enus "A workaround is needed after upgrading form Gen to CLM. Administrators need to wait an hour for their new CLM permissions. After an hour, they need to refresh the Docusign Setup tab to see the CLM administrator page.")
- [Docusign-Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ihe1618248306791.html&_LANG=enus "As a Gen for Billing administrator, you see an Unable to uninstall package error when uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher. You must first remove extra relationships, permission sets, and Apex class components from your organization.")
- [Duplicate Salesforce documents appear when
  sending Gen documents for signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=yhw1621279427803.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to work around the duplicate files issue when sending Gen agreements for signature.")
- [Envelope template error when creating merge
  fields: "Failed to connect to the Salesforce service."](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=mez1622585496682.html&_LANG=enus "Disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account and Salesforce organization to reset the connection. This will fix the issue.")
- [I still have problems adding merge fields to an
  envelope template after disconnecting and reconnecting Docusign and
  Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nug1622604076814.html&_LANG=enus "To fix this issue, first select Disconnect on the Docusign Setup tab. Then delete the Salesforce link in the Connect settings of your Docusign account.")
- ["The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=fbz1628532547751.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix the \"User is not the Owner of the Lock\" error when editing a Gen or envelope template.")

title: Access the Options Step of an Envelope Template
heading_path: Access the Options Step of an Envelope Template
text:
Find out how to access the Options step in an existing envelope template. This includes:
changing the sending experience, updating a field value, and using the document writeback
feature.

You can access the Options page in an existing envelope template. This
includes: changing the sending experience, updating a field value, and using the
document writeback feature.

Access the envelope template Options page as a
Salesforce administrator:

1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1OTcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.JY3eRVNLXmXv_oVpNDsh4_KhOUDfcenCB7SLksZvvI8&_LANG=enus) and
   select View All from the dropdown menu.
2. Select the Docusign Apps Launcher tile.
3. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates
   tab.
4. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1OTcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.JY3eRVNLXmXv_oVpNDsh4_KhOUDfcenCB7SLksZvvI8&_LANG=enus) next to the template to be
   edited.
5. Select Edit from the dropdown menu.

   The envelope template creation page opens.
6. Select the Options arrow. The arrow has no text
   until selected.

You have completed the process. You have accessed the
Options page of an existing envelope template.

title: Open Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Open Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in
your organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce is a package you install from the
Salesforce AppExchange. The package contains the following applications:

- Docusign CLM for Salesforce
- Docusign CLM Essentials for Salesforce
- Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
- Docusign Gen for Salesforce
- Docusign Negotiate for Salesforce
- Docusign eSignature Apex Toolkit for Salesforce

When you install one of these apps in your organization, Docusign
installs all apps. Depending on the apps you purchase, you see a custom Docusign administrator dashboard when you open Apps Launcher.

Docusign Gen customers see administrator controls for
Gen and eSignature.

Docusign CLM customers see administrator controls for
CLM, Gen, and eSignature.

CLM Essentials customers see controls for CLM
Essentials, Gen, and eSignature.

Use this procedure to access Docusign Apps Launcher in your Salesforce
organization:

1. Select the App Launcher grid ![A screenshot displays a square icon made of nine colored dots](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NDgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.FXTfRxcmyJ6qDdzjfrfA-sVjv7dHU-KNFeYiVSORVYw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a square icon made of nine colored dots") and select View All.
2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.

You have completed the process. You have opened all
default Docusign Apps Launcher tabs and the Docusign
Setup page.

title: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
heading_path: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
text:
Learn to fix issues with sending and signing from an Apple iOS device.

Note: This document refers to sending and signing from an Apple
iOS device, like an iPhone. Apple iPadOS users
can send and sign from Safari by changing these settings:

- Set Prevent Cross-Site Tracking to
  OFF.
- Navigate to Request Desktop Website and set
  All Websites to Enabled.

iPadOS users can also utilize the [Lightning for Mobile Browser](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000381165&type=1) for a
desktop experience.

When I try to send or sign a Docusign envelope from my iOS Apple mobile device, I see one of
these issues:

- I get a blank screen when trying to add Docusign fields to an envelope
- I get a blank screen when trying to sign an envelope
- I get an error page stating "URL has moved here..."

This happens when I am using the Safari mobile browser and the Salesforce mobile app.

I am unable to send or sign an envelope when these errors occur.

title: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
heading_path: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
Resolution 1 (Safari Mobile Browser): Use a different iOS browser
text:
Neither Docusign nor Salesforce support using the Safari mobile
browser. If you are signing in an iOS mobile browser, use Google Chrome
or Mozilla Firefox instead of Safari.

title: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
heading_path: I Am Having Trouble Sending and Signing Envelopes on My Apple iOS Mobile Device
Resolution 2 (Salesforce Mobile Application for iOS): Change iOS settings
text:
Docusign and Salesforce administrator

Important: Apple iOS/iPadOS
devices use Safari pages to display Docusign content in
the Salesforce mobile app. So Apple uses Safari in the Salesforce mobile app
regardless of your default browser. You must follow these instructions to
use Docusign in the Salesforce mobile app.

1. Update your device's iOS version.
2. Update the Salesforce mobile app on your iOS device.
3. Navigate to Settings >  Safari and change the following settings:

   1. Disable Prevent Cross-Site Tracking.
   2. Disable Block All Cookies.
4. Navigate to Settings >  Salesforce and enable Allow Cross-Website
   Tracking.

   You have completed the process. You have updated your iOS
   settings. You can use the Salesforce mobile app to send and sign with
   Docusign on your Apple iOS device.

title: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
heading_path: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
text:
Learn to fix the "User is not the Owner of the Lock" error when editing a Gen or
envelope template.

I tried to edit a Docusign Gen or envelope template. An error displayed:
The user is not the owner of the lock. The template is locked by
another user or in another application.

I cannot edit the template or save changes.

title: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
heading_path: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
Resolution: Re-Login to Salesforce or Empty Your Browser Cache
text:
Cause: You closed the browser tab while editing a template
without selecting Save and Close. You opened another
browser tab and tried to edit the template you closed.

1. Select the Salesforce user icon and select Log
   Out from the dropdown menu.
2. Log back in to Salesforce.
3. Try to edit your Gen template again.
4. If the error displays, empty your browser cache and re-login.

   You have completed the process. You have removed the template
   lock. You can edit or save changes to the Gen template or envelope
   template.

title: Learn About Using the Default Docusign for Salesforce Tabs
heading_path: Learn About Using the Default Docusign for Salesforce Tabs
text:
Learn to find and use the default Docusign Apps Launcher tabs.

A set of tabs displays when you open Docusign Apps Launcher in your Salesforce
organization. These tabs display functions commonly needed by a Docusign for Salesforce administrator.

![A screenshot displays the default Docusign tabs: Setup, Gen Templates, Envelope Templates, Logs, Envelopes, and Status.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-default-Docusign-for-Salesforce-tabs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2ODgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.zhiBln6mwYKkMgrMIEHV622BbbLQ05Q2mWiVLYUwPWQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the default Docusign tabs: Setup, Gen Templates, Envelope Templates, Logs, Envelopes, and Status.")

You can access these tabs by [opening Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).

[Docusign Setup](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=cay1654320876587.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Docusign Setup is the landing page for administering your Docusign account. You can manage Docusign
    users, components, templates, and more. Depending on the apps you purchased, you
    see a custom Docusign administrator dashboard.
    Gen customers see different administrator controls than
    CLM customers.

[Docusign Gen Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=fsc1646690056619.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create, edit, and delete Docusign Gen templates from this
    page. Users can generate agreements by selecting a Gen template button on a
    Salesforce record.

[Docusign Envelope Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=scr1646715662157.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create, edit, and delete Docusign envelope templates from this
    page. Users can send envelopes for signature by selecting an envelope template
    button on a record.

[Docusign Logs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=epu1646716431620.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View the DEBUG, INFO, WARN, and ERROR logs of envelopes sent from Salesforce
    records.

[Docusign Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nbq1646756159307.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View the details of all Docusign envelopes sent from
    Salesforce records.

[Docusign Status](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bek1646762309217.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View the status (Sent, Completed, and so on) of all Docusign
    envelopes sent from Salesforce records.

Select any tab to view the page.

title: Access the Docusign Gen Templates Tab to Build and Edit Gen Templates
heading_path: Access the Docusign Gen Templates Tab to Build and Edit Gen Templates
text:
Learn to access the Docusign Gen Templates tab in Salesforce.

Gen templates allow users to select a button on a record to instantly generate a contract. Use this procedure to access the Docusign Gen Templates tab in your Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
2. Make any selection:

   - [New](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zgw1621457735347.html&_LANG=enus): Create a new Gen template.
   - [Change Owner](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sut1646711991124.html&_LANG=enus): Change the owner of a Gen template.
   - [Import](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=juw1586134252760.html&_LANG=enus): Import up to 50,000 records at a time.
   - [Rename](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gww1586134337518.html&_LANG=enus): Change the name of a Gen template.
   - [Edit](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=xcs1646713533209.html&_LANG=enus): Add or edit documents, Salesforce fields, and Docusign fields in a Gen template.
   - [Delete](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=mbd1586134342525.html&_LANG=enus): Delete a Gen template.

   You have completed the task. You accessed the Docusign Gen Templates tab. From this page, you can create and edit Gen Templates.

title: Add Users to the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: Add Users to the Docusign Login Permission Set
text:
Learn to use the Docusign Login permission set to give Docusign access to your Salesforce users.

The Docusign Login permission set helps you manage Docusign access for your Salesforce users.

Important:

- The Docusign Login permission set is available in Docusign Apps Launcher v3.5 and higher.
- When you add an existing Docusign user to this permission set, Docusign imports the user's permissions.
- Administrators can use the Docusign Login permission set with their
  single sign-on (SSO) identity provider and Docusign
  just-in-time provisioning. Just-in-time provisioning lets users create their
  own Docusign accounts in Salesforce by entering their SSO
  credentials.

Use this procedure to add users to the Docusign Login permission set:

1. Select Docusign Login.
2. Select Manage Assignments.
3. Select Add Assignments.
4. Select the check box next to each user that you want to add.
5. Select Assign.
6. Select Done.

You have completed the process. You have added one or
more users to the Docusign Login permission set.

title: Add a Filter for Related List Recipients in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Add a Filter for Related List Recipients in an Envelope Template
text:
You can add filters to Related Lists recipients in the Add Recipient dialog box of a Docusign envelope template. This allows you to only add Related List recipients to an envelope if
certain conditions are met.

You can add filters to related lists recipients in the Add Recipient dialog box of a Docusign envelope template. This allows you to only add related list recipients to an
envelope if certain Salesforce record conditions are met.

There are two types of filter groupings:

- Any: "OR" statements that apply the filter if any one of your
  conditions is met:![A screenshot displays the Related List Any dialog box options with filter conditions and controls.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Related-List-Any-modal-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMjUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.IztGtzTukU6xHZdf-wGds68MywxgunqbvfKbeLwV4d4&_LANG=enus)
- All: "AND" statements that apply the filter only if all of the
  conditions are met:![A screenshot displays the Related List All dialog box options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Related-List-All-modal-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMjUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.IztGtzTukU6xHZdf-wGds68MywxgunqbvfKbeLwV4d4&_LANG=enus)

Table 1. ADD FILTER ICONS

| Icon | Name | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Copy Row | Copy a row of conditions and paste it below. |
|  | Delete Row | Delete a row of conditions. |
|  | or | Add a new blank row of "OR" conditions. |
|  | and | Add a new blank row of "AND" conditions. |

Table 2. ADD FILTER OPTIONS

| Name | Action |
| --- | --- |
| All | A group of conditions that add Related List recipients if all listed conditions are met |
| Any | A group of conditions that add Related List recipients if any listed conditions are met |
| equals | A Salesforce field exactly matches your condition |
| contains | Any part of a Salesforce field contains your condition |
| starts with | A Salesforce field begins with your condition |
| ends with | A Salesforce field ends with your condition |
| does not equal | A Salesforce field is not present in your condition |
| greater than | A Salesforce field (number, date, currency) is greater than your condition |
| less than | A Salesforce field (number, date, currency) is less than your condition |
| less than or equals | A Salesforce field (number, date, currency) is less than or equals your condition |
| greater than or equals | A Salesforce field (number, date, currency) is greater than or equals your condition |
| APPLY | The Any/All dropdown menu header |
| IF | The first condition you add |
| OR IF | Any additional "OR" conditions you add |
| AND IF | Any additional "IF" conditions you add |

title: Learn About Creating Docusign Envelope Templates in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Creating Docusign Envelope Templates in Salesforce
text:
Learn about building Docusign Envelope Templates to automate your
Salesforce eSignature process.

You can build Docusign Envelope Templates to automate your Salesforce
electronic signature process. The envelope template process works in the following
manner:

1. An administrator builds a template and a custom button.
2. A user selects the custom button on a Salesforce record to create a Docusign envelope.
3. The user sends the envelope for signature.

Your users select an envelope template button and are guided through a pre-built
Docusign sending experience. This ensures that your users always build envelopes
with the correct content.

Example: A Fresh Software sales representative wants to send a
contract to a customer. The representative selects the envelope template button on
an opportunity. The envelope template opens, displaying Fresh Software's standard
contract, recipients, and options. The sender can accept the default information or
make changes before sending. When recipients finish signing the envelope, Docusign saves a copy of the completed contract to the Salesforce record.

You can create the following types of envelope templates:

[Default](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a default envelope template to lead senders through a guided sending experience.")
:   This template type gives senders the most options for creating an envelope. Senders can
    add documents, choose recipients, and add fields before sending. These
    templates give your users more freedom to add content, but lack the
    speed of Quick Send templates.

[Quick Send](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a default envelope template to lead senders through a guided sending experience.")
:   This template type gives senders a faster way to create envelopes. Senders can only make
    minor changes. Quick Send users cannot add signer recipients, documents,
    Docusign fields, or Salesforce fields.

[Place Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a default envelope template to lead senders through a guided sending experience.")
:   This template type skips past the documents and recipients steps and brings senders to the
    place fields step. Senders can still choose to add and edit documents,
    recipients, and envelope options on the place fields step.

[Send Now](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a default envelope template to lead senders through a guided sending experience.")
:   This template type sends a pre-built envelope when senders select the button on a record.
    Senders are prevented from editing the envelope.

[Person Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hng1636580114162.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build an envelope template using the Salesforce Person Account object.")
:   Administrators can build envelope templates using the Salesforce Person
    Account object.

[Salesforce Experience Cloud](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ole1611646232631.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Using this template type, Salesforce Experience Cloud users can self-sign documents from
    records. The signed document can be routed to other recipients.

[Linked Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=rwo1660537941538.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Using this template type, administrators can create new envelope templates by linking to
    existing Docusign eSignature templates.

heading_path: Learn About Creating Docusign Envelope Templates in Salesforce
text:

:   Using this template type, administrators can create new envelope templates by linking to
    existing Docusign eSignature templates.

[Server Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nsh1649096506753.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how you can add options to an envelope template from a Docusign server template.")
:   Using this template type, administrators can add options to envelope templates that are
    not available in the Salesforce interface. Administrators build
    templates in Salesforce, then log on to Docusign to
    add options that are only visible there.

[Cloned Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xfb1664308717428.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to clone one or more envelope templates to use in another Salesforce organization.")
:   Administrators can use this feature to create new templates that are similar to existing
    templates. Administrators download templates and upload them to either
    the same organization or another. Administrators can also use cloning to
    copy sandbox templates to their production organization.

title: Enable Standard Docusign CLM Federated Security
heading_path: Enable Standard Docusign CLM Federated Security
text:
Learn how to enable standard Federated Security in your Docusign CLM
account.

Federated Security uses a standard mapping between Salesforce and Docusign security settings:

Table 1. Standard Federated Security Mapping

| Salesforce Security Access | CLM Security Access |
| --- | --- |
| Read | View |
| Edit | View and Edit |
| Delete | View, Edit, and Delete |

Users with Read access on a Salesforce record are given
View access to the CLM content on that page.

Users with Salesforce Edit access are given View
and Edit access in CLM.

Users with Salesforce Delete access are given View,
Edit and Delete access in CLM.

Important:

- Having No Access rights in Docusign CLM prevents
  Salesforce users from seeing CLM content
- Salesforce users must have at least View rights to
  all Salesforce folders in the CLM account
- Docusign recommends that all Salesforce users are given **View, Edit, and Delete**
  access to CLM folders. Federated security defaults to the most restrictive
  access. So a user with **Delete** access in Salesforce and **View**
  access in CLM would only have View access in CLM. Giving all Salesforce
  users **View, Edit, and Delete** access lets administrators manage all
  security through Salesforce.
- If a user logs in to CLM with the CLM username linked to their Salesforce
  user, Federated Security is respected.
- Creating separate CLM credentials (not tied to a Salesforce user) is not recommended. It
  circumvents the Federated Security model.

Use this procedure to enable standard Federated Security mapping in your Docusign CLM
account:

1. Log in to your Docusign CLM account as an administrator.
2. Select the Admin tab.
3. Select Connected Apps at the bottom of the left
   navigation pane.
4. Select the Manage link in the Salesforce tile.
5. Check the Enable federated security box under the
   Security header.

   ![Federated Security enabled.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Federated-Security-enabled.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5MDYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.OiRSgDjL_mr-9CSszIJTl-8AcWXjTfR-7yrCxO5mSXw&_LANG=enus)
6. (Optional):

   - New object mapping: Set custom security
     mappings.
   - Cache time: Change how often Docusign CLM syncs
     with Salesforce.
7. Select the Save button on the bottom of the page.

The process is complete. Salesforce Federated Security is enabled
on your CLM account. Salesforce users have access to Docusign CLM content security based
on their Salesforce Profile or Role security. No separate security model is needed for
Salesforce users in your CLM account.

title: Change the Sending Experience of an Envelope Template
heading_path: Change the Sending Experience of an Envelope Template
text:
Learn to change the sending experience of an envelope template.

Use this procedure to change the sending experience of an envelope template:

1. Sending Experience: Select an option:

- Default: Senders can add documents, specify recipients, and
  place fields before sending
- Quick Send: Senders can only add non-signing
  recipients
- Place Fields: Senders start at the place fields step,
  skipping the documents and recipients steps.

  Note: Senders
  can still choose to add and edit documents, recipients, and envelope options on
  the place fields step.
- Send Now: Senders do not see any controls. The envelope
  sends immediately.

2. Select the Next button in the upper-right.
3. Select Save and Finish on the
   Custom Button page.

You have completed the process. You have switched the sender
experience of the envelope template. Senders who click the Docusign custom button see
either the default or quick send experience.

title: Configure Standard eSignature Fields
heading_path: Configure Standard eSignature Fields
text:
You can customize Docusign eSignature fields that you add in the template creation
process.

You can customize Docusign eSignature fields that you add in the template creation process.
Options include:

- Required
- Read Only
- Add additional signers
- Add tooltips
- Customize data labels
- Add validation types
- Add character limits

Here's how to access the eSignature fields page in an existing Envelope Template:

1. Access the Envelope Template Place Fields tab:
   1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
   2. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MlYbn8CWcERuvsjD6H8zPIMSJftiYMZVNxV7YE780LY&_LANG=enus) dropdown menu next to a
      template name.
   3. Select Edit from the menu. The Docusign Envelope
      Template creation page opens.
   4. Select the Place Fields tab.![Place Fields tab](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Place-Fields-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MlYbn8CWcERuvsjD6H8zPIMSJftiYMZVNxV7YE780LY&_LANG=enus "Place Fields tab")
2. Use the actions in the menu.
3. Select the Save button when changes are complete.

   **Result**:
   The process is complete. You have added or edited fields in your Envelope
   Template.

Here are the standard Docusign signature fields and how to configure them for your
Envelope Template:

ADD RECIPIENT VIEWS

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/genacesignermenu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MlYbn8CWcERuvsjD6H8zPIMSJftiYMZVNxV7YE780LY&_LANG=enus)

Use this dropdown menu under the Docusign Fields header to add additional recipient
views to the template. Each recipient can have a customized version of the same
document, with different Docusign fields and placements specifically crafted for
them.

1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MlYbn8CWcERuvsjD6H8zPIMSJftiYMZVNxV7YE780LY&_LANG=enus) and select **Edit Recipients** from the dropdown
   menu.
2. Select the Add Another button to add additional recipient
   views.

title: Configure Standard eSignature Fields
heading_path: Configure Standard eSignature Fields
text:
jEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MlYbn8CWcERuvsjD6H8zPIMSJftiYMZVNxV7YE780LY&_LANG=enus) and select **Edit Recipients** from the dropdown
   menu.
2. Select the Add Another button to add additional recipient
   views.

   Note: You may add more than one additional recipient.
3. Enter a name for the signer in the **Name of Placeholder (Required\*)** text

4. **(Optional)**: Select the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/genacex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MlYbn8CWcERuvsjD6H8zPIMSJftiYMZVNxV7YE780LY&_LANG=enus) button to the right of a signer to remove that

**SIGNATURE FIELD**

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/genacesignature.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MlYbn8CWcERuvsjD6H8zPIMSJftiYMZVNxV7YE780LY&_LANG=enus)

Add this field to the place on a document that the recipient will add their
eSignature. The field can be customized to toggle to a different recipient, be
required or optional, add a tooltip and add a custom data label.

Select the Signature field on the template document. The Signature
dialog box opens.

**Options available:**

- Signer Select the dropdown to select the signer to which
  the field will apply. (Signer 1, Signer 2, etc.)
- Required Field: Uncheck box to make the field optional
- More Details: Select button at the bottom to expand the
  list of options![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/genacemoredetails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MlYbn8CWcERuvsjD6H8zPIMSJftiYMZVNxV7YE780LY&_LANG=enus)
- **Tooltip**: Enter custom text to show the user the purpose of the
  field.
- Data Label: Link Docusign fields. Data entered in one linked field
  populates to the others.
- Less Details: Select button to collapse the added
  options.
- X: Select the X in upper-right of
  the dialog box to close the options list.

title: Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to add new currency types to your Salesforce organization.

You can add new currency types to your Salesforce organization. Adding currencies lets users
select different currency types when generating documents with Docusign Gen.

Example: Your Salesforce organization uses U.S.
Dollar as the default currency. Your users want to generate
documents with Euro as the currency. You add
Euro to your organization's currency types. Your Gen
users set the currency to Euro, and Docusign generates contracts with Euro as the
currency.

Note: You must add at least one currency to your organization to use
template-level [Gen date and
currency formatting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kqf1686095608923.html&_LANG=enus).

Use this procedure to add a new currency to your Salesforce organization:

1. Edit the Company Information page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4MDEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ygpQz8IuPOczETymv10z0xY95SyBVM_K-YU5RSnIWUc&_LANG=enus) icon and
      choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Company Information in the
      Quick Find field.
   3. Select Edit.
2. Select the Activate Multiple Currencies check box and
   select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the Activate Multiple Currencies box selected on the Salesforce Company Information page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Activate-Multiple-Currencies-box-selected-on-the-Salesforce-Company-Information-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4MDEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ygpQz8IuPOczETymv10z0xY95SyBVM_K-YU5RSnIWUc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Activate Multiple Currencies box selected on the Salesforce Company Information page.")

   The Company Information page opens.
3. Select Currency Setup and select
   New.

   The New Currency page opens.
4. Add a new currency:
   1. Currency Type: Select a new currency from the
      dropdown menu.
   2. Conversion Rate: Enter the conversion rate from
      your default currency to this currency. Example:
      .092.
   3. Decimal Places: Enter the number of digits
      displayed to the right of the decimal point. This setting overrides the
      decimal places setting on all currency fields for users with this
      currency. You can choose between 0 and 6 digits.
   4. Select Save.

You have completed the process. Users can change
the Currency field on a record to the new currency. Gen users can
select the new currency before generating documents.

title: Check the Activation Status of a Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Check the Activation Status of a Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to check the activation status of a Docusign CLM, Gen,
Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.

Newly-created Docusign users must select an activation link before using Docusign apps in
Salesforce. You can check the activation status of newly-created users on the Docusign User
Management page.

Use this procedure to check the activation status of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or
eSignature for Salesforce user:

Check the Status column:

- Active: The user has activated their Docusign account
- Pending: The user has not activated their Docusign account

Tip: Pending
users should check their junk mail folder if they cannot find their Docusign activation email.

You have completed the process. You have verified the
activation status of your Docusign Users. Pending users must [activate their Docusign user accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus).

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
text:
Learn to fix the issue that prevents linking an envelope template and Gen
template.

I built a Gen template and tried to link it to an envelope template. On the Create
Button page, I searched in the Envelope
Templates field. The envelope template that I wanted to link did not
display in the results.

I built the Gen template and envelope templates on the Opportunity object, so I should be able
to link them.

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
Resolution 1: Switch to a New Source Object and Switch Back
text:
Cause: Docusign is not reading the Gen
template data source correctly.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open the Gen template Select
   Source page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ktj1621660808301.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a different object from the Salesforce
   Object dropdown menu and select
   Next.
3. Select Back.
4. Select the original object from the Salesforce
   Object dropdown menu and select Save and
   Close.
5. [Open the Gen template Create
   Button page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=way1621663319299.html&_LANG=enus).
6. Envelope Templates: Search for and select your
   envelope template.
7. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have reset the source object
   in your Gen template. You can link the envelope template in the
   Create Button step.

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
Resolution 2: Use the Developer Console to Fix the Error
text:
Cause: Docusign is not reading the Gen
template data source correctly.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open the Salesforce Developer
   Console](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.code_dev_console.htm&type=5).
2. Update the dfsle\_SourceObjectc on an affected
   dfsleGenTemplate\_c to the correct Application
   Programming Interface (API) value.

   You have completed the process. You used the Developer Console
   to reset the source object in your Gen template. You can link the
   envelope template in the Create Button
   step.

title: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
heading_path: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
text:
If administrator credentials from a Docusign Demo account are used to connect Docusign and Salesforce, you get the error: There were some problems with your document
generation when reaching the Preview Documents step of the template creation
process.

Identified an intermittent issue with creating an envelope template when a Docusign Demo
account is connected to Salesforce. If administrator credentials from a Docusign Demo account are used to connect Docusign and Salesforce, you get the error:
There were some problems with your document generation
when reaching the Preview Documents step of the template creation process. This
results in the following issues:

- Recipients' Docusign Roles are replaced with Salesforce Roles
- Docusign signature fields for those recipients are deleted from the Envelope
  Template

**Salesforce Roles Delete Docusign Role and Signature Fields from Envelope
Template**

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Add a Docusign recipient to an Envelope Template.
2. Add a Salesforce recipient from a Related List (like
   OpportunityContactRole).
3. Save the Envelope Template.
4. Start the envelope creation process with eSignature for Salesforce.
5. Advance to the Recipients step. Recipients have their Docusign Roles replaced with
   Salesforce Roles.

   Note: This only applies to recipients with Salesforce Roles
   (Decision Maker, Evaluator, etc.).
6. Advance to the Prepare and Send step. Docusign recipients' signature
   fields are missing.

   **Result**: Both the recipient's Docusign Role and
   eSignature tags are missing from the template.

title: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
heading_path: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
**Resolution 1: Remove the Role field from the Salesforce Contact**
text:
1. Visit the Salesforce record page containing the Contact Role.
2. Find the Contact Roles section.
3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2ODIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MpdrFZEb4Bx8HtLdgTP-qA23ei15HVUiwALJERBEOwQ&_LANG=enus) to the left of the Contact to be
   edited.
4. Select Edit from the dropdown menu. The Edit
   Contact Role dialog box opens.

   ![Edit Salesforce Role](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Edit-Salesforce-Role.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2ODIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MpdrFZEb4Bx8HtLdgTP-qA23ei15HVUiwALJERBEOwQ&_LANG=enus "Edit Salesforce Role")
5. Select the Role dropdown menu.
6. Select --None-- from the list.
7. Select the Save button.

   ![Clear Salesforce Role](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Clear-Salesforce-Role.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2ODIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MpdrFZEb4Bx8HtLdgTP-qA23ei15HVUiwALJERBEOwQ&_LANG=enus "Clear Salesforce Role")You are returned to the Contact Roles section. The
   Role field is now empty.

title: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
heading_path: Document Preview Error: "There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation"
**Resolution 2: Disconnect Docusign from Salesforce, clear cache and reconnect**
text:
1. Select the Docusign Setup tab.
2. Select the Disconnect link on the Docusign Setup tab in the upper-right
   of the page.
3. Log out of Salesforce.
4. Clear your browser cache.
5. Log back in to Salesforce.
6. Open Docusign Apps Launcher as an administrator. The Connect Docusign to Salesforce page opens.
7. Follow the instructions to [Connect Salesforce and
   Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=zwk1586134361708.html&bundleId=ygy1586134394050&_LANG=enus).

   Note: Connect using the same Docusign Demo administrator
   credentials.

   The process is complete. The recipient's Salesforce Role no
   longer conflicts. Docusign Roles and signature fields remain as expected
   when using an Envelope Template to send for signature.

title: Automatic AutoPlace Field Syntax
heading_path: Automatic AutoPlace Field Syntax
text:
Here are the automatic AutoPlace fields you can assign to Salesforce roles.

You can add automatic AutoPlace fields to envelope template documents.

These fields merge Docusign fields into your envelope.

You do not need to create, configure, or modify any Docusign fields to use
automatic AutoPlace fields.

Here are the automatic AutoPlace fields you can use:

| Field Type | Signer 1 | Signer 2 | Signer 3 | Signer 4 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Signature | \s1\ | \s2\ | \s3\ | \s4\ |
| Initial | \i1\ | \i2\ | \i3\ | \i4\ |
| Optional Initial | \oi1\ | \oi2\ | \oi3\ | \oi4\ |
| Name | \n1\ | \n2\ | \n3\ | \n4\ |
| Company | \co1\ | \co2\ | \co3\ | \co4\ |
| Title | \t1\ | \t2\ | \t3\ | \t4\ |
| Date Signed | \d1\ | \d2\ | \d3\ | \d4\ |

Here are some sample signer and AutoPlace field combinations:

- **Signer 2 signature field**: Enter **\s2\** in the document.
- **Signer 3 company field**: Enter **\co3\** in the document.
- **Signer 1 date signed field**: Enter **\d1\** in the document.

title: Learn About Using Envelope Events To Automatically Trigger Updates to Salesforce Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Envelope Events To Automatically Trigger Updates to Salesforce Fields
text:
You can set up your envelope template to automatically update up to 25 Salesforce
fields when envelope events occur. This is useful when you want changes that occur in
the signing process to automatically update your Salesforce records.

There are five
envelope events that can trigger Salesforce field changes:

Table 1. Envelope Event Types

| Envelope Event | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Envelope Sent | A Salesforce field updates as soon as an envelope is sent. |
| Envelope Delivered | A Salesforce field updates as soon as an envelope is received. |
| Envelope Completed | A Salesforce field updates only when all signatures are completed. |
| Envelope Declined | A Salesforce field updates if a recipient declines to sign. |
| Envelope Voided | A Salesforce field updates if an envelope is voided. |

Note:

- You can add up to **five events** for each envelope event type. So you
  can add up to five Envelope Sent events, five
  Envelope Delivered events, and so on.
- You can add a maximum of **25 envelope events** to an envelope template.
- Envelope events of the same type can not have the same Field
  Name value.

  For example, two Envelope
  Completed events can not use
  Description as the Field Name.

Important:
Docusign can not check these fields for validation rules.
If user input violates validation rules, the field
update fails. Test your template before using it to ensure it does not contain errors.

title: Create an Envelope Template Using the Salesforce Person Account Object
heading_path: Create an Envelope Template Using the Salesforce Person Account Object
text:
Learn how to build an envelope template using the Salesforce Person Account
object.

Person accounts are Salesforce objects that store information
about people by combining certain account and contact fields into one record.

The Person Account object is available in these Salesforce
editions:

- Professional
- Enterprise
- Performance
- Unlimited
- Developer

Note: You must enable Person accounts before you can use them.
Visit the Salesforce help site to learn how to enable Person accounts.

You can build a Docusign envelope template using the Salesforce Person
Account object.

Person Account is a part of the Salesforce
Account object.

When you select a Salesforce object at the start of the envelope template creation,
Person Account is not listed.

To build a Person Account envelope template, you must select Account as the data
source.

Use this procedure to build an envelope template using the Person Account object:

- [Step 1: Add a Template Name and Select the Account Object](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bkx1636643368884.html&_LANG=enus "The first step of the process involves naming the template and selecting the Salesforce Data Source.")
- [Step 2: Add Documents to Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hya1596146614785.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add documents to your envelope template.")
- [Step 3: Add Recipients to Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ooo1596146674305.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add recipients to your envelope template.")
- [Step 4: Create Salesforce Merge Fields for Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jdl1611042391715_1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Salesforce merge fields to add to your envelope template.")
- [Step 5: Place Docusign and Salesforce Fields on Your Template Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ywv1596146733047_1.html&_LANG=enus "The fifth step of the Envelope Template creation process involves placing Docusign and Salesforce fields on the document text.")
- [Step 6: Add Options Like Quick Send and Writeback](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ktu1596146796958.1.html&_LANG=enus "How to create reminders for recipients, set an expiration date, enable the Quick Send experience, have a Salesforce status changed when a signature process is complete, and more.")

- [Step 8: Remove the Person Account Custom Button from the Accounts Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jte1636651796525.html&_LANG=enus "The eighth step of the process involves removing the custom button from the Salesforce Account layout.")

writeback feature.

title: Step 2: Add Documents to Your Template
heading_path: Step 2: Add Documents to Your Template
text:
Learn to add documents to your envelope template.

Tip:

- [This video demonstrates the steps in this task](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6171919055001).
- [This topic demonstrates the advanced options available
  in this step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dad1595995063747.html&_LANG=enus).

In this step, you can add documents to your template. Word and PDF files are most commonly
used. These documents provide the text for the agreements that your users create
with this template.

You are not required to upload a document from your device at this step. You can select
Attach documents from Salesforce record to add documents
from a Salesforce record. However, if a record does not contain matching Salesforce
documents, users cannot create envelopes.

Use this procedure to add documents to your envelope template:

1. (Optional) Upload files from your device: Choose a method to add documents. You
   can add up to ten documents.
   1. Select Upload Document.
   2. Follow the prompts to add content from your device.
   3. Select Done.
2. (Optional) Upload documents from Salesforce:
   1. Select the Attach documents from Salesforce
      record check box.
   2. Choose an option from the dropdown menu:

      - Latest Document: Upload the latest
        document from the record.
      - File Name Contains: Upload documents from
        the record that match the file name keywords you enter.
      - All Documents: Upload all documents from
        the record.
3. (Optional) Drag documents to change their order:![A screenshot displays a document moving up in the order of uploaded files.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/DAL_Envelope_Template_drag_documents.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4MjMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.tdxnCRj54m-rcZNYkuIReCLANXGhwgJGmNvEhyu332U&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a document moving up in the order of uploaded files.")
4. Select Next.

title: Docusign CLM and Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Compatibility
heading_path: Docusign CLM and Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Compatibility
text:
Learn how you can use Docusign Gen and eSignature for Salesforce with your Docusign CLM
account.

The Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps contains Docusign CLM, Docusign Gen, Docusign Negotiate, and Docusign eSignature for Salesforce.

In the past, Docusign CLM users did not have access to the Docusign Gen and eSignature
for Salesforce apps in the CLM interface. This has changed.

**Why is eSignature for Salesforce available in CLM?**

CLM users can already send envelopes for electronic signature with the standard Docusign eSignature platform. The eSignature for Salesforce app opens up Salesforce-specific
signature functions that are unavailable to standard Docusign users.

**When should I use eSignature for Salesforce?**

- When you want to use Docusign Envelope Templates to deliver pre-built eSignature
  experiences
- When you want to add eSignature capability for Salesforce Experience Cloud
  users
- When you want to add eSignature custom buttons to Salesforce layouts
- When you do not want generated documents saved in CLM folders

**When should I use standard Docusign eSignature?**

- When sending for eSignature in a standard (non-Salesforce) CLM account
- When sending a file for eSignature from the CLM Files section of a Salesforce
  record

**What can Docusign CLM users do in eSignature for Salesforce?**

- Use the eSignature Configuration page on the Docusign Setup tab
- Create, add, edit, and delete eSignature for Salesforce Envelope Templates
- Enable a System Sender, which allows users who are not members of the Docusign account to send and sign envelopes

title: Docusign -Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Docusign-Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
As a Gen for Billing administrator, you see an Unable to uninstall
package error when uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher.
You must first remove extra relationships, permission sets, and Apex class components from
your organization.

As a Gen for Billing administrator, you see an Unable to uninstall
package error when uninstalling Docusign Apps
Launcher. You must first remove extra relationships, permission sets, and Apex class
components from your organization.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. You remove the standard Docusign-Salesforce dependencies (Docusign components, permission set assignments, and so
   on.)
2. You navigate to Setup > Installed
   Packages in your Salesforce organization.
3. You select the Uninstall link next to the Docusign
   Apps Launcher package of apps.
4. You follow the Salesforce prompts to finish the uninstallation process.

**Result**: You see an Unable to uninstall package error
message and an extra list of Gen for Billing dependencies to be removed.

title: Docusign -Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Docusign-Salesforce Relationships Prevent Gen for Billing Administrators From Uninstalling Docusign Apps Launcher
Resolution: Remove all Gen for Billing dependencies before uninstalling the package
text:
1. Access the Installed Packages page as a
   Salesforce administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3MTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.vGOEjwQaNnz0zGfRHE7L3BB2vBbQy9-mPYfD7kG0OMY&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Installed Packages in the Quick
      Find field.
   4. Select the Installed Packages link in the
      left column.
2. Select the Uninstall link next to the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps.
3. Follow the Salesforce prompts to finish the uninstallation process. The
   Unable to uninstall package error message
   appears, with a list of Docusign-Salesforce dependencies to be removed.
4. Remove all standard Docusign-Salesforce dependencies
   (Docusign components, permission set assignments,
   and so on.)
5. Remove the additional Gen for Billing
   dependencies:

   - InvoiceTriggerGenBilling: Delete this
     trigger from the Apex Trigger Detail page.
   - Docusign Log: Delete this custom object
     from the Fields & Relationships page
   - Docusign Billing Admin: Remove all users
     from this permission set
6. Follow the Salesforce prompts to finish the Docusign
   Apps Launcher uninstallation process.

   You have completed the process. You have removed all Docusign-Salesforce dependencies and uninstalled the
   Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps.

title: Change the Default Language for Gen, Negotiate, eSignature for Salesforce, and CLM
heading_path: Change the Default Language for Gen, Negotiate, eSignature for Salesforce, and CLM
text:
Learn to use Translation Workbench to let users choose their default Salesforce
language.

You can use Translation Workbench to let your users choose their default
Salesforce language. Docusign app content displays in the selected
language.

You can use the following options to enable language support in your Salesforce
organization:

- [Enable Translations for Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ehh1586134268380.html&_LANG=enus)

title: “This Account Lacks Sufficient Permissions to Use Merge Fields” Displays When I Add Envelope Template Merge Fields
heading_path: “This Account Lacks Sufficient Permissions to Use Merge Fields” Displays When I Add Envelope Template Merge Fields
text:
Learn to fix the “This account lacks sufficient permissions to use Merge Fields” error
when adding envelope template merge fields.

When adding a merge field to an envelope template, an error displays: This account
lacks sufficient permissions to use Merge Fields.

Example: I start building a new envelope template. I add a merge field on
the Merge Fields page and select Save. An error displays:
This account lacks sufficient permissions to use Merge
Fields.
Docusign does not save the new merge field.

title: “This Account Lacks Sufficient Permissions to Use Merge Fields” Displays When I Add Envelope Template Merge Fields
heading_path: “This Account Lacks Sufficient Permissions to Use Merge Fields” Displays When I Add Envelope Template Merge Fields
Resolution: Contact Your Docusign Representative
text:
Cause: Your Docusign account does not
include the Allow Merge Fields feature. Allow
Merge Fields is a paid feature and is not available on all
plans.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Contact your Docusign representative.
2. Request the Allow Merge Fields feature for your
   Docusign account.

   Note:
   - Allow Merge Fields is a paid feature
     and may incur additional charges.
   - Allow Merge Fields is not available on
     all plans. Web-only plans cannot gain access to this
     feature.

   You have completed the process. If your account allows the feature, you can add
   merge fields to envelope templates.

title: Add the Docusign Files Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Docusign Files Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add the Docusign Files component to a Salesforce page
layout.

Docusign Files is a component that displays all Docusign files associated with a record. Users can interact with Docusign
content without leaving Salesforce. You can add the Docusign Files
component to a Salesforce layout.

[Follow this how-to video](https://docusign.gallery.video/watch?videoId=6307338125112) or use this procedure to add the Docusign Files to a layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.VWWSjHjCWMb3i0qxSlsM9JqaLlVS_tXDw6hEVv8WuKs&_LANG=enus) and choose
   Edit Page from the dropdown menu.
2. Drag the Docusign Files component to the page layout.

   ![A screen capture displays the Docusign Files component being dragged to the Opportunity page layout.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Add-DocuSign-Files-component.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.VWWSjHjCWMb3i0qxSlsM9JqaLlVS_tXDw6hEVv8WuKs&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Docusign Files component being dragged to the Opportunity page layout.")
3. Activate the change to your layout:
   1. Select Activate.
   2. Select a tab to set your default options: Org Default,
      App Default, or App, Record Type, and
      Profile.
   3. Select Assign and choose options from the dialog box.
   4. Select Close.
4. Select Save.
5. Select the back arrow ![A screenshot of the Back arrow on the Salesforce Edit Page settings bar](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.VWWSjHjCWMb3i0qxSlsM9JqaLlVS_tXDw6hEVv8WuKs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Back arrow on the Salesforce Edit Page settings bar") to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the Docusign Files component to a Salesforce layout. Users can interact with Docusign files on Salesforce records.

Select the gear ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5MTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.-JJCXla0JG1GNIGxMyDtXIuikY21KVDUfIkK5d4BdwA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.") and choose Setup.

      field.
   3. Select the Person Account link in the left
      column.
   4. Select the Page Layouts link in the left
      column.
