   The Person Account Layout page opens.
2. Select the Mobile & Lightning Actions link in the
   left column of the Person Account Layout menu.
3. Drag the Person Account template button to the Salesforce Mobile and
   Lightning Experience Actions section.
4. Select Save.

   ![A screen capture displays the Salesforce Person Account Layout page with the Add Person Account custom button being dragged into the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience Actions section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Add-Person-Account-custom-button.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5MTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.-JJCXla0JG1GNIGxMyDtXIuikY21KVDUfIkK5d4BdwA&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Salesforce Person Account Layout page with the Add Person Account custom button being dragged into the Salesforce Mobile and Lightning Experience Actions section.")

   The Saving Layout message opens. The page refreshes.

You have completed the entire process. You have built a
template for the Person Account object, and added it to the Person Account page layout.

title: Step 6: Create Salesforce Merge Fields for Your Template
heading_path: Step 6: Create Salesforce Merge Fields for Your Template
text:
Learn to create Salesforce merge fields to add to your envelope template.

Tip:

- [This video demonstrates the steps in this
  task](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6171917638001).
- [This topic demonstrates the advanced options available
  in this step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qrg1611095157414.html&_LANG=enus).

In this step, you create custom Salesforce merge fields. These fields import data
from your Salesforce fields and add them to your agreements. You add these fields to
your uploaded documents in the Place Fields step.

Use this procedure to create custom Salesforce merge fields:

1. Select Add Field.
2. Create a new merge field in the Add Merge Field dialog box:
   1. Source Object: Select a primary Salesforce data
      source (Account, Opportunity, and so on) from the dropdown menu.
   2. Field: Select a Salesforce field from the
      dropdown menu. Additional child object dropdown menus may open. You can
      include up to four levels of child objects and two levels of related
      lists.
   3. Name: Keep or edit the field name.
3. (Optional) Field Properties: Select any available
   option:
   - Allow Writeback to Salesforce: Docusign updates Salesforce field data when signers make changes
     to an agreement.
   - Allow Sender to Edit: Senders can edit Salesforce
     merge field data.
   - Required Field: Signers must complete the
     field.
   - Read Only (for signer): Senders cannot edit the
     field.
4. (Optional) More Options: Select the arrow and choose any
   options:
   - Tooltip: Enter written guidance for the sender, like
     Adds the full billing address.
   - Anchor Text: Automatically place merge fields on key
     words in a document.
   - Validation: Set formatting rules for text, like
     requiring a xxx-xx-xxxx format for Social Security
     numbers.
5. Select Save to save the merge field, or Save
   and New to save and add another field.
6. Select Next.

   The page closes and the Place Fields page displays.

heading_path: Step 6: Create Salesforce Merge Fields for Your Template

title: Change the Docusign Template Used in a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Change the Docusign Template Used in a Salesforce Flow
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 6.12 or higher.

Note: The current API limitation is 100 generations per 10
minutes. If you want to increase your limit, [contact your Docusign representative](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-contact-DocuSign-Customer-Support) about
options.

You can edit a Flow to change the Docusign template or Salesforce object used in your Flow.
Use this procedure to edit a Salesforce Flow:

1. Open a Flow as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt in the center.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NjIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ffHnWz6AqyBrGnbfEkoQKeP1t4WV7HJA69Jh3QBZV6E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a gear icon with a lightning bolt in the center.") in the upper right and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Flows in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select a Flow from the All Flows list.
2. Edit the Docusign action in your Flow:
   1. Select your Docusign
      Apex Action
      ![A screenshot displays the blue and white Apex Action icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-blue-and-white-Apex-Action-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NjIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ffHnWz6AqyBrGnbfEkoQKeP1t4WV7HJA69Jh3QBZV6E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the blue and white Apex Action icon.").

      The action panel opens.
   2. Enter a new Docusign template ID or Salesforce
      Source Id.
   3. Select Done.
3. Select Save As New Version when you have completed editing your
   Flow.

   The Save as dialog box opens.
4. Select Save.
5. Select Activate.

   A message displays: Your automation was
   activated..

You have completed the process. You changed the template ID or
Salesforce object ID in your Flow.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
text: Learn how to use envelope custom fields in Salesforce envelopes.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
What is a Docusign envelope?
text:
A Docusign envelope is a digital container that you build that holds
documents, recipients, and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by
completing the fields assigned to them. Docusign marks the
envelope as Signed when all recipients complete their
fields.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. Users can add
recipients and documents manually, from a template, or dynamically from Salesforce
records, users, or contacts. Docusign saves the document to the
Salesforce record when all recipients finish signing.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
What is an envelope custom field?
text:
- Envelope custom fields (ECFs) let you tag and identify the envelopes you send for signature.
  Administrators create these fields, and senders enter values when preparing an
  envelope.
- Values are not visible to envelope recipients when signing. Only the sender, users with
  shared access, and account administrators see envelope custom fields and values.
- You can build envelope custom fields such as text fields or lists of values. You can mark
  ECFs as required or optional.
- Only the sender, users with shared
  access, and account administrators see
  envelope custom fields and values in the envelope details. Recipients do not see
  envelope custom fields and values.
- When senders prepare an envelope for signature, they must enter or select field values for
  all required ECFs. Senders can only advance to the next step if they complete
  all the required fields.
- You can enter up to 100 characters in an ECF text field. The values are visible to the
  sender and any account member with shared access.
- Users can enter ECF values from the Manage page to search for envelopes. Users can also
  generate an envelope report that includes ECF values.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do I create envelope custom fields?
text:
Log in to your Docusign account and go to Settings > Envelope Custom Fields to open the ECF page.

![A screenshot of the Docusign eSignature Settings page displays four envelope custom fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Envelope-custom-fields-in-DocuSign-Settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.xwN_JHrDRRYTHl2vjfClaP9sJnlkZb5stjGbdLFvI5w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign eSignature Settings page displays four envelope custom fields.")

Use this procedure to create envelope custom fields:

1. Select ADD FIELD.
2. Add a Field Name and configure the field.
3. Select ADD.

Important: Select Show field to envelope
creators, or the field does not display to senders.

![A screenshot of the Add a New Custom Fields dialog box displays the Field Name, Field Type, and List of values fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Show-field-to-envelope-creators-check-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.xwN_JHrDRRYTHl2vjfClaP9sJnlkZb5stjGbdLFvI5w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add a New Custom Fields dialog box displays the Field Name, Field Type, and List of values fields.")

See the [Custom Fields for
Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=mvu1583277383029.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information about adding and managing ECFs.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do I set default values for ECFs?
text:
You can customize envelope custom fields in your Docusign account. One
customization you can make is assigning default values to envelope custom fields.
Default values are data that you pre-populate for senders.

Set default field values and log into your Docusign account as an
administrator. Go to Settings > Templates >  (template name) >  Edit and add values for envelope custom fields.

Adding default values makes the envelope creation process more straightforward and
less error-prone for your users.

**Example**: Most of Fontara's customers operate in the United States. Fontara
administrators set the default value of the Country field to
United States and Currency to
Dollar. When a sender creates an envelope,
United States displays in the Country field, and
Dollar displays in the Currency field. Senders can change
the Country and Currency values if needed.

In this image, the Country and Currency
fields have default values. The Account Name field has no
default value.

![A screenshot of the Add envelope custom fields page displays the Account Name, Country, and Currency fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Default-values-for-envelope-custom-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.xwN_JHrDRRYTHl2vjfClaP9sJnlkZb5stjGbdLFvI5w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Add envelope custom fields page displays the Account Name, Country, and Currency fields.")

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do envelope custom fields work in Salesforce?
text:
You cannot create new envelope custom fields in Salesforce. You can only create ECFs in your
Docusign account. When you have created envelope custom
fields, Salesforce senders see ECFs on the Recipients page.

![A screenshot of the envelope Recipients page displays the Envelope Custom Fields section with four envelope custom fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Envelope-custom-fields-on-the-Recipients-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.xwN_JHrDRRYTHl2vjfClaP9sJnlkZb5stjGbdLFvI5w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the envelope Recipients page displays the Envelope Custom Fields section with four envelope custom fields.")

The following rules apply to envelope custom fields in Salesforce:

- Only envelopes sent from standard Send with Docusign
  buttons display envelope custom fields. Envelopes created from custom envelope
  template buttons do not display ECFs.
- Only envelope custom fields with Show field to envelope creators
  enabled display on the Recipients page.
- The Recipients page displays all envelope custom fields
  with Show field to envelope creators enabled.
- All "shown" ECFs from your Docusign account display on the envelope
  Recipients page.
- Senders must enter values for all required fields to advance to the Prepare &
  Send page.
- Senders can add envelope templates to their envelopes on the Documents
  page. Adding templates imports template documents, recipients, and signature
  fields to the envelope.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do default values work in Salesforce envelopes?
text:
When senders select multiple envelope templates, default values only display for the
last template in the list. So if a sender selects three templates, only the third
template's fields display default values. Fields from the first two templates are
blank.

In this image, default values from the Tally NDA template will display
for the sender. Envelope custom fields from the first two templates will be blank:
![A screenshot of the Select Docusign Template dialog box displays three envelope templates, each with a selected check box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Select-Docusign-Template-dialog-box-displays-three-envelope-templates-each-with-a-selected-check-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.xwN_JHrDRRYTHl2vjfClaP9sJnlkZb5stjGbdLFvI5w&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Select Docusign Template dialog box displays three envelope templates, each with a selected check box.")

Senders not adding templates see all ECFs from the Docusign account
displayed with blank values.

**Example**: Your Docusign account has 11 envelope custom fields with
Show field to envelope creators enabled. You select a
standard Send with Docusign button on a Salesforce record.
You do not add envelope templates on the Documents page. You
advance to the Recipients page and see all 11 envelope custom
fields from your Docusign account. All 11 fields are blank. You
can enter values for any of the 11 fields.

title: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Use Envelope Custom Fields in Salesforce
How do I create envelopes with envelope custom fields?
text:
There are two ways to create Salesforce envelopes with envelope custom fields:

- Add envelope templates:
  1. Select a Send with Docusign button on a
     Salesforce record.
  2. Select the Select Template button on the
     Documents page.
  3. Select each template that you want to add from the Select Docusign
     Template dialog box.
  4. Advance to the Recipients page.
     - All ECFs marked with Show field to envelope
       creators display
     - Fields from the *last* of the selected templates display
       default values
     - Fields from all other templates are blank.
  5. Enter values for all the required fields.
- Do not add envelope templates:
  1. Select a Send with Docusign button on a
     Salesforce record.
  2. Add documents on the Documents page.
  3. Advance to the Recipients page.

     All ECFs marked
     with Show field to envelope creators display.
     All values are blank.
  4. Enter values for all the required fields.

title: Upload a Document to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Upload a Document to an Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to upload documents from your device to an envelope template.

You can add up to ten documents to an envelope template.

Use this procedure to upload documents to an envelope template from your device:

1. Select the Upload button.
2. Follow the prompts to add a document from your device. You can add up to ten
   documents.

   Note: You can only upload one document at a time.
3. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have deleted the uploaded
   document.

title: Attach a Document From a Salesforce Record to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Attach a Document From a Salesforce Record to an Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to add Salesforce documents to an envelope template on the Documents
page.

You can add Salesforce documents to an envelope template on the
Documents page. This feature allows you to dynamically
attach documents from Salesforce Files to your envelope
template.

Use this procedure to attach documents from Salesforce Files to your envelope template

1. Select the Attach documents from Salesforce record check
   box.

   The Document Selection dropdown menu
   opens.
2. Choose an option from the Document Selection dropdown
   menu:
   - Latest Document: Uploads the latest document from the record
   - File Name Contains: Uploads documents from the record that match file name keywords you enter
   - All Documents: Uploads all documents from the record
3. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. Docusign dynamically adds documents from Salesforce
   Files to your envelope template.

title: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
heading_path: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
text:
Learn how to enable the SMS + Email feature and use it to send envelopes by text
message.

You can send envelopes to recipients by text message using the Email +
SMS option.

SMS delivery is a paid feature that is activated when your Docusign account manager
enables these settings:

- Allow SMS Delivery
- Signer can sign on a mobile device

Only your Docusign account manager can activate these settings for you.

Once activated, a Delivery dropdown menu displays in the Add Recipients
step of an envelope or envelope template:![A screenshot displays the Email + SMS Delivery option in the Delivery dropdown menu on the Add Recipients step of an envelope or envelope template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Email--SMS-Delivery-option.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwNTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.jpHWRwQDJSgyFBGRC1aLml5yOTAPlz4RCkfd_msiTYg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Email + SMS Delivery option in the Delivery dropdown menu on the Add Recipients step of an envelope or envelope template.")

title: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
heading_path: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
SMS guidelines:
text:
- You can use Email + SMS for up to four recipients per envelope. If you attempt to add
  more than four SMS recipients, an error message displays: You're limited to 4 SMS
  recipients.
- You must enter a space between the country code and the phone number.
- You do not need to enter a phone number for dynamic recipients, like contacts, that already
  have phone numbers saved.
- You can not send an SMS message without also sending the recipient an email.
- Email + SMS can only be used for these recipient types:
  - Needs to Sign
  - Needs to View
  - Receives a Copy
- You can only choose the Access Code option in the Add
  Access Authentication field when Email + SMS is selected.
- You cannot use Email + SMS with Docusign signing groups.

title: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
heading_path: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
SMS number formatting:
text:
You can use the following phone number formats:

- 9251111111 (country code defaults to 1)
- +1 9251111111
- +1 925-111-1111
- +1 (925)-111-1111

Note: If you enter a number format that is not
allowed, an error message displays. You must update the number format to save
the recipient to the envelope or template.

title: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
heading_path: Send an Envelope by Email or Text Message (SMS)
SMS country code formatting:
text:
- You must enter a space between the country code and phone number.
- Customer phone numbers are stored in Salesforce in two format types:
  - **United States and Canada**: (+1 PhoneNumber) format
  - **All other regions**: (+CountryCode PhoneNumber) format
- If you enter a phone number with the wrong country code format, 1 is
  automatically added as the country code.
- The (+CountryCode PhoneNumber) format is required when adding SMS + Email to a By
  Role recipient (not a Users/Contacts recipient).

title: Step 8: Remove the Person Account Custom Button from the Accounts Layout
heading_path: Step 8: Remove the Person Account Custom Button from the Accounts Layout
text:
The eighth step of the process involves removing the custom button from the Salesforce
Account layout.

Use this procedure to remove the custom button from the Salesforce
Account layout.

1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5MTIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.RT1FJVBXF1mk0yHJtPBWrIAoseVr6byWqU1EXlYKgfM&_LANG=enus) next to the
   Person Account custom button that you built.
2. Select Delete from the dropdown menu.

   The are you sure? dialog box opens.
3. Select Delete from the dropdown menu.

   The are you sure? dialog box closes. The page
   refreshes.

You have completed step eight. Move to step nine to add the custom
button from the Person Account layout.

title: Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device
heading_path: Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device
text:
Learn how to activate a new device for use with your Docusign account.

You must enter a verification code to access your Docusign account on a new device. You must verify your device again if
you empty your browser cache.

Use this procedure to activate your Docusign account on a new device:

1. Enter Docusign credentials when prompted.

   The Login from a new device dialog box
   displays.
2. Select Email. Docusign sends a verification code to your
   email address.
3. Open the Verify a New Device email from Docusign. Check
   your junk mail folder if you do not see the message in your Inbox.
4. Copy the verification code from the email.
5. Enter the verification code in the Login from a new device
   dialog box.
6. Select Confirm.

You have completed the process. You can access your Docusign account on this device.

title: Add a Custom Signing Button With URLFOR Scripting to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add a Custom Signing Button With URLFOR Scripting to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn how to user custom URLFOR scripting to add a custom button to a Salesforce layout.

You can use Docusign URLFOR scripting to create signing buttons without building an envelope
template. URLFOR buttons also let you build signing experiences not available in the
Docusign envelope template user interface. You can use [pre-built scripts provided by Docusign, or create
your own](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nxl1649872087756.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use URLFOR scripting to add custom Docusign eSignature for Salesforce buttons to object layouts."), using Docusign parameters. You can then add a custom button
with Docusign URLFOR scripting to a Salesforce layout. Users who select this button
on a record are guided through your custom signing experience.

Use this procedure to build a URLFOR custom button and add it to a layout:

1. Select New Button or Link.

   The New Button or Link page opens.
2. Complete the required steps on the page:
   1. Label: Enter a name for your custom button.
   2. Name: Select the field and accept the default name that
      populates.
   3. Display Type: Select the Detail Page
      Button radio button.
   4. Behavior: Keep the default or select another option from
      the dropdown menu.
   5. Content Source: Keep the default selection of
      URL.
   6. Paste [custom Docusign
      URLFOR script](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nxl1649872087756.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use URLFOR scripting to add custom Docusign eSignature for Salesforce buttons to object layouts.") in the field under the Select Field
      Type dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays configuring a URLFOR button in the New Button or Link settings.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Configure-URLFOR-New-Button-or-Link-settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2MjEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.-v55C0fsaenSbhmK7c2HOYysrfIrwwp2SFS8LIKzp0Q&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays configuring a URLFOR button in the New Button or Link settings.")
   7. Select Save.

      A message opens: The new custom button will not be displayed
      to users until you add it to the page layout.
   8. Select OK.

   The page refreshes, showing the new button details.
3. Select the Page Layouts link in the left column.

   The Page Layouts page opens.
4. Select the link to the layout you want to update.

   The object layout page opens.
5. Add your URLFOR custom button to the layout:
   1. Select Buttons in the left column of the layout
      menu.
   2. Drag the URLFOR custom button from the layout menu to the Custom
      Buttons section of the page layout.

      ![A screenshot displays dragging a URLFOR button to the Custom Buttons section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Drag-URLFOR-button-to-Custom-Buttons-section.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2MjEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.-v55C0fsaenSbhmK7c2HOYysrfIrwwp2SFS8LIKzp0Q&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays dragging a URLFOR button to the Custom Buttons section.")
   3. Select Save.

   The Saving Layout message opens. The page
   refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have created a custom
button with Docusign URLFOR scripting and added it to a Salesforce layout. Users who
select this button on a record are guided through your custom signing experience.

title: Learn About Rebuilding Your Docusign Connection After Changes to Your Salesforce Domain Name
heading_path: Learn About Rebuilding Your Docusign Connection After Changes to Your Salesforce Domain Name
text:
Learn to rebuild the connection between Docusign and Salesforce after domain name
changes.

All Salesforce organizations have a unique domain name called My Domain.
With My Domain, you can include your organization name in your URLs.

Example:
`https://mycompany.my.salesforce.com`.

You can set up a custom login page and policy, offer single sign-on, and enable social
account logins. My Domain also enables working in multiple Salesforce organizations in
the same browser at the same time.

You can change your domain name. The following are reasons for changing your domain name:

- You want to update your brand.
- You want to use [Enhanced Domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.domain_name_enhanced.htm&type=5).
- You want to enable partitioned domains.

When you change your My Domain name, your Salesforce-hosted URLs also change. You must
rebuild the connection to your Docusign account.

You can rebuild your connection to Docusign after a domain change with the
Sync Domains tool.

Important: The Sync Domains process may
take up to 72 hours to complete. Enable Redirect previous My Domain URLs to
your current My Domain in your Salesforce organization. Enabling
redirects during this time period prevents errors with your Docusign integration.

The rebuilding procedure differs depending on the Docusign apps that you
use:

- [Docusign Gen, Negotiate, eSignature, and
  eSignature Apex Toolkit customers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ppq1681966676237.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature.")
- [Docusign CLM and CLM Essentials for Salesforce
  customers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ynp1680122164220.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature. Sync Domains fixes issues with Enhanced Domains and any changes that you make to your Salesforce domain.")

title: Enable a System Sender to Let Non- Docusign Users Send and Sign Envelopes
heading_path: Enable a System Sender to Let Non-Docusign Users Send and Sign Envelopes
text:
Learn to enable a System Sender so that non-Docusign users can send and sign envelopes.

When you enable a System Sender, Salesforce users without a Docusign license can send and sign envelopes. When non-Docusign users send envelopes, the System Sender user displays as the sender.

Envelopes sent using a System Sender are owned by the System Sender user. Users sending through the System Sender cannot view, correct, resend, or void envelopes. Non-Docusign users can track envelopes sent by a System Sender in the [Docusign Envelope Status component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=all1586134265721.html&_LANG=enus).

Important:

- To use a System Sender, you must write Salesforce Apex code for the [Docusign Apex Toolkit for Salesforce](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/salesforce/salesforce101/apex-toolkit/). The Apex Toolkit only works with eSignature for Salesforce. You cannot use it with Docusign Gen.
- To send envelopes, non-Docusign users must be active Salesforce users with Docusign Sender permission set membership.

1. Go to eSignature >  Settings.
2. Select a Docusign administrator from the Enable System Sender dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays the eSignature Settings page with the Settings tab and Enable System Sender field highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Enable-System-Sender-field-populated-by-a-name-and-email-address.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.khQYfAZ7zbiHAn_xe-kyBXIaYpOn37QET1CShzcns_8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the eSignature Settings page with the Settings tab and Enable System Sender field highlighted.")
3. [Install and configure the Docusign connected app](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=tsk6312c996-938f-4f24-b11f-9da2f7887493.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to install and configure the Docusign connected app for your Salesforce organization.").

You have completed the process. You have enabled a System Sender for your Docusign for Salesforce integration.

title: Enable a System Sender to Let Non- Docusign Users Send and Sign Envelopes
heading_path: Enable a System Sender to Let Non-Docusign Users Send and Sign Envelopes
Next steps
text:
- Write Salesforce Apex code for the [Docusign Apex Toolkit for Salesforce](https://developers.docusign.com/docs/salesforce/salesforce101/apex-toolkit/).
- Grant Docusign Sender permission set membership to participating users.

title: Configure Salesforce Settings to Simplify Docusign App Administration
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Settings to Simplify Docusign App Administration
text:
Learn how to update settings in your Salesforce organization to ease the management and
use of your Docusign apps.

You can update settings in your Salesforce organization to ease the management and use of your
Docusign apps.

You can use the following settings to help Docusign apps work in your Salesforce
organization:

- [Salesforce Connected App Security Changes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=refbdaee1ac-b187-4df9-94f2-a094f9f47ef2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the actions that you must take to use Docusign apps after Salesforce security changes.")
- [Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=tsk6312c996-938f-4f24-b11f-9da2f7887493.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to install and configure the Docusign connected app for your Salesforce organization.")
- [Add Docusign IP Ranges to Salesforce to Prevent Errors](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=lip1592574174158.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add Docusign IP ranges to your Salesforce organization's Trusted IP Ranges.")
- [Use Manage App Policies to Allow or Prevent Docusign User Access](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=akc1598285785361.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use Manage App Policies to allow or prevent users from authorizing their Docusign access.")
- [Understanding Docusign Permission Sets in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xxm1623778416524.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the types and functions of Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets.")
- [Change the Default Language for Gen, Negotiate, eSignature for Salesforce, and CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ioe1586134255116.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use Translation Workbench to let users choose their default Salesforce language.")
- [Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce
  Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gzh1686882277803.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add new currency types to your Salesforce organization.")
- [Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce
  Record](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xrh1686885796196.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the type of currency Salesforce displays on a record.")
- [Federated Security for Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=plx1597758869161.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about how you can apply Salesforce security settings to your CLM content using Federated Security.")
- [Use Hyperforce with Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=swo1740519895843.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add domains to your allowlist so that Docusign apps work with your Hyperforce organization.")

title: Search for Docusign Users on the Docusign User Management Page
heading_path: Search for Docusign Users on the Docusign User Management Page
text:
Learn to use the search feature to find Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users.

You can use the Search by name or email field to find Gen,
CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users.

Use this procedure to search for Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users:

1. Search by name or email: Enter a name or email address
   in the field.

   ![Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature user management search](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Gen-Negotiate-and-eSignature-user-management-search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NDYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.m8ae0xJwYkDnCs8JnSYebTiTZZvZ8Xdxi6AWnzpobyQ&_LANG=enus "Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature user management search")

   Results update automatically as you enter characters.
2. Select ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Clear-results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NDYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.m8ae0xJwYkDnCs8JnSYebTiTZZvZ8Xdxi6AWnzpobyQ&_LANG=enus)
   to clear your search.

You have completed the process. You have used the search field on
the User Management page to find Docusign users.

title: View a Diagram of the Recipient Signing Order
heading_path: View a Diagram of the Recipient Signing Order
text:
You can select the View button to see a diagram of the recipient signing order, from
start to completion. This option gives administrators a quick snapshot of the signing
process.

You can select the View button to see a
diagram of the recipient signing order, from start to completion. This option gives
administrators a quick snapshot of the signing process.

Use this procedure to
view the Signing Order Diagram of an existing envelope template:

1. Select the View button.

   The Signing Order Diagram dialog box opens.
2. Select the X button in the upper-right of the dialog box to
   close the diagram.

   ![A screenshot displays the Signing Order Diagram.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplaterecipientsigningorder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMzQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.__crm9e_rC5xpOncV10613_6A4DYPh-Kwx4W-lexdO4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Signing Order Diagram.")
3. Select Save and Close to exit the
   template.

You have completed the process. You have viewed the recipient signing
order.

title: Enable or Disable Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: Enable or Disable Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
text:
Learn to enable Docusign as your eSignature app or disable it to use
another app.

You can disable the built-in Apps Launcher eSignature app and
use another app. You can also switch your eSignature app to Docusign
if another app is managing your signing experience.

1. Go to eSignature >  Settings.
2. Select an option:

   - Enable Built-In eSignature : Select
     Disabled. The button changes to
     Enabled.
   - Disable Built-In eSignature : Select
     Enabled. The button changes to
     Disabled.![A screenshot displays the eSignature Settings page with the Settings tab and Disabled switch highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Enable-BuiltIn-eSignature-switch-set-to-Disabled.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2ODEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.IEbnTHVRv-FxsCrSsdAWW99Npx3Dc4lkkIHh0L2GHo8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the eSignature Settings page with the Settings tab and Disabled switch highlighted.")

You have completed the process. You have turned on or off the Docusign eSignature app. If turned on, Docusign
manages your Salesforce eSignature actions. If turned off, another app manages your
Salesforce eSignature actions.

title: Step 6: Add Options Like Quick Send and Writeback
heading_path: Step 6: Add Options Like Quick Send and Writeback
text:
How to create reminders for recipients, set an expiration date, enable the Quick Send
experience, have a Salesforce status changed when a signature process is complete, and
more.

Use this procedure to add options to your Envelope Template:

Tip:

- [This video](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6171919622001) walks you through the process.
- [This topic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hwi1611095724834.html&_LANG=enus) shows you all of the
  advanced options available on this step.

1. Configure any of the following options:

- Reminders: Add a reminder to your recipients from the dropdown menu.
  Select Custom from the dropdown menu to add a custom
  reminder of up to 999 days.
- Expiration: Set a date or a number of days (up to
  999) after which the envelope expires.
- Sending Experience: Choose the options available for senders:
  - Default: Senders can add documents, choose
    recipients, and add fields before sending
  - Quick Send: Senders can add non-signing recipients, edit
    reminders, expiration, and the email subject and message fields
  - Place Fields: Senders are taken to the Place Fields step, skipping
    the documents and recipients steps.
  - Send Now: Skip all controls and send the envelope immediately
- Document Writeback: Select the check box for any
  option:
  - Save completed documents back to the Opportunity
    Record
  - Save certificate of completion (COC) document back to the
    Opportunity Record

  Important: Document, merge field, and data writeback will not work if the user has
  Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins turned on in their Salesforce
  profile.
- Field Updates: Automatically update Salesforce fields when envelope
  events occur:
  1. Select the + Add Update button. A row of fields displays.
  2. Envelope Event: Select an event
     (Envelope Completed, Envelope
     Voided and so on) that triggers a Salesforce field
     update.
  3. Field Name: Enter or select the Salesforce Field Name
     (Amount, Name, and
     so on) that is updated when the envelope event occurs.
  4. Field Update: Enter or select a value that will update the
     Salesforce field name when the envelope event occurs.![Field Updates events.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Field-Updates-events.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4MzgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.s6Ufi2_8r0IC1YtISzaGK5wCb6rdityRra-TSJ1U_K4&_LANG=enus "Field Updates events.")

     Note:
     - You can add up to **five events** for each envelope
       event type. So you can add up to five
       Envelope Completed events,
       five Envelope Sent events, and so
       on.
     - Five field updates per envelope event means that you can
       add a maximum of **25 envelope events**.
     - Envelope events of the same type cannot have the same
       Field Name value. For
       example, two Envelope Completed
       events cannot use Stage as the
       Field Name.

2. Select Next.

title: "You Are Not the Owner of the Envelope" Displays When I Try to Remind, View, Correct, or Void an Envelope
heading_path: "You Are Not the Owner of the Envelope" Displays When I Try to Remind, View, Correct, or Void an Envelope
text:
Learn to fix the "You Are Not the Owner of the Envelope" error.

While on vacation, I asked a coworker to void an envelope that I had sent. My coworker opened
the record and selected Void Envelope from the Docusign Envelope Status component.

You Are Not the Owner of the Envelope displayed, and they could
not void my envelope.

When they tried the View Envelope, Correct Envelope, and Send Reminder actions, and
the same error displayed.

title: "You Are Not the Owner of the Envelope" Displays When I Try to Remind, View, Correct, or Void an Envelope
heading_path: "You Are Not the Owner of the Envelope" Displays When I Try to Remind, View, Correct, or Void an Envelope
Resolution: Enable Envelope Sharing in Docusign
text:
Cause: You have not granted Share envelopes with
user permissions to your coworker. Without these permissions, you
cannot perform administrative actions in Gen, including adding new users and
creating templates.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Log on to Docusign as an administrator and go to Admin >  Users.
2. Next to the user, select Actions >  Manage Shared Access.
3. Select Share Access with Others.
4. Select one of the following permissions, and select
   Next:

   - Allow to manage on behalf of [User Name]: The user can view
     envelopes, view history, download files, and move envelopes into
     folders.
   - Allow to edit on behalf of [User Name]: The user can correct,
     resend, manage, edit, and void the user's envelopes.
   - Allow to send on behalf of [User Name]: The user can edit drafts,
     resend, void, manage, edit, send, and correct the user's
     envelopes.

   Note: Depending on your account plan, you might not have access to all
   permissions. Users that do not have sending permissions can only be
   granted access to manage another user’s envelopes. Inactive users or
   users pending activation cannot be granted access and cannot have
   their envelopes accessed by other users. Users with the send
   permission can send and manage envelopes on behalf of the sharing
   user. Users with the manage permission can manage envelopes on
   behalf of the sharing user, but cannot send.
5. Select the start and finish dates to control the duration of the shared
   access.

   The start date can be the current date or any future date. An end date
   is optional and can be any date equal to or after the start date. If an
   end date is specified, the access will expire on that date without any
   manual action needed. If you do not specify an end date, the access
   continues until someone manually removes it.
6. Select Next.
7. Select the check box next to a user and select
   Save.

   You have completed the process. You have shared access to your
   envelopes with another user. The user can take action on your envelopes
   without error. View the [Manage Shared Access to Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=imk1656607611659.html&_LANG=enus) topic for more information.

title: Edit Merge Fields From an Envelope Template
heading_path: Edit Merge Fields From an Envelope Template
text:
You can edit the details of merge fields in an envelope template at any
time.

You can edit the details of merge fields in an envelope template
at any time.

Use this procedure to edit merge fields in an envelope template.

1. Select the name of a merge field under the Name header.
   The Edit Merge Field dialog box opens.
2. Edit any merge field options.
3. Select the Save button. The Edit Merge Field dialog box
   closes. A message displays: Merge field updated.
4. Select the Save and Close button at the top-right of the
   page.

   You have completed the process. You have edited a merge field.

title: Use the Sender Option to Add the Sender of the Envelope as a Recipient
heading_path: Use the Sender Option to Add the Sender of the Envelope as a Recipient
text:
Learn to use the Sender option to add the sender of the envelope as a
recipient.

You can add the Sender recipient type to an envelope or envelope
template. Using the Sender option adds the sender of the envelope to the recipient
list. The sender is the person who selects the envelope template custom button on a
Salesforce record.

The following specifications apply to the Sender recipient type:

- You can place the sender anywhere in the recipient order (Signer 1, Signer 2, and so
  on.)
- Docusign turns off the In Person Signer option for Sender
  recipients.
- Docusign turns off the Delivery option (SMS or
  Email + SMS) for Sender recipients.
- You can only use the Sign Now option when a Sender recipient is first
  in the recipient order.

Use this procedure to add the sender of the envelope as a recipient in an envelope
template:

1. Select the name of the recipient you want to update.
2. Complete the following tasks in the Add Recipient dialog
   box:
   1. Select Sender in the left column.
   2. Action: Select a recipient action.
   3. (Optional) Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now): Select this check box if you want the sender to sign the envelope immediately after sending.

      Note: This
      option only displays when you select Needs to
      Sign as the action.
   4. Select + Add Role and enter a recipient role (Signer 1, Signer 2, and so on.)
   5. Select the Add button.
3. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You added the sender as a
recipient. When a sender creates an envelope using this template, Docusign adds them as
a recipient.

title: Preview a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Preview a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
text:
You can preview uploaded documents in the envelope template by following these
steps.

You can preview uploaded documents in the envelope template by following these steps:

1. Select from the arrow ![A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.RADSaUBLSvkzvJbISiUnmfZa_eESIcD8hQBOjaLLENM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.") dropdown menu next to a document.
2. Select Preview from the options.

   ![A screenshot displays the document options menu for an envelope template, highlighting the Preview option for an uploaded document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplatedocumentoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5ODEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.RADSaUBLSvkzvJbISiUnmfZa_eESIcD8hQBOjaLLENM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the document options menu for an envelope template, highlighting the Preview option for an uploaded document.")

   A new tab opens with the document preview page.
3. Select the document pane to open the preview in full-screen view.
4. Select the X in the upper-right of the page to close the
   preview.

   You have completed the process. You have previewed the uploaded
   document.

title: Place Fields Options: Actions Button
heading_path: Place Fields Options: Actions Button
text:
You can use the Actions button on the Place Fields page to access a menu of Docusign and
Salesforce options.

You can use the Actions button on the
Place Fields page to access a menu of Docusign and Salesforce options.

![A screenshot displays the Place Fields Actions button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Place-Fields-Actions-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxMzUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.5Ud9763DuiZyA9FnoMEC7e-TQy7uaaPpGVrwKxWs9TU&_LANG=enus)

Options include:

Table 1. Actions Button Options

| Menu Item | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Discard Changes | Undo all actions taken in the field placement process. |
| Delete | Delete the envelope template and all attached documents. |
| Edit Message | Change the email subject and email message seen by recipients. |
| Edit Recipients | Edit details and actions of existing recipients or add new recipients. |
| Edit Documents | View, replace, download, rename, delete, upload, or switch documents. |

title: Delete a Recipient From an Envelope Template
heading_path: Delete a Recipient From an Envelope Template
text:
Administrators needing to delete recipients from an existing envelope template can do so
at any time.

Administrators needing to delete recipients from an existing envelope template can do so at
any time.

Warning: If you remove a recipient that
has signature fields assigned to them, those fields will need to be removed or
re-assigned to another recipient.

**Example**: You deleteSigner 1 from
an envelope template. Document 1 in the template contains signature
fields assigned to Signer 1. The signature process will fail unless Signer
1's fields are removed from Document 1 or reassigned to another
recipient.

Use this procedure to delete recipients from an existing envelope template:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwNDUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.vN9yHkntiqoRYtOOcODV97upsPOxlVCMDtjMYw-fPMI&_LANG=enus)dropdown menu next to a recipient
   name.
2. Select Delete.

   ![A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplateeditrecipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwNDUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.vN9yHkntiqoRYtOOcODV97upsPOxlVCMDtjMYw-fPMI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.")

   The recipient no longer displays on the page.
3. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You have removed the recipient from the
template.

title: Add Docusign IP Ranges to Salesforce to Prevent Errors
heading_path: Add Docusign IP Ranges to Salesforce to Prevent Errors
text:
Learn to add Docusign IP ranges to your Salesforce organization's
Trusted IP Ranges.

You can add all Docusign IP ranges to your Salesforce
organization's Trusted IP Ranges.

Adding IP ranges ensures that all Docusign apps function without
issue in your Salesforce organization.

For more information, view the
following Salesforce documentation: [Set Trusted IP Ranges for Your Organization](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.security_networkaccess.htm&type=5).

Use
this procedure to add Docusign IP ranges to your Salesforce
organization's Trusted IP Ranges.

Note: Also use this procedure if your organization specifies [IP ranges on user profiles](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.login_ip_ranges.htm&type=5).

1. Access the Network Access page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3ODIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.E1oMhenvEA-X-PmHh_R0mWOl-AfIY5KkY1xgnlhcIzE&_LANG=enus)and
      choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Network Access in the Quick
      Find field.

   ![A screenshot of the Salesforce Network Access page displays Trusted IP Ranges.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Salesforce-Network-Access-Settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3ODIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.E1oMhenvEA-X-PmHh_R0mWOl-AfIY5KkY1xgnlhcIzE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Salesforce Network Access page displays Trusted IP Ranges.")
2. Add IP ranges from the Docusign Trust Center to
   your Trusted IP Ranges:

   - [eSignature for Salesforce ranges](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/esignature)
   - [Gen and CLM for Salesforce ranges](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/clm)
3. (Optional) [Add Salesforce IP ranges](https://ip-ranges.salesforce.com/ip-ranges.json) if your Salesforce organization is built on [Hyperforce code](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000388902&type=1).
4. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have added Docusign IP ranges
to your Salesforce organization's Trusted IP Ranges section.

title: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
heading_path: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
text:
Learn how to work around the file size limit error in an envelope template.

When I try to upload a file to the envelope template Documents step, I
sometimes see an error. This does not happen with every file.

Note: This error displays when administrators try to upload files larger
than 3 MB to an envelope template. However, you can access the same envelope
templates directly in your connected Docusign account. Templates
in your Docusign account have a file size limit of **50
MB**.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. I upload a file to an envelope template at the Documents step. The document fails to
   load.
2. An error displays: This file exceeds the maximum size
   limit.

   Result: The file is not added
   to the envelope template.

title: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
heading_path: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
Resolution 1: Upload the file to the envelope template in your connected Docusign account
text:
1. Select the Save and Close button for the
   envelope template in your Salesforce organization.
2. Log in to your Docusign account on another browser tab.
3. Select Templates.
4. Select the envelope template's name and choose
   Edit.

   A page with the template settings opens.
5. Select Upload and upload the file from your
   device.
6. Select SAVE AND CLOSE.

   ![A screenshot displays the Save and Close button on a Docusign template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Save-and-close-changes-to-your-DocuSign-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3MzksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.UqZmY55fq1OoJ1LqnpnCDrwEGJcsedvIQMVyWACkufw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Save and Close button on a Docusign template.")

   You have completed the process. You have avoided the Salesforce file size
   limitation by uploading the file to your Docusign
   template. This document is included in all envelopes that are sent from
   a Salesforce record using this template.

   Important: If you later edit this envelope template in
   Salesforce, you will get an error and will be unable to make
   additional changes. You must edit the template in your Docusign account.

title: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
heading_path: When I Upload a File to an Envelope Template, I See an Error: "This File Exceeds the Maximum Size Limit"
Resolution 2: Upload the file to Salesforce Files Home or Notes and Attachments
text:
1. Upload the file to your Salesforce Files Home, or
   the record's Notes and Attachments section.
2. Complete the envelope template creation process.
3. Select the envelope template custom button from a Salesforce
   record.
4. Select the Add from Salesforce button. The Add
   from Salesforce dialog box opens.
5. Check the box for the file saved in Notes and Attachments.
6. Select the Add button.
7. Complete the envelope sending process.

   The process is complete. Docusign attaches the large file from
   Notes and Attachments during the sending process.

title: Resend the Activation Email for a Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Resend the Activation Email for a Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to resend the activation email to a Docusign CLM, Gen,
Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.

When you create a Docusign user in Salesforce, Docusign sends an activation email. The user
selects a link in the email and activates their Docusign user. If the user cannot find their
original activation email, you can send them another.

Use this procedure to resend a Docusign activation email to a Salesforce user:

1. Access the Docusign
   Users page as an administrator:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account.
   2. Select Settings at the top of the page.
   3. Select Users in the left column.
2. Select Actions next to a user and choose Resend
   Invitation from the dropdown menu.
3. (Optional) Access Code: Add a security code in the dialog box
   that opens. You must send the access code to the user.
4. Select OK.

You have completed the process. You have sent a new
activation email to the inactive user.

title: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
heading_path: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
text:
This occurs when no phone number is specified for a Salesforce Users/Contacts or By Role
recipient with SMS authentication applied.

Recipients sometimes see a Security Requests From Sender dialog box when
they open envelopes sent from my envelope template. This dialog box presents the
recipient with a long list of security questions. The recipient can not open and
complete the envelope until they have answered all of the questions.

Note: This error only occurs for envelope templates matching these
criteria:

- The template contains at least one By Role or
  Users/Contacts recipient.
- At least one By Role or Contact recipient has
  SMS/Phone authentication applied, but with no
  phone number entered.
- There is no space entered between the country code and the phone
  number.
- The envelope template has the Send Now radio button
  selected on the Options step.
  ![Send Now radio button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Send-Now-radio-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3NTMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.q7nWbt87sFmi8s21Xwf7mG0ang3QXRbeoki_EC40OSk&_LANG=enus "Send Now radio button")

title: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
heading_path: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
Resolution 1: Upgrade to Docusign Apps Launcher version 4.3 or higher
text:
Docusign and Salesforce administrator

1. Log in to your Salesforce organization as an administrator.
2. [Follow these instructions to upgrade
   the Docusign Apps Launcher package to version 4.3 or
   higher.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus)

   You have completed the process. The new package will not allow
   senders to add recipients with SMS/Phone security applied and no phone
   number present.

title: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
heading_path: When Recipients Open Envelopes, They See a “Security Requests From Sender” Dialog Box
Resolution 2: Add a phone number to all By Role or Users/Contacts recipients
text:
Docusign and Salesforce administrator

Note: Only use this
resolution if you are unable to upgrade to the latest version of the
Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package.

1. Select the name of a By Role or
   Users/Contacts recipient. The Add Recipient
   dialog box opens.
2. Enter the recipient phone number if the
   SMS/Phone field is blank.

   Important: You must enter a space
   between the country code and the phone number.
3. Select the Add button. The Add Recipient dialog
   box closes.
4. Repeat the process for any other By Role or
   Users/Contacts recipient in the
   template.
5. Select the Save and Close button in the
   upper-right. The page refreshes. The envelope template page opens.

   You have completed the process. You have added the missing phone number to any
   By Role or
   Users/Contacts recipient. By Role and
   Users/Contacts recipients can open and complete envelopes without
   answering security questions.

title: Edit Server Template Options from an Envelope Template
heading_path: Edit Server Template Options from an Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to edit Docusign server template options by editing an
envelope template in your Salesforce organization.

Docusign server templates are signing experiences that you build in your Docusign account. You log on to your Docusign
account and select the Templates tab to build or access a
server template.

Docusign envelope templates are signing experiences that you build in your
Salesforce organization with the Docusign eSignature for
Salesforce application. You open Docusign Apps Launcher in your
Salesforce organization to build an envelope template.

Docusign offers some options in server templates that are not yet available
in envelope templates. However, you can add many of these options to envelope
templates from the server template interface.

Use this procedure to add, edit, or remove Docusign server template options
from the Docusign envelope template interface:

1. Select the information ![A screenshot displays a lowercase letter i in a gray circle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Server-template-information-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.swvJtoclQwPtxw39u7iMz0VGauioG8r6dy8HOUWu5Ps&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a lowercase letter i in a gray circle.") icon next to a recipient. Docusign only displays the information icon for recipients
   with server template options added. ![A screenshot displays the information icon on the Recipients step of an envelope template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Recipients-information-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.swvJtoclQwPtxw39u7iMz0VGauioG8r6dy8HOUWu5Ps&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the information icon on the Recipients step of an envelope template.")

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens. A message displays some of the added
   server template features. The message does not display the following options,
   even if added:
   - Custom email and language for each recipient
   - Knowledge-based authentication (KBA)
   - Phone Call
   - Docusign ID Verification
   - Standards-based signature (SBS)
   - Don’t allow senders to edit the recipient
   - Don’t allow senders to delete the recipient
   - Draw a new signature for each signature or initial field
2. Select View Template.![A screenshot displays the View Template link in the Add Recipient dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-View-Template-link-on-the-Add-Recipient-dialog-box.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.swvJtoclQwPtxw39u7iMz0VGauioG8r6dy8HOUWu5Ps&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the View Template link in the Add Recipient dialog box.")

   Your Docusign account opens to the template editing
   page.
3. [Add, edit, or remove supported server
   template options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nsh1649096506753.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how you can add options to an envelope template from a Docusign server template.").
4. Select SAVE AND CLOSE.

   A message displays: saving. The page refreshes.
5. Select the back arrow in your browser to return to Salesforce, and select the
   envelope template Recipients tab.
6. Select Save and Close.

   The page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have added, edited, or deleted
server template options for a recipient. Envelopes sent from the server template and the
envelope template each contain all the options that you added.

title: Edit or Delete a Docusign Envelope Template to Update Your Contract Process
heading_path: Edit or Delete a Docusign Envelope Template to Update Your Contract Process
text:
This guide demonstrates how to rename, edit, delete, or change the owner of an existing
envelope template.

This guide demonstrates how to rename, edit, delete, or change
the owner of an existing envelope template.

- [Rename template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bzu1596146928480.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to rename envelope templates.")
- [Edit template details](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xow1596147003565.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit envelope templates.")
- [Delete template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=yzr1596147036291.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to delete envelope templates.")
- [Change template owner](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ain1596147068228.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the owner of envelope templates.")

title: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
heading_path: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
text:
Learn to prevent sending duplicate envelopes when using Docusign in Salesforce
console view.

When I send an envelope from a record in console view, Docusign sends a duplicate
envelope.

Example:

1. I visit the Burlington Textiles record on a console view
   tab.
2. I select a Docusign eSignature button.
3. A Docusign console tab opens. I send an envelope from this
   tab.
4. I take any of these actions after sending the envelope:
   - I select the Burlington Textiles tab, then return to the
     Docusign tab.
   - I refresh the browser tab.
   - I select the Return to Record button on the
     sending tab.

   Result: Docusign sends a duplicate envelope.

title: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
heading_path: Duplicate Envelopes Send When I Use Docusign in Salesforce Console View
Resolution: Close the eSignature console tab
text:
Docusign envelope sender

Close the Docusign console tab that says
Your envelope was sent!

The signature process closes without sending a duplicate
envelope.

title: Envelope Template Error When Creating Merge Fields: “Failed To Connect to the Salesforce Service.”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error When Creating Merge Fields: “Failed To Connect to the Salesforce Service.”
text:
Disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account and Salesforce organization to reset
the connection. This will fix the issue.

When I try to add merge fields to a Docusign envelope template, I see one or more of
these error messages:

- Save Error: Some fields might be out of sync.
- Unable to connect to the service.
- Failed to connect to the Salesforce service.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
2. Click the Edit button in the upper-right of the
   page.
3. Select the Merge Fields tab.
4. Select the Add Field button.
5. Immediately click the Save and Close button on the
   Documents page.

   **Result**: I see an error message: Save Error:
   Some fields might be out of sync. Unable to connect to the service.
   Failed to connect to the Salesforce service.. I am unable to
   add new merge fields.

title: Envelope Template Error When Creating Merge Fields: “Failed To Connect to the Salesforce Service.”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error When Creating Merge Fields: “Failed To Connect to the Salesforce Service.”
Resolution: Disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account and your Salesforce organization
text:
1. Select the [Disconnect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus) link on the
   Docusign Setup tab in Salesforce.
2. Select the [Log in to Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus)
   button.

   You have completed the process. You have reset the connection
   between your Docusign account and Salesforce organization. You can now
   add merge fields to a Docusign envelope template.

title: Add a Send with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Add a Send with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to add a Send With Docusign action to your Salesforce Flow to
trigger envelope creation.

title: Add a Send with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Add a Send with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher v6.1 or higher to
use this feature.

You can build a Send With Docusign action to add envelope-sending
capabilities to a Salesforce Flow. The Send With Docusign action
lets your Flow trigger a Docusign envelope template that creates
and sends envelopes.

Example: You build a Record-Triggered Flow and link it to a Docusign envelope template. You configure the Flow to trigger
envelope creation when a record's stage changes to
Negotiation/Review. A user changes the stage of the
Burlington Textiles opportunity to Negotiation/Review. Docusign creates an envelope using data from the record and sends
it to Burlington Textile representatives.

Note:
Docusign does not support using the Sign
Now option when you add an In Person Signer to a
Flow.

Use this procedure to add a Send With Docusign action to a Salesforce
Flow:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, create or edit a Salesforce Flow.
2. Add a Send with Docusign action:
   1. Locate the point in your Flow where you want to add your action.
   2. Hover over the circle icon and select the plus +
      icon that displays.

      ![A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screen-capture-displays-the-Add-Element-plus-icon-in-a-Salesforce-Flow.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._1XjFBzX-1RcmidiFtG9hnfMwsFyBi-_zbFfHw1JuKI&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.")
   3. Add Element: Enter and select
      Send with Docusign in the search field.

   The Send with Docusign dialog box opens.
3. Add a label and API name for your action:
   1. Label: Enter Send with Docusign.
   2. API Name: Keep or change the default name.
4. Envelope Template ID: Add the ID of the envelope
   template used in the Flow.
   1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
   2. Select the envelope template that you want to use in the
      Flow.
   3. Copy the alpha-numeric template ID between the / marks at the end of the URL.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-template-ID-highlighted-in-the-envelope-template-URL.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._1XjFBzX-1RcmidiFtG9hnfMwsFyBi-_zbFfHw1JuKI&_LANG=enus)
   4. Paste the ID (without / marks) in the Envelope
      Template ID field.
5. Source Id: Choose the ID of the object your Flow is
   built on. For example, if your Flow is built on Opportunity, choose: Triggering Opportunity >  Opportunity ID.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Send_with_Docusign_flow_action.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._1XjFBzX-1RcmidiFtG9hnfMwsFyBi-_zbFfHw1JuKI&_LANG=enus)
6. Select Save As New Version > Save > Activate.

   A message displays: Your automation was
   activated.

You have completed the process. You added a Send With
Docusign action to your Salesforce Flow. Your Flow is connected to a Docusign envelope template. When conditions are met, your Salesforce
Flow triggers the Docusign envelope template to create an envelope.

title: Use Merge Fields To Customize the Email Subject and Message of an Envelope
heading_path: Use Merge Fields To Customize the Email Subject and Message of an Envelope
text:
Learn how to use merge field syntax to dynamically fill the Email
Subject and Email Message fields of a Docusign envelope.

You can use merge field syntax to dynamically fill the Email
Subject and Email Message fields of a
Docusign envelope. Merge field syntax can add this information from standard and
custom Salesforce objects.

Using merge field syntax is helpful if you want Docusign to fill email fields with Salesforce
record names or descriptions.

**Example**: You create an envelope template for the Opportunity
object. When users create envelopes, you want Docusign to add the record's description in the
Email Subject field.

You enter `{!Opportunity.Description}` in the Email
Subject field of your envelope template.

When a user creates an envelope, the Opportunity Description of that record is added
to the Email Subject.

Use this procedure to add merge field syntax to the Email
Subject and Email Message fields of an
envelope template:

1. Add merge field syntax to any field:
   - Email Subject: Add merge field syntax to
     dynamically add content from a record, like
     `{!Opportunity.Description}`.
   - Email Message: Add merge field syntax to
     dynamically add content from a record to the email message of an envelope.
     ![A screenshot displays merge field syntax used in the Email Subject and Email Message fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Merge-field-syntax-used-in-the-Email-Subject-and-Email-Message-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwNjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.qTIEtTK3W8IzUfirXWyai6euVILPnaPpN46a9Bud3ho&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays merge field syntax used in the Email Subject and Email Message fields")
2. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You have added merge field
syntax to the Email Subject or Email
Message fields of your envelope template. When users create envelopes
from this template, Docusign dynamically adds this information from the Salesforce
record.

title: Add SMS (Text Message) Delivery to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Add SMS (Text Message) Delivery to an Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to add the Email + SMS option to a recipient in your envelope
template.

Use this procedure to add the Email + SMS option to a recipient in your
envelope template.

Note: You can also use this procedure on the Recipients step when sending an
envelope.

1. Configure Email + SMS options, depending on the
   recipient type:

   Important: You must enter a space
   between the country code and the phone number.

   - Lookup Fields: Select Email + SMS from the Delivery dropdown menu
   - Related Lists: Select Email + SMS from the Delivery dropdown menu
   - Users/Contacts:
     1. Delivery: Select Email + SMS
        from the dropdown menu
     2. (Required) Phone Number: Enter
        the recipient's phone number
   - By Role:
     1. Delivery: Select Email + SMS
        from the dropdown menu.
     2. (Optional) Name: If known,
        enter the recipient's name.
     3. (Optional) Email: If known,
        enter the recipient's email address.
     4. (Optional) Phone Number: If
        known, enter the recipient's phone number. You can
        use Email + SMS for up to four (4) recipients per
        envelope.
2. Select a save option:
   - Add&New: Save the recipient and add another
   - Add: Save the recipient and close the dialog box

You have completed the process. You have added
theEmail + SMS option to a recipient in your envelope
template. When users send an envelope from this template, the recipient receives a text
message and an email. The recipient can choose to open the envelope using either method.

title: Compare the Differences Between Envelope Template Types
heading_path: Compare the Differences Between Envelope Template Types
text:
