Learn about the differences between the different envelope template types.

Sending Experience is a feature on the envelope template
Options page. You can use it to switch the options a sender
sees when creating an envelope.

You can use the following Sending Experience types:

- Default: Senders can add documents, specify
  recipients, and place fields before sending
- Quick Send: Senders can only add non-signing
  recipients
- Place Fields: Senders start at the place fields step, skipping
  the documents and recipients steps.
- Send Now: Senders do not see any controls. The envelope
  sends immediately.

The following comparison compares the sender options available for each template type:

| Envelope Template Sender Options | Default | Quick Send | Place Fields | Send Now |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Add documents | X |  | X |  |
| Add Docusign fields | X |  | X |  |
| Add Salesforce fields | X |  | X |  |
| Add standard recipients | X |  | X |  |
| Add non-signing recipients | X | X | X |  |
| Edit recipients | X | X | X |  |
| Edit email subject text | X | X | X |  |
| Edit email message text | X | X | X |  |
| Set signature reminders | X | X | X |  |
| Set signature expiration | X | X | X |  |
| Send for signature | X | X | X | X |

title: Use the Docusign Envelopes Tab to View the Details of an Envelope
heading_path: Use the Docusign Envelopes Tab to View the Details of an Envelope
text:
Learn how to view the details of envelopes sent from your Salesforce
records.

Docusign envelopes are bundles of information sent to recipients for
electronic signature. These include documents, recipients, and Docusign and Salesforce fields. You can use the Docusign
Envelopes page to view the details of envelopes sent from Salesforce
records.

Envelope entries include these details:

- Docusign Envelope name
- Email Subject
- Owner
- Salesforce Source ID
- Email Message
- Docusign envelope ID
- Sent status
- Remind signers via email
- Send reminder in N day(s)
- Remind every N day(s) until complete
- Expire/void Envelope
- Expire/void envelope in N day(s)
- Warn of expiration in N day(s) prior
- Created By
- Last Modified By

Use this procedure to view the details of a Docusign envelope in your
Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Envelopes tab.

   The Docusign Envelope page opens.
2. Select an envelope from the Docusign Envelope
   column.

   The Docusign Envelope details page opens for that
   item.

You have completed the task. You have viewed the details of
an envelope sent from a Salesforce record.

title: Clear User Settings Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Clear User Settings Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn to fix the CUSTOM\_SETTINGS\_LIMIT\_EXCEEDED issue with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

Salesforce Experience Cloud users sometimes see this error message when sending
envelopes: CUSTOM\_SETTINGS\_LIMIT\_EXCEEDED.

Users with too many stored access tokens see this error message. Access tokens can be stored
in custom settings without users or administrators knowing. Excessive access tokens
can violate Salesforce storage limits and prevent Experience Cloud users from
sending Docusign envelopes.

Administrators can use the Clear User Settings feature of the
TroubleShooting tool to delete access tokens for all users. Clear User
Settings deletes cached access tokens to free up storage.

Follow this procedure to use the Clear User Settings feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3OTQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.w6R-T4fnMMld-sOtr81T12r_k5bKVMKYIWbr4Duvel0&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3OTQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.w6R-T4fnMMld-sOtr81T12r_k5bKVMKYIWbr4Duvel0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview. The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Clear User Settings: Select
   Execute.

   A message opens: Successfully enqueued job. Check Apex Batch Jobs
   page and Docusign Logs for results.

You have completed the process. You have cleared the cached access
tokens. Salesforce Experience Cloud users can send envelopes again.

title: Access the Merge Fields Step of an Envelope Template
heading_path: Access the Merge Fields Step of an Envelope Template
text:
Learn to add, edit, or delete merge fields in an envelope template.

Follow this procedure to add, edit, or delete merge
fields in an envelope template:

Access the envelope template Merge Fields page as a
Salesforce administrator:

1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwOTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.uTaWkAxlOyNNpvInAkhrsdYhcs9dBQsx66CzkN-FP14&_LANG=enus) and choose View All.
2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
3. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
4. Select the arrow ![A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwOTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.uTaWkAxlOyNNpvInAkhrsdYhcs9dBQsx66CzkN-FP14&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.") next to a template and choose
   Edit.
5. Select the Merge Fields arrow. The arrow has no
   text until selected.

You have completed the process. You have accessed the merge fields
step of an envelope template.

title: I See an “eSignature Is Currently Disabled. You Can Enable It in Settings.” Error When I Try To Edit an Envelope Template
heading_path: I See an “eSignature Is Currently Disabled. You Can Enable It in Settings.” Error When I Try To Edit an Envelope Template
text: TEXTML log show error -112 on startup

title: I See an “eSignature Is Currently Disabled. You Can Enable It in Settings.” Error When I Try To Edit an Envelope Template
heading_path: I See an “eSignature Is Currently Disabled. You Can Enable It in Settings.” Error When I Try To Edit an Envelope Template
Out of disk space
text:
Company internal IT

1. Clean the temp folder, delete any dangling backup files.
2. Ask your IT to increase disk size.

title: When I Select a Docusign Button in Salesforce, I See an Error Message: "The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again."
heading_path: When I Select a Docusign Button in Salesforce, I See an Error Message: "The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again."
text:
Work around an error message when you select a Docusign object in Salesforce as a new
Docusign user.

I am a Salesforce user and just got assigned a Docusign user account.

When I select a Docusign button or component in Salesforce, I am asked to authorize.

I enter my Docusign and Salesforce credentials, but see an error message.

The message reads: The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session.
Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again.

I am unable to access Docusign apps and features in Salesforce.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Receive a user activation email from Docusign.
2. Create a Docusign password and activate my user.
3. Select a Docusign button or component in Salesforce.
4. Enter my Docusign and Salesforce credentials when prompted to
   authorize.

   **Result**: I see an error message: The link
   you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page,
   refresh it, and try again. I am unable to access Docusign apps and features in Salesforce.

title: When I Select a Docusign Button in Salesforce, I See an Error Message: "The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again."
heading_path: When I Select a Docusign Button in Salesforce, I See an Error Message: "The link you followed wasn’t valid for your session. Return to the previous page, refresh it, and try again."
Resolution: Select the "back" button on your browser and then refresh the page
text:
1. Select the "back" button on your browser.
2. Select the "refresh" button on your browser.
3. Select the Docusign object again.

   You have completed the process. You have finished the
   authorization process for Docusign objects in Salesforce. You can now
   use all Docusign apps and services to which your administrator granted
   you access.

title: Upgrade Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Upgrade Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn how to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce is a package that includes the following
Docusign applications:

- [Docusign eSignature for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000001taX4EAI&tab=e)
- [Docusign CLM for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000004e3VUEAY&tab=e)
- [Docusign Gen for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N3A00000FeGX6UAN&tab=e)

When you install any of these apps, Docusign installs the complete Docusign Apps
Launcher package. Docusign regularly releases updated versions of the Apps Launcher
package that include product enhancements and bug fixes. If you have an older
version of Apps Launcher installed, you can upgrade the package by visiting the
Salesforce AppExchange.

Use this procedure to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your
Salesforce organization:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, choose a Docusign app from the
   AppExchange:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2MjEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.5dRBnopyfAUm4bSaRdQyLEkbmZAvjX94DpB1Qo7t27M&_LANG=enus) and choose
      View All.
   2. Select Visit AppExchange >  Go to AppExchange.
   3. Enter `Docusign
      eSignature`,`Gen`, or
      `CLM` in the search field.
   4. Select an app and choose Get it Now when the app
      page displays.
2. Log in to the AppExchange with Salesforce administrator credentials:

   Note: Enter production credentials even if installing in a
   sandbox organization. These credentials are used to access the
   AppExchange.

   1. Select Log In on the LET'S GET
      STARTED page.
   2. Select Salesforce on the Hello
      Trailblazer! dialog box that displays.
   3. Enter Salesforce production administrator credentials on the page that
      displays and select Log In.

      The Where do you want to install this
      package? dialog box displays.
3. Confirm where to install the package:
   1. Install in This Org: Select Install
      Here.
   2. Select I have read and agree to the terms and
      conditions.
   3. Select Confirm and Install.

      The page refreshes. The Salesforce login page displays.
4. Install the Apps Launcher package:
   1. Enter production or sandbox administrator credentials and select
      Log In.
   2. Select Install for All Users.

      Note: If you want to choose Install for Admins Only, or Install
      for Specific Profiles, see this topic about [how Salesforce applies app permissions](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.distribution_installing_packages.htm&type=5).
   3. Select Upgrade.
   4. Select Done when the page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You upgraded the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps. Your Salesforce organization
contains the latest version of Docusign eSignature for Salesforce,
CLM, CLM Essentials, and Gen. You receive an email notification when Salesforce
completes the installation.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
text:
Learn how the Docusign Login permission set helps manage Docusign user access in Salesforce.

The Docusign Login permission set helps you manage Docusign access for your Salesforce users. Users with Docusign Login membership can authorize Docusign
app access themselves. This permission set lets you bypass the Docusign User Management tool and manage user activation with
Salesforce controls.

Important:

- The Docusign Login permission set is available in Docusign Apps Launcher v3.5 and higher.
- When you add an existing Docusign user to this permission set, Docusign imports the user's permissions.
- Administrators can use the Docusign Login permission set with their
  single sign-on (SSO) identity provider and Docusign
  just-in-time provisioning. Just-in-time provisioning lets users create their
  own Docusign accounts in Salesforce by entering their SSO
  credentials.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
When to use the Docusign Login permission set:
text:
Use the Docusign Login permission set in the following
scenarios:

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Administrator wants to use Salesforce controls
text:
A Salesforce administrator feels more comfortable using settings for permission sets than the
Docusign User Management tool. The administrator uses the Docusign Login permission set to give Docusign
app access to Salesforce users.

Note: This method only works for Salesforce users who already have an
active Docusign user in the connected Docusign
account.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Administrator disconnects or refreshes a Salesforce organization
text:
An administrator refreshes their Salesforce organization or disconnects and reconnects from
their Docusign account. Docusign deletes all
users not added to the Docusign Login permission set, and the administrator must
re-add them. Docusign
Docusign preserves all
users added to the Docusign Login permission set. The
administrator does not need to re-add these users after the disconnection or
refresh.

Note: Salesforce does not preserve Docusign users when you uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package.
Salesforce deletes the Docusign Login permission set when you
uninstall the package.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Users already exist and have permissions in the connected Docusign account
text:
A Docusign administrator has already created users and assigned permissions
in the connected Docusign account. The administrator adds
Salesforce users to the Docusign Login permission set. The users
do not receive activation emails. They activate their Docusign
access directly on Salesforce records by selecting a Docusign
component or custom button.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Users log on with single sign-on (SSO)
text:
An organization uses a single sign-on (SSO) provider for user logins. The administrator adds
Salesforce users to the Docusign Login permission set. Users do
not receive an activation email. Users select a Docusign component
or button on a record and use SSO credentials to authorize Docusign access.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
When to use Docusign User Management:
text: Use Docusign User Management in the following scenarios:

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Users do not exist in the connected Docusign account
text:
An administrator wants to add Docusign app access to a user that does not
exist in the connected Docusign account. In this case, the
administrator cannot use Docusign Login and must use User
Management.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Users exist in the connected Docusign account but lack permissions
text:
An administrator wants to add Docusign app access to an existing user from
the connected Docusign account. The existing Docusign user lacks needed permissions, like
Generator or eSignature User. In
this case, the administrator cannot use Docusign Login and must
use User Management.

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
text:
Learn how you can add options to an envelope template from a Docusign
server template.

You can build two types of Docusign eSignature templates:

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Docusign server templates
text:
Docusign server templates are signing experiences that you build in your Docusign account. You log on to your Docusign
account and select the Templates tab to build or access a server
template.![A screenshot displays the Docusign server templates page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Docusign-server-templates-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.J6Ty7bN2bTgawBcrWcvFGrZrW9gFusedHKEfz2OSabk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign server templates page.")

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Docusign envelope templates
text:
Docusign envelope templates are signing experiences that you build in
your Salesforce organization with the Docusign eSignature for
Salesforce application. You open Docusign Apps Launcher in your
Salesforce organization to build an envelope template.![A screenshot displays the envelope template tab.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the--envelope-template-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.J6Ty7bN2bTgawBcrWcvFGrZrW9gFusedHKEfz2OSabk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the envelope template tab.")

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Using server template options in envelope templates
text:
Docusign offers some options in server templates that are not yet
available in envelope templates. However, you can add many of these options to
envelope templates from the server template interface.

**Example**:

1. Create a Docusign envelope template.
2. Log in to your Docusign account. Your envelope template displays on the
   Templates page.
3. Add supported server template options to the envelope template.
4. Return to Salesforce and save your envelope template.

   The options you added to the server
   template now display in the envelope template. The previously
   unavailable server template features display when users send
   envelopes.

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Supported server template features
text:
You can add the following server template options that are not available in the envelope
template interface:

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Advanced options
text:
![A screenshot displays the Advanced options page of a Docusign server template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Advanced-Options-page-of-a-Docusign-server-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.J6Ty7bN2bTgawBcrWcvFGrZrW9gFusedHKEfz2OSabk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Advanced options page of a Docusign server template.")

- Recipient Privileges:
  - Allow recipients to sign on paper: Give
    recipients the option to sign manually
  - Allow recipients to change signing responsibility or assign a
    delegated signer: Recipients can change their status
    (Needs to Sign, Receives a Copy) or choose another person to sign for
    them
- Password: Create a password that senders must enter

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Add documents
text:
![A screenshot displays the three-dot menu with Set as Supplement, Replace, and other options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-threedot-menu-with-Set-as-Supplement-Replace-and-other-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.J6Ty7bN2bTgawBcrWcvFGrZrW9gFusedHKEfz2OSabk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the three-dot menu with Set as Supplement, Replace, and other options.")

- Upload: Add documents from your device or Box, Dropbox,
  Google Drive, and OneDrive
- More options:
  - Set as Supplement: Set a document as an
    attachment that does not require signing
  - Replace: Replace a document with one you upload
    from your device

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Add recipients
text:
![A screenshot displays the Add Recipients section of a Docusign server template with Advanced Settings highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Server-template-Customize--Advanced-settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.J6Ty7bN2bTgawBcrWcvFGrZrW9gFusedHKEfz2OSabk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add Recipients section of a Docusign server template with Advanced Settings highlighted.")

- Add From Contacts: Add contacts from your Docusign account to a template.
- Advanced Settings:
  - Draw a new signature for each signature or initial
    field: Recipients draw their signature or initials with
    a cursor
  - Don’t allow senders to edit recipient: Senders
    cannot change recipient data
  - Don’t allow senders to delete recipient: Senders
    cannot remove recipients from an envelope

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Add identity verification
text:
![A screenshot displays the Add Recipients section of a Docusign server template with Add Identity Verification highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Server-template-Customize--Add-identity-verification.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.J6Ty7bN2bTgawBcrWcvFGrZrW9gFusedHKEfz2OSabk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Add Recipients section of a Docusign server template with Add Identity Verification highlighted.")

- Phone Call: The recipient receives an access code by phone call. A
  fee is charged per usage.
- Allow recipient to provide phone number: The recipient
  can add their own phone number.
- Knowledge-based (KBA): The recipient answers a set of questions based
  on their public records. A fee is charged per usage.
- Docusign ID Verification: The signer identifies themselves with a
  valid government ID.

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Add message
text:
![A screenshot displays a Docusign server template with custom email and language highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Add-Message-section-of-a-Docusign-server-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.J6Ty7bN2bTgawBcrWcvFGrZrW9gFusedHKEfz2OSabk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Docusign server template with custom email and language highlighted.")

- Custom email and language for each recipient: Send the
  email message and attached documents in the recipient's language
- Email Subject: Add a custom subject in the recipient's
  language
- Email Message: Add a custom message in the recipient's
  language

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Unsupported server template features
text:
These options cannot be added to an envelope template from a server template:

- Template Usage: Change the options that are available to
  a sender
- Signs with Witness: A witness is present for the signing
  ceremony
- Sign with Notary: Require Notary approval for the signed document
- Send without fields: An envelope is sent with no
  signature fields
- Adding Access Code and SMS
  authentication to the same recipient
- Template Modification:
  - Don't allow senders to edit, add, or remove
    recipients
  - Don't allow senders to edit the subject, email, or private
    messages

title: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Features from a Docusign Server Template in an Envelope Template
Unsupported envelope template features
text:
These options cannot be added to server templates from envelope templates:

- Sending Experience: Change the options that are
  available to a sender
- Document Writeback:
  - Save completed documents back to the Salesforce
    Record: Envelope documents are added to a Salesforce
    record when the signature process is complete
  - Save certificate of completion (COC) document back to the Salesforce
    Record: Docusign adds a
    certificate of completion to a Salesforce record when the signature
    is complete
- Field Updates: Envelope events trigger updates to Salesforce
  fields

title: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
heading_path: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
text:
Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or
Login IP Ranges to fix issues with Restricted
IP errors in login history for your Docusign connected app.

If you see Restricted IP errors in login history for your Docusign connected app, there are two possible causes:

- The Salesforce organization has Multi-Factor Authentication for
  API Logins enabled on the user's' profile. This is
  incompatible with Docusign’s server-to-server API updates.
- Login IP Ranges are specified on the user's profile.
  This prevents API logins for the user from Docusign servers.

title: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
heading_path: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
Resolution 1: Disable Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3MDAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.g55M4Q8m7lMkAAN6qn_b6-OOrfDNS-C4-XeIFvzD8LE&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Edit button.
4. Uncheck the Multi-Factor Authentication for API
   Logins box.
5. Select the Save button.

   The process is complete. You no longer see Restricted
   IP errors in login history for your Docusign Connected
   App.

title: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
heading_path: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
Resolution 2: Disable Login IP Ranges on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3MDAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.g55M4Q8m7lMkAAN6qn_b6-OOrfDNS-C4-XeIFvzD8LE&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User) The
   Profile page opens.
3. Select the Login IP Ranges link at the top of the Profile
   page.
4. Select the Del link to the left of an IP
   range.
5. Select the OK button on the Are you sure you
   want to delete the IP Login Range dialog box.
6. Repeat the Del action for all remaining
   ranges.

   The process is complete. You no longer see Restricted
   IP errors in login history for your Docusign Connected
   App.

title: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
heading_path: “Restricted IP” Errors Show in Login History for Docusign Connected App
Resolution 3: Add the relevant Docusign endpoint IP addresses to the allow-list
text:
1. [Add Trusted IP Ranges for Docusign Gen for
   Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=lip1592574174158.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus).
2. Add any other restricted IP addresses shown in the user’s login
   history.

   The process is complete. You no longer see Restricted
   IP errors in login history for your Docusign Connected
   App.

title: Use the Disconnect Link to Disconnect and Reconnect Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: Use the Disconnect Link to Disconnect and Reconnect Docusign and Salesforce
text:
Use this procedure to disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account
from your Salesforce organization.

Occasionally, you must disconnect and reconnect your Docusign
connection. The following are reasons to disconnect and reconnect:

- You want to connect your Salesforce organization to a different Docusign account
- An administrator deleted your Docusign integration user
- [Salesforce applied enhanced domains to your organization](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000393816&type=1)

Warning:
Docusign recommends that you
[disconnect your Docusign account
and preserve user settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus). This option preserves your Docusign users and settings. After reconnecting, all Docusign users and permissions remain.

If you want to disconnect and reconnect without preserving user settings, you can
use the Disconnect link on the Docusign Setup page.
After reconnecting, you must rebuild all Docusign users and settings.

Use this procedure to disconnect and reconnect without preserving users and settings:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, [open Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select the Docusign Setup tab.
3. Select Disconnect. ![A screenshot displays the Disconnect link highlighted on the Docusign Setup page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Disconnect-link-highlighted-on-the-Docusign-Setup-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3NzksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.B7TiKTPCOCRIbJkaLJZup7c2l6mNHTR7lCO3lMnH9Ls&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Disconnect link highlighted on the Docusign Setup page.")

   The Disconnect Account and Remove Users dialog box displays.
4. Select Disconnect.

   Warning: Disconnecting Docusign from
   Salesforce disables Docusign for all Salesforce users. Disconnecting
   also deletes all Docusign user configurations. When you reconnect, you
   must rebuild all users and settings.

   The page refreshes. The Connect Docusign to Salesforce page
   displays.
5. [Reconnect Docusign and
   Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

You have completed the process. You have updated the
connection between your Salesforce organization and your Docusign account.

title: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
text:
To fix this issue, first select Disconnect on the Docusign Setup tab.
Then delete the Salesforce link in the Connect settings of your Docusign
account.

When I try to add merge fields to a Docusign envelope template, I see one or
more of these error messages:

- Save Error: Some fields might be out of sync.
- Unable to connect to the service.
- Failed to connect to the Salesforce service.

When I disconnect and reconnect my Docusign account in Salesforce, I still
have problems with some functions, like adding merge fields to a Docusign envelope template. Using the [Docusign TroubleShooting page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=rag1597638195677.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus)
to disconnect and reconnect does not fix the issue, either.

Note: This is not a typical scenario. Administrators can
normally select the Disconnect link on the Docusign Setup tab to
cleanly disconnect and reconnect.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Select the Disconnect link on the Docusign Setup tab.
2. Select the Log in to Docusign button.
3. Reconnect Salesforce and Docusign.
4. Open a Docusign envelope template.
5. Try to add new merge fields.

   **Result**: I see an error message:
   Save Error. Some fields might be out of sync. I
   am unable to add any merge fields.

title: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
Resolution 1: Disconnect Docusign and then delete the Salesforce link in the Connect settings of your Docusign account
text:
1. Select the [Disconnect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus) link on the
   Docusign Setup tab in Salesforce.
2. Log in to the connected Docusign account as an
   Administrator.
3. Select the Settings tab.
4. Select Connect on the left-hand navigation
   pane.
5. Select the Actions dropdown menu next to the
   Docusign Apps Launcher listing.
6. Select Delete from the list.
7. Select the Delete button on the Delete
   Connect Configuration dialog box.
8. Return to Salesforce and select the [Log in to Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus)
   button.

   You have completed the process. You have disconnected the account links in both
   your Docusign account and your Salesforce
   organization. You have then reconnected the accounts in Salesforce. You
   can now add merge fields to an envelope template.

title: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: I Still Have Problems Adding Merge Fields to an Envelope Template After Disconnecting and Reconnecting Docusign and Salesforce
Resolution 2: Reconfigure the Legacy Connect Configuration
text:
1. Log in to the connected Docusign account as the
   Connect for Salesforce integration user.
2. Go to Settings >  Integrations >  Connect
3. Select Actions and choose
   Edit from the dropdown menu.
4. Select Reconfigure Login.
5. Select OAuth and follow this link to [refresh the
   connection](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=psy1581360698967&topicId=voz1581360675739.html&_LANG=enus).

   You have completed the process. You have refreshed the Connect
   configuration between Docusign and Salesforce. You can
   now add merge fields to an envelope template.

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
text:
Learn how to use URLFOR scripting to add custom Docusign eSignature
for Salesforce buttons to object layouts.

Important:

URLFOR scripting is a custom solution that is not supported by Docusign
Support. Docusign Professional Services can help you build and
troubleshoot URLFOR custom buttons:

- [Docusign Professional Services
  Overview](https://www.docusign.com/sites/default/files/DocuSign-Professional-Services-Overview.pdf)
- [Packages and services available](https://www.docusign.com/sites/default/files/Enterprise%20Professional%20Services%20Overview.pdf)

You can create many different signing experiences with Docusign eSignature
for Salesforce. You can use a standard Send with Docusign
template, build an envelope template, or build a URLFOR custom button.

You can use URLFOR scripting to build custom signing experiences that aren't provided by the
application. You can add these custom buttons to Salesforce layouts.

URLFOR logic determines what custom actions are performed by the button. The following is a
sample syntax:

{!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Opportunity.Id,OCO =
'Tag'])}

Setting OCO to Tag brings the sender directly to the Prepare
& Send page, where they add Docusign and
Salesforce fields. The Tag field skips the sender past the
Documents and Recipients pages.

You can use the following URLFOR scripting to create customized sending
experiences:

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Custom Contact Note Map
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | CCNM= |
| Values | See Notes |
| Notes | Use this parameter to map a Salesforce Role to a specified Note.  Example: SF Role 1~Note 1;SF Role 2~Note 2;Role 3~Note 3;  The standard selections apply if you do not add a value. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id, CCNM = 'Decision Maker~This Note will be added just to the Decision Maker;Business User~This Note will be added just to the Business User' ] )} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign adds “This Note will be added just to the Decision Maker” to the Decision Maker's message.  Docusign adds “This Note will be added just to the Business User” to the personal message of the Business User. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Custom Contact Role Map
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | CCRM= |
| Values | See Notes |
| Notes | Use this parameter to map a Salesforce role to a Docusign role.  Example syntax:  CCRM = 'SalesforceContactRole1~DocuSignRole1;SalesforceContactRole2~DocuSignRole2'  The standard selections apply if you do not add a value.  Example use case:  An Opportunity's Contact Role Related List has three contacts with three different Salesforce roles:   - Decision Maker - Business User - Economic Buyer   Docusign only adds these three recipients when the CCRM URLFOR script executes. The following is the resulting role mapping:   - The Decision Maker contact becomes Signer 1 and is Signer 1 in the template - The Business User contact becomes Signer 2 and is Signer 2 in the template - The Economic Buyer contact becomes Signer 3 and is Signer 2   in the template |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id, CCRM = 'Decision Maker~Signer 1;Business User~Signer 2;Economic Buyer~Signer 3', DST = 'c41b225b-72ac-4727-bf3e-f9df1517a639' ] )} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign adds a Salesforce contact with the Decision Maker role as a recipient with the Signer 1 role.  Docusign adds the remaining contacts as recipients and assigns them to their respective recipient roles.  Docusign assigns fields associated with these roles based on template settings. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Custom Contact Type Map
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | CCTM= |
| Values | See Notes |
| Notes | Use this parameter to map a Salesforce role to a Docusign signer type.  Example syntax:  CCTM = 'SalesforceContactRole1~DocuSignRecipientAction1;SalesforceContactRole2~DocuSignRecipientAction2'  The standard selections apply if you do not add a value.  You can execute the script in a Salesforce object that has Contact Roles Related List.  You can use these Docusign recipient actions:   - Needs to Sign - Receives a Copy - Needs to View - Specify Recipients - Allow to Edit - Update Recipients - In Person Signer |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id, CCTM = 'Decision Maker~Needs to Sign;Business User~Receives a Copy;Economic Buyer~Needs to View;Economic Decision Maker~Specify Recipients;Evaluator~Allow to Edit;Executive Sponsor~Update Recipients' ] )} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign adds a Salesforce contact with the Decision Maker role as a recipient. Docusign assigns the Needs to Sign action to the recipient.  Docusign adds the remaining contacts as recipients and assigns their respective actions. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Custom Email Message
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | CEM= |
| Values | A text string with the email message. |
| Notes | The message string supports custom tags, such as Opportunity.Name. Example: CEM = 'Please sign this envelope for '+Opportunity.Name  The text string is limited to 2000 characters.  Docusign truncates anything beyond 2000 characters in the email message.  Upgrade to Docusign Apps Launcher 6.4 or higher to use line breaks, commas, and semicolons with the CEM parameter:   - \\n Add a line break to the text - \_COMMA\_ Add a comma to the text - \_SEMI\_ Add a semicolon to the   text   Example scripting:   ``` Hi_COMMA_\\nThis is a wonderful opportunity_SEMI_please sign.\\nThank you. ```   Example email message output:   ``` Hi, This is a wonderful opportunity; please sign. Thank you. ``` |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Opportunity.Id, CEM = 'Please sign this envelope for '+Opportunity.Name+''])} |
| Example Description | In this example, the customized email message reads, Please sign this envelope for followed by the opportunity name. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Custom Email Subject
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | CES= |
| Values | A text string with the email subject. |
| Notes | The subject text string supports custom tags such as these examples:   - User.FirstName: Sender's first   name - User.LastName: Sender's last   name - User.Email: Sender's email   Example: CED = 'Envelope re: Order Number:'+Opportunity.OrderNumber  Upgrade to Docusign Apps Launcher 6.4 or higher to use commas and semicolons with the CES parameter:   - \_COMMA\_ Add a comma to the text - \_SEMI\_ Add a semicolon to the   text   You cannot use \\n (line break) scripting with the CES parameter.  Example scripting:   ``` Welcome_COMMA_valued customer_SEMI_it's time to renew. ```   Example email message output:   ``` Welcome, valued customer; it's time to renew. ``` |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Lead.Id, CES =Lead.Name +', please sign this envelope'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, the customized email subject reads, (Lead Name), please sign this envelope. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Custom Field Setting
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | CFS= |
| Values | name1~value1~required1~show1,name2~value2~required2~show2,...,nameN~valueN~requiredN~showN |
| Notes | Use this parameter to create or set a value for an envelope custom field.  - (Required) name: You can enter a   custom field or add an envelope custom field from your Docusign account. - (Optional) value: The value you want   to specify for the name field. If you   do not add a value, Docusign leaves the   field blank. - (Optional) required: A boolean (0/1)   parameter specifying whether senders must add a value for   this field. If the field exists in your Docusign account, Docusign uses its required setting. - (Optional) show: A boolean (0/1)   parameter that indicates whether Docusign   shows the custom field to senders. If the field exists in   your Docusign account, Docusign uses its   show setting.   Existing fields: Docusign uses Show field to envelope creators and Make field required for envelopes values from the Docusign account.  New fields: Docusign uses the values that you define in the URLFOR script. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Opportunity.Id, CFS=‘OpportunityId~' + Opportunity.Id + '~1~0'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign creates a custom field called OpportunityId with a value set from Salesforce Opportunity.Id. This field is required, but Docusign does not display it to the sender. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Custom Recipient List
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | CRL= |
| Values | See Notes |
| Notes | Use this parameter to add recipients to an envelope.  You can add the recipient information manually or dynamically.  Possible values are:   - Email~; - FirstName~; - LastName~; - Role~; - SigningGroup~; - SignInPersonName~; - RoutingOrder~; - AccessCode~; - SMSAuthPhone~; - AccessCode~; - RecipientNote~; - SignNow~, - LoadDefaultContacts~1   You can specify multiple comma-separated recipients. But the length of the entire CRL string must be 1000 characters or fewer.  Example: Docusign adds two recipients from an Opportunity in this scenario:   - Recipient 1: A custom recipient list is created, with one   recipient associated with the opportunity's owner. - Recipient 2: Docusign adds this recipient with   predefined values: CRL =   'Email~'+Opportunity.OwnerEmail+';FirstName~'+Opportunity.OwnerFirstName+';  LastName~'+Opportunity.LastName+';RoutingOrder~1,Email~John.doe@docusign.com;FirstName~John;LastName~Doe;  AccessCode~1234;SMSAuthPhone~5105551212;RecipientNote~Please   sign this document,LoadDefaultContacts~1'   If you do not want to specify both First Name and Last Name, omit the FirstName~ mapping and define the LastName~ mapping.  Your custom object must have a mapping for Email.  Role, RoutingOrder, AccessCode, RecipientNote, SignNow, and SignInPersonName are optional.  RoutingOrder, if specified, must be a positive integer value.  RoutingOrder supersedes any RROS and RRSO options that are specified.  Docusign ignores the RoutingOrder if you add the Docusign Template option.  SignNow, if specified, must be either '0' or  '1'.  SignNow='1': Docusign sends the envelope to the recipient, and the recipient sees the Docusign signing page.  SignNow='0': Docusign sends the envelope to the recipient, and the recipient does not immediately see the Docusign signing page.  SignNow only functions if the recipient's email and user name match the Salesforce user's email and user name.  Docusign ignores SignNow when the recipient's email and user name do not match the Salesforce user's email and user name.  The SignNow recipient must be the first signer when there are multiple signers.  LoadDefaultContacts determines whether the default contacts load for the source object type:   - 0: Do not load - 1: Load   Docusign adds these contacts after the Custom Related Contacts recipients, with the CRL recipient added first.  If you specify DST in the script, the template Routing Order takes precedence over the CRL routing order.  If you specify both CRCL and CRL, Docusign processes CRCL recipients first and then adds CRL recipients.  When creating an in person signer (sign now) recipient with CRL, you must meet the following: requirements:   - The CRL recipient's name and email must match the Salesforce user - The sender's Docusign and Salesforce user names and email addresses   must match - Use the SignNow='1' parameter - The sender must be the first recipient - If you do not set a value for   SignInPersonName, the sender is   the signer   In this scenario, a user sends an envelope and Docusign immediately asks the sender to sign. |
| Example 1 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Lead.Id, CRL='Email~'+Lead.Email+';FirstName~'+Lead.Name+';RoutingOrder~1;Role~Signer 1,SigningGroup~AR;RoutingOrder~2;Role~Signer 2'])} |
| Example 1 Description | In this example, Docusign uses the lead’s name and email for Signer 1. Docusign creates Signer 2 from the AR Signing Group. |
| Example 2 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Account.Id,CRL = 'Email~'+User.Email+';FirstName~'+User.FirstName+';LastName~'+User.LastName+';Role~Sender;SignNow~1'])} |
| Example 2 Description | In this example, Docusign immediately asks the sender to sign. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Custom Related Contact List
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | CRCL= |
| Values | See Notes |
| Notes | Use this parameter to select recipients from a custom list related to the object containing the button.  Possible values are:   - SFObjectRelations\_\_r,Email~SFObjectEmailCol\_\_c; - FirstName~SFObjectFirstNameCol\_\_c; - LastName~SFObjectLastNameCol\_\_c; - SigningGroup~SFObjectSigningGroup\_\_c; - Role~SFObjectRoleCol\_\_c; - RoutingOrder~SFObjectRoutingOrderCol\_\_c; - AccessCode~SFObjectAccessCodeCol\_\_c; - RecipientNote~SFObjectRecipientNoteCol\_\_c; - SMSAuthPhone~SFObjectSMSAuthPhone\_\_c; - SignNow~SFObjectSignNowCol\_\_c;   Example: If sending from an opportunity, a custom object with the following fields can be created and associated with the Opportunity:  FirstName~First\_Name\_\_c;LastName~Last\_Name\_\_c; Role~Role\_\_c;Email~Email\_\_c  The SFObjectRelationName\_\_r value is the Salesforce object name.  The other items are column mappings that determine the values for how Docusign adds contacts to the envelope.  Docusign omits the FirstName~ mapping and only uses the LastName~ mapping in these scenarios:   - The custom object does not have values for both   First Name and Last   Name - The custom object contains only one column for   Name   Your custom object must have a mapping for Email. Role, RoutingOrder, AccessCode, SMSAuthPhone, and RecipientNote are optional.  If a record contains First Name, Last Name, Email, and SigningGroup values, Docusign only uses SigningGroup.  If a record contains both AccessCode and SMSAuthPhone, Docusign only uses SMSAuthPhone.  RoutingOrder, if specified, must be a positive integer value.  The RoutingOrder value supersedes any RROS and RRSO options specified.  Docusign ignores RoutingOrder if you add the Docusign Template option.  SignNow, if specified, must be either '0' or '1'.  SignNow='1': Docusign sends an envelope to the recipient, and the recipient sees the Docusign signing page.  SignNow='0': Docusign sends an envelope to the recipient, and the recipient does not immediately see the Docusign signing page.  SignNow only functions if the recipient's email and user name match the Salesforce user's email and user name.  SignNow Docusign ignores when the recipient's email and user name do not match the Salesforce user's email and user name. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id,CRCL = 'CRCLs\_\_r,Email~Email\_\_c;FirstName~FirstName\_\_c;LastName~LastName\_\_c;SigningGroup~SigningGroup\_\_c;Role~Role\_\_c'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign adds records in the CRCL custom object, which is a child of the Opportunity.  Docusign assigns them as Recipients or SigningGroups, depending on the record values. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Docusign Template
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | DST= |
| Values | DSGUID |
| Notes | Use this parameter to create envelopes based on a Docusign Template ID. The Docusign template defines the envelope's documents, recipient roles, and signer types.  Use Custom Contact Role Map with this parameter to align roles.  The standard selections apply if you do not add a value. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id,DST = 'F4EE991B-AA14-4226-B87A-D526FFD29A86'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, you can list multiple template IDs inside the quotes, separated by commas (,) |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Files
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | FILES= |
| Values | See notes |
| Notes | This is a comma-separated list of File IDs from shared Salesforce libraries. FILES adds Salesforce Files to an envelope. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId=Opportunity.Id, FILES='069Ec000004XOUHIA4,069Ec000004XCXxIAO'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign uses the IDs to add two files to the envelope from shared libraries. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Load Attachments
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | LA= |
| Values | 1 (ON)  0 (OFF) |
| Notes | When ON, Docusign adds all Attachments to the envelope.  The following order of precedence applies for document-related parameters:   1. MRL 2. LA 3. LF   MRL = 1 has priority if LA = 1 (or null) or LF = 1 (or null).  LA = 1 has precedence over LF = 1. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Account.Id, LA = 0])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign uploads zero attachments to the envelope with LA set to 0. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Load Files
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | LF= |
| Values | 1 (ON)  0 (OFF) |
| Notes | When ON, Docusign adds all files in the Salesforce object's Files Related List to the envelope.  The following order of precedence applies for document-related parameters:   1. MRL 2. LA 3. LF   MRL = 1 has priority if LA = 1 (or null) or LF = 1 (or null).  LA = 1 has precedence over LF = 1. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Account.Id, LF = 0])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign uploads zero files to the envelope with LF set to 0. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Most Recently Uploaded
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | MRL= |
| Values | 1 (ON)  0 (OFF) |
| Notes | When ON, Docusign adds only the most-recently-uploaded document to the envelope.  The following order of precedence applies for document-related parameters:   1. MRL 2. LA 3. LF   MRL = 1 has priority if LA = 1 (or null) or LF = 1 (or null).  LA = 1 has precedence over LF = 1. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Account.Id, LF = 1])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign adds only the most-recently-uploaded document to the envelope with MRL set to 1. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
One Click Options
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | OCO= |
| Values | Send  Tag |
| Notes | Docusign attempts to send the envelope when OCO='Send'.  If successful, Docusign displays the send confirmation page.  If a required element (recipient email, document, and so on) is missing, the review screen opens with the errors listed.  If you use OCO='Tag', Docusign takes the user to the Docusign envelope Prepare & Send page. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Opportunity.Id,OCO = 'Tag'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, setting OCO to Tag skips the sender past the Documents and Recipients pages. Docusign brings the sender directly to the Prepare & Send to add Docusign and Salesforce fields. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Recipient Signer Limit
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | RSL= |
| Values | Positive Integer |
| Notes | Use the RSL parameter to assign the maximum number of envelope recipients.  Example: If you use RSL=3, Docusign limits the number of recipients to three. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Account.Id, RSL = '3'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign limits the number of recipients to three with RSL set to RSL=3. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Reminder and Expiration Settings
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | RES= |
| Values | See Notes |
| Notes | You can use the following comma-separated values for this parameter:   - Remind Via Email - Remind In N Days - Repeat Remind In N Days - Expire-Void Envelope - Expire-Void Envelope In N Days - Warn Expire Void In N Days   Remind Via Email and Expire-Void Envelope are ON (1)/OFF (0) switches.  The other items set the number of days for the other settings.  Example: If RES ='1,2,3,1,120,3':   1. Docusign sends an email reminder 2. Docusign sends a reminder two days after the    original email 3. Docusign repeats the reminder every three    days 4. The envelope expires/voids 5. The envelope expires/voids in 120 days 6. Docusign sends an email warning that the envelope will expire/void    three days before the    expiration |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Opportunity.Id,RES = '1,2,3,1,120,3'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, the RES setting sends an email reminder.  The reminder sends two days after the original email, and repeats every three days.  The envelope expires/voids in 120 days.  Docusign sends an expire/void email three days before the expiration. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Require Quote Documents
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | RQD= |
| Values | See notes |
| Notes | Use this parameter to include the generated quote PDF.  The custom button accesses the Opportunity associated with the Quote object that contains the PDF.  It also accesses the Quote object holding the PDF. |
| Example 1 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id, RQD=Opportunity.Id} In this example, Docusign adds all PDFs associated with the Opportunity to the envelope.  You can use this script in a button on the Opportunity Detail Page. |
| Example 2 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Quote.Id, RQD=Quote.Id} In this example, Docusign adds all PDFs associated with the Quote to the envelope.  You can use this script in a button on the Quote Detail Page. |
| Example 3 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id, RQD= Opportunity.Id+',latest~1'} In this example, Docusign adds only the latest PDF from the Quote associated with the Opportunity. You can use this script in a button on the Opportunity Detail Page.  NOTE: If there is more than one Quote associated with an Opportunity, Docusign adds the latest PDF for each Quote. So if an Opportunity has three associated Quotes, Docusign adds three PDFs to the envelope. |
| Example 4 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [ SourceID = Opportunity.Id, CRL = ‘LoadDefaultContacts~0’, LF = '0', LA = '0', RQD = Opportunity.Id ] )}  In this example, Docusign uses this URL to retrieve Quotes with an Opportunity containing Quotes, Contact Roles, and Attachments. |
| Example 5 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [ SourceID = Opportunity.Id, CRL = 'Email~'+User.Email+';FirstName~'+User.FirstName+';LastName~'+User.LastName+',LoadDefaultContacts~0', LF = '1', LA = '1', RQD = Opportunity.Id] )}  In this example, an Opportunity contains Attachments and Quotes. Docusign uses this script to retrieve Attachments and Quotes and adds the Salesforce User as a signer. |
| Example 6 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [ SourceID = Opportunity.Id, LF = '1', LA = '1', RQD = Opportunity.Id ] )}  In this example, an Opportunity contains Attachments and Quotes. Docusign uses this script to retrieve Attachments, Quotes, and the contact in the Opportunity Contacts. |
| Example 7 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [ SourceID = Opportunity.Id, CRL = 'Email~'+User.Email+'; FirstName~'+User.FirstName+';LastName~'+User.LastName, LF = '1', LA = '1', RQD = Opportunity.Id ] )}  In this example, an Opportunity contains Attachments and Quotes. Docusign uses this script to retrieve Attachments, Quotes, the contacts in the Opportunity Contacts, and the names defined in CRL. |
| Example 8 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [ SourceID = Opportunity.Id, CRL = 'Email~'+User.Email+';FirstName~'+User.FirstName+';LastName~'+User.LastName+',LoadDefaultContacts~0', LF = '0', LA = '0', RQD = Opportunity.Id ] )}  In this example, an Opportunity contains Attachments and Quotes. Docusign uses this script to retrieve just the Quotes and the names defined in CRL. |
| Example 9 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [ SourceID = Opportunity.Id, LF = '0', LA = '0', RQD = Opportunity.Id ] )}  In this example, an Opportunity contains Attachments and Quotes. Docusign uses this script to retrieve just the Quotes and the contacts in the Opportunity Contacts. |
| Example 10 | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [ SourceID = Opportunity.Id, CRL = 'Email~' +User.Email+';FirstName~'+User.FirstName+';LastName~'+User.LastName, LF = '0', LA = '0', RQD = Opportunity.Id] )}  In this example, an Opportunity contains Attachments and Quotes. Docusign uses this script to load the Quotes, the contacts in the Opportunity Contacts, and the names defined in CRL. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Contact Routing Order Sequential
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | RROS= |
| Values | 1 (ON)  0 (OFF)  Not supplied |
| Notes | Use this parameter to turn on or off an envelope's sequential routing order. Docusign ignores this setting if you add the “Docusign Template” option.  The standard selections apply if you do not add a value.  Docusign adds recipients to the envelope and assigns a routing order in the following way:   1. Recipients from the CRCL option 2. Recipients from the CRL option 3. Recipients from the base Salesforce object   Using CRL and CRCL in RROS or RSRO scripting produces the following results:   - If you do not define RROS, Docusign sets the   default signing order to OFF. OFF   makes all recipients simultaneous. - If you define RSRO but RROS has no value   or a value of 0 (simultaneous), the   RSRO routing order applies. - If you define RSRO, and RROS is set to   1, the first recipient has the   routing order specified in RSRO. The next recipients are   sequential. - If you specify RoutingOrder in CRL or   CRCL, Docusign   ignores both RROS and   RSRO, if defined. When Docusign ignores both parameters, Docusign uses the specified   RoutingOrder for CRL and CRCL.  Any additional   recipients are always sequential, and Docusign ignores the RSRO   value. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id, RROS = 1 ] )} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign uses the sequential routing order. Recipients must sign sequentially. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Contact Starting Routing Order
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | RSRO= |
| Values | Positive Integer |
| Notes | Note: Currently RSRO only works for contact roles.  Use this parameter to set the routing order number for the first contact. The routing order for the next contacts uses either of these settings:   - Default settings (Docusign assigns everyone to routing order 1) - Other routing order settings (the Contact Routing   Order Sequential.)   Docusign ignores this option if you add the “Docusign Template” option.  The standard selections apply if you do not add a value.  The following behavior applies when you use CRL and CRCL in RROS or RSROscripting:   - If you do not define RROS, the default sending order is   OFF. OFF means that all   recipients are simultaneous. - Docusign uses RSRO signing order if you define   RSRO but do not define   RROS or set RROS to   0 (simultaneous). - Docusign uses RSRO routing order for the first   recipient if you define RSRO and set   RROS to 1.   The next recipients are sequential. - If you specify RoutingOrder in CRL or   CRCL, Docusign   ignores any defined RROS and   RSRO values. Docusign respects the specified   RoutingOrder for CRL and CRCL. Any additional recipients are   always sequential, and Docusign ignores   the RSRO value. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null, [sId = Opportunity.Id, RSRO = 3 ] )} |
| Example Description | In this example, the starting routing order starts at 3. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Show Remind Expire
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | SRS= |
| Values | 1 (ON)  0 (OFF) |
| Notes | If SRS='1'; the envelope Reminder and Expiration fields are shown. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Opportunity.Id,SRS = '0'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign does not display the Remind and Expire section during envelope creation with SRS set to 0. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Show Email Message
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | SEM= |
| Values | 1 (ON)  0 (OFF) |
| Notes | Docusign shows the Email Message field if you use SEM='1'. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Opportunity.Id,SEM = '0'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign hides the Email Message field with SEM set to 0. |

title: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
heading_path: Use URLFOR Scripting To Build Custom eSignature Buttons
Show Email Subject
text:
| About this parameter | Technical details |
| --- | --- |
| Parameter Name | SES= |
| Values | 1 (ON)  0 (OFF) |
| Notes | Use this parameter to display or hide the Email Subject field.  SES='1': Docusign shows the Email Subject field.  SES = '0': Docusign hides the Email Subject field. |
| Example | {!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle\_\_CustomEnvelopeCreation', null,[sId = Opportunity.Id,SES = '0'])} |
| Example Description | In this example, Docusign hides the Email Subject field with SES set to 0. |

title: Use Envelope Events To Automatically Trigger Updates to Salesforce Fields
heading_path: Use Envelope Events To Automatically Trigger Updates to Salesforce Fields
text:
Learn how to add envelope events to automatically update up to 25 Salesforce
fields.

Use this procedure to add envelope events to your envelope template:

1. Select the + Add Update button in the Field
   Updates section.

   ![A screenshot displays the Field Updates section with the + Add Update button and a list of envelope field events, showing that you can add up to 25 field updates.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Add-up-to-25-Field-Events.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2MDksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.tgY2bOnU5XVBbdQMcALR94gp-MMSvUtmNqgcuJ-X_2M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Field Updates section with the + Add Update button and a list of envelope field events, showing that you can add up to 25 field updates.")
2. Add a new Envelope Event to your template:

   1. Envelope Event: Select a Docusign envelope event
      from the dropdown menu. This is the event that triggers a Salesforce
      field update.
   2. Field Name: Select a Salesforce field from the
      dropdown menu. This is the field that is updated when the envelope
      event occurs.
   3. Field Value: Enter a value, or select a dropdown
      menu option. This is the information that updates the field that you
      selected.

   Note: You can add up to 25 Salesforce fields (five per event type.)
3. (Optional):

   - ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Delete-field-event.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2MDksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.tgY2bOnU5XVBbdQMcALR94gp-MMSvUtmNqgcuJ-X_2M&_LANG=enus): Select the trash icon to remove an Envelope Event
   - ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Remove-Field-Name.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2MDksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.tgY2bOnU5XVBbdQMcALR94gp-MMSvUtmNqgcuJ-X_2M&_LANG=enus): Select the X icon next to a field name to
     replace it
4. Select Save and Close when all options are set.

   The process is complete. Up to 25 Salesforce source fields update when triggered by
   Docusign field events.

title: Reset the Password for a Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Reset the Password for a Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn to reset the password of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for
Salesforce user.

Use this procedure to reset the password of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce user:

1. Access the Docusign
   Users page as an administrator:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account.
   2. Select Settings at the top of the page.
   3. Select Users in the left column.
2. Select Actions next to a user and choose Reset
   Password from the dropdown menu.

   A message opens: A reset password email has been sent to (user
   name).

You have completed the process. You sent a password
reset link to the user's email address.

title: Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for
Salesforce is a package that includes the following Docusign apps:

- [Docusign eSignature for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000001taX4EAI&tab=e)
- [Docusign CLM for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000004e3VUEAY&tab=e)
- [Docusign Gen for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N3A00000FeGX6UAN&tab=e)

When you install any of these apps, Docusign installs the complete Docusign Apps Launcher package.

Use this procedure to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, choose a Docusign app from the
   AppExchange:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2MTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.MQRY-drzsS8DE10cDroSEP-gAqQomVe1MdHXFLe-kLY&_LANG=enus) and choose View
      All.
   2. Select Visit AppExchange >  Go to AppExchange.
   3. Enter `Docusign eSignature`, `Gen`, or
      `CLM` in the search field.
   4. Select an app and choose Get it Now when the app page
      displays.
2. Log in to the AppExchange with Salesforce administrator credentials:

   Note: Enter production credentials even if installing in a sandbox
   organization. These credentials are used to access the AppExchange.

   1. Select Log In on the LET'S GET STARTED
      page.
   2. Select Salesforce on the Hello
      Trailblazer! dialog box that displays.
   3. Enter Salesforce production administrator credentials on the page that displays and
      select Log In.

      The Where do you want to install this package? dialog
      box displays.
3. Choose where to install the package:
   1. Select Install in Production or Install in
      Sandbox.
   2. Select I have read and agree to the terms and
      conditions.
   3. Select Confirm and Install.

      The page refreshes. The Salesforce login page displays.
4. Install the Apps Launcher package:
   1. Enter production or sandbox administrator credentials and select Log
      In.
   2. Select Install for All
      Users.

      Note: If you want to choose ​Install for Admins Only, or ​Install for Specific Profiles​, see this topic about [how Salesforce applies app permissions](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.distribution_installing_packages.htm&type=5).
   3. Select Install or Upgrade.
   4. Select Done when the page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have installed the
Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps. This package includes Docusign eSignature, CLM, and Gen. You receive an email notification when
Salesforce completes the installation.

title: Disconnect Your Docusign Account and Preserve User Settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Disconnect Your Docusign Account and Preserve User Settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user
settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

Occasionally you must disconnect and reconnect your Docusign connection. The following are reasons to disconnect and
reconnect:

- You want to connect your Salesforce organization to a different Docusign account
- An administrator deleted your Docusign integration
  user
- Salesforce applied [enhanced domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000393816&type=1) to your organization

You can use the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool to reset your
integration safely.

Follow this procedure to use the Disconnect Account and Preserve User
Settings feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3ODcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.LayvtrGEwVwLNvA-ZIFLhPud6FrwTGYqyYsH-tDvcHA&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2.
