Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3ODcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.LayvtrGEwVwLNvA-ZIFLhPud6FrwTGYqyYsH-tDvcHA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Disconnect Account and Preserve User Settings: Select
   Execute.

   A message opens: Successfully logged out from Docusign account. The
   Troubleshooting page displays the message: No Docusign Account
   connected.

You have completed the process. You have disconnected your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization. You can [re-connect Salesforce and Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus) or
connect to a different Docusign account.

title: Create a Salesforce Experience Cloud Envelope Template
heading_path: Create a Salesforce Experience Cloud Envelope Template
text:
You can build a Experience Cloud (formerly known as Salesforce Communities) envelope
template and custom button. An Experience Cloud envelope template provides a way for your
Experience Cloud users to sign a document from a Salesforce record.

Docusign envelope templates are sending experiences administrators build in
Salesforce, containing documents, recipients, and signature fields. You can build
Experience Cloud envelope templates to let your Experience Cloud users sign a
document from a Salesforce record. When users select the Experience Cloud custom
button, Docusign starts the signing process. The Experience Cloud user signs first,
followed by any other recipients in the template.

Use this procedure to build a Salesforce Experience Cloud envelope template:

- [Step 1: Prepare Your Salesforce Organization for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dmy1659560408366.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to prepare your Salesforce organization before creating a Docusign Experience Cloud envelope template.").
- [Step 2: Prepare Your Salesforce Users for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=tska4688422-0c61-4376-8b45-fb2262a80599.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to prepare your Salesforce users for Experience Cloud envelope template use.").
- [Step 3: Add a Name, Data Source, and Experience Cloud Option](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wiw1617391189933.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to add a name, data source, and enable the Experience Cloud option.").
- [Step 4: Add Documents to Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hya1596146614785_1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add documents to your envelope template.").
- [Step 5: Add Recipients to an Experience Cloud Envelope Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qcg1617393962475.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add recipients to the Experience Cloud envelope template.").
- [Step 6: Create Salesforce Merge Fields for Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jdl1611042391715.1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Salesforce merge fields to add to your envelope template.").
- [Step 7: Place Docusign and Salesforce Fields on Your Template Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ywv1596146733047.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to place Docusign and Salesforce fields on the document text.").
- [Step 8: Add Options to Your Experience Cloud Envelope Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dtt1617395353324.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add options like Delivered by to your Experience Cloud envelope template.").
- [Step 9: Add a Custom Button to Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=owi1596146834259.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add an Experience Cloud envelope template button to Salesforce layouts.").

title: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App From Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App From Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Find out how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your
Salesforce organization.

There are two Docusign eSignature apps that you can use in your Salesforce
organization:

- **Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce**: The first
  Docusign for Salesforce app. It was de-listed from the Salesforce
  AppExchange in 2020.
- **Docusign eSignature for Salesforce**: The current version of
  the Docusign for Salesforce app. It is part of the Docusign Apps Launcher
  for Salesforce package of apps, along with Docusign Gen, Negotiate, and CLM.

The legacy app is still supported, but is not being developed, and will
eventually be deprecated.

You can upgrade now to enjoy the latest advances of the current eSignature
for Salesforce app.

When you have upgraded and migrated your legacy eSignature users, records,
and buttons, you can uninstall the legacy app.

In order to uninstall the package, you must first remove all legacy eSignature for
Salesforce dependencies. Dependencies are references from the legacy eSignature for
Salesforce that are active in your Salesforce organization. These include legacy
eSignature permission sets, components, and custom buttons.

Use this procedure to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature app from your
Salesforce organization:

- [Step 1: Remove Users from Legacy eSignature for Salesforce Permission Sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qwo1642613574891.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove all users from legacy eSignature for Salesforce permission sets.")
- [Step 2: Remove Legacy eSignature Components from Your Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dlc1642622890465.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove all legacy eSignature for Salesforce components from Salesforce layouts.")
- [Step 3: Remove Legacy eSignature Custom Buttons From Your Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ddz1642632234520.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce custom buttons from your Salesforce page layouts.")
- [Step 4: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=the1642634056297.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your Salesforce organization.")

title: Step 3: Add Recipients to Your Template
heading_path: Step 3: Add Recipients to Your Template
text:
Learn to add recipients to your envelope template.

Tip:

- [This video demonstrates the steps in this
  task](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6171919623001).
- [This topic demonstrates the
  advanced options available in this step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=psq1595994131623.html&_LANG=enus).

In this step, you can add recipients to your template. Recipients are the people who sign the
agreement when you send an envelope.

Use this procedure to add recipients to your envelope template:

1. Select Add Recipient.
2. Select a recipient type in the Add Recipient dialog box:
   - From Salesforce
     - Lookup Fields: Enter Salesforce fields
       like Owner or Created
       By to add recipients.
     - Related Lists: Enter Salesforce fields
       related to the source object to add recipients.
     - Users/Contacts: Enter Salesforce users or
       contacts to add recipients.
     - Sender: Enter the sender of the envelope
       as a recipient. Select +Add Role and
       enter a role, like Sender 1.
   - Other Sources
     - By Role: Use a placeholder title, like
       Approver. The sender can specify a name and email address later.
       You can use custom syntax to  [add a By Role
       recipient from a Salesforce object](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus).
     - Signing Group: Add a group of recipients
       from the connected Docusign account. Any
       recipient can sign for the group.
3. (Optional) Delivery: This field displays for customers
   with Allow SMS Delivery and Signer can sign on
   a mobile device enabled.
   - Email: Send an envelope by email.
   - Email + SMS: Send your envelope by email and text message to as many as four recipients.

     Important: Enter a space between the country code and the phone number.
4. Action: Select a recipient option from the dropdown
   menu:
   - Needs to Sign: The recipient must sign the document.
   - Receives a Copy: The recipient gets a courtesy copy.
   - Needs to View: The recipient must view the document.
   - Specify Recipients: The recipient can add names and emails for placeholder recipients (like Approver.)
   - Allow to Edit: The recipient can make changes to the document.
   - In Person Signer: The recipients take part in a signing session with all signers in the same room.
5. Select Add to save the recipient, or Add
   & New to save and add another recipient.
6. Choose any optional steps:
   - Signing Order: Change numbers next to recipients to choose who signs first. You can also drag recipients to change order.
     ![A screenshot displays one recipient highlighted with arrows indicating that it is moving above another recipient.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/DAL_Envelope_Template_drag_recipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4MjcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.uvc6055yeA1RDcFz23fZeRQVMFczUXiRoaU4z8M3580&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays one recipient highlighted with arrows indicating that it is moving above another recipient.")
   - View: Select to see a visual diagram of the signing order.
   - Email Subject: Enter up to 100 characters of text.
   - Email Message: Enter up to 2,000 characters of text.
   - More Options: Select the arrow > to open the menu and choose the following options:
     - Reminders: Set the number of days between recipient reminders.
     - Expiration: Enter the number of days before the envelope expires.
7. Select Next.

title: Step 3: Add Recipients to Your Template
heading_path: Step 3: Add Recipients to Your Template
Next steps
text: You have completed step three. Move to [Step 4: Create Salesforce Merge Fields for Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jdl1611042391715.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Salesforce merge fields to add to your envelope template.").

title: Remove Users From the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: Remove Users From the Docusign Login Permission Set
text:
Learn to remove users from the Docusign Login permission
set.

The Docusign
 Login permission set helps you manage Docusign access for your Salesforce users. Users with Docusign Login membership can authorize Docusign
app access themselves.

Use this procedure to remove users from the Docusign Login permission set:

1. Select Docusign Login.
2. Select Manage Assignments.
3. Select the check box next to each user that you want to remove.
4. Select Remove Assignments.

   The Are you sure you want to remove these users from this
   permission set? dialog box opens.
5. Select OK.
6. Select Done.

You have completed the process. You have removed one or
more users from the Docusign Login permission set.

title: Best Practices for Creating AutoPlace Fields
heading_path: Best Practices for Creating AutoPlace Fields
text:
Learn about using AutoPlace fields to add Docusign fields to specified
places in a document.

AutoPlace fields are a feature that you can use when building a Docusign
envelope template. AutoPlace automatically adds Docusign fields to
specified places in a document.

This saves time and reduces error because senders do not manually place fields for their
recipients.

Consider the following guidelines when creating AutoPlace fields:

- [Use generic Docusign credentials to
  create custom fields.](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Best-Practices-for-Setting-up-DocuSign-for-Salesforce-Connected-User-accounts-in-both-DocuSign-and-Salesforce) These fields are linked to the creating Docusign user, and fail if someone deletes that user. If you
  have a [Docusign system
  sender](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=fsg1646663268427&topicId=jxz1611642321527.html&_LANG=enus), create merge fields with those credentials.
- [Create a Connect configuration for
  Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=vob1727899215236&topicId=ctv1583277395112.html&_LANG=enus). A Connect configuration gives you the following advantages:
  - Writes data back to Salesforce when envelopes are completed.
  - Enables the creation of merge fields in the Connect page of your Docusign account.
  - Solves some known issues with merge field functionality.
- Use automatic AutoPlace fields when adding the following fields to a
  template:
  - Signature
  - Date Signed
  - Initial
  - Optional Initial
- Use Document custom fields and Salesforce merge fields when adding the
  following types of text value fields to a template:
  - Name
  - Company
  - Title
- Use the following guidelines when you create Document custom fields and
  Salesforce merge fields:
  - Use a font size of seven points or larger when you create anchor text
    strings.
  - Change the text color of the anchor text to match the background color
    of your document. So if your document is white, change the font color to
    white to hide it from recipients.
  - Use text strings with no spaces in them, like
    \cb\_{r}\_yes\ for a check box field.
  - Create unique anchor text strings for all custom fields. Docusign adds an AutoPlace field to your document
    wherever it finds matching text.

title: Step 7: Create an Envelope Template Custom Button on a Salesforce Page Layout
heading_path: Step 7: Create an Envelope Template Custom Button on a Salesforce Page Layout
text:
The seventh step of the envelope template creation process involves adding a custom
button to the layout of the Salesforce object chosen in step one.

Use this procedure to add a custom button to the Salesforce page layout of the object chosen
in step one.

Tip: [This video](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6172186652001) walks you through the process.

1. Enter a descriptive name for the custom button in the Button
   Label text field.
2. Check the box for each page layout to which the custom button will be
   added.
3. Select Save and Finish.

   A message displays: Successfully modified
   layouts.

The entire process is complete. You have built a new envelope
template, and added a custom button to a Salesforce layout.

title: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Administrator Overview
heading_path: Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Administrator Overview
text:
Learn to configure, customize, and administer the Docusign eSignature
for Salesforce application.

Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce is a package of apps that work
together to generate and send contracts for review, approval, and signature.

Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
:   Send completed contracts for electronic signature.

Docusign CLM for Salesforce
:   Store documents, build workflows, build document generation configurations, and send for
    approval or signature.

Docusign Gen for Salesforce
:   Create a new contract with the selection of a button.

Docusign Negotiate for Salesforce
:   Send a contract for internal approval or external review.

You can use Docusign eSignature for Salesforce to streamline and automate
your Salesforce contract process. You can build templates that merge data from Salesforce
records to your contracts and send them to recipients for signature. When recipients finish
signing, Docusign uses writeback to update fields and add documents to
records. This guide covers administration of the Docusign eSignature for
Salesforce app, including the following topics:

s finish
signing, Docusign uses writeback to update fields and add documents to

  Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=esy1597029449495.html&_LANG=enus "Administrators learn to troubleshoot issues they are having with the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.")

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Learn the Docusign Apps Launcher requirements for browsers, operating
systems, and devices.

You can use the Docusign for Salesforce apps with the following browsers,
operating systems, and devices:

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported browsers
text:
Important: Salesforce and Docusign do not
support any version of Microsoft Internet Explorer.

| Browser | Supported version | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Google Chrome | Latest | n/a |
| Safari (Apple) | Latest | Desktop-only. Salesforce does not support the mobile version of Safari. Enabling third-party cookies is required. |
| Microsoft Edge | Latest | n/a |
| Mozilla Firefox | Latest | n/a |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported operating systems
text:
| Operating system | Version |
| --- | --- |
| Microsoft Windows | Windows 10 or newer |
| MacOS (Apple desktop) | 10.14 (Mojave) or newer |
| iOS (Apple mobile) | iOS 14.x or newer |
| Android OS | Android 8.0 or newer |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Salesforce mobile app requirements
text:
Required settings: Configure the following settings to use Docusign in the Salesforce mobile app:

- Enable third-party cookies in the [Salesforce mobile app settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.salesforce_app.htm&type=5).
- Turn off Include the instance name in Visualforce URLs when
  third-party cookies are blocked in Salesforce My
  Domain settings.

Approved Docusign apps: You can use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
- Docusign Gen

Non-approved Docusign apps: You cannot use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign Negotiate
- Docusign CLM
- Docusign CLM Essentials

Approved envelope template types: You can only use the following
envelope template types in the Salesforce mobile app:

- [Quick send envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Send now envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)

Mobile app tagging requirements: Meet the following requirements
to add fields to Docusign envelopes:

- Install the Docusign mobile app on your Android or iOS
  device.
- Turn off mobile app tagging when using an iPad.

For more information about Salesforce requirements, see the [Supported Browsers and Devices](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.getstart_browser_overview.htm&type=5) page on the
Salesforce support site.

title: View the Documents of an Envelope on the Docusign Status Page
heading_path: View the Documents of an Envelope on the Docusign Status Page
text:
Learn how to view the documents of an envelope by accessing the Docusign Status page.

A Docusign envelope is a digital container you build that holds documents,
recipients, and signature fields. Recipients sign the envelope by completing the
fields assigned to them.

Salesforce users can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. The
Docusign Status tab lets you view the status of your
Docusign envelopes.

Docusign Status details include: Docusign envelope ID, Sender
Name, Sender Email, Email
Subject, and Related Objects.

You can also view the documents of an envelope sent from a Salesforce record on the Docusign
Status page.

Use this procedure to view the documents of a sent envelope:

1. Select the Docusign Status tab.
2. Select an envelope from the Envelope # column.
3. Select View Envelope in the upper right.

   The envelope documents open in a preview window.

You have completed the task. You have viewed the documents
of an envelope sent from a Salesforce record.

title: Rename a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Rename a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
text:
You can change the file names of uploaded documents in the envelope template by
following these steps.

You can change the file names of uploaded documents in the envelope template by following
these steps:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.l5hD3P9mf62Bu6wXJiDUmSNKp_wf0c_g4P5d-kn6rsk&_LANG=enus) dropdown menu next to a document.
2. Select Rename from the options.

   ![A screenshot displays the envelope template document options menu with the Rename option highlighted for an uploaded document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplatedocumentoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5ODYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.l5hD3P9mf62Bu6wXJiDUmSNKp_wf0c_g4P5d-kn6rsk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the envelope template document options menu with the Rename option highlighted for an uploaded document.")

3. Enter a new name for the document.
4. Select the Rename button.
5. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have changed the name of the
   uploaded document.

title: Federated Security for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Federated Security for Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about how you can apply Salesforce security settings to your CLM content using
Federated Security.

There are two Docusign CLM apps that you can use in Salesforce:

- Docusign CLM: The current app that is part of the Docusign
  Apps Launcher package of apps
- Docusign Document Management: The legacy CLM app, formerly
  known as SpringCM File It

This article applies to the Docusign CLM app.

See this page for legacy app instructions: [Federated Security for Docusign Document Management (SpringCM File
It)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=fmk1599229525077.html&_LANG=enus).

You can use Federated Security to apply Salesforce security to content in your CLM
account. Federated Security lets you manage CLM security from Salesforce without needing
to set separate permissions.

Federated Security applies Salesforce Profile or
Role security to all CLM content. When you update security
settings in Salesforce, CLM content security also updates.

You can find Federated Security settings on the CLM Admin page.

Warning:

- Do not refresh a Salesforce organization with Federated Security applied
- Do not connect a Salesforce organization to a different CLM account when
  Federated Security is applied

[You must follow these steps before you refresh or
reconnect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ytt1602710410891.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about best practices for refreshing a Salesforce organization with Federated Security enabled."). Failure to do so destroys all CLM file security, and you must
rebuild the entire CLM directory.

Note: Federated Security cannot be applied to CLM reports because you can
only run reports in your CLM account.

**Example**: A user has no access to the
Finance folder in Salesforce. That user has
View access to the Finance folder in
CLM. The user runs a report in the CLM account. The user sees documents from the
Finance folder in the report results. Federated security does not apply because you
cannot run the CLM report in Salesforce.

title: Step 1: Remove Users from Apps Launcher Permission Sets
heading_path: Step 1: Remove Users from Apps Launcher Permission Sets
text:
Learn to remove all users from Docusign permission sets.

You are required to remove at least one user from the following permission sets:

- Docusign Administrator
- Docusign CLM Admin

You may be required to remove users from [other Docusign Apps Launcher
permission sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xxm1623778416524.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the types and functions of Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets.").

1. Open the Permission Sets page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MjgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.2I84s953OCn-UsBZP-iFCnrTaYVx3kXVTq8aKSCCw7c&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Permission Sets in the Quick
      Find field.

   The Permission Sets page opens.
2. Remove all users from the Docusign Administrator
   permission set:
   1. Select Manage Assignments.
   2. Select the Action check box to select all
      users.
   3. Select Remove Assignments.![A screenshot displays the Action check box and the remove assignments button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/daluninstallremoveassignments.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MjgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.2I84s953OCn-UsBZP-iFCnrTaYVx3kXVTq8aKSCCw7c&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Action check box and the remove assignments button highlighted.")

      A dialog box displays: Are you sure you want to remove
      these users from this permission set?.
   4. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes. The Docusign
      Administrator permission set page opens.
3. Select Done.
4. Repeat this procedure for the Docusign CLM Admin
   permission set.
5. Repeat this procedure for any other Docusign permission sets
   containing users.

You have completed step one of the process. You have
removed all users from Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets. Move to
[Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uxu1623708462949.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce layouts.").

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change

v5.2](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04t3u000002wLcp)

Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4OTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.h12TT0FHopoz2m5ZgsU8XZcJXHrgtCSrBWIMLZX7Uos&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")

title: Rebuild Your Docusign Connection, Users, and Templates After Cloning or Refreshing a Salesforce Sandbox
heading_path: Rebuild Your Docusign Connection, Users, and Templates After Cloning or Refreshing a Salesforce Sandbox
text:
Learn how to rebuild your Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates after
refreshing or cloning a Salesforce sandbox.

When you refresh or clone a Salesforce sandbox organization, Salesforce imports all settings
from your production organization. After the cloning or refreshing process, your
Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates remain linked to your Salesforce
production organization. To get your Docusign account working in your sandbox, you
must rebuild your Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates.

For more information about refreshing or cloning sandbox organizations, see these sites:

- [Refresh a Salesforce sandbox](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.data_sandbox_refresh.htm&type=5)
- [Clone a Salesforce sandbox](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?language=en_US&id=sf.data_sandbox_clone.htm&type=5)

Use this procedure to rebuild envelope templates after you clone or refresh your
Salesforce sandbox organization:

1. [Disconnect the sandbox from Docusign and remove your
   Docusign users.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vli1610514116162.html&_LANG=enus)
2. [Connect a Docusign Demo account to your
   Salesforce organization.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uxq1614636863027.html&_LANG=enus)
3. [Rebuild your Docusign users.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus)
4. [Clone your Docusign envelope templates from Salesforce
   production to your sandbox.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uhn1664569454728.html&_LANG=enus)

You have completed the process. You have rebuilt your Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates after refreshing or cloning your Salesforce
sandbox organization.

heading_path: Envelope Template Options: Recipients Step
text:
This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the Recipients step of the
envelope template creation process.

nts step of the

title: Step 5: Add Recipients to an Experience Cloud Envelope Template
heading_path: Step 5: Add Recipients to an Experience Cloud Envelope Template
text:
Learn to add recipients to the Experience Cloud envelope template.

Use this procedure to add recipients to the Experience Cloud envelope template:

Tip:
[This video demonstrates the steps in this task](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6171919623001).

Note:
Docusign adds an Experience Cloud User by
default. The Experience Cloud User is locked as the first recipient in the signing
order. You cannot edit or delete the Experience Cloud User recipient. This user is named
Community Cloud User in older versions of the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.

1. Select Add Recipient.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
2. Select a recipient type from the left column:
   - From Salesforce:
     - Lookup Fields: Fields like
       Owner that dynamically add recipients from a
       record.
     - Related Lists: Fields that an administrator chooses
       to relate to a parent object.
     - Users/Contacts: Salesforce Users or Contacts.
     - Sender: The sender of the envelope. Select
       +Add Role and enter a role, like Sender
       1.
   - Other Sources:
     - By Role: A placeholder title (like Approver.) Senders
       can specify a name and email address later. You can also [dynamically add By Role recipients
       using custom syntax](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus).
     - Signing Group: A group of recipients in the connected
       Docusign account. Any signer can complete the signature
       for the group. You can also select the + Add Role
       link under the Action dropdown menu for any recipient type.
3. Action: Select an option from the dropdown menu:
   - Needs to Sign: The recipient must sign the
     agreement.
   - Receives a Copy: The recipient is not required to view
     or sign the agreement.
   - Needs to View: The recipient must view the
     agreement.
   - Specify Recipients: The recipient can add names and
     emails for placeholder recipients like Approver.
   - Allow to Edit: The recipient can make changes to the
     document.
4. (Optional) Select additional options:
   - Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now): Select this
     check box if you want the sender to sign the envelope as soon as it is
     sent.
   - Add Access Authentication:
     - SMS/Phone: Send an access code to a recipient's
       mobile device for extra security.

       Important: You must enter a space between the country code and the
       phone number.
     - Access Code: Create a code that a recipient must
       enter to start the signing process.
   - Add Private Message: Send a message to just this
     recipient or group.
5. Select:
   - Add & New: Save the recipient and add
     another.
   - Add: Save the recipient and close the dialog
     box.
6. (Optional) Choose additional steps:

   Note: You cannot change the signing order of an Experience
   Cloud recipient.

   - View: Select this button to see a visual diagram of
     the signing order.
   - Email Subject: Enter up to 100 characters of
     text.
   - Email Message: Enter up to 2,000 characters of
     text.
   - More Options: Select the arrow
     > to open the menu and choose any options:
     - Reminders: Set the number of days between recipient
       reminders from the dropdown menu.
     - Expiration: Enter the number of days before the
       envelope expires.
7. Select Next.

title: Step 5: Add Recipients to an Experience Cloud Envelope Template
heading_path: Step 5: Add Recipients to an Experience Cloud Envelope Template
Next steps
text: You have completed step five. Move to [Step 6: Create Salesforce Merge Fields for Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jdl1611042391715.1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Salesforce merge fields to add to your envelope template.").

title: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
heading_path: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
text:
Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or
Login IP Ranges to fix issues with users being prompted to
re-enter credentials every hour.

Some users are prompted to enter their credentials every hour. This could occur for
two reasons:

- The Salesforce organization has Multi-Factor Authentication for API
  Logins enabled on the user's' profile. This is incompatible with
  Docusign’s server-to-server API updates.
- Login IP Ranges are specified on the user's profile. This
  prevents API logins for the user from Docusign servers.

title: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
heading_path: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
Resolution 1: Disable Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2ODgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.gHZMwCkL2GWwd_q4UJlaRB8A28tjfWt0oS_RwKwGHtE&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Edit button.
4. Uncheck the Multi-Factor Authentication for API
   Logins box.
5. Select the Save button.

   The process is complete. Users are no longer prompted to enter
   credentials every hour.

title: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
heading_path: Users are Prompted to Reauthorize Every Hour
Resolution 2: Disable Login IP Ranges on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2ODgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.gHZMwCkL2GWwd_q4UJlaRB8A28tjfWt0oS_RwKwGHtE&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the

4. Select the Del link to the left of an IP

title: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
heading_path: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
text:
Learn to make a standard Salesforce user your Docusign integration
user.

Connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account to use Docusign Apps Launcher in Salesforce.

To create the connection, you must enter Docusign and Salesforce
credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The connecting
Salesforce user is called the integration user.

If you don't want to use a Salesforce administrator as your integration user, you can use
standard credentials with some modifications.

Use this procedure to make a standard Salesforce user your integration user:

1. Clone a standard Salesforce user profile and name it Docusign
   Integration User.
2. Add permissions to the Integration User profile:
   1. Open the new profile and select Edit.
   2. Administrative Permissions: Select the following
      options:

      - Manage Users
      - API Enabled
      - View Setup and Configuration
      - Modify Metadata Through Metadata API Functions
      - Customize Application
      - Manage Custom Permissions
   3. Standard Object Permissions and Custom
      Object Permissions:

      - View All: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must read.
      - Modify All: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must update.

      Note: These settings grant access to your Salesforce organization's
      ownership hierarchy. These permissions do not grant unrestricted
      access to all data on these objects. You can also restrict access to
      specific data points using [Field Level
      Security](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ind.fsc_admin_configure_field_level_security.htm&type=5) or [Sharing
      Settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ind.cg_task_update_sharing_settings.htm&type=5).
   4. Open Object Manager and set Field Level
      Security for all fields Apps Launcher must read or
      update. These settings grant access to features like document generation
      or workflow automation:

      - Visible: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must read.
      - Read-Only: Deselect for each object Apps
        Launcher must update, including all Docusign
        objects.![A screenshot displays field-level security settings for the Docusign Integration User profile.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-fieldlevel-security-settings-for-the-Docusign-Integration-User-profile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2MzYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.xQ3I0vmksUjObyEhZ64bPfF2xqODAem9ovc5_qdFNas&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays field-level security settings for the Docusign Integration User profile.")
   5. Select Save.
3. Create a standard Salesforce user to act as your integration user.

   Note: Create generic credentials not tied to a person, like
   docusign-integration-user@yourorg.com. View this
   topic for more information about [integration user best practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
4. Set Docusign access for your Salesforce integration
   user:

   1. Log on to your Docusign account as an
      administrator.
   2. Create a Docusign administrator using your Salesforce integration user
      credentials.
5. Add your integration user to the Docusign Integration User
   profile.
6. Add your integration user to the Docusign Administrator
   permission set.
7. [Connect your Docusign
   account to your Salesforce organization using the Docusign
   and Salesforce integration user credentials](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

title: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
heading_path: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
text:
credentials](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

You have completed the process. You made a standard Salesforce
user your integration user and connected Docusign and Salesforce. This
integration user maintains the connection between accounts without requiring
unrestricted administrator permissions.

title: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign
production account.

title: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")

To use Docusign applications, you must connect a Docusign
account to your Salesforce organization. You create this connection by entering
Salesforce and Docusign administrator credentials on the
Docusign Setup page. The Docusign
administrator used to connect accounts is called the integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your Salesforce organization to a Docusign
production account:

1. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that displays.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page displays.
2. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login
      dialog box displays.
   2. Enter Docusign administrator credentials in the
      dialog box that displays and select Log in.
   3. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box displays.
3. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page displays.

You have completed the process. You have connected your
Salesforce organization to your Docusign production account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Access the Documents Step of an Envelope Template
heading_path: Access the Documents Step of an Envelope Template
text:
Learn to access the Documents step to add, edit, or delete envelope template
files.

You can add, edit, or delete envelope template files on the
Documents step.

Use this procedure to access the envelope template Documents step:

1. Access the envelope template Documents page as a
   Salesforce administrator:

   The envelope template creation page opens to the Documents
   step.
2. Select the App Launcher grid ![A screenshot of the Salesforce App Launcher grid, containing nine colored dots.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NzEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.iSqzMmhg-LwijJb99JYe0YJpQN0FOH7FS5rPpfwG3P0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Salesforce App Launcher grid, containing nine colored dots.") and choose View All.
3. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
4. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
5. Select the arrow ![A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NzEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.iSqzMmhg-LwijJb99JYe0YJpQN0FOH7FS5rPpfwG3P0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.") next to a template and choose Edit.

You have completed the process. You have accessed the
Documents step of an envelope template.

title: Envelope Template Field Options
heading_path: Envelope Template Field Options
text:
How to customize e-signature field options in a Docusign envelope template.

You can customize Docusign envelope template fields from the Place Fields
page of an existing envelope template.

Select any field to open an options panel on the far-right of the page.

Different options are shown, depending on the field type that is selected.

Follow this procedure to change a field that is placed on a page:

- Enlarge field: Drag a field anchor outward.
- Shrink field: Drag a field anchor inward.
- Delete field: Select the field and press the Delete key.

![A screen capture displays enlarging a signature field by dragging the corner.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/DAL_envelope_template_drag_field_corner_to_elongate.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxMjcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.4dpFZFP6sCwfpA-11SV6uIJK09e9-oxESaD4GLaaacM&_LANG=enus)

Here are the configurable options available:

Table 1.
Docusign Field Options

| Option | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Required Field | The recipient must take action. |
| Read Only | This field option cannot be changed. |
| Formatting | Change font type, size, color, etc. |
| Data Label | Link fields of the same type by using a shared label. When one field is completed, the rest are as well. |
| Tooltip | Instructive text that appears when the cursor hovers over a field. |
| Location | Use pixels from the left and top to set the exact field location on the page. |
| AutoPlace | Fields are auto-placed on document every time a keyword is matched. |
| Sender Permissions | Make a field mandatory, or prevent senders from making changes. |
| Add Text | Add a text box field to the document. |
| Validation | Force user input into a set format, like xxx-xx-xxxx for Social Security number fields, xx/xx/xxxx for Date, etc. |
| Collaboration | Allow recipients to collaborate on envelope content. |
| Conditional Logic | Fields only appear to the recipient when a specified condition is met. |
| Collect Payment | Allow recipient to pay for services. |
| Save Payment Method | Allow recipient to store payment info for future transactions. |
| Payment Gateway | Set the method for transferring funds. |
| Payment Amount | Set the amount to be paid. |
| Payment Description | Add a note about the purpose of a payment. |
| Edit Formula | Edit an existing formula built from number and date fields in an envelope. |
| Save as Custom Field | Add a customized field to the Custom Fields menu for re-use. |
| Delete | Remove a selected field from the page. |
| Resize Field | Drag field corners to enlarge or shrink field boundaries on the page. |

title: Envelope Template Options: Merge Fields Step
heading_path: Envelope Template Options: Merge Fields Step
text:
There are many configurable options in the Merge Fields tab of the envelope template
creation process

This guide demonstrates the field types and configurable options that you can use in the Merge
Fields step of the envelope template creation process. This includes:

- [Access the Merge Fields Step of an Envelope Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ncw1616798238564.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add, edit, or delete merge fields in an envelope template.")
- [Use Merge Field Relationships in an Envelope Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zce1616797759905.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use merge field relationships in an envelope template. Merge fields can access up to four levels of relation to a source object.")
- [Docusign Envelope Template Merge Field Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=elw1616795980338.html&_LANG=enus "Here are the options available on the Add Merge Fields dialog box:")
- [Edit a Merge Field](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=kzl1611128354046.html&_LANG=enus "You can edit the details of merge fields in an envelope template at any time.")

title: Step 1: Remove Users from Legacy eSignature for Salesforce Permission Sets
heading_path: Step 1: Remove Users from Legacy eSignature for Salesforce Permission Sets
text:
Learn how to remove all users from legacy eSignature for Salesforce permission sets.

To continue uninstalling the legacy app, remove all
users from the following permission sets:

- Docusign Administrator
- Docusign Sender
- Docusign User

Important: You will see permission sets with
duplicate names. Do not edit permission sets with Docusign Apps
Launcher in the Description field.

![A screenshot displays three highlighted Docusign permission sets.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Remove-all-assignments-from-these-three-permission-sets.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.sahLNpgTJchWVpDPI3oCnexSRnejSLZXdS91Li1xWzw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three highlighted Docusign permission sets.")

1. Open the Permission Sets page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.sahLNpgTJchWVpDPI3oCnexSRnejSLZXdS91Li1xWzw&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Permission Sets in the Quick
      Find field.

   The Permission Sets page opens.
2. Select the link for the legacy Docusign Administrator
   permission set. Do not select the entry with Docusign Apps
   Launcher in the Description field.
3. Select Manage Assignments and select all users.
4. Select Remove Assignments.![A screenshot displays check boxes selected for all users of a permission set.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/daluninstallremoveassignments.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.sahLNpgTJchWVpDPI3oCnexSRnejSLZXdS91Li1xWzw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays check boxes selected for all users of a permission set.")
5. Select OK on the Are you sure you want to
   remove these users from this permission set? dialog box.

   The Docusign Administrator page opens.
6. Select Done.
7. Repeat this procedure for the legacy Docusign Sender and
   Docusign User permission sets.

You have completed step one of the process. You
have removed all users from legacy eSignature for Salesforce permission sets. Move to
[Step 2: Remove Legacy eSignature Components from Your Salesforce Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dlc1642622890465.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to remove all legacy eSignature for Salesforce components from Salesforce layouts.").

title: Fix Account-Wide Issues Using the Docusign Apps Launcher Troubleshooting Page
heading_path: Fix Account-Wide Issues Using the Docusign Apps Launcher Troubleshooting Page
text:
Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting is a Salesforce page that
allows administrators to troubleshoot and resolve issues with these Docusign apps.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of apps that you install in your Salesforce organization.
These apps include Docusign Gen, Negotiate, CLM, CLM Essentials, and eSignature for
Salesforce.

Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting is a tool that lets
administrators troubleshoot and resolve issues with Docusign apps. The page is available
in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce administrators with
Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

The TroubleShooting page helps you resolve common administration issues. You can fix complex
problems by selecting the Execute button next to a task.

Here are the functions available on the Troubleshooting page:

[Disconnect Account and Preserve User Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Disconnect your Docusign account from Salesforce without deleting your Docusign
    users.

[Disconnect Account and Remove Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=vli1610514116162.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect a Docusign account and remove users with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Disconnect your Docusign account and remove all Docusign users and settings from
    Salesforce.

[Clear User Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ncl1610479844444.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix the CUSTOM_SETTINGS_LIMIT_EXCEEDED issue with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Delete cached user access tokens to free up Salesforce storage space.

[Folder Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=sqj1610517836441.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to reset folder permissions for Docusign content with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Trigger a root folder permissions update. This update resets user permissions for all
    content.

[Refresh Products](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=agy1623993092688.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to activate and update Docusign for Salesforce apps and settings using the Refresh Products feature.")
:   Activate and update apps and settings connected to your Salesforce organization.

[Sync Domains](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ynp1680122164220.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature. Sync Domains fixes issues with Enhanced Domains and any changes that you make to your Salesforce domain.")
:   Resolve errors related to Enhanced Domains or domain changes that you make.

[Manage CLM Integration User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=yyh1698871133582.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Reset the token used by your Docusign CLM integration user. This feature is only available
    for Docusign CLM for Salesforce accounts.

title: Add a By Role Recipient to an Envelope Using Merge Field Syntax
heading_path: Add a By Role Recipient to an Envelope Using Merge Field Syntax
text:
Find out how to add merge field syntax to a By Role recipient in an envelope
template.

Use this procedure to add merge field syntax to a By Role recipient in an
envelope template:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwNjksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.a0Y39w4wkMMhb07mZ7q12uVgQpjSI0YcOxc0M0KGAvI&_LANG=enus)
   dropdown menu next to a recipient name.
2. Select Edit from the options.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
3. Complete the following from the Add Recipient dialog box:
   1. Select By Role in the left column.
   2. Role: Enter a recipient role like
      Signer 1.
   3. Add Name and Email: Select the link to add merge
      field syntax.
   4. Name: Add merge field syntax for the object name,
      like `{!Contact.Name}` for a Salesforce contact.
   5. Email Address: Add merge field syntax for the
      object email address, like `{!Contact.Email}` for a
      Salesforce contact.
   6. Select the Add button to save the recipient and
      close the dialog box.

      ![A screenshot displays merge field syntax added to contact fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210706-at-20211-PM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwNjksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.a0Y39w4wkMMhb07mZ7q12uVgQpjSI0YcOxc0M0KGAvI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays merge field syntax added to contact fields.")

You have completed the process. You have added a By
Role recipient to an envelope template for a Salesforce user, contact, or
lead. When a user selects the envelope template button on a record, the recipient name
and email is dynamically added.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
text:
Learn about the actions that you must take to use Docusign apps after
Salesforce security changes.

Important: You are not required to make any changes if
you have taken the following steps:

- Installed the Docusign connected app.
- Set the OAuth policy to Admin-approved users are pre-authorized.

  Note: Changing policy revokes existing
  user tokens. Existing users must re-authorize.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What is changing with Salesforce connected app security?
text:
Starting in September 2025, Salesforce began enforcing [restrictions for uninstalled connected apps](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=005132365&type=1). This usage
restriction blocks access to uninstalled connected apps. If you have not installed
the Docusign connected app and assigned permissions, your users
cannot access Docusign apps.

As part of this change, Salesforce created the Approve uninstalled connected apps user permission (defaulted to System
Administrator standard profile.)

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How do these security changes impact Docusign integrations?
text:
Connected apps installed before the security change continue to function
without interruption. Take action only if you did not install the connected app
before the changes.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What is an uninstalled connected app?
text:
An [uninstalled connected app](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.connected_app_how_to_install.htm&type=5) is an application in your
organization that has not been installed by a System Administrator. Organizations
integrated with Docusign contain an uninstalled connected app
named DocuSign on the Connected Apps OAuth
Usage page. By default, this application is not installed, and must
be manually installed by a System Administrator. ![A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_install_button_highlighted_on_the_Docusign_connected_app_OAuth_Usage_page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NzUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.5tzVsOW6NrE8k7A8YIcr_Da1w5uemJhb8Hh9oE7zVEs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.")

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
Which Docusign managed packages contain the connected app?
text:
The following Docusign for Salesforce packages contain the Docusign connected app:

- Docusign Apps Launcher (Namespace: dfsle)
- Docusign for Salesforce (Legacy Namespace: dsfs)
- FileIt (Legacy Namespace: SpringCMEos)

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How are new users impacted?
text:
If you have not installed the app before the security change, your new
users cannot access the Docusign connected app. Users see the
authorization button, but a Salesforce OAuth error displays after they enter their
Docusign credentials: We can't authorize you because
of an OAuth error. For more information, contact your Salesforce
administrator.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How are existing users impacted?
text:
If you never installed the app, existing users can access it if they meet the
following criteria:

- The user has previously authorized the app.
- The app isn’t using the [OAuth 2.0 device flow](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.remoteaccess_oauth_device_flow.htm&language=en_US&type=5).

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
Does Docusign Apps Launcher use OAuth 2.0 device flow?
text: No. Docusign Apps Launcher does not use OAuth 2.0 device flow.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What actions should I take?
text:
[Install the connected app to grant Docusign
for Salesforce access to new users](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Install-the-DocuSign-Apps-Launcher-to-Manage-Connected-Apps-in-Salesforce-Setup).

Note: Salesforce
and Docusign recommend selecting Admin-approved
users are pre-authorized. Each user must enter credentials to
gain Docusign for Salesforce app access if you select
All users may self-authorize.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What happens when I change the connected app policy?
text:
Make the following changes when you update the Permitted Users
setting to Admin approved users are pre-authorized:

Administrators
:   - All Apps Launcher apps: Add all necessary
      profiles and permission sets to the Docusign
      connected app. An error message displays for users not included
      in these profiles and permission sets: User is not
      admin approved to access this app.
    - CLM for Salesforce: [Disconnect your account and
      preserve user settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.") if you use CLM Document
      Generation. Otherwise, a Salesforce session is
      invalid error displays when users try to generate
      agreements.

Existing users
:   Authorized users must re-authorize. Steps to re-authorize vary for each
    installed package:

    - Docusign Apps Launcher: [Activate your Docusign
      for Salesforce user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qob1643667997351&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html).
    - Docusign Connect for Salesforce (Legacy):
      [Reconfigure your login with Docusign Connect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=tsk6312c996-938f-4f24-b11f-9da2f7887493.html). There is no
      option to authorize within the legacy Docusign
      for Salesforce integration.
    - FileIt (Legacy SpringCM): Take no action.
      Existing users are already authorized to use SpringCM FileIt. If
      users must re-authorize, use these procedures:
      - [Configure FileIt app security
        settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=qgu1583188662524.html).
      - [Connect Salesforce to
        SpringCM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=btt1576610138405.html).

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How do I resolve connected app errors?
text:
User is not admin approved to access this app
:   Resolution: Install and configure the Docusign
    connected app. Add the missing profile or permission set if you have
    already installed the app.

You have not granted or have revoked your consent to be impersonated by Docusign Apps Launcher
:   Resolution: Install the Docusign connected app. [Refresh the user's consent flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/DAL-Error-You-have-not-granted-or-have-revoked-your-consent-to-be-impersonated-by-DocuSign-Apps-Launcher) if you have
    already installed the app.

We can't authorize you because of an OAuth error. For more information, contact your Salesforce administrator
:   Resolution 1: Install the Docusign connected app. Add access to missing profiles
    or permission sets on the Connected App Detail page
    if you have already installed the app.
:   Resolution 2: Install the Docusign connected app. Go to Setup >  Connected Apps OAuth Usage and choose All users may
    self-authorize for the Docusign
    connected app.

Salesforce session is invalid
:   Resolution 1: Install the Docusign connected app. [Disconnect and reconnect your account and
    preserve user settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.") after changing OAuth policy to
    All users may self-authorize.
:   Resolution 2: Enable Allow
    Visualforce pages to access APIs if either
    Restrict Access to APIs with Connected
    Apps or Restrict Customers and Partners
    from Access APIs is enabled.

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
text:
Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.

The integration user is a Docusign administrator that connects your Docusign account to your Salesforce organization. When you connect accounts, you must enter Docusign and Salesforce administrator credentials. When your accounts are connected, users can access Docusign functions like sending agreements for signature.

Docusign uses OAuth 2.0 for communicating between Salesforce and Docusign. These tokens continue to operate unless an administrator chooses to break them or force an expiration.

Follow these guidelines to avoid issues with your integration:

Create dedicated integration users
:   Create generic integration administrators for your Docusign account and your Salesforce organization. Ensure that these generic credentials aren't tied to a person. For example, you can create a user named integration-user@yourcompany.com. Creating generic integration users protects your integration when administrators leave or change roles. Deactivating your integration user deletes custom Docusign fields and templates created by that user. So it is best to create an integration user that you will never deactivate.

Enable password login for at least one Docusign administrator
:   If you enforce SSO login for your integration user, [enable
    password login for at least one Docusign administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fji1583359148678.html&_LANG=enus). Your Docusign integration breaks if your SSO setup experiences
    an outage and no administrator can log in with a password.

Utilize a compatible Salesforce user license
:   Your Docusign for Salesforce integration user must be a member of the Docusign Administrator permission set. Since you cannot add [Salesforce Integration Users](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=release-notes.rn_api_integration_license.htm&release=242&type=5) to permission sets, you cannot use this license type as your integration user. Docusign recommends that you use a Salesforce [Standard User](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.users_license_types_available.htm&type=5) license when creating your Docusign integration user.

Note: Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce organizations to a single Docusign account.

The following is a list of integration user scenarios:

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
Actions that break your integration
text:
You close (deactivate) the Docusign integration user
:   - Docusign deletes all envelope templates
    - Docusign send buttons do not work
    - Docusign deletes custom merge fields created by the Docusign integration user
:   Before closing your Docusign integration user, create a template in your Docusign account and re-save each custom merge field.

(Gen and CLM only) When you deactivate your Salesforce integration user
:   Your Gen and CLM for Salesforce functions break. The Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app continues to function without issue.

You enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for your Salesforce integration user
:   A Salesforce integration user needs the following access:

    - Salesforce System Administrator access
    - [Enable MFA for API logins](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus) for
      the System Administrator profile set to
      False

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
Actions that do not break your integration
text:
You change the Docusign integration user password
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Only enter the Docusign integration user's password for the initial authorization.

You change the Salesforce integration user password
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Only enter the Salesforce integration user's password for the initial authorization.

You deactivate the Salesforce integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Important:

    - Deactivating an integration user that's used for both Docusign Apps Launcher **and** legacy eSignature for Salesforce breaks the legacy app.
    - Deactivating the Salesforce integration user breaks your Docusign Gen and CLM for Salesforce functionality.
    - If the deactivated administrator is also a system sender, Apex toolkit flows relying on the system sender break. Add a new active user as the system sender to prevent this.

You replace the integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.

    Note: Closing (deactivating) the Docusign integration user before adding a new one breaks the integration.

You enable single sign-on (SSO) for the Docusign integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Enabling SSO in the Docusign account does not interfere with communications between Salesforce and Docusign.

title: Envelope Template Field Categories and Functions
heading_path: Envelope Template Field Categories and Functions
text:
The Place Fields page in the envelope template process contains more than just standard
eSignature fields. You can add Salesforce fields, Notary fields, Seal fields, Smart Sections
or build custom fields.

The Place Fields page in the envelope template process contains
more than just standard eSignature fields. You can add Salesforce fields, Notary
fields, Seal fields, Smart Sections or build custom fields.

Here are all categories of fields that can be placed on documents:

Table 1. Field Categories and Functions

| Button | Field Type | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Standard Fields | Add standard Docusign fields such as Signature, Initial, Date Signed, and more. |
|  | Custom Fields | Add custom to place on the agreement. |
|  | Merge Fields | Add fields that link to Salesforce data. |
|  | Notary Fields | Add fields for a signer and notary (in the same physical location) to complete signing together. |
|  | Pre-fill Tools | Add a text field, checkbox, radio button, and more. |

title: Edit the Details of an Envelope Template Recipient
heading_path: Edit the Details of an Envelope Template Recipient
text:
You can edit the details of recipients in an existing envelope template at any time.

Use this procedure to edit the details of recipients in an existing envelope template:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMjEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.RWs9Q-57pSQqcLzakkPPah5m9szP9XyzlXnNdE6zWNo&_LANG=enus) next to a recipient
   name and choose Edit.

   ![A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplateeditrecipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMjEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.RWs9Q-57pSQqcLzakkPPah5m9szP9XyzlXnNdE6zWNo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an envelope template recipient with the Edit and Delete dropdown menu highlighted.")

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens

   Note: You can also select the name link (i.e. Owner) to edit the
   recipient.
2. Edit recipient details or actions.
3. Select the Add button. The Add Recipient dialog box
   closes.
4. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You have edited recipient details.

title: Learn About Using AutoPlace Fields in Envelope Templates
heading_path: Learn About Using AutoPlace Fields in Envelope Templates
text:
Learn how to build AutoPlace fields in template documents to specify where signer fields
appear.

AutoPlace fields are a feature you can use when building a Docusign envelope
template. AutoPlace automatically adds Docusign fields to specified places in a
document.

AutoPlace fields save time and reduce errors because senders do not have to manually place
fields for recipients.

- You can add AutoPlace fields throughout your documents for each recipient role.
- You can add automatic AutoPlace fields for up to 99 recipients
  (Signer 1, Signer 2, and so
  on.)
- You can build custom fields to add Salesforce fields and additional recipients
  to your document template.

Table 1. AutoPlace terms

| Term | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Anchor text | Text that tells Docusign what field to add to a document that is sent for e-signature. For example, if you add  \s1\ to a document, Docusign automatically adds a signature field to that location. This text is typically white, so it blends into the background of the page, and is not visible to the signer. |
| AutoPlace | A type of field that Docusign places over anchor text. Docusign automatically places these fields so that senders do not have to manually add them. |
| Envelope template recipient roles | Placeholder text that tells Docusign the role and signing order of recipients. |
| Merge field | A Docusign custom field that is connected to a Salesforce field. These fields are connected by the Relate to Salesforce property. |
| Writeback | A Docusign field that updates a Salesforce field when the signature process is complete. |

Administrator view:

An
administrator builds a Docusign envelope template. The
administrator wants to add AutoPlace fields in the template document. The
administrator adds documents and recipients to the template. The administrators
moves to the Place Fields page and takes the following actions:

Add
AutoPlace fields: The administrator adds these AutoPlace fields to
the document for the Signer 1 role:![A screenshot displays AutoPlace field examples for Name, Company, Signature, and Date Signed.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/AutoPlace-field-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNDQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.U6VMyy00ENDZwMQ4rr6L3pO1s_NepBKimD-cYZohskU&_LANG=enus)

Change AutoPlace text color: The administrator
changes the font color of the fields to match the page color of the template
document. This ensures that the AutoPlace text is not visible to recipients. In this
example, the administrator changes the font color to white:![A screenshot displays the Name, Company, Signature, and Date Signed fields appearing blank because the text is white.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/AutoPlace-fields-with-white-font.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNDQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.U6VMyy00ENDZwMQ4rr6L3pO1s_NepBKimD-cYZohskU&_LANG=enus)

Sender view:

A sender uses the
administrator's template to create an envelope. The AutoPlace fields add the correct
signature fields for Signer 1. Note that the fields are
visible to the sender, but not the AutoPlace text, which has white font:![A screenshot displays what senders see when AutoPlace adds Name, Company, Signature, and Date Signed fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/AutoPlace-fields-example-output.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNDQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.U6VMyy00ENDZwMQ4rr6L3pO1s_NepBKimD-cYZohskU&_LANG=enus)

Recipient view:

title: Learn About Using AutoPlace Fields in Envelope Templates
heading_path: Learn About Using AutoPlace Fields in Envelope Templates
text:
I1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNDQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.U6VMyy00ENDZwMQ4rr6L3pO1s_NepBKimD-cYZohskU&_LANG=enus)

The
Signer 1 recipient opens your envelope, and sees the
