title: Create a Linked Envelope Template
heading_path: Create a Linked Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to create an envelope template that links to one or more Docusign templates.

A Docusign envelope template is an eSignature sending experience that you
build for use in Salesforce. Your users select an envelope template button on a
Salesforce record, and are guided through the sending experience you built.

You decide whether your users can add documents, recipients, and signature fields to
those you included.

You can build envelope templates from scratch, or you can link to existing Docusign templates to build a linked envelope template.

Note: The Place Fields step does not display in a linked envelope template, even when documents are attached.

Use this procedure to build a linked envelope template:

- [Step 1: Select Templates and Choose a Name and Salesforce Object](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=vtl1660539049867.html&_LANG=enus "Start by naming your template, choosing a Salesforce object, and selecting the existing templates you will use.")
- [Step 2: (Optional) Add Documents to Your Linked Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=tbs1660542119912.html&_LANG=enus "In the second step of creating a linked envelope template, you add documents to use as content for the template. Word and PDF documents are most commonly used.")
- [Step 3: (Optional) Add Recipients to Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dqp1660543842156.html&_LANG=enus "In the third step of creating a linked envelope template, you add recipients and a message to recipients.")
- [Step 4: Create Salesforce Merge Fields for Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ktu1596146796958.1.html&_LANG=enus "How to create reminders for recipients, set an expiration date, enable the Quick Send experience, have a Salesforce status changed when a signature process is complete, and more.")
- [Step 5: PlaceDocusignand Salesforce Fields on Your Template Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ktu1596146796958.1.html&_LANG=enus "How to create reminders for recipients, set an expiration date, enable the Quick Send experience, have a Salesforce status changed when a signature process is complete, and more.")
- [Step 6: Add Options Like Quick Send and Writeback](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=owi1596146834259.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add an Experience Cloud envelope template button to Salesforce layouts.")
- [Step 7: Create an Envelope Template Custom Button on a Salesforce Page Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=owi1596146834259_1.html&_LANG=enus "The seventh step of the envelope template creation process involves adding a custom button to the layout of the Salesforce object chosen in step one.")

title: Use Document Custom AutoPlace Fields
heading_path: Use Document Custom AutoPlace Fields
text:
Learn to use document custom auto-place fields for recipient information that does not
write back to Salesforce.

You can add document custom field AutoPlace fields to envelope template
documents.

Use document custom AutoPlace fields in templates that don’t need Docusign fields
mapped to Salesforce fields. When recipients complete their assigned fields,
Docusign does not update any fields in Salesforce.

Important:
[You must create document custom fields in your
Docusign account before you can use them in your envelope template.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=zus1583277381502.html&_LANG=enus)

Follow this procedure to add document custom AutoPlace fields to an envelope template
document:

1. Select the name of the document you will edit.

   The document preview page opens.
2. Select the Download button in the upper-right.

   The document is saved to the downloads folder of your
   device.
3. Add document custom AutoPlace fields to your template document:

   1. Open the document and move the cursor to where you will add document custom AutoPlace
      fields.
   2. Enter the text for AutoPlace fields (\s1\ for
      Signer 1 signature, \n1\ for Signer 1 name, and
      so on.)
      ![AutoPlace field examples](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/AutoPlace-field-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1OTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.zCr1H4xDSQYjxef2tBFwVczzHb7TFJfZKUY1n5MD5WE&_LANG=enus "AutoPlace field examples")
   3. Highlight the AutoPlace text and change the font color to match the page
      background. So, if the page is white, change the AutoPlace font to
      white.
      ![AutoPlace fields with white font](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/AutoPlace-fields-with-white-font.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1OTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.zCr1H4xDSQYjxef2tBFwVczzHb7TFJfZKUY1n5MD5WE&_LANG=enus "AutoPlace fields with white font")

      Note: This prevents the AutoPlace text from
      being visible in your envelope. Your recipients only see the
      signature fields.
   4. Save the changes to your template document.
4. Replace your old template document with the new version:

   1. Return to the envelope template Documents
      page.
   2. Select the arrow dropdown menu next next to the document
      you updated.
   3. Select Delete. The document disappears.
      ![Delete template document](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Delete-template-document.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1OTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.zCr1H4xDSQYjxef2tBFwVczzHb7TFJfZKUY1n5MD5WE&_LANG=enus "Delete template document")
   4. Select the Upload button.
   5. Select the name of the updated file from your device.
   6. Select Open. The Upload File dialog box
      opens.
   7. Select the Done button.

   The dialog box disappears. The name of your updated document
   appears.
5. Select the Save and Close button in the
   upper-right.

   The envelope template page opens.

You have completed the process. You have added standard
AutoPlace fields to your envelope template document. When you send an envelope from this
template, Docusign or Salesforce fields are automatically placed where you added
AutoPlace fields. The AutoPlace text is not visible to your recipients, because you
colored it to match the page. Your recipients only see the fields they need to complete.

title: Access the Docusign Envelope Templates Tab to Build and Edit Envelope Templates
heading_path: Access the Docusign Envelope Templates Tab to Build and Edit Envelope Templates
text:
Learn to access the Docusign Envelope Templates tab in
Salesforce.

Docusign Envelope Templates allow users to select a button on a record to
launch an administrator-built sending experience.

Use this procedure to access the Docusign Envelope Templates tab in
your Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
2. Make any selection:

   - [New](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus): Create a new envelope template.
   - [Change Owner](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ain1596147068228.html&_LANG=enus): Change the owner of an envelope template.
   - [Import](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=juw1586134252760.html&_LANG=enus): Import your data into Salesforce. You can import up to 50,000 records at a time.
   - [Rename](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bzu1596146928480.html&_LANG=enus): Change the name of an envelope template.
   - [Edit](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xow1596147003565.html&_LANG=enus): Add or edit documents, recipients, Salesforce fields, Docusign fields, and envelope template options.
   - [Delete](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=yzr1596147036291.html&_LANG=enus): Delete an envelope template.

   You have completed the task. You accessed the Docusign Envelope
   Templates tab. From this page, you can create and edit Envelope
   Templates.

:   Add, edit, and revoke Docusign app permission to Salesforce users.

title: Use Sign Now to Let a Sender Sign Their Own Envelope
heading_path: Use Sign Now to Let a Sender Sign Their Own Envelope
text:
Learn how to use the Sign Now option to let the sender to sign their own envelope
immediately after sending it.

The Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now) option lets the
sender sign their own envelope immediately after sending it. This simplifies the
signing process for the sender if they are also a recipient.

Here are the specifications of the Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign
Now) option:

- This option only appears when you select In Person Signer
  as the recipient action
- When Signing Order is *enabled*, this option only appears if the previous
  recipient has Sign Now enabled
- If the first recipient in the signing order has Sign Now *unchecked*, no
  recipients can use the Sign Now option
- When Signing Order is *disabled*, you can add the Sign Now option for any
  In Person Signer recipient

Use this procedure to add the Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign
Now) option to an envelope template recipient.

Note: You can also use this procedure on the Recipients step when sending an
envelope.

1. Select the name of the recipient you want to update.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
2. Select In Person Signer from the Action dropdown
   menu.
3. Select the Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now)
   check box.
4. Select Add.
5. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You have added
theInitiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now) option to your
envelope template recipient. The sender is prompted to sign their own envelope
immediately after they send it.

title: Reset Folder Permissions Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Reset Folder Permissions Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to reset folder permissions for Docusign content with the
Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

You can reset folder permissions for Docusign content in your Salesforce
organization using the Folder Permissions feature of the Docusign TroubleShooting tool. Administrators can select one
button and quickly reset all Docusign folders to their default
permissions, removing all custom security changes.

Use this procedure to use the Folder Permissions feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3OTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.V9N-JWtHuAvozH4i1GM2u2hi4RfF9Bvhr5dR6fdRxkk&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3OTgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.V9N-JWtHuAvozH4i1GM2u2hi4RfF9Bvhr5dR6fdRxkk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.
4. Disconnect Account and Reset Users: Select
   Execute.

   The page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have cleared all custom folder
permissions for your Docusign content.

title: Envelope Template Field Types and Functions
heading_path: Envelope Template Field Types and Functions
text:
Learn about the field types that you can use in envelope templates.

The Docusign envelope template Place Fields tab contains many different fields that can be placed on uploaded documents.

Here are the fields that can be used in an envelope template:

Table 1.  Docusign field icons and actions

| Field Icon | Field Name | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Signature | Add a recipient's signature. |
|  | Initial | Add a recipient's initials. |
|  | Stamp | Add a stamp image instead of a standard signature. |
|  | Date Signed | Add the date of a recipient's signature. |
|  | Name | Add the full name of a recipient. |
|  | Email | Add a recipient's email address. |
|  | Company | Add recipient's company. |
|  | Title | Add recipient's title. |
|  | Automatic Renewal | Add an AI-assisted attribute to indicate that an agreement will automatically renew at the end of its term. |
|  | Text | Add a free-form field for recipient to enter text. |
|  | Number | Add a field that only accepts numbers. You can format the field as a plain number or as currency. |
|  | Checkbox | Add a check box for recipient options. |
|  | Dropdown | Add a dropdown menu for recipient options. |
|  | Radio | Add a radio button for recipient options. |
|  | Payment Item | Add a field to accept recipient payments. You must configure a payment account to use this feature. |
|  | Drawing | Add a rectangular canvas where a recipient can draw or upload an image, diagram, and more. |
|  | Formula | Add a formula to calculate a value based on number or date fields in your document. |
|  | Attachment | Add a field to request additional documentation from a recipient. |
|  | Note | Add a read-only informational field that displays text to recipients during signing. |
|  | Approve | Add an alternative to a required Signature or Initial field. Selecting Approve is the equivalent of signing. |
|  | Decline | Add a field that terminates the recipient signing session. |

title: Use Hyperforce with Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Use Hyperforce with Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Learn to add domains to your allowlist so that Docusign apps work with
your Hyperforce organization.

[Hyperforce](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000388902&type=1) is a Salesforce infrastructure architecture
that is built for the public cloud. Hyperforce infrastructure is composed of code
rather than hardware. Hyperforce uses enhanced standards for compliance, security,
agility, scalability, and privacy.

You must update your Salesforce security settings to use Docusign apps in
your Hyperforce organization. Otherwise, Salesforce denies connections to your Docusign account and causes the integration to fail.

You can update your security settings by adding either IP ranges or
domains to your allowlist.

If you want to allow IP addresses, use the following ranges:

- [Salesforce](https://ip-ranges.salesforce.com/ip-ranges.json)
- [Docusign eSignature](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/esignature)
- [Docusign CLM](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/clm)

However, due to the dynamic nature of cloud environments, Salesforce recommends allowing
domains, rather than IP ranges.

Use this procedure to add domains to your allowlist:

1. Access the Trusted URLs page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4MDksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Ol1-TPvoWZw2Pkd6n2t_L0rP_1Ngg6OenO1Fndik8BE&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Trusted URLs in the Quick Find
      field.

   The Trusted URLs page opens.
2. Add a trusted URL to the list:
   1. Select New Trusted URL.
   2. API Name: Enter a simple display name, like
      salesforce for salesforce.com.
   3. URL: Enter
      https://\*.salesforce.com.
   4. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the API Name and URL fields of the Trusted URLs page filled with Salesforce domain information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-API-Name-and-URL-fields-of-the-Trusted-URLs-page-filled-with-salesforce-domain-information.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4MDksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Ol1-TPvoWZw2Pkd6n2t_L0rP_1Ngg6OenO1Fndik8BE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the API Name and URL fields of the Trusted URLs page filled with Salesforce domain information.")
3. Repeat the process to add the following domains:

   - https://\*.force.com
   - https://\*.lightning.com
   - https://\*.visualforce.com
   - https://\*.docusign.com
   - https://\*.docusign.net
4. (Optional) [Add domains from this list](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.setup_domains.htm&type=5) if you
   control user or server access to the internet through allowlists.
5. (Optional) For IP-restricted user profiles. To access Docusign
   Apps Launcher, Salesforce users must have valid authorization to the Docusign connected app.
   1. Copy all restricted Hyperforce IPs from your login history.
   2. Add the restricted Hyperforce IPs to your allowlist.

   You have added Hyperforce IPs to your allowlist. For the complete list of
   Hyperforce IPs, visit the [Salesforce Help site](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=release-notes.rn_hyperforce_inboud_ips.htm&release=260&type=5).

You have completed the process. You have added domains to
your allowlist. You can use Docusign Apps Launcher apps in your
Hyperforce organization.

title: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
heading_path: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
text:
Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or
Login IP Ranges to fix issues with envelope and recipient
statuses not updating.

If your Docusign Envelope Status and Docusign Recipient Status do not update, there are two
possible causes:

- The Salesforce organization has Multi-Factor Authentication for API
  Logins enabled on the user's' profile. This is incompatible with
  Docusign’s server-to-server API updates.
- Login IP Ranges are specified on the user's profile. This
  prevents API logins for the user from Docusign servers.

title: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
heading_path: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
Resolution 1: Disable Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2OTMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.84dMPvSvkke7Gpfg9ISxrFrMThUDIlMDBchay4Pneno&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Edit button.
4. Uncheck the Multi-Factor Authentication for API
   Logins box.
5. Select the Save button.

   The process is complete. Your Docusign Envelope Status and
   Docusign Recipient Status now update.

title: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
heading_path: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
Resolution 2: Disable Login IP Ranges on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2OTMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.84dMPvSvkke7Gpfg9ISxrFrMThUDIlMDBchay4Pneno&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Login IP Ranges link at the top of
   the Profile page.
4. Select the Del link to the left of an IP
   range.
5. Select the OK button on the Are you
   sure you want to delete the IP Login Range dialog box.
6. Repeat the Del action for all remaining
   ranges.

   The process is complete. Your Docusign Envelope Status and
   Docusign Recipient Status now update.

title: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
heading_path: Envelope and Recipient Status are Not Updated
Resolution 3: Add the relevant Docusign endpoint IP addresses to the allow-list
text:
1. [Add Trusted IP Ranges for Docusign Gen for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=lip1592574174158.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus)
2. Add any other restricted IP addresses shown in the user’s login
   history.

   The process is complete. Your Docusign Envelope Status and
   Docusign Recipient Status now update.

title: Step 2: (Optional) Add Documents to Your Linked Template
heading_path: Step 2: (Optional) Add Documents to Your Linked Template
text:
In the second step of creating a linked envelope template, you add documents to use
as content for the template. Word and PDF documents are most commonly used.

Use this procedure to add or remove documents, or change the linked Docusign templates you
selected in step one.

Note: This step is optional. You can
add new documents, or keep the documents imported from your linked templates.

1. Choose a method to add documents. You can add up to ten documents.
   - Upload from your device:
     1. Select the Upload Document button.
     2. Follow the prompts to add content from your device.
     3. Select Done.
   - Add dynamically from a Salesforce record:
     1. Select the Attach documents from Salesforce
        record check box.
     2. Choose an option from the dropdown menu:
        - Latest Document: Uploads the latest
          document from the record
        - File Name Contains: Uploads documents
          from the record that match file name keywords you enter
        - All Documents: Uploads all documents
          from the record
2. (Optional) Change Template:
   1. Select the Change Template button.

      The Link Docusign Template dialog box opens.
   2. Select the check box next to each template that you want to add or remove, or use the search field to find a template.
   3. Select the Select button.

      The Link Docusign Template dialog box closes. The Documents page opens.
3. Select Next.

title: Step 2: (Optional) Add Documents to Your Linked Template
heading_path: Step 2: (Optional) Add Documents to Your Linked Template
Next steps
text: You have completed step two. Move to [Step 3: (Optional) Add Recipients to Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dqp1660543842156.html&_LANG=enus "In the third step of creating a linked envelope template, you add recipients and a message to recipients.").

title: Remove Docusign App Access From a Salesforce User
heading_path: Remove Docusign App Access From a Salesforce User
text:
Learn to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from Salesforce
users.

You can use the Remove command to remove Docusign app access from
Salesforce users.

- Remove removes a Salesforce user's access to Docusign apps. This command does not remove users from the connected Docusign account or
  delete Salesforce users.
- You can not remove and close your user while logged on.
- You can not remove and close the only eSignature administrator user.

Use this procedure to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from
Salesforce users:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to remove.
2. Select Remove from the Add
   Users
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NTMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.W0rf1KqfsbN8Ph1zpflP509hLz5mSyFicn8r1-5Wa4k&_LANG=enus) dropdown menu.
3. Select Remove.

You have completed the process. You removed a user's access to the
Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce apps. The user remains in the Salesforce
organization and the connected Docusign account.

title: Step 4: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App from your Salesforce organization
heading_path: Step 4: Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce App from your Salesforce organization
text:
Learn how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your
Salesforce organization.

You have removed all legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce dependencies. You can now
uninstall the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your Salesforce organization
using this procedure:

1. Open the Installed Packages page as an
   administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MjEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.vVmoVM2biXM64w9ElEzT0kashGeV2s8xCYPkJUKUq6M&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Installed Packages in the Quick Find
      field.

   The Installed Packages page opens.
2. Select the Uninstall link next to the
   Docusign eSignature for Salesforce package
   name.

   The Uninstalling a Package page opens.
3. Scroll to the bottom of the page and select the Yes, I want to
   uninstall this package and permanently delete all associated
   components check box.
4. Select the Uninstall button.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/daluninstall.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MjEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.vVmoVM2biXM64w9ElEzT0kashGeV2s8xCYPkJUKUq6M&_LANG=enus)

   Note: If you have
   remaining dependencies from the legacy eSignature for Salesforce app, an
   Unable to uninstall package message opens.
   Remove the remaining dependencies listed, and then repeat the
   instructions to uninstall the app.

You have completed the entire process. You have
removed all legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce dependencies, and have uninstalled
the app from your Salesforce organization. Your migration from the legacy app to the
current eSignature for Salesforce app is complete.

title: Troubleshoot and Fix eSignature for Salesforce Issues
heading_path: Troubleshoot and Fix eSignature for Salesforce Issues
text:
Learn to identify, troubleshoot, and fix Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
issues.

If you have issues with eSignature for Salesforce functions, you can troubleshoot and fix them
using these resources:

- [eSignature for Salesforce Troubleshooting
  Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=esy1597029449495.html&_LANG=enus "Administrators learn to troubleshoot issues they are having with the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app.")
- [Use the Disconnect Link to Disconnect and Reconnect Docusign and Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization.")
- [Fix Account-Wide Issues Using the Docusign Apps
  Launcher Troubleshooting Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=rag1597638195677.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting is a Salesforce page that allows administrators to troubleshoot and resolve issues with these Docusign apps.")
- [Rebuild Your Docusign Connection, Users, and
  Templates After Cloning or Refreshing a Salesforce Sandbox](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=prl1678213109376.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to rebuild your Docusign connection, users, and envelope templates after refreshing or cloning a Salesforce sandbox.")
- [Uninstall the Legacy Docusign eSignature for
  Salesforce App From Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=olq1642610020299.html&_LANG=enus "Find out how to uninstall the legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app from your Salesforce organization.")
- [Uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher Package of
  Apps From Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=yxe1623689627503.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps from your Salesforce organization.")
- [Download Previous Versions of Docusign CLM, Gen,
  and eSignature for Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hkh1622610912812.html&_LANG=enus "Find links to previous versions of the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps.")

title: Add In Person Signing to Envelope Template Recipients
heading_path: Add In Person Signing to Envelope Template Recipients
text:
Learn how to use the In Person Signing feature to create a multi-recipient signing
session, with all signers in the same room.

In Person signing creates a multi-recipient signing
session, where all signers must be in the same room. The host opens the envelope,
and then each signing recipient signs on the host's device.

The following
rules apply to In Person signing sessions:

- The host of the in-person signing session must be a Docusign eSignature for
  Salesforce user
- You can add an In Person Signer recipient anywhere in the signing order
- Other In Person Signer recipients do not need to be Docusign users
- You can add other recipients to the envelope that are not in person signers
  (Needs to Sign, Receives a Copy, and so on)
- You can set the signer name using Lookup Fields,
  Related Lists, Users/Contacts,
  and By Role
- You cannot use SMS delivery for In Person Signer
  sessions
- You can only use the Sign Now option if the In Person
  Signer is first in the signing order (serial or parallel)
- You can use [merge field syntax](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus) to complete the Host Name and Host Email
  Address fields

Example:

Your customer's president and head of finance
meet in your office to review a contract. You create an envelope for an In Person Signer
session. You host the session and guide your customers through the signing process. You
answer questions they have as they sign. You pass your tablet to each signer until all
have completed the session.

Use this procedure to add an In Person
Signer to an envelope template:

Note: You can also use this procedure on the
Recipients step when sending an envelope.

1. Select the name of the recipient you want to update

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
2. Complete the following fields in the Add Recipient dialog box:
   1. Action: Select In Person Signer from the dropdown menu.
   2. (Optional): Specify a host for the session:

      1. De-select the Make the Sender Host
         box.
      2. Host Name: Enter the name of the Docusign user who will host the In Person Signer session.
      3. Host Email: Enter the email address of the Docusign user who will host the In Person
         Signer session.

         Note: You can also [use merge field
         syntax](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wtk1625247088017.html&_LANG=enus) to add a host name and email from Salesforce objects.
   3. (Optional) Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now): Select this check box if you want the in person signing session to begin as soon as the envelope is sent.
   4. Select Add.

   The Add Recipient dialog box closes.
3. (Optional): Add Recipient: Add more In Person Signer
   recipients to your template.
4. (Optional): + Signing Order: Set the order in which
   recipients sign the envelope.
5. Select the Save and Close button.

You have completed the process. You have added theIn
Person Signer option to your envelope template recipient.

title: "This Page Has an Error. You Might Just Need to Refresh It" Displays When I Open Docusign for Salesforce Apps
heading_path: "This Page Has an Error. You Might Just Need to Refresh It" Displays When I Open Docusign for Salesforce Apps
text:
Learn to fix the "This page has an error. You might just need to refresh it." error that
displays.

When I open Docusign apps in Salesforce, an error displays. The
error message reads: This page has an error. You might just need to
refresh it. Error in $A.getCallback() [TypeError: d is not a function] Failing
descriptor: {ltng:outApp}. I cannot open Docusign
apps in Salesforce.

title: "This Page Has an Error. You Might Just Need to Refresh It" Displays When I Open Docusign for Salesforce Apps
heading_path: "This Page Has an Error. You Might Just Need to Refresh It" Displays When I Open Docusign for Salesforce Apps
Resolution: Upgrade your version of Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: You have Docusign Apps Launcher
version 5.4 or lower installed in your organization.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3NzYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.N3J8IWSCinFkV93IWnLtwyq3REOqpNfiFWV8QeJ7x8Y&_LANG=enus)
      and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. If your version number is 5.4 or lower, [upgrade Apps Launcher in your
   Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.").
3. Select Save.

   You have completed the process. You have upgraded the Apps
   Launcher package in your Salesforce organization. Docusign apps and services load normally.

title: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
heading_path: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
text:
Learn to install and configure the Docusign connected app for your Salesforce organization.

Important: You are not required to make any changes if you have taken the
following steps:

- Installed the Docusign connected app
- Set the OAuth Policy to Admin-approved users are
  pre-authorized

Starting in September 2025, Salesforce began enforcing [restrictions for uninstalled connected apps](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=005132365&type=1). These
restrictions impact some organizations using Docusign Apps
Launcher for Salesforce. If you have not installed the Docusign
connected app and assigned permissions, your users cannot access Docusign apps.

Use this procedure to install the Docusign connected app and grant
access to your users:

1. Access the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NzgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Cl_DOfJW3jqgbtHnwzbXFaQ1VS3zpIX-CmlvsjpoEsU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Connected Apps OAuth Usage in the
      Quick Find field.

      The Connected Apps OAuth Usage page opens.
2. Install the Docusign connected app:
   1. Docusign: Select
      Install.

      The Install connected app? dialog box
      opens.
   2. Select Install.![A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_install_button_highlighted_on_the_Docusign_connected_app_OAuth_Usage_page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NzgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Cl_DOfJW3jqgbtHnwzbXFaQ1VS3zpIX-CmlvsjpoEsU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.")

      The Docusign Connected App Detail page
      displays.
3. Edit the connected app policies:

   Note: Salesforce and Docusign recommend
   selecting Admin-approved users are pre-authorized.
   Each user must enter credentials to gain Docusign for
   Salesforce app access if you select All users may
   self-authorize.

   1. Select Edit Policies.

      The Connected App Edit page displays.
   2. Permitted Users: Select
      Admin-approved users are
      pre-authorized.![A screenshot displays the permitted users field set to Admin approved users are pre-authorized.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_permitted_users_field_set_to_Admin_approved_users_are_pre-authorized.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NzgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Cl_DOfJW3jqgbtHnwzbXFaQ1VS3zpIX-CmlvsjpoEsU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the permitted users field set to Admin approved users are pre-authorized.")

      A dialog box opens: Enabling this option will result
      in all users currently using this app being denied access. Please
      reference the Connected Apps OAuth Usage Report if you are unsure
      who is using the app.
   3. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes.
   4. Select Save.

title: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
heading_path: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
text:
is app being denied access. Please
      reference the Connected Apps OAuth Usage Report if you are unsure
      who is using the app.
   3. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes.
   4. Select Save.

      The Docusign Connected App Detail page
      displays.
4. Grant Apps Launcher access to Salesforce users:
   1. Select Manage Profiles or Manage
      Permission Sets at the bottom of the Connected App
      Detail page.![A screenshot displays the OAuth Usage page with the Manage Profiles and Manage Permission Sets buttons highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_OAuth_Usage_page_with_the_Manage_Profiles_and_Manage_Permission_Sets_buttons_highlighted_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NzgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Cl_DOfJW3jqgbtHnwzbXFaQ1VS3zpIX-CmlvsjpoEsU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the OAuth Usage page with the Manage Profiles and Manage Permission Sets buttons highlighted.")
   2. Select the check box for each profile or permission set you want to
      include.
   3. Select Save.

   The Docusign Connected App Detail page
   displays.
5. If your organizations has API Access Control configured, take the following actions:
   1. Enable Allow Visualforce pages to access APIs.
   2. Go to Setup > Permission Sets > your Docusign permission set > Assigned Connected Apps and assign at least one permission set to the Docusign connected app.![A screenshot displays the API Access Control Docusign permission set page with the Assigned Connected Apps link highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/API_Access_Control_Assigned_Connected_Apps.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3NzgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Cl_DOfJW3jqgbtHnwzbXFaQ1VS3zpIX-CmlvsjpoEsU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the API Access Control Docusign permission set page with the Assigned Connected Apps link highlighted.")

You have completed the process. You have installed the Docusign connected
app and assigned permissions to your users. Users can access Docusign
apps in your Salesforce organization. It you selected All users may
self-authorize, your users must [re-authenticate to use Docusign apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html).

title: Step 2: Prepare Your Salesforce Users for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
heading_path: Step 2: Prepare Your Salesforce Users for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
text:
Learn to prepare your Salesforce users for Experience Cloud envelope template
use.

Use this procedure to prepare your Salesforce
users for Experience Cloud envelope templates:

1. Open the Docusign Sender permission set:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays the Salesforce Setup gear icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.h1wUeSqb2LaLtQ5q978ug0AFtjDkq3wvMWgfFUz43Go&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Salesforce Setup gear icon.") and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Permission Sets in the Quick Find
      field.

      The Permission Sets page displays.
   3. Permission Set Name: Select the
      Docusign Sender link.

      The Docusign Sender page
      displays.
2. Add Experience Cloud users to the Docusign Sender permission
   set:
   1. Select Manage Assignments.

      The Current Assignments page displays.
   2. Select Add Assignment.

      The Select Users to Assign page displays.
   3. Select the check box next to each user that you want to add.

      Note: You cannot add Experience Cloud
      Guest users.
   4. Select Next.

      The Select an Expiration Option For Assigned Users page
      displays.
   5. Select Assign.

      The Docusign Sender page displays with
      selected users added.
   6. Select Done.

   The Sharing Settings page refreshes, displaying your
   changes.
3. Turn on the API Enabled permission for your Experience
   Cloud user profiles:

   Note: If you are using a default
   profile, clone it first so that you can edit the
   permissions.

   1. Enter and select Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.

      The Profiles page displays.
   2. Profile Name: Select Edit
      next to the profile you want to update.

      The Profile Edit page displays.
   3. ![A screenshot displays the Administrative Permissions section of a Profile page with the API Enabled box checked and highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/DAL_Experience_Cloud_envelope_template_prepare_users.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.h1wUeSqb2LaLtQ5q978ug0AFtjDkq3wvMWgfFUz43Go&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Administrative Permissions section of a Profile page with the API Enabled box checked and highlighted.")Administrative Permissions: Select the check box
      next to API Enabled.
   4. Select Save.

      The Profiles page displays.

title: Step 2: Prepare Your Salesforce Users for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
heading_path: Step 2: Prepare Your Salesforce Users for Experience Cloud Envelope Templates
Next steps
text: You have completed step two of the process. Move to [Step 3: Add a Name, Data Source, and Experience Cloud Option](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=wiw1617391189933.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to add a name, data source, and enable the Experience Cloud option.").

title: Customize the Email Subject and Message Seen by Recipients
heading_path: Customize the Email Subject and Message Seen by Recipients
text:
You can customize the email subject and message to recipients by editing the envelope
template Recipients step.

Envelope templates have a pre-built email subject and
message for all recipients. You can customize the email subject and message to
recipients by editing the envelope template Recipients step.

Use this procedure to
change a message to recipients in an existing envelope template:

1. Customize any field:
   - Email Subject: Enter up to 100 characters of text
   - Email Message: Enter up to 2,000 characters of text
2. Select the Save and Close button at the top-right of the
   page.

You have completed the process. You have customized the recipient
message.

title: Create a Default Envelope Template to Automate Your Contract Process
heading_path: Create a Default Envelope Template to Automate Your Contract Process
text:
Learn to create a default envelope template to lead senders through a guided sending
experience.

You can create a default envelope template to lead senders through a guided sending experience.

You decide what documents, recipients, and signature fields each envelope will contain.

You also decide whether senders can customize the envelope to add documents, recipients,
and fields.

Tip: This video guides you through the process
of [creating an envelope template](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6171917923001). Each step in the
documentation also has its own video guide.

Follow this procedure to build an envelope template:

- [Add a Name and Data Source](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xwj1596146541944.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to select a name and Salesforce object for your template.")
- [Add Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hya1596146614785.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add documents to your envelope template.")
- [Add Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ooo1596146674305.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add recipients to your envelope template.")
- [Create Merge Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jdl1611042391715.1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Salesforce merge fields to add to your envelope template.")
- [Place Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ywv1596146733047.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to place Docusign and Salesforce fields on the document text.")
- [Add Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ktu1596146796958.1.html&_LANG=enus "How to create reminders for recipients, set an expiration date, enable the Quick Send experience, have a Salesforce status changed when a signature process is complete, and more.")
- [Create a Custom Button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=owi1596146834259.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add an Experience Cloud envelope template button to Salesforce layouts.")

title: Clone Envelope Templates in Salesforce
heading_path: Clone Envelope Templates in Salesforce
text:
Learn how to clone one or more envelope templates for use in the same or another
organization.

title: Clone Envelope Templates in Salesforce
heading_path: Clone Envelope Templates in Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
- Required: Salesforce and Docusign administrator status.
- Required: Install Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce v4.5 or
  higher.

You can clone a Docusign envelope template from one Salesforce organization
and use it in another organization. You can also clone templates in the same
Salesforce organization to address similar use cases. Cloning helps administrators
quickly build templates, without manually recreating them.

Important: You must take extra steps when uploading cloned
templates to Salesforce environments with different cloning envelope templates with
custom fields. Custom fields are tied to the user who created them. If the creating
user is not present in the new organization, imported custom fields fail. Add the
creating user to the new organization or manually rebuild the custom fields.

Use this procedure to clone a Docusign envelope template:

1. View your envelope templates as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NjMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.yQp_Y6z0Pp_Ca6_el_npG82hXuUiINRlEgxLJ0UD7wE&_LANG=enus) and select
      View All.
   2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
   3. Select eSignature in the left column.

   The eSignature Configuration page
   opens.
2. Select the check box next to each template that you want to download, and
   select Download Template from the Create
   Template
    ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NjMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.yQp_Y6z0Pp_Ca6_el_npG82hXuUiINRlEgxLJ0UD7wE&_LANG=enus) dropdown
   menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the Envelope template Download button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Envelope-template-Download-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NjMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.yQp_Y6z0Pp_Ca6_el_npG82hXuUiINRlEgxLJ0UD7wE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Envelope template Download button.")

   The screen refreshes. A message opens: Templates
   downloaded.

   Important: Due to
   Salesforce limitations, you cannot download templates larger than 4
   MB.
3. Upload your cloned templates to an organization:

title: Clone Envelope Templates in Salesforce
heading_path: Clone Envelope Templates in Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
eshes. A message opens: Templates
   downloaded.

   Important: Due to
   Salesforce limitations, you cannot download templates larger than 4
   MB.
3. Upload your cloned templates to an organization:

   - Current organization:
     1. Select Create
        Template ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NjMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.yQp_Y6z0Pp_Ca6_el_npG82hXuUiINRlEgxLJ0UD7wE&_LANG=enus) and choose
        Upload Template from the dropdown
        menu.
     2. Follow your device's prompts to select the downloaded file.
   - Another organization:
     1. Open the eSignature Configuration page in
        your destination Salesforce organization.
     2. Select Create Template ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5NjMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.yQp_Y6z0Pp_Ca6_el_npG82hXuUiINRlEgxLJ0UD7wE&_LANG=enus) and choose
        Upload Template from the dropdown
        menu.
     3. Follow your device's prompts to select the downloaded file.
4. Complete the actions in the Upload Template dialog
   box:
   1. (Optional) Add custom button to matching layouts: Select this option if you want Docusign to add a template button to the same layouts as the original organization. If the template is on the Account layout in the original organization, Docusign adds it to the Account layout here. Docusign does not add custom buttons to unmatched layouts.
   2. Select Upload.

   The page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have downloaded one or more
envelope templates and uploaded the file to create a clone. The cloned templates display
on the eSignature Configuration page of the importing Salesforce
organization.

title: Step 4: Uninstall Apps Launcher from Your organization
heading_path: Step 4: Uninstall Apps Launcher from Your organization
text:
Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your
Salesforce organization.

You have removed all Docusign Apps Launcher dependencies.

Use this procedure to uninstall the Apps Launcher package from your organization:

1. Open the Installed Packages page as an
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ONayRkX9tbxY90c0_VtZVFdk3yKDrYorAgQzymjyb0Q&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in the Quick
      Find field.

      The Installed Packages page opens.
2. Select Uninstall next to **Docusign
   Apps Launcher**.

   The Uninstalling a Package page opens.
3. Scroll to the bottom and select Yes, I want to uninstall this
   package and permanently delete all associated components.
4. Select Uninstall. ![A screenshot displays the uninstall button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/daluninstall.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ONayRkX9tbxY90c0_VtZVFdk3yKDrYorAgQzymjyb0Q&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the uninstall button highlighted.")

   Note: If an
   Unable to uninstall package message displays, you
   have remaining Docusign dependencies. Remove the listed
   dependencies and repeat the uninstall process.

You have completed the entire process. You have removed
all Docusign Apps Launcher dependencies, and have uninstalled the
package from your Salesforce organization. You can [install the
latest version from the Salesforce AppExchange](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.").

title: Delete a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Delete a Document Uploaded to an Envelope Template
text:
You can delete documents from an envelope template at any time.

You can delete documents from an envelope template at any time.

Warning: If you delete a document that
contains signature fields for one of the template recipients, those fields will need
to be re-added to another document in the template.

**Example**: You delete a
document containing Sender 1's signature fields. The signature process
will fail unless Sender 1 is removed from the envelope template
recipients list or Sender 1's fields are added to another document in the
template.

Use this procedure to delete a document from your envelope template:

1. Select from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5OTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.pCtIYsgyNs6yeLC8eGtsUCvUN2962t6ZFMgWmm4gBzk&_LANG=enus) dropdown menu next to a document.
2. Select Delete from the options.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplatedocumentoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5OTAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.pCtIYsgyNs6yeLC8eGtsUCvUN2962t6ZFMgWmm4gBzk&_LANG=enus)

   The document disappears from the page.
3. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have deleted the uploaded
   document.

title: Access the Recipients Step of an Envelope Template
heading_path: Access the Recipients Step of an Envelope Template
text:
Find out how to access the Recipients step in an existing envelope template. Here, you
can add, edit and delete recipients. You can also change the email subject and message, view
a signing order diagram and more.

You can edit existing envelope templates on the Recipients step.
Here, you can add, edit and delete recipients. You can also change the email subject
and message, view a signing order diagram and more. Follow this procedure to access
the envelope template Recipients step:

1. Access the envelope template Recipientspage as a Salesforce administrator:

   The envelope template creation page opens to the Recipients
   step.
2. Select the App Launcher grid ![A screenshot of the Salesforce App Launcher grid, containing nine colored dots.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.sbdeJdnZjKvs0PWYTv2NnGgV3NGaL22XoR6o5FMpuxM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Salesforce App Launcher grid, containing nine colored dots.") and chooseView All.
3. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
4. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
5. Select the arrow ![A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMDAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.sbdeJdnZjKvs0PWYTv2NnGgV3NGaL22XoR6o5FMpuxM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.") next to a template and choose Edit.
6. Select the Recipients arrow. The arrow has no text until
   selected.

You have completed the process. You have accessed the
Recipients step of an existing envelope template.

title: When I Save Changes to an Envelope Template, Multiple Headers Display on My Browser with the Message: “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”
heading_path: When I Save Changes to an Envelope Template, Multiple Headers Display on My Browser with the Message: “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”
text:
Learn to fix the issue of multiple browser headers displaying the error: “You Have
Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”.

When I save changes to an envelope template, my browser opens multiple headers, each
displaying the message: You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode
Enabled.

![A screenshot displays three browser bars with the message “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-three-browser-bars-with-the-message_-You-Have-Lightning-Components-Debug-Mode-Enabled.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3NTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.932XPibl6CuKQq2PGyjwvLDKIa23f3oUjyhwOXTC4mQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays three browser bars with the message “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”.")

title: When I Save Changes to an Envelope Template, Multiple Headers Display on My Browser with the Message: “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”
heading_path: When I Save Changes to an Envelope Template, Multiple Headers Display on My Browser with the Message: “You Have Lightning Components Debug Mode Enabled”
Resolution: Disable Lightning Debug Mode in your Salesforce user settings
text:
Salesforce administrator

1. Log in to your Salesforce organization as an administrator.
2. Select Setup.
3. Enter and select Debug Mode in the Quick Find
   field.
4. Select the check box next to your user and choose
   Disable.

   You have completed the process. You have disabled Lightning Debug Mode for your
   user. The error messages no longer display when you save changes to Docusign envelope templates.

title: Change the Recipient Signing Order in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Change the Recipient Signing Order in an Envelope Template
text:
You can change the signing order of recipients in an envelope template. This lets you
change the order in which the envelope must be signed for the signature process to complete.

You can change the signing order of recipients in an envelope template. This lets you change the order in which the envelope must be signed for the signature
process to complete. You can drag recipients to a new order, or change their recipient numbers (1, 2, 3,
etc.) Envelope templates can also be configured to have recipients sign at the same time, or in no
particular order.

Use this procedure to change the recipient signing order in an existing envelope template:

Note: You can also use this procedure on the Recipients step when sending an
envelope.

1. Select the Signing Order button.
2. Pick an option:
   - **Drag recipients**: Select a recipient and drag it up or down in the list.
     ![A screenshot displays the envelope template recipients list with drag handles, showing how to change the signing order by dragging recipients up or down.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/envelopetemplatechangesigningorder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMzAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.R9BhEdi8z3d7CR0WZRTzoPZrbO2oSft_PH7mZBnVH0c&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the envelope template recipients list with drag handles, showing how to change the signing order by dragging recipients up or down.")
   - **Change recipient number**: Enter a new number in the field to the left of the recipient.
     ![A screen capture displays the Signing Order options dialog with settings for recipient signing order, including options to sign in order, in parallel, or with no specific order.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Signing-Order-options.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwMzAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.R9BhEdi8z3d7CR0WZRTzoPZrbO2oSft_PH7mZBnVH0c&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Signing Order options dialog with settings for recipient signing order, including options to sign in order, in parallel, or with no specific order.")
3. Select the Save and Close button at the top-right of the
   page.

You have completed the process. You have changed the recipient order.

title: Create Docusign for Salesforce Users
heading_path: Create Docusign for Salesforce Users
text:
Learn to create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users on the user
management page.

Use this procedure to create Docusign
eSignature, CLM, Gen, or Negotiate users on the user management page. You can only add
permissions for Docusign products you have purchased.

Important: A new user displays a Pending
status [until activated by the user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus). After activation, user status displays as Active.

1. Select Add Users.
2. Complete the following tasks in the Select Users dialog box:
   1. Value: Enter a Salesforce user name or email address.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce user that you want to add.
   3. (Optional) Filtered by Filter your search by
      User, Profile, or
      Permission Set.
   4. Select Continue.![A screenshot displays the Select Users dialog box with filters added for User and Profile and a user check box selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Use-filters-to-add-a-user.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3MzEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ZeHVq1kkPIubbJpKH0AReewImBsBdg-1oZhmQyAoJL4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Select Users dialog box with filters added for User and Profile and a user check box selected.")
3. Apply Permissions: Select permissions for your users:

   - eSignature:
     - Administrator: Administrators can create templates and
       manage all Docusign users in Salesforce.
     - User: Users can send documents for signature.
   - CLM:
     - Administrator: Administrators can access everything in
       CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. Administrators cannot manage Docusign users in Salesforce.
     - User: Users can access workflows and view, edit, and
       upload documents.
     - Viewer: Viewers can only preview and upload
       documents.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the CLM app.
   - Gen:
     - Generator: Generators can create new documents and
       templates.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the Gen app.
   - Negotiate:
     - Negotiator: Negotiators can collaborate with signers and
       viewers.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the Negotiate
       app.
   - (Optional) Update permissions for any existing users: Select
     to update a user's permissions in the connected Docusign
     account.
4. Select Apply.

   The Summary dialog box opens, displaying the message:
   (User name or number of users) was added.

You have completed the process. You have granted or updated Docusign permissions to Salesforce users.

title: Envelope Template Options: Place Fields Step
heading_path: Envelope Template Options: Place Fields Step
text:
This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the Place Fields step of the
envelope creation process.

As an envelope creator, you have many advanced options available to you. In the
Place Fields step, you add Docusign and Salesforce fields
to your envelope. Recipients use these fields to add data or signatures to your
agreement.

This guide demonstrates the advanced options that you can use in the Place
Fields step:

title: Envelope Template Options: Place Fields Step
heading_path: Envelope Template Options: Place Fields Step

nd Salesforce fields
to your envelope. Recipients use these fields to add data or signatures to your
agreement.
