title: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce org to your Docusign Demo
account.

title: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
- [Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")
- [Create a Docusign Developer account](https://developers.docusign.com/)

To use Docusign applications, you must connect a Docusign account to your Salesforce organization. You create this
connection by entering Salesforce and Docusign administrator
credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The Docusign administrator used to connect accounts is called the
integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your Salesforce organization to a Docusign
demo account:

1. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that displays.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page displays.
2. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login
      dialog box displays.
   2. Select Advanced Options and choose
      Log in to Demo Account.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Docusign-login-page-with-Advanced-Options-and-Log-in-to-Demo-Account-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2MzAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.k-HzZwXLXT0-pvn8utpH9jgTvjblytjoB-WnLlQjCww&_LANG=enus)
   3. Enter Docusign
      *demo* administrator credentials in the dialog box that displays
      and select Log in.
   4. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box displays.
3. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page displays.

You have completed the process. You have connected
your Salesforce organization to your Docusign demo account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts
heading_path: Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts
text:
Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce
layouts.

Before you can uninstall Apps Launcher, you must remove all Docusign
components that are present on your layouts.

Use this procedure to remove Docusign components from your layouts:

1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.cFjEDMiOVOZaQg3UCBdsFSfphHMDkcIuJHd_UX-Ccco&_LANG=enus)and choose Edit Page from the dropdown
   menu.

   The Lightning App Builder page opens.
2. Remove all Docusign components from the layout:
   1. Select a Docusign component and select the trash icon
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-123257-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.cFjEDMiOVOZaQg3UCBdsFSfphHMDkcIuJHd_UX-Ccco&_LANG=enus) that displays. ![A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted on the envelope status component.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-trash-icon-highlighted-for-the-Docusign-Files-component.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.cFjEDMiOVOZaQg3UCBdsFSfphHMDkcIuJHd_UX-Ccco&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted on the envelope status component.")
   2. Select Save.
   3. Repeat this procedure for all Docusign components on
      this layout.

   Salesforce removes the components from the layout.
3. Select the back arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-13251-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4MzIsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.cFjEDMiOVOZaQg3UCBdsFSfphHMDkcIuJHd_UX-Ccco&_LANG=enus) when you have removed all components.
4. Repeat this procedure for each Salesforce layout that contains Docusign Apps Launcher components.

You have completed step two of the process. You have
removed all Docusign Apps Launcher components from your Salesforce
layouts. Move to [Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=cif1623740064281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.").

title: Envelope Template Recipient Actions
heading_path: Envelope Template Recipient Actions
text:
Find out about the actions you can assign to the recipients of an envelope built with a
Docusign envelope template.

There are many different recipient actions that can be assigned to recipients in an envelope
or envelope template.

You can use the following Action types:

Table 1. Recipient Actions

| Action Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Needs to Sign | The recipient must sign the envelope. |
| Receives a Copy | The recipient receives a copy of the envelope. They do not have to take action. |
| Needs to View | The recipient must open and view the document. They do not have to sign. |
| In Person Signer | Conduct a multi-recipient signing session. The host signs, and then each additional recipient signs on the host's device.  All signers must be in the same room for the signing session. |
| Specify Recipients | The recipient can fill in the name and email address of other recipients in their stage of the signing order or later. |
| Allow to Edit | Grant another Docusign user full edit rights for an envelope. This recipient can edit the entire envelope, using the correct mode to make changes. The recipient is not assigned any fields. |
| Update Recipients | The recipient can fill in the name and email address of other recipients in their stage of the signing order or later. |
| Embedded Signer | The recipient can edit the details of any remaining recipients in the envelope. |

title: Disable Docusign CLM Federated Security
heading_path: Disable Docusign CLM Federated Security
text:
Learn how to disable the Federated Security feature in your Salesforce
organization.

Federated Security can be disabled at any time. Here's how to disable Federated Security in
your Docusign CLM account:

1. Log in to your Docusign CLM account as an administrator.
2. Select the Admin tab.
3. Navigate to Integrations  >  Connected Apps.
4. Uncheck the Enable federated security box under the
   Security header.

   ![Disable Federated Security.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Disable-Federated-Security.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5MDQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.FjKEKUu3v-Ed4UInN2gcYqOaBkODv8gcdUmvNUdnwpo&_LANG=enus "Disable Federated Security.")

   A modal opens, asking: Are you sure you want to disable federated
   security?
5. Select Disable federated security.
6. Select the Save button on the bottom of the page.

The process is complete. Salesforce Federated Security is disabled
on your CLM account. Salesforce and CLM security settings are separate, and
administrators must maintain security models for each.

title: Disconnect Docusign and Salesforce and Remove Users with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Disconnect Docusign and Salesforce and Remove Users with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to disconnect a Docusign account and remove users with the
Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

If you want to connect your Docusign account to a different
Salesforce organization, use Disconnect Account and Remove
Users. Docusign removes your connection to
Salesforce and deletes all Docusign for Salesforce users.

Important:
Disconnect Account and Remove
Users does not remove users from your connected Docusign account. This feature performs the following actions:

- Disconnects your Docusign account from your Salesforce
  organization
- Removes Docusign access from all Salesforce users
- Resets custom user settings
- Removes all permission set assignments
- Resets the integration user token stored in the connected CLM account

Follow this procedure to use the Disconnect Account and Remove
Users feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3OTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.r7BS9HlBGi4s65gNlks-y0idSZLzZwne-frr6SG7YWU&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3OTEsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.r7BS9HlBGi4s65gNlks-y0idSZLzZwne-frr6SG7YWU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Disconnect Account and Remove Users: Select
   Execute.

   A message opens: Successfully logged out from Docusign account. User custom
   settings and Docusign Apps Launcher Permission set assignments should be
   cleared shortly. The Troubleshooting page displays the message:
   No Docusign Account Connected.

You have completed the process. You have disconnected your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization. You can [connect Salesforce to a different Docusign
account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Add Automatic AutoPlace Fields to Documents
heading_path: Add Automatic AutoPlace Fields to Documents
text:
Learn how to add AutoPlace fields to documents used in envelope templates.

You can add automatic AutoPlace fields to envelope template documents.

These fields merge Docusign fields into your envelope.

You do not need to create, configure, or modify any Docusign fields to use automatic
AutoPlace fields.

Use automatic AutoPlace fields for these signer fields:

- Signature
- Date Signed
- Initial
- Optional Initial

Note: Use [document custom AutoPlace
fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ryo1673648971228.html&_LANG=enus) or merge field AutoPlace fields for Name,
Company, Title, and other
text-based fields.

Follow this procedure to add AutoPlace fields to an envelope template document:

1. Select the name of the document you will edit.

   The document preview page opens.
2. Select the Download button in the upper-right.

   The document is saved to the downloads folder of your
   device.
3. Add AutoPlace fields to your template document:
   1. Open the document and move the cursor to where you will add AutoPlace
      fields.
   2. Enter the text for AutoPlace fields (\s1\ for
      Signer 1 signature, \n1\ for Signer 1 name, and
      so on.)![A screenshot displays a document with sample AutoPlace field codes such as \s1\ for Signer 1 signature and \n1\ for Signer 1 name entered in the text.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/AutoPlace-field-examples.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.n1xm6pQfoZAUXAiO_3zThp-2NhaIrtNljuE-fGp3n54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a document with sample AutoPlace field codes such as \s1\ for Signer 1 signature and \n1\ for Signer 1 name entered in the text.")
   3. Enter the text for AutoPlace fields (\s1\ for
      Signer 1 signature, \n1\ for Signer 1 name, and
      so on.)
   4. Highlight the AutoPlace text and change the font color to match the
      page background. So, if the page is white, change the AutoPlace font to
      white.

      Note: This prevents the AutoPlace text from being
      visible in your envelope. Your recipients only see the signature
      fields.

      ![A screenshot displays a document where AutoPlace field codes such as \s1\ and \n1\ are entered in the text and formatted in white font so they blend into the page background.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/AutoPlace-fields-with-white-font.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.n1xm6pQfoZAUXAiO_3zThp-2NhaIrtNljuE-fGp3n54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a document where AutoPlace field codes such as \s1\ and \n1\ are entered in the text and formatted in white font so they blend into the page background.")
   5. Save the changes to your template document.
4. Replace your old template document with the new version:
   1. Return to the envelope template Documents
      page.
   2. Select the arrow dropdown menu next next to the document
      you updated.
   3. Select Delete. The document disappears.

title: Add Automatic AutoPlace Fields to Documents
heading_path: Add Automatic AutoPlace Fields to Documents
text:
new version:
   1. Return to the envelope template Documents
      page.
   2. Select the arrow dropdown menu next next to the document
      you updated.
   3. Select Delete. The document disappears.

      ![A screenshot displays the envelope template Documents page with a document’s options menu open and the Delete action selected to remove the template document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Delete-template-document.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxNTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.n1xm6pQfoZAUXAiO_3zThp-2NhaIrtNljuE-fGp3n54&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the envelope template Documents page with a document’s options menu open and the Delete action selected to remove the template document.")
   4. Select the Upload button.
   5. Select the name of the updated file from your device.
   6. Select Open. The Upload File dialog box
      opens.
   7. Select the Done button.

   The dialog box disappears. The name of your updated document
   appears.
5. Select the Save and Close button in the
   upper-right.

   The envelope template page opens.

You have completed the process. You have added automatic AutoPlace
fields to your envelope template document. When you send an envelope from this template,
Docusign or Salesforce fields are automatically placed where you added AutoPlace fields.
The AutoPlace text is not visible to your recipients, because you colored it to match
the page. Your recipients only see the fields they need to complete.

title: Access the Place Fields Step of an Envelope Template
heading_path: Access the Place Fields Step of an Envelope Template
text:
Find out how to access the Place Fields step in an existing envelope template. This
includes: place fields actions (copy, paste, undo, redo, and zoom), customizing eSignature
field options, creating a new custom field, using the Actions button, and using recipient
preview actions, and more.

You can edit existing envelope templates on the Place Fields step.
This includes: place fields actions (copy, paste, undo, redo, and zoom), customizing
eSignature field options, creating a new custom field, and more.

Follow this
procedure to access the envelope template Place Fields
step:

Access the envelope template Place Fields page as a
Salesforce administrator:

1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxMTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Qyw042-QelaKgitGlcUOvlXpkqBvl40Nr_s2VAAWyEA&_LANG=enus) and
   select View All.
2. Select the Docusign Apps Launcher tile.
3. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates
   tab.
4. Select the arrow ![A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcxMTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.Qyw042-QelaKgitGlcUOvlXpkqBvl40Nr_s2VAAWyEA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a downward-facing triangle.") next to the template to be edited.
5. Select Edit from the dropdown menu.

   The envelope template creation page opens.
6. Select the Place Fields arrow. The arrow has no
   text until selected.

You have completed the process. You have accessed the
Place Fields step of an existing envelope template.

title: Step 1: Select Templates and Choose a Name and Salesforce Object
heading_path: Step 1: Select Templates and Choose a Name and Salesforce Object
text:
Start by naming your template, choosing a Salesforce object, and selecting the existing
templates you will use.

You start by naming your template, choosing a Salesforce object, and selecting the existing
templates you will use. You can choose as many templates as you want.

1. Select Templates >  Create Template and choose Link Template from the arrow
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5MjUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.aoTP70-FZOVNBf0UNnLXni38eLKb7Wnt9QBPCAO8dEI&_LANG=enus) menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the Envelope Template page in Salesforce with the Create Template dropdown menu open, showing the Link Template option selected from the arrow menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Envelope-template-Create-Template-dropdown-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY5MjUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.aoTP70-FZOVNBf0UNnLXni38eLKb7Wnt9QBPCAO8dEI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Envelope Template page in Salesforce with the Create Template dropdown menu open, showing the Link Template option selected from the arrow menu.")

   The Link Docusign Template dialog box
   opens.
2. Complete the actions in the Link Docusign Template dialog
   box:
   1. Select the check box next to each template that you want to add, or use the search field
      to find a template.
   2. Select the Select button.

      The Link
      Docusign Template dialog box refreshes.
   3. Name: Enter a descriptive template name.
   4. Select Data Source: Select a Salesforce object from the dropdown
      menu.
   5. Select Next.

   The dialog box closes. The Documents page
   opens.

title: Step 1: Select Templates and Choose a Name and Salesforce Object
heading_path: Step 1: Select Templates and Choose a Name and Salesforce Object
Next steps
text: You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: (Optional) Add Documents to Your Linked Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=tbs1660542119912.html&_LANG=enus "In the second step of creating a linked envelope template, you add documents to use as content for the template. Word and PDF documents are most commonly used.").

title: Step 7: Create a Temporary Envelope Template Custom Button on the Account Layout
heading_path: Step 7: Create a Temporary Envelope Template Custom Button on the Account Layout
text:
The seventh step of the process involves temporarily adding a custom button to the
Salesforce Account layout.

Use this procedure to temporarily add a custom button to the Salesforce
Account layout.

Tip:
[Here is a video that walks you through the process](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6172186652001).

Note:

- You cannot add a custom button to the Person Account layout on this
  step
- You must add a custom button to at least one Account layout to save
  and finish your envelope template
- You must manually remove the button from your Account layout
- You will then add it to the Person Account layout

1. Enter a descriptive name for the custom button in the Button
   Label text field.
2. Check the box for one Account layout.

   Note: Only select one check box so that you only have to remove the button from
   one layout.
3. Select Save and Finish.

   A message displays: Successfully modified
   layouts.

You have completed step seven. Move to [Step 8: Remove the Person Account Custom Button from the Accounts Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jte1636651796525.html&_LANG=enus "The eighth step of the process involves removing the custom button from the Salesforce Account layout.").

title: Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”
text:
A workaround is needed when adding a Salesforce picklist merge field to an envelope
template.

A workaround is needed when adding a Salesforce picklist merge field to an envelope
template.

When you try to add that field to a document on the Place Fields page, the action fails. You
see an error message that reads: One or more of the dropdown fields
doesn't have any options.

This is because the Place Fields page is a Docusign account page, and can not access fields
from your Salesforce organization.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a Docusign envelope template as a Salesforce administrator.
2. Select the Place Fields arrow. The Place Fields page opens.
3. Select the Merge Fields menu in the left column.
4. Drag a Salesforce picklist field to the document.

**Result**: You see an error message that reads: One or more of the
dropdown fields doesn't have any options.

title: Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”
heading_path: Envelope Template Error: “One or More of the Dropdown Fields Doesn’t Have Any Options.”
Resolution: Select the Back button and then the Save and Close button
text:
1. Select the Back button at the lower-right of the
   page. The Template Name and Description page
   opens.
2. Select the Save and Close button at the
   lower-right of the page.

   The picklist field is correctly added to the envelope template.
   This issue will be fixed in a future release.

title: Step 3: Add a Name, Data Source, and Experience Cloud Option
heading_path: Step 3: Add a Name, Data Source, and Experience Cloud Option
text:
Use this procedure to add a name, data source, and enable the Experience Cloud
option.

Use this procedure to add a name, data source, and enable
the Experience Cloud option.

1. Access the Docusign eSignature Configuration page as a
   Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.uSVbb78jT764n0zKNP4nVEK0QC9LhFU5SSGZiqj_-yE&_LANG=enus) and
      select View All.
   2. Select the Docusign Apps Launcher tile.
   3. Select eSignature in the left column.
2. Select Create Template at the right of the page.

   The Create Template dialog box displays.
3. Complete all steps in the Create Template dialog box:
   1. Name: Enter a descriptive template name.
   2. Select Data Source: Select a Salesforce object from the
      dropdown menu.
   3. Select the Enable Experience Cloud Template check
      box.
   4. Select the Next button.

   ![A screenshot displays the Create Template dialog box with the Enable Community Cloud Template box checked and highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Community-Cloud-envelope-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4NTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.uSVbb78jT764n0zKNP4nVEK0QC9LhFU5SSGZiqj_-yE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Create Template dialog box with the Enable Community Cloud Template box checked and highlighted.")

   Note: The check box is named
   Enable Community Cloud Template (Embedded Signing) in
   older versions of the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
   app.

title: Step 3: Add a Name, Data Source, and Experience Cloud Option
heading_path: Step 3: Add a Name, Data Source, and Experience Cloud Option
Next steps
text: You have completed step three. Move to [Step 4: Add Documents to Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hya1596146614785_1.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add documents to your envelope template.").

title: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
heading_path: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
text:
Learn to fix the issue of a spinning wheel icon displaying when you try to open Docusign applications.

A spinning wheel icon displays when I try to open Docusign applications. Docusign apps and features never load.

title: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
heading_path: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
Resolution: Upgrade your version of Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: You have Docusign Apps Launcher
version 5.4 or lower installed in your organization.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3NjAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.AFfMjPsx_HW31eqokPk2XJLj80G07a7MmCTbq2Q2V1E&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. If your version number is 5.4 or lower, [upgrade Apps Launcher in your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.").
3. Select Save.

   You have completed the process. You have upgraded the Apps Launcher package in your Salesforce organization. Docusign
   apps and services load normally.

title: Recipient Preview Actions: Viewing As and Form Factor View
heading_path: Recipient Preview Actions: Viewing As and Form Factor View
text:
You can use the Actions button on the Place Fields page to access a menu of Docusign and
Salesforce options.

You can use the Actions button on the Place Fields page to access a menu
of Docusign and Salesforce options.

Table 1. Recipient Preview Actions

| Button | Name | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Viewing As | Select from dropdown menu to preview as Owner or Recipient |
|  | Desktop View | Preview document as it would appear on a computer |
|  | Tablet View | Preview document as it would appear on a tablet |
|  | Mobile View | Preview document as it would appear on a mobile device |
|  | Close | Close preview screen and return to Place Fields page |

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
text: Learn to use merge field syntax to add By Role recipients to an envelope template.

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Using By Role recipients
text:
Using the By Role recipient type, you can dynamically add recipients to
a Docusign envelope template. By Role allows you to add a placeholder recipient like
Signer 1. The sender can complete the recipient's details
when creating an envelope.

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Using merge field syntax for By Role recipients
text:
You can use merge field syntax to add By Role recipients and recipient details.

Example: You want a template to add a
recipient using any record's Contact field. You add a By Role
recipient with {!Contact.Name} and
{!Contact.Email} as the recipient details. A sender selects
the envelope template button, and Docusign adds the record's contact as a recipient.
![A screenshot displays a By Role recipient with Salesforce contact merge field syntax in the Name and Email Address fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210706-at-20211-PM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwNjQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.acI3u4gEXgmLibVx9F3eW4t_u29kjRXZNuvTsyIrZTA&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Common objects used with merge field syntax
text:
Administrators commonly use the following objects when creating merge field syntax:

- Users
- Contacts
- Leads
- Person Account
- Account owner when sending from Opportunities

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Using Lookup Fields
text:
You can use the Lookup Fields option to add recipients from
Users, Contacts, and Leads without using merge field syntax. You must use merge
field syntax when adding recipients from the same object as the custom button.

Example: The envelope template button
is on a Contact layout and is importing the name and email from a
contact.

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Finding merge field syntax
text:
You can copy Salesforce merge field syntax from the following locations:

- Object syntax: Setup >  Object Manager > API NAME
- Field syntax: Setup >  Object Manager >  (select object) >  Fields & Relationships  > FIELD NAME

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Using custom merge field syntax

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
User, Contact, and Lead examples
text:
| Merge field syntax | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `{!$User.FirstName}` | Specify a Salesforce user's first name |
| `{!$User.LastName}` | Specify a Salesforce user's last name |
| `{!$User.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce user's email address |
| `{!Contact.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce contact name |
| `{!Contact.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce contact email address |
| `{!Lead.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce lead name |
| `{!Lead.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce lead email address |

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Lookup Relationship examples
text:
| Merge field syntax | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `{!Account.Owner.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce Account owner name |
| `{!Account.Owner.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce Account owner email address |
| `{!Opportunity.Owner.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce Opportunity owner name |
| `{!Opportunity.Owner.Email}` | Specify a Salesforce Opportunity owner email address |
| `{!Opportunity.LastModifiedBy.Name}` | Specify the name of the Salesforce user who last modified a record |
| `{!Opportunity.LastModifiedBy.Email}` | Specify the email address of the Salesforce user who last modified a record |
| `{!Opportunity.Account.Owner.Name}` | Specify the name of an Opportunity account owner |
| `{!Opportunity.Account.Owner.Email}` | Specify the email address of an Opportunity account owner |
| `{!Opportunity.OpportunityContactRoles.Role}` | Specify the role of an Opportunity contact |

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Salesforce Person Account examples
text:
You can add Salesforce Person Account merge fields if you have any of these
Salesforce editions: Professional, Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and
Developer.

| Merge field syntax | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `{!Account.Name}` | Specify a Salesforce person account name |
| `{!Account.PersonEmail}` | Specify a Salesforce person account email address |
| `{!Account.PersonBirthdate}` | Specify a Salesforce person account birthday |
| `{!Account.PersonTitle}` | Specify a Salesforce person account title |
| `{!Account.IsPersonAccount}` | Determine whether you are working with a Salesforce person account or a business account |

title: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Syntax to Add By Role Recipients to an Envelope Template
Access Code syntax
text:
You can add merge text to the recipient Access Code field to
create an access code using Salesforce data.

Example: You want to secure an envelope but
avoid the added risk of messaging an access code to a recipient. You add
`{!Contact.Last4SSN__c}` to the recipient's Access
Code field. When sending, Docusign creates an access code using the last
four digits of the recipient's Social Security number. You inform the recipient that the
access code is the last four digits of their Social Security number.

title: Recipient Sending Options: Access Code, In-Person Signing, Sign Now, SMS and More
heading_path: Recipient Sending Options: Access Code, In-Person Signing, Sign Now, SMS and More
text:
Learn how to add the role, access code, filter, private message, in person signing, SMS,
or sign now options when creating an envelope recipient.

You can add many options when creating an envelope or envelope template recipient.

Here are the option types and actions you can add to a recipient:

Table 1. Recipient Options

| Option Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add Role | Enter a placeholder title (like Approver) that allows the sender to specify a name and email address later. |
| Add Filter | Add recipients based on a set of conditions being met or not met. This option only appears when you select the Related Lists recipient type. |
| Make the Sender Host | The person who sends the envelope becomes the host of the in-person signing session. The host signs, and then each additional recipient signs on the host's device.  This option only appears when you select the In Person Signer action for a recipient. |
| Host Name | Enter the name of the Docusign user who will host the In Person Signer session. This field only appears when you uncheck the Make the Sender Host box. |
| Host Email | Enter the email address of the Docusign user who will host the In Person Signer session. This field only appears when you uncheck the Make the Sender Host box. |
| Initiate Signing After Sending (Sign Now) | The sender is asked to sign their own envelope immediately after sending it. This option only appears in the following cases:   - You select Sender as the recipient   type and Needs to Sign as the action - You select any other recipient type (Users/Contacts, By   Role, and so on) and In Person Signer   as the action |
| Delivery | Select this dropdown menu to show the Email and SMS/Email options. The Delivery field only appears for customers with Allow SMS Delivery and Signer can sign on a mobile device enabled.  Contact your Docusign account manager to enable these features. |
| Email | Send an envelope to a recipient by email. |
| Email + SMS | Send an envelope to up to four recipients by email and text message. You must enter a space between the country code and the phone number. |
| WhatsApp | Send an envelope to a recipient by email and WhatsApp message. |
| Add Access Authentication | Select this link to show the SMS/Phone and Access Code options. |
| SMS/Phone | Send an access code to a recipient's mobile device to add an extra layer of security to the signing process. |
| Access Code | Create a code that a recipient must enter to start the signing process. You must send the code to the recipient separately by email or another messaging platform. |
| Add Private Message | Send a message to just this recipient or group. |
| Add Name and Email | Enter the name and email address of a recipient. This option only appears when you select the By Role recipient type. |

title: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
heading_path: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
text:
Change Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins settings or
Login IP Ranges to fix issues with documents and data not being
written back to Salesforce.

If your Docusign templates have writeback enabled and the process is not working, there are
two possible causes:

- The Salesforce organization has Multi-Factor Authentication for API
  Logins enabled on the user's' profile. This is incompatible with
  Docusign’s server-to-server API updates.
- Login IP Ranges are specified on the user's profile. This
  prevents API logins for the user from Docusign servers.

title: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
heading_path: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
Resolution 1: Disable Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2OTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.H0MmXAq4nSZj6j8brGQByD0HXZyxsONBFM4eQyvgMYo&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of
      the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Edit button.
4. Uncheck the Multi-Factor Authentication for API
   Logins box.
5. Select the Save button.

   The process is complete. Salesforce records are now updated when
   changes are made to Salesforce merge fields on your
   envelope.

title: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
heading_path: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
Resolution 2: Disable Login IP Ranges on user's profile
text:
1. Find the Salesforce user Profile:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2OTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.H0MmXAq4nSZj6j8brGQByD0HXZyxsONBFM4eQyvgMYo&_LANG=enus) in the upper-right of the
      page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Profiles in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Profiles link in the left
      navigation bar.
2. Select the name of the Profile to be edited. (Example: Standard User)
   The Profile page opens.
3. Select the Login IP Ranges link at the top of
   the Profile page.
4. Select the Del link to the left of an IP
   range.
5. Select the OK button on the Are you
   sure you want to delete the IP Login Range dialog box.
6. Repeat the Del action for all remaining
   ranges.

   The process is complete. Salesforce records are now updated when
   changes are made to Salesforce merge fields on your
   envelope.

title: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
heading_path: Documents and Data are Not Written Back to Salesforce
Resolution 3: Add the relevant Docusign endpoint IP addresses to the allow-list
text:
1. [Add Trusted IP Ranges for Docusign Gen for
   Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=lip1592574174158.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus).
2. Add any other restricted IP addresses shown in the user’s login
   history.

   You have completed the process. You have now fixed the document writeback feature
   of your template.

title: Learn About Cloning an Envelope Template in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Cloning an Envelope Template in Salesforce
text:
Learn how to clone one or more envelope templates to use in another Salesforce
organization.

Important: To use the cloning feature, install Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce v4.5 or higher. You cannot clone
envelope templates larger than 4 MB.

You can clone Docusign envelope templates from a Salesforce organization.
Cloning helps administrators quickly build templates, without manually recreating them.
You can import cloned templates to the original Salesforce organization or another
organization.

To clone a template, download it from your organization.

When you download a single envelope template, Docusign saves it to your
device as a .JSON file.

When you download multiple envelope templates, Docusign saves them to
your device as a .ZIP file.

Downloaded templates contain all documents, recipients, standard Docusign fields, and Salesforce merge fields with writeback.

You then upload the .JSON or .ZIP file to the target organization. The cloned template is fully
editable, so you can make adjustments.

The following examples show how you can use the cloning feature:

title: Learn About Cloning an Envelope Template in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Cloning an Envelope Template in Salesforce
Cloning to the same organization
text:
You want to create a new envelope template that is similar to an existing template.
You download the template and upload it to the same organization. You rename the
cloned template, make adjustments, and add a new custom button to your layouts.

title: Learn About Cloning an Envelope Template in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn About Cloning an Envelope Template in Salesforce
Cloning to a different organization
text:
You want to use a sandbox envelope template in your production organization without
manually rebuilding it. You download the template from your sandbox and upload it to
production. The template built in the sandbox organization is ready to use in
production.

Important: Custom fields are tied to the user who
created them. If the creating user is not present in the new organization, imported
custom fields fail. Add the creating user to the new organization or manually
rebuild the custom fields.

title: Add Docusign Components to Salesforce Layouts
heading_path: Add Docusign Components to Salesforce Layouts
text:
You can add Docusign components to your Salesforce layouts. Components give your users a space to view and interact with Docusign content on Salesforce records.

You can add Docusign components to your Salesforce page layouts. Docusign components display as sections on your Salesforce records. In these sections, users can view and interact with Docusign content without logging on to their Docusign account.

You can add the following components to your Salesforce layouts:

[Docusign Agreement Requests](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk151c1542-bdf4-4c8e-ba20-37affb7adc7e.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View Docusign Agreement Desk requests on your records. You can use this component with Docusign Agreement Desk. Use Agreement Desk to view and act on your Docusign requests.

[Docusign CLM Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=dny1626816139155.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View and act on files and folders from your connected Docusign CLM account. You can use this component with the CLM for Salesforce app.

[Docusign CLM Obligations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=tvw1659667572631.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View and act on your CLM obligations from a Salesforce record. You can use this component with the CLM for Salesforce app.

[Docusign CLM Tasks](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=lbi1628268219401.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View and act on tasks from your connected Docusign CLM account. You can use this component with the CLM for Salesforce app.

[Docusign Completed Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pwq1747797355782&topicId=cmv1746652068556.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View Docusign agreements on your records. You can use this component with Docusign Agreement Manager. Use Agreement Manager to view and act on your Docusign agreements.

[Docusign Envelope Status](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=all1586134265721.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Monitor the status of Docusign envelopes sent from Salesforce records. You can use this component with these apps:

    - Gen for Salesforce
    - Negotiate for Salesforce
    - eSignature for Salesforce

[Docusign Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=isj1586134263707.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Add files to a record that can be sent for Docusign signature or review. You can use this component with the Negotiate for Salesforce app.

[Docusign IAM Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk590e18e7-9dbc-418f-9fb8-0169f182eb3a.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Start Docusign workflows from your records. You can use this component with Docusign Workflow Builder. Workflow Builder reads Salesforce data and writes changes back to records when workflows complete.

title: Edit an Envelope Template
heading_path: Edit an Envelope Template
text:
Learn to edit envelope templates.

Use this procedure to edit an envelope template:

1. Select a tab from which to edit details:

   - Documents
   - Recipients
   - Place Fields
   - Options
   - Custom Button
2. Select Save and Close when you have completed your
   changes.

You have completed the process. You have edited the
template.

title: Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce Record
text:
Learn to change the type of currency Salesforce displays on a record.

Your Salesforce organization has a default currency format. However, your administrator can
add new currencies to your organization. Adding currencies lets you change the
default currency on a record.

Example: Your Salesforce organization uses U.S.
Dollar as the default currency, but your administrator has added
Euro. You change the Opportunity
Currency field of a French company to Euro.
The Amount field displays the value in Euros.

Note: If you want to [choose the currency format when generating documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=hhz1686691127617.html&_LANG=enus), you must change a record's currency first.

Use this procedure to change the currency type of a Salesforce record:

1. Select the Details tab of a record.
2. Select the pencil icon ![A screenshot of the pencil icon used to edit fields on a Salesforce record.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-pencil-icon-used-to-edit-fields-on-a-Salesforce-record.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY4MDUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.bsKVIcptSYiwpE5WiUK42OXjFASKp1fDcxxQ13AEsfU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the pencil icon used to edit fields on a Salesforce record.") on the (object) Currency field.
3. Select a new currency type from the dropdown menu.
4. Select Save.

   The Amount field displays the new
   currency.

You have completed the process. You have changed the default
currency of a record.

title: Configure Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Using the Templates, Buttons, and Settings Tabs
heading_path: Configure Docusign eSignature for Salesforce Using the Templates, Buttons, and Settings Tabs
text:
Learn about configuring templates, custom buttons, and settings on the eSignature Configuration page.

The Docusign Apps Launcher package contains the Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app. You can use this app to create envelope templates, add buttons to layouts, and manage eSignature administrator settings.

You can access your eSignature for Salesforce controls by selecting eSignature on the Docusign  Setup page.![A screenshot displays the Templates, Button, and Settings tabs highlighted on the e-signature configuration page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Templates-Button-and-Settings-tabs-highlighted-on-the-esignature-configuration-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NjksInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.72QmyqUbequXKthcnfihippd4NOHOMGM96t0m4haJdU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Templates, Button, and Settings tabs highlighted on the e-signature configuration page.")

You can access the following settings on the eSignature page:

- [Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus): Build Docusign Envelope Templates to automate your eSignature process.
- [Button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zsw1586134270460.html&_LANG=enus): Add a Send with Docusign button to Salesforce layouts.
- [Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=kor1611642110382.html&_LANG=enus):
  - [Enable Built-in eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=kor1611642110382.html&_LANG=enus): Turn access to the eSignature for Salesforce app on or off. Turn off access if you want to use another app, like legacy Docusign eSignature for Salesforce.
  - [Enable System Sender](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=jxz1611642321527.html&_LANG=enus): Enable a user that can send envelopes on behalf of non-Docusign users.

title: Step 1: Add a Template Name and a Salesforce Data Source
heading_path: Step 1: Add a Template Name and a Salesforce Data Source
text:
Learn to select a name and Salesforce object for your template.

Tip:
[This video demonstrates the steps in this task](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6171917923001).

In this step, you name your template and select a Salesforce data source. The data source is
the Salesforce object on which you build your template. After you select your data
source, you can add fields from that object and related objects.

Use this procedure to name the template and select the Salesforce data source:

1. Select Create Template.

   The Create Template dialog box opens.
2. Choose a name and data source:
   1. Name: Enter a descriptive template name.
   2. Select Data Source: Select a Salesforce object from the dropdown menu.
   3. Select Next.

   The Documents page opens.

title: Step 1: Add a Template Name and a Salesforce Data Source
heading_path: Step 1: Add a Template Name and a Salesforce Data Source
Next steps
text: You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: Add Documents to Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=hya1596146614785.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add documents to your envelope template.")

title: Understanding Docusign Permission Sets in Salesforce
heading_path: Understanding Docusign Permission Sets in Salesforce
text:
Learn about the types and functions of Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets.

Permission sets are a collection of Salesforce settings and permissions that give users access to various tools and functions. Permission sets extend Salesforce users’ functional access without changing their profiles.

Docusign adds a group of permission sets when you install the Docusign Apps Launcher package. Docusign permission sets display which Salesforce users have Docusign app access.

Important:

- Administrators grant most Docusign app access with  [Docusign User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus). Administrators can only use the Docusign Login permission set to help grant access.
- Administrators should not manually add or remove users from Docusign permissions sets except Docusign Login.

The following is a list of the Docusign permission sets and their functions:

| Permission Set Name | Permission Set Function |
| --- | --- |
| Docusign Login | Allows users to authorize Docusign Apps Launcher and auto-assign permission sets corresponding to existing Docusign user permissions |
| Docusign Administrator | Allows administrative access to Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Admin | Allows administrative access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Experience Cloud User | Allows access to CLM in Communities (Experience Cloud) |
| Docusign CLM User | Allows subscriber access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Viewer | Allows guest access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign Document Generator | Allows users to generate documents with Docusign Gen |
| Docusign Document Negotiator | Allows users to send files for internal approval and external review with Docusign Negotiate |
| Docusign Sender | Allows users to send envelopes as the system sender in Docusign Apps Launcher﻿ |
| Docusign User | Allows access to eSignature in Docusign Apps Launcher |

Users Send and Sign Envelopes With the Apex Toolkit

title: An "INVALID_SESSION_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
heading_path: An "INVALID\_SESSION\_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
text:
I see a web service error: INVALID\_SESSION\_ID when listing layouts, publishing buttons,
or fetching documents from Salesforce when sending for signature.

I see a web service error: INVALID\_SESSION\_ID when I invoke a Salesforce API
command. The error displays when I execute the following commands:

- List layouts
- Publish buttons
- Fetch documents from Salesforce when sending for signature

title: An "INVALID_SESSION_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
heading_path: An "INVALID\_SESSION\_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
Resolution 1: Disable "Lock sessions to the IP address from which they originated"
text:
1. Access the Session Settings page as a Salesforce
   administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg3MzQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.p-qghixd9A2RgazKSO_tRoeyxxTENQj1BWvUBHh3-6w&_LANG=enus) in the upper right
      of the page.
   2. Select Setup.
   3. Enter Session Settings in the Quick Find
      field.
   4. Select the Session Settings link in the
      left navigation bar.
2. Uncheck the Lock sessions to the IP address from which they
   originated box under the Session
   Settings header.
3. Select the Save button.
4. Log out of Salesforce and log back in.
5. Retry your API transaction.

   You have completed the process. You have disabled the Lock sessions to the IP
   address from which they originated function in your
   Salesforce organization.

title: An "INVALID_SESSION_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
heading_path: An "INVALID\_SESSION\_ID" Error Displays When I Invoke Salesforce APIs
Resolution 2: Add Docusign IP ranges to your allowlist
text:
1. [Add Docusign IP
   ranges to your Salesforce allowlist](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=lip1592574174158.html).
2. Add any other restricted IP addresses shown in the user’s login
   history.

   You have completed the process.
   INVALID\_SESSION\_ID errors no longer
   display.

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
text:
Learn to work around the duplicate files issue when sending Gen agreements for
signature.

I built a Gen template that is connected to an envelope template. When I send generated
agreements for signature, I see duplicate documents in the
Documents step.

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
Resolution 1: Uncheck the Boxes of Duplicate Salesforce Files Before Sending
text:
Cause: You selected the Attach documents from
Salesforce record check box on the envelope template
Documents page. Selecting this option in an envelope
template that is linked to a Gen template causes file duplication.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select a Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   Gen creates a new agreement, and opens a preview page.
2. Select Send for Signature.

   Your linked envelope template displays the
   Documents page.
3. De-select the check box for any duplicate Salesforce documents that
   display.
4. Proceed with the rest of the envelope creation process.

   You have completed the process. You have removed any duplicate Salesforce
   documents and sent the envelope.

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
Resolution 2: Uncheck the "Attach Documents from Salesforce Record" Box Before Sending
text:
Cause: You selected the Attach documents from
Salesforce record check box on the envelope template
Documents page.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select a Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   Gen creates a new document, and opens a preview page.
2. Select Send for Signature.

   Your linked envelope template displays the
   Documents page.
3. De-select the Attach documents from Salesforce
   record check box.
4. Proceed with the rest of the envelope creation process.

   You have completed the process. You have removed any duplicate
   Salesforce documents and sent the envelope.

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
text:
Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature. Sync Domains
fixes issues with Enhanced Domains and any changes that you make to your Salesforce
domain.

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce
v5.2](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04t3u000002wLcp)

You can change your Salesforce domain name to update your brand, adopt [Enhanced Domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.domain_name_enhanced.htm&type=5), or enable partitioned domains. When
your My Domain name changes, your Salesforce-hosted URLs also change. You must
rebuild the connection to your Docusign account.

The Sync Domains feature of the Docusign
TroubleShooting tool rebuilds your connection to Docusign after a
domain change.

Use this procedure to rebuild your CLM or CLM
Essentials integration with the Sync Domains
feature:

1. Access the My Domain page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._0xkcq6aOowBr0kxks1vQG4Bee--JmKLUPqT44OuPnc&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select My Domain in the Quick Find
      field.
2. Enable redirects for your domain:

   Note: The Domain Name System (DNS) server takes up to 72 hours to update
   the connection between Docusign and Salesforce. Keep the
   redirect setting enabled during this time.

   1. Routing: Select Edit.
   2. Select the Redirect previous My Domain URLs to your current
      My Domain check box.
   3. Select Save. The My Domain page opens.
3. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._0xkcq6aOowBr0kxks1vQG4Bee--JmKLUPqT44OuPnc&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
4. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4ODgsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9._0xkcq6aOowBr0kxks1vQG4Bee--JmKLUPqT44OuPnc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
5. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
6. Sync Domains: Select Execute.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Successfully synced
   domains on Docusign account.
7. (Optional): After 72 hours, uncheck the Redirect previous My Domain
   URLs to your current My Domain box.

You have completed the process. You have rebuilt the CLM or CLM
Essentials integration with your Salesforce organization.

title: Add SMS Delivery to an Envelope Recipient
heading_path: Add SMS Delivery to an Envelope Recipient
text:
Learn how to add the Email + SMS option to a recipient in your envelope.

Use this procedure to add the Email + SMS option to a
recipient in your envelope.

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, etc.)
2. Select the envelope template custom button in the upper right of the
   page.

   The button is named by your administrator. It may be hidden in the arrow
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTcwNTcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.elejPZis91P7m_9xaqjle9UZqxfI-7HLLnXk__h_G-Y&_LANG=enus) menu.

   The Documents page opens, displaying the documents that have been added by
   your administrator.
3. Select Next.

   The Recipients page opens.
4. Select the name of a recipient.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
5. Delivery: Select the Email + SMS
   option from the dropdown menu.

   Note: Delivery only appears
   for Docusign customers with Allow SMS Delivery
   enabled in their account.
6. (Optional) Phone Number: Enter the recipient's
   phone number.

   Important: You must enter a space between the
   country code and the phone number.
7. Select Add.

   The Add Recipients dialog box closes.

You have completed the process. You have added theEmail
+ SMS option to a recipient in your envelope. When users send an
envelope from this template, the recipient receives a text message and an email. The
recipient can choose to open the envelope using either method. [Complete the sending process by adding Docusign and
Salesforce fields and sending your envelope.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xmx1596487118055.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and eSignature Tags are Missing
heading_path: Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and eSignature Tags are Missing
text:
When adding recipients to an Envelope Template in Docusign eSignature for Salesforce,
conflicts between Docusign Role and Salesforce Roles can cause problems.

When adding recipients to an Envelope Template in Docusign eSignature for Salesforce ,
conflicts between Docusign Role and Salesforce Roles can cause problems.

When a user prepares a document for signature using an Envelope Template that
contains recipients with both Salesforce Roles **and**
Docusign Roles, the
following can occur:

- Recipients' Docusign Roles are replaced with Salesforce Roles
- Docusign signature fields for those recipients are deleted from the Envelope
  Template

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Add a Docusign recipient to an Envelope Template.
2. Add a Salesforce recipient from a Related List (like
   OpportunityContactRole).
3. Save the Envelope Template.
4. Start a signature process with eSignature for Salesforce.
5. Advance to the Recipients step. Recipients have their Docusign Roles replaced with
   Salesforce Roles.

   Note: This only applies to recipients with Salesforce Roles
   (Decision Maker, Evaluator, etc.).
6. Advance to the Prepare and Send step. Docusign recipients' signature
   fields are missing.

   **Result**: Both the recipient's Docusign Role and
   eSignature tags are missing from the template.

title: Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and eSignature Tags are Missing
heading_path: Envelope Template Recipient’s Docusign Role and eSignature Tags are Missing
Resolution: Remove the Role field from the Salesforce Contact
text:
1. Visit the Salesforce record page containing the Contact Role.
2. Find the Contact Roles section.
3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NzMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.BFcV0BQ_EMlRv8mBJhhRljZ0XrsYKbeAPBJQOUeAGFU&_LANG=enus) to the left of the Contact to be
   edited.
4. Select Edit from the dropdown menu. The Edit
   Contact Role dialog box opens.

   ![Edit Salesforce Role](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Edit-Salesforce-Role.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NzMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.BFcV0BQ_EMlRv8mBJhhRljZ0XrsYKbeAPBJQOUeAGFU&_LANG=enus "Edit Salesforce Role")
5. Select the Role dropdown menu.
6. Select --None-- from the list.
7. Select the Save button.

   ![Clear Salesforce Role](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Clear-Salesforce-Role.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2NzMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.BFcV0BQ_EMlRv8mBJhhRljZ0XrsYKbeAPBJQOUeAGFU&_LANG=enus "Clear Salesforce Role")You are returned to the Contact Roles section. The
   Role field is now empty.

   The process is complete. The recipient's Salesforce Role no longer conflicts.
   Docusign Roles and signature fields remain as expected when using an
   Envelope Template to send for signature.

title: Refresh or Reconnect a Salesforce Organization With Docusign CLM Federated Security
heading_path: Refresh or Reconnect a Salesforce Organization With Docusign CLM Federated Security
text:
Learn about best practices for refreshing a Salesforce organization with Federated
Security enabled.

Salesforce organizations using CLM Federated Security should avoid taking these actions:

- Refreshing their organization
- Connecting their CLM account to a different Salesforce organization

If a Salesforce organization must be refreshed or re-connected, follow these steps
first (in order):

1. [Turn off Federated Security in your CLM
   account.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=vbn1599207323944.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disable the Federated Security feature in your Salesforce organization.")
2. Open the CLM **Other Sources** folder and rename the Salesforce folder:
   **Salesforce\_Old**.
3. Create a new **Salesforce** folder under Other Sources parent folder.

   ![Rename Salesforce folder.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Rename-Salesforce-folder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg4OTUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.0pSUkWUIgAP-CmoIfSyvFIz7JLaN5qB0ogcCPNqxymo&_LANG=enus "Rename Salesforce folder.")
4. [Perform a refresh of your Salesforce
   Sandbox](https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=data_sandbox_refresh.htm&type=5).
5. Add records to your Sandbox.
6. [Turn on CLM Federated Security](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gqm1599194045432.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable standard Federated Security in your Docusign CLM account.").
7. Test your updates to ensure that all is working properly.

The process is complete. The Sandbox is refreshed and
Federated Security is properly applied to the new content. All archived content is found
in the **Salesforece\_Old** folder.

text:
The fifth step of the Envelope Template creation process involves placing Docusign and

text. The Merge Fields that you created in step four are available on the Salesforce

  advanced options available on this step.

1. Add Salesforce fields to the document:

   1. Select the Merge Fields button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/isy1595858937605.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5MDAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.9qQRei1ikngvA2IzSCo4zZMnLBflKHdgAvkoGvAN9-g&_LANG=enus) from the left

2. Add Docusign fields to the document:

Select the Standard Fields tab ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/jcw1595860961076.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5MDAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.9qQRei1ikngvA2IzSCo4zZMnLBflKHdgAvkoGvAN9-g&_LANG=enus) in the left

      Initial, and so on.)
   3. Drag the field to the document.
3. (Optional):

   - Recipient Preview: Select this button to see what

     1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/vga1586134207550.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg5MDAsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.9qQRei1ikngvA2IzSCo4zZMnLBflKHdgAvkoGvAN9-g&_LANG=enus) next to the recipient
        name.
     2. Select another recipient from the dropdown menu.
     3. Drag Docusign fields for this recipient to the document
        text.

text:
The fifth step of the Envelope Template creation process involves placing Docusign and

Use this procedure to place Docusign and Salesforce fields on the document.
