title: Uninstall Docusign Apps Launcher from Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Uninstall Docusign Apps Launcher from Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps from
your Salesforce organization.

You may be required to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from
your Salesforce organization. The Apps Launcher package contains the CLM, Gen,
Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce applications.

You might uninstall the package for the following reasons:

- You want to revert to an earlier version of the Apps Launcher package.
- You installed the latest version for Admins Only, and must reinstall
  for All Users.
- You installed a Beta version of the package and must install the official release.

To uninstall the package, you must first remove all Apps Launcher dependencies from your
Salesforce organization. Dependencies are relationships between Docusign apps and Salesforce buttons, layouts, and objects. The
following examples demonstrate the dependencies between Salesforce and Docusign:

- You add the **Docusign CLM Files** component to the Account
  layout.
- You create a Gen template and add a Gen button to the Opportunities layout.
- You add Salesforce users to the Docusign Generator
  permission set.

Salesforce prompts you to remove these dependencies when you uninstall Apps
Launcher.

Use these procedures to remove all dependencies and uninstall Docusign Apps
Launcher from your organization:

1. [Remove users from Docusign
   Apps Launcher permission sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=pnw1623705527946.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all users from Docusign permission sets.")
2. [Remove Docusign Apps
   Launcher components from Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uxu1623708462949.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce layouts.")
3. [Remove Docusign Apps
   Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=cif1623740064281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.")
4. [Uninstall the Docusign Apps
   Launcher package from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ump1623741000284.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your Salesforce organization.")

title: Delete an Envelope Template
heading_path: Delete an Envelope Template
text:
Learn to delete envelope templates.

Use this procedure to delete an envelope template:

1. Access an envelope template as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2MzcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.AaO4-jAOPmciyep8otyXDxvN44UUnjbZ540W4vmPUgE&_LANG=enus) and choose
      View All.
   2. Select the Docusign Apps Launcher tile.
   3. Select the Docusign envelope templates tab.
   4. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2MzcsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.AaO4-jAOPmciyep8otyXDxvN44UUnjbZ540W4vmPUgE&_LANG=enus) next to the Gen template to be edited.
   5. Select Delete from the options.

      A message displays: Are you sure you want to delete this
      envelope template?
2. Select Delete.

   A message displays: The envelope template has been
   deleted.

You have completed the process. You have deleted the
template.

title: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User
heading_path: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher
apps.

You can change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher apps by changing the
language settings for your Salesforce user.

1. Access the Salesforce Language & Time Zone settings:
   1. Select the Salesforce user icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/salesforceusericon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3OTYsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.XgniVOSdkUVhFdwsfpIkFzhtF0IpClNXt3OQSJwxL8k&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Settings.
   2. Select Language & Time Zone.
2. Language: Select a language from the dropdown menu.
3. Select Save.

   A message displays: Your settings have been successfully
   saved.
4. Empty your browser cache and restart the browser.

You have completed the process. Docusign Apps Launcher content displays in the language that you
chose.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
text:
Learn how to grant Docusign app access to your Salesforce
users.

There are several ways that you can grant Docusign app access to your
Salesforce users:

[Docusign user management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create Docusign users with the Docusign Apps Launcher
    user management page.

[Docusign Login permission set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xxm1623778416524.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create Docusign users by adding Salesforce users to the Docusign
     Login permission set.

Single sign-on (SSO)
:   Create Docusign users with the Docusign Login
    permission set and your SSO identity provider.

When you grant Docusign access to a Salesforce user, Docusign searches for a matching Docusign name
and email address. If matching credentials exist, Docusign links
the Salesforce and Docusign users. If matching credentials do not
exist, Docusign creates a new user.

Important: Never change the name or email address of a
Salesforce or Docusign user after activation. If Docusign and Salesforce credentials don't match, the user may be
unable to send envelopes and access Docusign objects.

You can use the following methods to grant Docusign access to Salesforce
users:

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates from an email link
text:
A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user receives an email from Docusign, selects the activation
link, and creates a password.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their Docusign and Salesforce credentials.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates with SSO credentials
text:
A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their SSO
credentials.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
An existing Docusign user activates with standard or SSO credentials
text:
You can grant app access to an existing Docusign user on the user management
page. Existing Docusign users can activate their Docusign app access from an email link or with SSO credentials.

Important: Make sure the Salesforce user's name and email
address match those of the existing Docusign user. If the
Salesforce user's credentials differ from the existing Docusign
user's, Docusign creates a new user.

A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their credentials:

- Standard authentication: The user enters Salesforce and Docusign credentials
- SSO authentication: The user enters SSO credentials

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A user with Docusign Login permissions self-activates
text:
You can let existing Docusign users activate their Docusign app access by adding them to the Docusign Login permission
set. Existing users can self-activate using Docusign and
Salesforce credentials or with SSO credentials. This permission set lets you manage
user activation in Salesforce, not on the Docusign user management
page.

A Salesforce administrator adds a Salesforce user to the Docusign
Login permission set.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their credentials:

- Standard authentication: The user enters Salesforce and Docusign credentials
- SSO authentication: The user enters SSO credentials

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates with SSO just-in-time provisioning
text:
You can create new Docusign users by enabling Docusign SSO
and adding Salesforce users to the Docusign Login permission set.
Users can self-activate Docusign app access using SSO credentials.

Important:

- You must enable just-in-time user provisioning for Docusign in your SSO
  identity provider.
- You can only use just-in-time provisioning to add new eSignature for Salesforce users. You
  can add Gen, Negotiate, or CLM permissions after you create eSignature
  users.

A Salesforce administrator adds a Salesforce user to the Docusign
Login permission set.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their SSO
credentials.

title: Use Merge Field Relationships in an Envelope Template
heading_path: Use Merge Field Relationships in an Envelope Template
text:
Learn to use merge field relationships in an envelope template. Merge fields can access
up to four levels of relation to a source object.

You can create merge fields in an envelope template if they are related to the source
object.

Example:

An envelope template that uses Account as the source object sees
Opportunity fields, but not Case fields.

Merge fields can access up to four levels of relation to a source object. The following are
the possible relationship paths:

- Source object >  Field
- Source object >  Reference >  Field
- Source object >  Child >  Field
- Source object >  Reference >  Reference >  Field
- Source object >  Child >  Reference >  Field

Note: You cannot access related list fields. Use [Document Custom Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=crp1583277382187.html&_LANG=enus) to access related list fields.

title: Learn About Connecting Salesforce and Docusign
heading_path: Learn About Connecting Salesforce and Docusign
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign
account.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a unified package that includes these
applications:

- CLM for Salesforce
- CLM Essentials for Salesforce
- Gen for Salesforce
- eSignature for Salesforce
- eSignature for Salesforce Apex Toolkit

You must connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account to
use these apps. To create the connection, you must enter Docusign and
Salesforce administrator credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The
Docusign administrator credentials that you use to connect accounts is
called the integration user.

The integration user must have Salesforce System Administrator access.
The integration user's Salesforce profile must have the Enable MFA for API
logins value set to False.

You can also follow these instructions to [make a
standard Salesforce user your integration user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qig1698704091391.html&_LANG=enus).

Note:

- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.
- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration user. For
  more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").

Example: An administrator at Fontara creates a generic Docusign administrator named
docusign-admin@fontara.com. No person at Fontara uses these
credentials to access their Docusign account. The Fontara administrator
connects Docusign and Salesforce by entering the
`docusign-admin@fontara.com` credentials on the Docusign
Setup page. Since no person uses these credentials, the integration is
protected from administrators leaving or changing roles.

Use a procedure to connect your Docusign account to your Salesforce
organization:

- [Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qnq1614667628308.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign production account.")
- [Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uxq1614636863027.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce org to your Docusign Demo account.")
- [Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bye1699036089712.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.")

title: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
heading_path: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
text:
Learn how to find the Salesforce object from which users sent a Docusign envelope.

The Docusign Status page displays the status of Docusign envelopes sent from Salesforce records. You can find the page by opening
Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce and selecting the
Docusign Status tab.

However, Docusign Status does not display the name of the Salesforce object
from which you sent the envelope. You can use Salesforce object key
prefixes to identify the Salesforce object type.

Object key prefixes are the first three characters in a Docusign envelope's
Salesforce Source ID. Each three-character prefix
represents a different Salesforce object.

title: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
heading_path: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
Example
text:
You want to know from which objects (Accounts, Opportunities, and so on) you sent an envelope.
You follow these steps:

1. Open Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce.
2. Select the Docusign Status tab.
3. Check the first three digits of the envelope IDs in the Salesforce Source
   ID column.![A screenshot of the Docusign Status page highlights the first three digits of envelopes' Salesforce Source IDs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Docusign-Status-page-highlights-the-first-three-digits-of-envelopes-Salesforce-Source-IDs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY3MTMsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.ExfnxoG-iAJ-IFfOs52rHPjfsSJ39zVX9qlHbM02utY&_LANG=enus)

title: After Upgrading From Gen to CLM, Administrators Need To Wait an Hour for CLM Permissions To Take Effect
heading_path: After Upgrading From Gen to CLM, Administrators Need To Wait an Hour for CLM Permissions To Take Effect
text:
A workaround is needed after upgrading form Gen to CLM. Administrators need to wait an
hour for their new CLM permissions. After an hour, they need to refresh the Docusign Setup
tab to see the CLM administrator page.

A workaround is needed when you upgrade your Docusign Gen account to Docusign CLM.
Administrators need wait an hour for their new CLM permissions to take effect. After
a full hour, they need to refresh the Docusign Setup tab to see the CLM
administrator page.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. You upgrade your Gen account to CLM.
2. Less than an hour later, as a new CLM administrator, you log in to
   Salesforce.
3. You select the Docusign Setup tab.

**Result**: You see the Gen-Negotiate administrator view, rather than the CLM
administrator view on the Docusign Apps Launcher Home page.

title: After Upgrading From Gen to CLM, Administrators Need To Wait an Hour for CLM Permissions To Take Effect
heading_path: After Upgrading From Gen to CLM, Administrators Need To Wait an Hour for CLM Permissions To Take Effect
Resolution: Wait a full hour and refresh the Docusign Setup page twice
text:
1. Wait a full hour after the Gen-to-CLM upgrade.
2. Log in to your Salesforce organization.
3. Select the Docusign Setup tab. The Gen-Negotiate
   administrator page appears.
4. Refresh the tab once. The Gen-Negotiate administrator page appears
   again.
5. Refresh the tab a second time. The CLM administrator page
   appears.

   You have CLM administrator permissions and can access the CLM
   administrator page in the Docusign Setup tab. You do not need to repeat
   this process again. This issue will be fixed in a future
   release.

title: Add a Standard Send with Docusign Button to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add a Standard Send with Docusign Button to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn how to add a standard Send with Docusign button to a Salesforce
layout.

A Docusign envelope is a digital agreement that you build that contains
documents, recipients and signature fields. Recipients complete the fields assigned to them
to complete the signature process.

You can create and send envelopes from Salesforce records. Docusign can
add data from record fields to the envelope. When recipients enter or change Salesforce data
in the envelope, Docusign can update the data on the record.

When the signature process is complete, Docusign saves the signed
agreement to the Salesforce record.

When you add a Send with Docusign button to a layout, users can
send envelopes from records. When senders select this button, Docusign
opens a pre-built sending experience. Senders can add documents, recipients, Salesforce
fields, and Docusign fields to the envelope before sending.

Use this procedure to add a Send with button to a
Salesforce layout from the Docusign Setup page:

1. Open Docusign Apps Launcher as an
   administrator.
2. Go to eSignature >  Button.
3. Choose an object from the Select a Salesforce object dropdown
   menu.
4. Select the check box next to one or more layouts and select Update
   Layouts.![A screenshot displays the eSignature Configuration page with the Button tab and the Opportunity layout highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-eSignature-Configuration-page-with-the-Button-tab-and-the-Opportunity-layout-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY2NzQsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.NQjtwI9IVdvPNeWbzPG8aW229cLdbYDRtiGSYdv5UNk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the eSignature Configuration page with the Button tab and the Opportunity layout highlighted.")

   A message displays: Successfully modified
   layouts.

You have completed the process. You have added a Docusign eSignature button to a Salesforce layout. Users can select the button
to create and send envelopes.

title: Add Docusign Server Template Options to Your Envelope Template
heading_path: Add Docusign Server Template Options to Your Envelope Template
text:
Learn how to add previously unavailable options to an envelope template by editing it in
your Docusign account.

Docusign server templates are signing experiences that you build in
your Docusign account. You log on to your Docusign account and select the Templates tab to build or access a
server template.

Docusign envelope templates are signing experiences that you build in your
Salesforce organization with the Docusign eSignature for
Salesforce application. You can open Docusign Apps Launcher in
your Salesforce organization to build an envelope template.

Docusign offers some options in server templates that are not yet
available in envelope templates. However, you can add many of these options to
envelope templates from the server template interface.

Use this procedure to add server template options to your envelope template:

1. Edit an envelope template in your Docusign account:

   1. Log in to your Docusign account as an
      administrator.
   2. Select the Templates tab. The My Templates page
      opens.
   3. Select the template name. The template page opens.
   4. Select Edit. The template layout opens for
      editing.
2. [Add supported server template
   options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nsh1649096506753.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how you can add options to an envelope template from a Docusign server template.").
3. Select SAVE AND CLOSE.

   A message displays: saving. The page
   refreshes.
4. Select Save and Close.

   The page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have added server template
options that are not available in the Docusign envelope template
interface. Envelopes sent from the server template and the envelope template each
contain all options that you added.

title: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
heading_path: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
text:
There is an intermittent issue with adding PDF files to the Documents step of the
envelope template creation process. This does not happen with every PDF file.

I sometimes see an error message when I try to upload a PDF file to my envelope template:
The Validation of the PDF File Failed.

This does not happen with every PDF file.

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Upload a PDF file to an envelope template at the Documents step. The document fails to
   load.
2. An error displays: The validation of the PDF file
   failed

   **Result**: The PDF is not added to the envelope
   template.

   Note: Envelope templates currently
   support PDF file sizes of 3MB or smaller. This limit will be increased in a
   future release.

title: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
heading_path: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
**Resolution 1: Upload the PDF file to the envelope template in your connected Docusign account**
text:
1. Select the Save and Close button for the
   envelope template in your Salesforce organization.
2. Log in to your Docusign account on another browser tab.
3. Select the Templates tab.

   The My Templates page opens.
4. Select the envelope template's name.

   The template's page opens.
5. Select the Edit button.

   A page with the template settings opens.
6. Select the Upload button.
7. Upload the PDF file from your device.
8. Select the SAVE AND CLOSE button.

   ![Save and close changes to your Docusign template](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/srl1586134368658/page/images/Save-and-close-changes-to-your-DocuSign-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg2ODUsInNoZWFmIjoic3JsMTU4NjEzNDM2ODY1OCJ9.SZCbX2dnBEGAHXz5twGJMBjD7-5jsC501ghsoPCmYgA&_LANG=enus "Save and close changes to your Docusign template")

   You have completed the process. You have added the PDF file that
   was too large to add to the template in Salesforce. This document is
   included in all envelopes that are sent from a Salesforce record using
   this template.

   Important: If you later
   edit this envelope template in Salesforce, you will get an error and
   will be unable to make additional changes.

title: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
heading_path: When I Upload a PDF to an Envelope Template, I Sometimes See This Error Message: “The Validation of the PDF File Failed.”
**Resolution 2: Upload the PDF file to Salesforce first and then attach it when creating an Envelope**
text:
1. Upload the PDF file to Notes and Attachments in
   a Salesforce record.
2. Complete the Envelope Template creation process.
3. Select the Envelope Template custom button from a Salesforce
   record.
4. Select the Add from Salesforce button. The Add
   from Salesforce dialog box opens.
5. Check the box for the PDF saved in Notes and Attachments.
6. Select the Add button.
7. Complete the envelope sending process.

   The process is complete. The PDF file that could not be added to
   the Envelope Template is added from Notes and Attachments during the
   sending process.

title: Learn How Document Writeback Updates Salesforce Fields When Signers Change Data on an Envelope
heading_path: Learn How Document Writeback Updates Salesforce Fields When Signers Change Data on an Envelope
text:
Document Writeback is a process that updates Salesforce data when it is changed by a
Docusign recipient. Salesforce fields are changed, and documents are updated or added to
Salesforce as a result of the eSignature process.

Document Writeback is a process that updates Salesforce data
when it is changed by a Docusign recipient.

Important:

- Document, merge field, and data writeback fails for Salesforce users with
  Multi-Factor Authentication for API Logins
  enabled.
- Data writeback is only available for text fields and picklist (dropdown) fields.
- The Date Signed value writes back to the Salesforce
  Recipient Status record by default.

The following examples demonstrate how writeback works:

Merge Fields Update: An eSignature
recipient updates a Salesforce merge field that was placed on a contract. The recipient
changes the Opportunity Amount field from $20,000 to $25,000.
When the recipient completes the signature, that Amount field in Salesforce is updated
to $25,000.

Save completed documents back to the Salesforce
Record: You send a document for eSignature using a Docusign custom
button. The recipient completes the signing process. Docusign uploads the final signed
version to your Salesforce record.

Stage Update: You send a document
for eSignature using a Docusign custom button. The recipient completes the signing
process. Docusign updates the Salesforce record Stage (Needs Analysis, Closed Won, etc.)
You can set these Document Writeback options when building an envelope template:

Table 1. Writeback Types

| Writeback Type | Writeback Action |
| --- | --- |
| Save completed documents back to the Salesforce Record | Save signed documents back to Salesforce when the signing process is complete |
| Combine all documents into one PDF file | Merge all documents from the envelope into one PDF file |
| Save certificate of completion (COC) document back to the Salesforce Record | Save a certificate containing all envelope events from the signing process |
| Stage Update | Set Salesforce status (Needs Analysis, Closed Won, etc.) when the signature is completed |
